## 2026-06-02 — Lines 2a/2b: Seller-financed mortgage interest (Gap 8 Tier 2) full implementation across 6 phases

Closed the long-standing seller-financed mortgage interest gap (`lines/2ab.md` Gap 8 / `XLS/Computations/2a.md` §3.2 Gap 2 / `outstanding.md` "Lines 2a/2b: Seller-Financed Mortgage Interest…" deferred entry from 2026-05-11). Per IRS 2025 Schedule B instructions, seller-financed mortgage interest is an independent Schedule B trigger (no $1,500 threshold), each row must list buyer name + address + SSN-or-EIN alongside the interest amount, and rows must appear FIRST in Part I.

**Phases:**
- **Phase 1 — V39 migration + entity.** New `pf_seller_financed_loan` child table (`parent_id` FK to `pf_interest_income.id` ON DELETE CASCADE, `idx` ordering, `buyer_name` NVARCHAR(200), `buyer_address` NVARCHAR(MAX) single text block, `buyer_ssn_or_ein` NVARCHAR(11) fits both SSN and EIN formats, `interest_amount` DECIMAL(19,4), standard timestamps, `UNIQUE(parent_id, idx)`). `PfSellerFinancedLoan` Panache entity. Master changelog `<include>` added per Liquibase no-auto-discover rule.
- **Phase 2 — Mapper round-trip.** `InterestIncomeMapper` extended with `sellerFinancedLoans` parent/child save+load+deleteForUid mirroring `MedicaidWaiverMapper`. Children delete + re-insert on every save (no orphan accumulation, free reorder).
- **Phase 3 — Backend compute + ordering + §17 flags.** `InterestPersonTotals` record gains separate `sellerFinancedScheduleBItems` field so the orchestrator can place them FIRST in Part I. `computeInterestForPerson` reads `sellerFinancedLoans` (null-safe via `getList`), validates each row's four required fields, sums positive amounts into `taxableInterestBeforeExclusion`, emits one item per valid row labelled with buyer name, fires `SELLER_FINANCED_LOAN_MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELDS_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}` listing which rows + which fields are incomplete. `computeInterestIncome` orchestrator OR's `hasSellerFinancedLoans` into the `scheduleBRequired` chain (independent trigger; no $1,500 check) and reorders Part I aggregation: `taxpayer-seller + spouse-seller + taxpayer-other + spouse-other`. Two new §17 codes added to `NonOverrideableFlags.CODES`.
- **Phase 4 — UI per-buyer repeater.** New "Seller-financed mortgage interest" section in `form-interest-income-{taxpayer,spouse}.component.ts` (top-level peer to "Interest adjustments", not nested). Empty-state CTA; `<section>` per row with buyer name, SSN/EIN via `TinDirective` (`appTin="any"`), multiline address textarea, interest amount; per-row remove + "Add another buyer" footer. Spouse version respects `isJointReturn` via `[disabled]`. `SellerFinancedLoanEntry` interface + model defaults + ngOnInit defensive array guard + `normalizeForSave` wipe for opt-out paths + 4 help map entries.
- **Phase 5 — E2E spec.** 4 tests added to `line2ab-interest-income.spec.ts`: trigger below threshold ($500 alone → Schedule B + Line 2b = $500); Part I ordering (2 seller-financed rows appear before 1 1099-INT row; Line 2b sum = $600); missing-fields §17 block (raw `?overrideFlags=true` returns 409 with non-overrideable flag naming the row + missing fields); save→load round-trip via `GET /api/personal/interest-income-taxpayer` (collection preserved with all 4 fields per row in original order).
- **Phase 6 — Spec/doc updates.** This entry. `lines/2ab.md` §12 verification log + `XLS/Computations/2a.md` §3.2 Gap 2 → CLOSED + `outstanding.md` "Lines 2a/2b…" entry → RESOLVED.

**Test counts:** 4 backend unit tests + 4 e2e tests = **8 tests**. Backend: 816/816 pass (was 812; +4 new). E2E: 12/12 pass on `line2ab-interest-income.spec.ts` (round-trip test required a one-line fix: `loaded.data.sellerFinancedLoans` since `PersonalResource.getForm` wraps payload in `PersonalFormResponse(formId, data)`).

**Migrations:** V39 (`pf_seller_financed_loan` child table).

**Files:** 2 new (V39 SQL + `PfSellerFinancedLoan.java`); modifications across `db.changelog-master.xml`, `InterestIncomeMapper.java`, `TaxReturnComputeService.java`, `NonOverrideableFlags.java`, `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`, both `form-interest-income-{taxpayer,spouse}.component.ts`, `line2ab-interest-income.spec.ts`, `lines/2ab.md`, `XLS/Computations/2a.md`, `outstanding.md`.

**Design notes:**
- Address shape: single multiline text block (not structured street/city/state/zip). IRS Schedule B Part I cell is a single text block per payer; structured split would over-engineer the actual output requirement. Per the field-source-of-truth rule (working code/IRS form over stale YAML), the planning-stage `yaml/sellerFinancedLoan.yaml` structured shape was not the authoritative spec.
- SSN-OR-EIN single text column (NVARCHAR(11) fits both `xxx-xx-xxxx` and `xx-xxxxxxx`); UI uses `appTin="any"` for client-side format validation; §17 missing-required-fields flag enforces presence at compute time.
- Section placement: top-level peer to "Interest adjustments" (NOT nested under `hasInterestAdjustments`) because seller-financed mortgage interest is conceptually an income source, not a less-common adjustment.

Companion entry below: same date, separate scope (Form 8815 Series EE/I bond exclusion).


## 2026-06-02 — Form 8815 (Series EE/I savings bond exclusion) full implementation across 6 phases

Closed the long-standing manual-entry-only limitation for Form 8815 by implementing the IRS Form 8815 lines 1-14 phaseout math + Line 6 + Line 9 Worksheets end-to-end. Closes Gap 1 from `XLS/Computations/2a.md` §3.2.

**Phases:**
- **Phase 0 — Specs.** Wrote `lines/f8815.md` (220 lines, developer-ready rule map) and `XLS/Computations/8815.md` (IRS-first audit).
- **Phase 1 — Backend pass-through.** Created `Form8815` POJO + `OutForm8815` entity + `Form8815OutputMapper` + V36 Flyway migration (`out_form_8815` table with 32 columns). Added `form8815` field to `TaxReturnComputation` record (touched 4 call sites: prepare(), TaxReturnDataService load, 2 test files). Added `computeForm8815()` pass-through method routing the existing `savingsBondExclusionAmount` to line 14. Created `us-tax-be/yaml/8815.yaml` output contract. 4 unit tests.
- **Phase 2 — UI sidebar + renderer.** Created `form-tax-return-8815.component.ts` using the `pdf-readonly-preview` pattern. Wired into `shell.component.ts` (8th gate flipped under the 2026-05-18 TEMP unconditional toggle). 2 e2e tests (`form8815-sidebar.spec.ts`).
- **Phase 3 — 15 intake fields + grouped accordion.** Added 15 fields to `form-interest-income-taxpayer.component.ts` (3 nested `<details>` sub-sections: institutions, expenses+proceeds, MAGI override). V37 migration. Updated YAML.
- **Phase 4 — Full compute + phaseout.** Added 6 ReferenceData constants (2025 phaseout thresholds). Replaced pass-through with full lines 1-14 math: MFS gate emits `FORM_8815_MFS_BLOCKED` flag; special-circumstance block; manual override precedence. 8 unit tests covering all phaseout boundaries.
- **Phase 5 — Line 6 + Line 9 Worksheets.** Added 8 worksheet input fields (3 Line 6 WS + 5 Line 9 WS) + 2 new accordion sub-sections + V38 migration + 2 helper methods + worksheet output writes. 3 unit tests.
- **Phase 6 — Lock-in + docs.** `e2e/tests/line2ab-form8815-fullflow.spec.ts` (5 tests). `test_cases/8815.md` per-test audit. Closed Gap 1 in `XLS/Computations/2a.md` §3.2. Updated `XLS/Computations/8815.md` Part 3 + Part 4.

**Test counts:** 15 backend unit tests + 7 e2e tests = **22 tests**. All 15 backend tests passing; e2e to be run from a normal terminal.

**Migrations:** V36 (out_form_8815 table), V37 (15 intake columns), V38 (8 worksheet columns).

**Files:** 11 new + 25 modifications. Semantic PDF + CSV generated under `pdfs/` and `us-tax-ui/public/irs/`.

**Known follow-up:** Gap 7 in `XLS/Computations/8815.md` §3.2 — when user provides line-by-line WITHOUT manual override, Form 1040 line 2b is not retroactively reduced (computeInterestIncome runs before computeForm8815). Workaround: also fill in manual override. Permanent fix requires moving computeForm8815 earlier in prepare().


## 2026-05-28 — Firestore→SQL cutover: e2e regression triage (buckets C–F + latent sweep) — committed `504fd9e` (be) / `777164d` (ui)

Closed the remaining e2e regressions left by the Firestore→Azure SQL cutover — all of the typed-mapper / schema bug classes. The full regression recovered from a near-total `clearUserData` failure to **458/479 green**; the only reds now are deliberately deferred (Bucket A K-1 1065, Bucket B 1099-DA box-2a) plus two pre-existing auth-gate tests.

**Migrations V24–V29 (us-tax-be):**
- **V24** — `pf_kiddie_income`: drop `fk_pf_kiddie_income_child_fs` / `_parent_fs` and widen `child/parent_filing_status` to `NVARCHAR(32)`. The kiddie form sends its own filing-status vocabulary (`single` / `married-filing-jointly` / …), not `ref_filing_status` codes; compute never reads these fields.
- **V25** — `se_1099_misc`: add `box3_is_strike_benefits` BIT (line 1h pools box-3 strike pay).
- **V26** — `pf_adoption_expenses`: add `magi_for_adoption_benefits_exclusion` (Form 8839 Part II/III phaseout; compute reads it with no AGI fallback).
- **V27** — `out_form_8949_page.term`: drop the stale `IN ('short','long')` CHECK + widen to 16 — compute writes `'Short-term'` / `'Long-term'`.
- **V28** — `pf_prior_min_tax_credit` + `out_form_8801`: drop the `prior_year_filing_status` → `ref_filing_status` FK on **both** input and output; they store the normalized label, not a code.
- **V29** — `out_form_8949_page.box`: expand CHECK to A–L (1099-DA digital-asset boxes G/H/J/K).

**Mappers / entities (us-tax-be):**
- `KiddieIncomeMapper` — persist the dependent-scoped `{ [dependentId]: {…} }` envelope via `dependent_id`; the taxpayer form stays flat.
- `Form1099MiscMapper` — round-trip `box3IsStrikeBenefits`.
- `AdoptionExpensesMapper` — round-trip `imports.magiForAdoptionBenefitsExclusion`.
- `DigitalAssetsMapper` — read all three input keys on save; emit `hadActivity` + `digitalAssetsYes` + `hadDigitalAssetActivity` on load so the per-person OR logic, both components, and the round-trip test all resolve.

**Reset / dependent delete (us-tax-be):**
- `UserDataBulkDelete` — delete `dependent` LAST. Four tables carry `dependent_id → dependent(id) ON DELETE NO ACTION` (SQL Server can't cascade them — multiple cascade paths from `app_user`). This was the cause of the mass `clearUserData` 500 once the kiddie mapper began populating `dependent_id`.
- `DependentService.deleteDependent` — clear the dependent's `pf_kiddie_income` rows before deleting the dependent.

**Frontend (us-tax-ui):**
- `shell.component.ts` — restore the real computation-presence guards for Schedule B / Form 6251 / Form 8606 (×2) / Schedule D / Form 8949 / Form 8814 (the 2026-05-18 dev toggle had force-shown them on low/simple-path returns). The remaining toggled output forms are intentionally left force-shown.

**e2e (us-tax-be):**
- `helpers/person-tabs.ts` — bound `selectPersonTab`'s direct click (5 s) so the taxpayer-tab relabel race (Family Head → first name, async `OwnerNamesService`) falls through to the first-tab fallback instead of hanging the whole test timeout.

**Deferred by decision (not cutover defects):** Bucket A — Schedule K-1 (1065) is an opaque-PDF-slot UI (`pdfRaw['page1_0_…']`) vs semantic-key compute (`section199AQualifiedBusinessIncomeAmount`) fork; the typed `ScheduleK1Form1065Mapper` is intentionally unregistered (`formIds()` empty), so reviving it needs a slot→semantic derivation layer or a UI rewrite. Bucket B — 1099-DA box-2a withholding mapper gap. Auth — the phone-existence gate isn't rendering its messages in the local ephemeral DB (pre-existing, unrelated). New guardrails recorded in `rules.md`.


## 2026-05-24 — Line 1f Gap #8 — G1 autoMagi now includes student-loan / bond / housing-deduction add-backs

Closed Gap #8 in `XLS/Computations/1f.md`. The G1 fallback in `applyAdoptionCredit` auto-derived MAGI from `AGI + Form 2555 lines 45/50 + Puerto Rico + Form 4563 line 15` — which covered most filers. The 2025 Form 8839 MAGI Worksheet asks for three additional add-backs that were missing: student-loan interest deduction (Schedule 1 line 21), Series EE/I U.S. savings bond interest exclusion (Schedule B line 3, from Form 8815), and foreign-housing deduction self-employed (Schedule 1 line 24j). For filers populating any of those lines, the pre-fix autoMagi was too LOW, producing a phaseout fraction that was too LOW, and a credit/exclusion that was slightly too GENEROUS.

**Backend (`applyAdoptionCredit`):**

1. **Signature extended.** Two new parameters: `Schedule1 schedule1`, `ScheduleB scheduleB`. The caller at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:1564` passes the already-built `schedule1` and `scheduleB` objects — both populated well before the adoption-credit application step (Schedule 1 at line 635, ScheduleB at line 669).
2. **Formula extended.** The G1 autoMagi computation now includes all six add-backs from the 2025 Form 8839 MAGI Worksheet:
   ```
   autoMagi = AGI
            + Puerto Rico excluded income
            + Form 2555 line 45 (foreign earned income exclusion)
            + Form 2555 line 50 (foreign housing exclusion — employee)
            + Form 4563 line 15 (American Samoa)
            + Student-loan interest deduction (Sch 1 line 21)        // NEW Gap #8
            + Savings bond interest exclusion (Sch B line 3)         // NEW Gap #8
            + Foreign-housing deduction self-employed (Sch 1 line 24j) // NEW Gap #8
   ```
3. **Safe-amount guards.** All add-backs use `safeAmount(...)` so missing values contribute 0. Returns without those Schedule 1 / B line items behave identically to pre-Gap-#8. Backwards-compatible with all existing G1 tests (which use empty Schedule 1/B).

**Direction-of-fix.** The new add-backs can only INCREASE autoMagi (never decrease), which can only REDUCE the credit / exclusion (never increase it). So the pre-fix error direction was always over-claiming, matching the audit's "slightly too generous a phase-out" description.

**Manual-entry path unchanged.** When the user enters `magiForAdoptionBenefitsExclusion` on the adoption-expenses form, the G1 gate `if (form8839.getPart2Line7ModifiedAgi() == null)` short-circuits and the user-entered value is used as-is. Backfilling a manual entry would override an explicit user choice. A future enhancement to cross-check manual MAGI against the augmented worksheet (emitting `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_MAGI_VERIFY_ADD_BACKS` advisory on $50+ mismatch) is tracked in `outstanding.md` "Form 8839 manual MAGI cross-check vs autoMagi worksheet".

**No interaction with other gaps.** §1372 (Gap #1), shared-adoption (Gap #2), gross/reimbursement (Gap #3), special-needs off-W-2 (Gap #4), L23 source (Gap #5), foreign-finality deferred (Gap #6), and PYAB write-in (Gap #7) all interact with the G1 result solely via `part2Line7ModifiedAgi` and `part3Line27PhaseoutFraction`, both updated correctly post-fix. The Gap #6 deferred amount, which augments L24 inside the L24-clamp, picks up the new phaseout fraction in the Part III re-run loop.

**Tests.** 4 new unit tests:
- `g1IncludesStudentLoanInterestAddBackInAutoDerivedMagi` — student loan alone; AGI $239,190 + $2,500 = $241,690 autoMagi (below threshold; locks wiring).
- `g1IncludesSeriesEeBondInterestExclusionAddBackInAutoDerivedMagi` — Form 8815 bond exclusion alone; AGI $235,190 + $4,000 = $239,190 autoMagi.
- `g1IncludesForeignHousingDeductionAddBackInAutoDerivedMagi` — Schedule 1 line 24j alone; AGI $239,190 + $6,000 = $245,190 autoMagi.
- `g1CombinesAllMagiAddBacksCorrectly` — student loan ($2,500) + bond exclusion ($3,500) + housing deduction ($6,000) + Puerto Rico ($35,000) all present; AGI $232,690 → autoMagi $279,690 → fraction 0.5125 (crosses threshold).

Full backend suite **790/790 pass** (was 786, +4 net). No frontend / YAML / E2E impact.

**Docs:** `XLS/Computations/1f.md` Gap #8 → RESOLVED (full close-out section with the add-back table + direction-of-fix note); `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` G1 autoMagi formula updated + gap-table entry added + verification log row appended; `outstanding.md` new "Form 8839 manual MAGI cross-check vs autoMagi worksheet" deferred entry; this entry.

**MILESTONE:** With Gap #8 closed, **all 8 issues raised in `XLS/Computations/1f.md` are now resolved** — Gap #1 §1372 (2026-05-23) / Gap #2 shared-adoption split (2026-05-23) / Gap #3 §137(d) gross/reimbursement (2026-05-23) / Gap #4 §137(a)(3) special-needs off-W-2 (2026-05-23) / Gap #5 line 23 source (2026-05-24) / Gap #6 foreign-finality deferred (2026-05-24) / Gap #7 PYAB write-in (2026-05-24) / Gap #8 MAGI worksheet add-backs (2026-05-24). Line 1f / Form 8839 implementation is now complete against the 2025 IRS instructions for all in-scope filer scenarios.


## 2026-05-24 — Line 1f Gap #7 — "PYAB" write-in rendered on Form 1040 line 1f

Closed Gap #7 in `XLS/Computations/1f.md`. When line 1f comes from a negative Form 8839 line 31 (line 30 > line 23 — the prior-year-adoption-benefits retroactive-exclusion scenario), the 2025 Form 1040 instructions ask the filer to write **PYAB** on the dotted line to the left of the line 1f amount. Previously the rendered Form 1040 omitted the annotation.

**Frontend (`form-tax-return-1040.component.ts`):** new generic `F1040Overlay` interface and `extraTextOverlays()` computed signal:

```typescript
interface F1040Overlay {
  page: number;
  rect: [number, number, number, number];
  text: string;
  ariaLabel: string;
}

extraTextOverlays = computed<F1040Overlay[]>(() => {
  const overlays: F1040Overlay[] = [];
  const adoptionNum = Number(this.form1040()?.income?.adoptionBenefits);
  if (!Number.isNaN(adoptionNum) && adoptionNum < 0) {
    overlays.push({
      page: 1,
      rect: [470, 270, 500, 281.999],   // dotted-line area left of line 1f field [504, 270, 576, 281.999]
      text: 'PYAB',
      ariaLabel: 'PYAB prior year adoption benefits write-in for line 1f'
    });
  }
  return overlays;
});
```

The template renders each overlay as an absolutely-positioned `<span>` via a new `*ngFor` block parallel to the existing field overlay loop. Coordinates use the same PDF system as `F1040Field.rect`, so `leftPercent` / `topPercent` / `widthPercent` / `heightPercent` helpers are reused without modification.

**SCSS** adds one rule `.form-field.text-overlay` that matches the regular text-field font (Arial, `1.34cqw`, right-aligned to hug the dotted line; non-interactive via `pointer-events: none`).

**Why generic overlays rather than a hardcoded PYAB span.** Form 1040 page 1 has several IRS-prescribed write-ins beyond PYAB: "DCB" (line 1e taxable dependent-care benefits), "F" prefix (line 1h Form 8919 wages), "PSO" + "ROLLOVER" (line 5b pensions). The generic `extraTextOverlays` mechanism reduces each future write-in to a single push into the overlay array. Gap #7 fills the PYAB slot; the other write-ins stay deferred until separately requested. (The `showDcbTag` computed signal added 2026-05-08 as part of 1e Issue #3 is preserved — a future DCB closure can wire it into the overlay array in one line.)

**Trigger condition.** `form1040.income.adoptionBenefits < 0`. Two known scenarios produce negative line 1f after the recent gap closures:
- Gap #4 (special-needs off-W-2; line 1f = −$17,280 typical) — closed 2026-05-23
- Gap #6 (foreign-finality deferred prior-year benefits; line 1f = varies) — closed 2026-05-24

The positive-line-1f case (regular taxable adoption benefits) does NOT show the badge.

**No backend change.** The PYAB write-in is purely presentational; the IRS does not reject a return for the missing annotation. All compute logic is untouched. Backend test count unchanged at 786/786.

**Tests.** Two existing E2E tests in `e2e/tests/line1f-adoption-benefits.spec.ts` extended with UI visibility assertions:
- `Special-needs off-W-2: negative line 1f produced even with no W-2 box T (IRC §137(a)(3))` (Gap #4 scenario)
- `Foreign-finality deferred prior-year benefits produce negative line 1f (IRC Pub 968)` (Gap #6 scenario)

Each test, after the API compute, navigates to the Form 1040 view via the sidebar and asserts:
```typescript
await openSidebarFormById(page, 'tax-return-1040', 'Tax Return', /Form 1040/i);
await page.locator('.f1040-pure-html-document').first().waitFor({ state: 'visible' });
await expect(page.locator('.f1040-pure-html-document .form-field.text-overlay[data-overlay="PYAB"]').first()).toBeVisible();
```

Frontend build clean.

**Docs:** `XLS/Computations/1f.md` Gap #7 → RESOLVED (full close-out section with the generic-mechanism rationale + trigger condition); `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` gap-table entry struck through + verification log row appended; this entry.

**MAJOR MILESTONE:** With Gap #7 closed, **all 7 open gaps in `XLS/Computations/1f.md` are now resolved** (Gap #1 §1372 — 2026-05-23; Gap #2 shared-adoption split — 2026-05-23; Gap #3 §137(d) gross/reimbursement — 2026-05-23; Gap #4 §137(a)(3) special-needs off-W-2 — 2026-05-23; Gap #5 line 23 source — 2026-05-24; Gap #6 foreign-finality deferred — 2026-05-24; Gap #7 PYAB write-in — 2026-05-24). Line 1f / Form 8839 implementation is complete against the 2025 IRS instructions for all in-scope filer scenarios. Gap #8 (Modified Adjusted Gross Income additional add-backs) remains deferred per `XLS/Computations/1f.md` as a narrow-population edge case.


## 2026-05-24 — Line 1f Gap #6 — Foreign-child finality-year retroactive exclusion computed

Closed Gap #6 in `XLS/Computations/1f.md`. For a foreign adoption finalising in 2025, employer-provided adoption benefits paid by the employer in YEARS PRIOR to 2025 (and taxed as wages on those prior-year returns) can be retroactively excluded in the finality year — producing a NEGATIVE Form 8839 line 31 → negative Form 1040 line 1f that reduces total wages (IRS Pub 968). Previously the engine only emitted a non-blocking advisory (`ADOPTION_BENEFITS_FOREIGN_CHILD_FINALITY_RETRO_PARTIAL`, added 2026-05-08 as a deferral marker) and the math under-claimed for affected filers.

**Backend (`computeAdoptionBenefits`):**

1. **New input map.** `deferredPriorYearForeignBenefitsByChild["child{N}DeferredPriorYearForeignBenefits"]` — per-child amount being retroactively claimed.
2. **L24 clamp augmented.** When `foreignChild && adoptionFinal && !specialNeeds`:
   ```java
   BigDecimal part3Line24 = part3Line21.min(safeAmount(part3Line22).add(safeAmount(deferred)));
   ```
   L24 still clamped at L21 (per-child remaining lifetime cap) — over-claiming structurally impossible.
3. **L22 unchanged.** Augmentation happens at L24, not L22. Preserves Gap #5's IRS-form-23 definition (`L23 = ΣL22 = current-year W-2 box T allocation`) and keeps the allocation-mismatch check working without a special-case relaxation. Negative L31 then arises naturally: `L31 = L23 − L30 = ΣL22 − Σ(L22 + deferred) = −Σdeferred` (before phaseout).
4. **`allowPartIII` gate opened.** Was `hasBenefits || hasSpecialNeedsFinalChild`; now also opens when any child has `foreignChild && adoptionFinal && !specialNeeds && deferred > 0`. Mirrors the Gap #4 special-needs gate-opening pattern.
5. **`hasAdoptionInputs` + `hasPartIIIInputs` extended.** Deferred-only entries now count as valid Part III inputs, triggering Form 8839 generation.
6. **Off-W-2 gate exception.** The `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_OFF_W2_NOT_SPECIAL_NEEDS_CHILD_N` blocking gate (added Issue #11, 2026-05-08) given a narrow exception: foreign-final non-special-needs children with a positive deferred amount may legitimately have L20 > 0 (prior-year lifetime-cap consumption) without being blocked.
7. **Advisory removed.** `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_FOREIGN_CHILD_FINALITY_RETRO_PARTIAL` deleted — the deferred mechanism now satisfies the rule structurally.

**Output model.** New `Form8839Child.part3DeferredPriorYearForeignBenefits` read-only audit-trail field, populated only when the deferred path is active for that child; null otherwise (including for the special-needs branch, which structurally ignores the input).

**Frontend (`form-adoption-expenses`).** New per-child input under the existing "Less common situations" toggle, visible only when child is `isForeignChild && adoptionFinal2025OrEarlier && !hasSpecialNeeds`. Help text distinguishes the new field from the existing L20 prior-year-used input (L20 reduces the remaining cap; this one claims the exclusion). The `mergeDefaults` block initialises the new map for legacy returns; auto-expand toggle now also fires when any deferred amount is saved.

**Design choice — L24 vs L22 injection.** The original deferred sketch (2026-05-08) proposed adding the deferred amount to L22 and relaxing the allocation-mismatch check. Final design instead augments L24, which (a) preserves L22's IRS-defined meaning, (b) leaves the allocation-mismatch check intact, and (c) interacts cleanly with the §1372 partition (Gap #1) and shared-adoption cap (Gap #2). Verified by trace through every other gap.

**No interaction with other gaps:** §1372 partition rebinds `w2TotalBenefits` to the eligible-only sum (deferred sits outside); shared-adoption per-child cap feeds L19 → L21 and still clamps deferred; gross/reimbursement input split (Gap #3) is Part II only; special-needs branch is `L24 = L21` regardless (deferred ignored); Gap #5 (L23 = ΣL22) is a prerequisite (without it L23 would shadow the W-2 total and the negative math would miscompute).

**Tests.** 2 old advisory tests deleted (`emitsForeignChildFinalityRetroAdvisoryWhenPriorYearBenefitsPresent`, `noForeignChildFinalityRetroAdvisoryWhenSpecialNeeds`). 5 new unit tests:
- `foreignFinalityYearDeferredPriorYearBenefitsProduceNegativeLine1f` — headline case: $5,000 deferred + zero W-2 → line 1f = −$5,000.
- `foreignFinalityYearDeferredAmountClampedByRemainingLifetimeCap` — L20 = $12,000 + deferred = $10,000 → L24 clamped at L21 ($5,280), line 1f = −$5,280.
- `foreignFinalityYearDeferredAmountInteractsWithMagiPhaseoutCorrectly` — MAGI = $279,190 (50% phaseout) → line 1f = −$2,500 (halved).
- `deferredPriorYearForeignBenefitsIgnoredWhenNotForeignChild` — domestic adoption with deferred entry: line 1f = $0 (input ignored).
- `deferredPriorYearForeignBenefitsIgnoredForSpecialNeedsChild` — special-needs branch: line 1f = −$17,280 (full cap, deferred NOT additive).

Plus 1 new E2E test: `Foreign-finality deferred prior-year benefits produce negative line 1f (IRC Pub 968)`. Full backend suite **786/786 pass** (was 783 + 5 − 2 = 786).

**Docs:** `XLS/Computations/1f.md` Gap #6 → RESOLVED (full close-out section with the L24 vs L22 design rationale + cross-gap interaction trace); `dependencies/1f.md` adds the new output field; `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` gap-table entry struck through + verification log row appended; `outstanding.md` deferred entry replaced with RESOLVED block; `lines/1f.md` §5.2 per-child user inputs list extended; this entry.

**Affected user population:** rare — foreign adoptions finalising in 2025 with employer-provided benefits paid in earlier years. Filers in this scenario typically use a tax professional; before this fix the advisory directed them to consult Form 8839 instructions, and the line 1f would have shown the under-claimed positive number. After this fix the negative-L31 mechanism is automatic when the user enters the deferred amount.


## 2026-05-24 — Line 1f Gap #5 — Form 8839 line 23 source change: `sum(L22[i])` instead of `w2TotalBenefits`

Closed Gap #5 in `XLS/Computations/1f.md`. The IRS Form 8839 definition of line 23 is the literal sum of the per-child line 22 amounts. The previous code wrote `part3Line23 = safeAmount(w2TotalBenefits)` (the W-2 box 12 code T total) and relied on the allocation-mismatch check (±$1 tolerance) to enforce agreement. Within that tolerance the two values could differ by up to $1 — and the literal per-child sum is what the IRS form asks for.

**Backend:** one-line change in `TaxReturnComputeService.computeAdoptionBenefits` (around line 13799):

```java
// was: part3Line23 = allowPartIII ? safeAmount(w2TotalBenefits) : null;
BigDecimal part3Line23 = allowPartIII ? safeAmount(totalPart3Line22) : null;
```

The `totalPart3Line22` accumulator is already populated inside the per-child loop, so no new fields or wiring needed.

**Behaviour:**
- Allocation matches W-2 box T exactly (common case): no change. `totalPart3Line22 == w2TotalBenefits`.
- Allocation within ±$1 tolerance (rare): line 23, line 30, line 31, and Form 1040 line 1f now follow the literal per-child sum. Strictly more accurate.
- Allocation diff > $1: blocking `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_ALLOCATION_MISMATCH` still fires; behaviour unchanged.

**No interaction with other gaps:** traced through §1372 partition (Gap #1; `w2TotalBenefits` is rebound to eligible-only sum and allocation check enforces match against it), shared-adoption split (Gap #2), gross/reimbursement input split (Gap #3), special-needs off-W-2 (Gap #4; `totalPart3Line22 = null` → `safeAmount(null) = 0` correctly), and the G1 `applyAdoptionCredit` fallback (preserves Phase 1's `part3Line23TotalBenefits` and doesn't recompute it).

**Tests:** 1 new unit test `part3Line23ReflectsLiteralAllocationSumNotW2BoxTWhenWithinTolerance` — single child, W-2 box T = $5,000, user allocates $5,001 (within tolerance), MAGI = $279,190 (exactly 50% phaseout). Pre-fix line 1f = $2,500 (HALF_UP of $5,000 − $2,500.50 = $2,499.50). Post-fix line 1f = $2,501 (HALF_UP of $5,001 − $2,500.50 = $2,500.50). Diff exactly $1 — the same amount the user entered above the W-2 box T. All 580 existing compute-service tests unchanged. Full backend suite **783/783** (was 782).

**Docs:** `XLS/Computations/1f.md` Gap #5 → RESOLVED with the IRS-form-definition note + the unchanged behaviour matrix + the cross-gap interaction trace; `dependencies/1f.md` Form8839 return-level field note enriched on `part3Line23TotalBenefits`; `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` gap table entry added + verification log row appended; `outstanding.md` Gap #4 closure note updated to record that the L23 source change was applied separately as Gap #5; `lines/1f.md` already states `L23 = sum(L22[i])` — no change needed (it described the IRS definition, not the prior code shape); this entry.

**Cosmetic gap, but worth tightening:** the difference is invisible in 99% of returns. The fix matters in the narrow case where a user's explicit allocation differs from the W-2 box T total by up to $1 — typically because of a per-child rounding choice. Now the user's allocation is honoured.


## 2026-05-23 — Line 1f Gap #4 (IRC §137(a)(3)) — special-needs off-W-2 negative line 1f

Closed Gap #4 in `XLS/Computations/1f.md`. IRC §137(a)(3) allows a special-needs adoption finalised in 2025 to exclude the full per-child remaining lifetime cap even when the W-2 box 12 code T is zero — producing a NEGATIVE Form 8839 line 31 → Form 1040 line 1f. Previously the `allowPartIII = hasBenefits` gate blocked Part III from running when there was no W-2 box T, so the negative line 1f was never computed.

**Backend (`computeAdoptionBenefits`):** two small edits:
1. New predicate `hasSpecialNeedsFinalChild` OR'd into the gate: `allowPartIII = hasBenefits || hasSpecialNeedsFinalChild`. Part III loop now runs whenever the special-needs override is active, even with zero W-2 box T.
2. Final `line1f` derivation triggers on `partIIIProducedResult = allowPartIII && line31 != null` rather than `hasBenefits`, so a negative `line31` reaches `Form1040.income.adoptionBenefits`.

No frontend changes (existing `hasSpecialNeeds` + `adoptionFinal2025OrEarlier` + `priorYearEmployerBenefitsByChild` inputs sufficient). No new output fields. No reference data changes.

**Edge cases:** phaseout to full (MAGI ≥ $299,190) zeros the negative adjustment; prior-year benefits ≥ $17,280 zero L21 (cap consumed); MFS-disallowed early-return supersedes; §1372-disallowed slice still stacks on top; mixed-children scenarios (special-needs-final + regular) correctly contribute only from the special-needs child.

**Tests:** 1 existing test renamed (`specialNeedsOffW2BenefitsDoNotBlockButPartIIINotEnabled` → `specialNeedsOffW2EnablesPartIIIAndProducesNegativeLine1f`) and its assertions flipped from "Part III not enabled" to the actual negative-line-1f assertions; 3 new unit tests (prior-year-benefits-reduces, mixed-children-only-special-contributes, fully-phased-out-zeros-the-adjustment); 1 new E2E test. Backend suite 782/782.

**Docs:** `XLS/Computations/1f.md` Gap #4 → RESOLVED; validation row #5 updated; `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` Issue #11 deferred remainder closed; `outstanding.md` "Form 8839 Special-Needs Off-W-2 Part III Enablement" entry struck through; `lines/1f.md` §4.2 implementation note added; this entry.

**Surprise:** the deferred sketch suggested also changing `part3Line23 = safeAmount(totalPart3Line22)` (instead of `w2TotalBenefits`). Wasn't needed — `safeAmount(null) = 0` already produces the correct L23 value when `hasBenefits=false`. The math works out of the box.


## 2026-05-23 — Line 1f Gap #3 (IRC §137(d)) — same-expense coordination: gross + reimbursement two-input model

Closed Gap #3 in `XLS/Computations/1f.md`. IRC §137(d) prohibits using the same dollar of qualified adoption expense for both the §137 exclusion (Part III) AND the §23 credit (Part II). Previously enforced by a per-child non-blocking advisory (`ADOPTION_BENEFITS_VERIFY_NO_DOUBLE_DIP_CHILD_N`) that asked the user to verify — naive users could over-claim silently. Now structurally enforced by the input shape.

**Frontend:** the per-child "Qualified adoption expenses" input was split into two:
- `qualifiedAdoptionExpensesByChild["child{N}QualifiedExpensesGross"]` — gross paid.
- `qualifiedAdoptionExpensesByChild["child{N}EmployerReimbursementToSubtract"]` — reimbursement to subtract. Pre-fills from the per-child Part III allocation when the user enters a gross value; user can override.
- A read-only **net** display shows `max(0, gross − reimbursement)` in real time so users see what's flowing to Form 8839 Part II line 5.

**Backend:** `computeAdoptionBenefits` per-child loop replaces the single `part2Line5 = parseAmount(...)` with `part2Line5 = max(0, gross − reimbursement)`. The same dollar can no longer reach both Part II and Part III because the inputs are separated.

**Advisory removed:** `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_VERIFY_NO_DOUBLE_DIP_CHILD_N` is deleted entirely (along with its 3 unit tests). The math now enforces the rule; there's nothing to advise about.

**Migration:** None (pre-production scope). The old `child{N}QualifiedExpenses` field is no longer read by the backend.

**Tests:** 2 new unit tests + 1 E2E test. 7 existing unit tests + 8 E2E tests updated to the new field names. 3 obsolete unit tests deleted. Backend suite 779/779.

**Docs:** `XLS/Computations/1f.md` Gap #3 → RESOLVED; validation row #13 verdict flipped; `dependencies/1f.md` (new field rows); `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` Issue #8 closed; `outstanding.md` entry struck through; `lines/1f.md` §4.4 marked IMPLEMENTED; xlsx regenerated.


## 2026-05-23 — Line 1f Gap #2 (IRC §137(b)(1)) — shared adoption with a non-spouse: per-child cap split

Closed the second of the eight known Gap items in `XLS/Computations/1f.md`. When the same eligible child is being adopted by the taxpayer AND another adult who is not the spouse on a joint return, the $17,280 per-child cap must be divided between them in any way they agree.

**Inputs (`form-adoption-expenses`):** New under "Less common situations" toggle —
- `children.sharedAdoptionAnyChild` outer screening (required when any child has Part II expense or Part III benefit).
- `children.entries[i].sharedAdoptionWithNonSpouse` per-child Yes/No.
- `children.entries[i].sharedAdoptionAllocatedShare` per-child dollar amount (0–$17,280).

**Backend (`TaxReturnComputeService.java`):** New helper `resolveSharedAdoptionCap(maxPerChild, childIndex, adoptionData)`. The per-child loop in `computeAdoptionBenefits` now reads the resolved cap and uses it for BOTH `part2Line2` (Part II credit base) and `part3Line19` (Part III exclusion base). The existing line 4 / line 21 / line 24 / line 29 arithmetic propagates the reduced cap automatically.

**Output (`Form8839Child`):** Existing `part2Line2MaxCredit` and `part3Line19MaxExclusion` per-child fields now reflect the resolved per-child cap (was always $17,280). No new output fields.

**Advisory flag (non-blocking):** `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_SHARED_ADOPTION_CAP_SPLIT` fires when any child's cap was reduced, listing the affected children + reduced amounts for the audit trail.

**Tests:** 3 unit + 1 E2E; backend suite 780/780. Existing 5 §1372-affected E2E tests adopted the renamed helper `answerLessCommonNo` which now handles both §1372 and shared-adoption screening questions.

**Docs:** `XLS/Computations/1f.md` Gap #2 → RESOLVED; `dependencies/1f.md`; `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` Issue #6 closed; `outstanding.md` entry struck through; `lines/1f.md` §9.3 marked IMPLEMENTED.

**Out of scope:** No cross-party allocation validation (cannot detect coordinated over-claim where both parties allocate more than $17,280 in aggregate); each side is clamped at $17,280 independently.


## 2026-05-23 — Line 1f Gap #1 (IRC §1372) — more-than-2% S-corp owner exclusion disallowance

Closed the first of the eight known Gap items in `XLS/Computations/1f.md`. When the employer is a Subchapter S corporation in which the employee owns more than 2% of the stock or voting power at any point in the year, the §137 adoption-assistance exclusion is disallowed and the entire W-2 box 12 code T amount is taxable wages.

**Inputs (`form-adoption-expenses` only — W-2 form untouched per user directive):** New under a new "Less common situations" toggle —
- `employerBenefitsPartIII.section1372AnyEmployer` outer screening (required when any W-2 has box 12 code T > 0).
- `employerBenefitsPartIII.section1372ByEmployer` (Record<W-2 entryId → boolean>) per-W-2 review shown when screening is Yes.

**Backend:** Helper `computeSection1372DisallowedFromEligibleW2BoxT(w2Entries, adoptionData, ssns…)`. Inside `computeAdoptionBenefits`, after the IRC §137(f) MFS-disallowed early-return, partitions `w2TotalBenefits` into eligible and disallowed slices. The Form 8839 Part III computation runs only on the eligible slice; the disallowed slice stacks directly onto Form 1040 line 1f. The G1 fallback in `applyAdoptionCredit` also re-stacks the disallowed slice when MAGI is auto-derived.

**Output:** New `Form8839.section1372DisallowedFromW2BoxT` field surfaces the disallowed amount for the audit trail.

**Advisory flag (non-blocking):** `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_SECTION_1372_DISALLOWED` fires when the disallowed slice is positive.

**Tests:** 3 unit + 1 E2E; backend suite 777/777.

**Out of scope:** IRC §1372(b) constructive ownership via §318 attribution is captured in the screening question's wording but not separately computed.


## 2026-05-21 — Child and dependent care form: 5-section restructure + dependent picker + W-2 attribution panel + SSN formatting

Comprehensive UX overhaul of the Child and dependent care form (`form-childcare-expenses.component`) per a four-part audit (W-2 auto-extraction, SSN/EIN handling, question order, language simplicity). Phases:

- **Phase 1 — SSN/EIN formatting + autocomplete-off.** Applied `appTin` directive (`appTin="individual"` on qualifying-person SSN, `appTin="any"` on provider identifying number), `autocomplete="off"`, and `data-1p-ignore="true"` to prevent browser password managers from offering to store third-party SSNs. Closes the gap that left these fields plain `pInputText` while every other form in the system (tip income, Form 8919) was already using the directive.
- **Phase 2 — 5-section restructure + plain-English labels.** Reordered into: Screening → **People you cared for** (moved up; was Qualifying Persons Part II) → **Care providers** → **Employer-provided benefits** (with MFS exception inline when MFS) → **Less common situations** (new collapsible). Dropped the `(Part I)` / `(Part II)` / `(Part III)` suffixes from section headers. Rewrote labels for MFS exception, §129(d) nondiscrimination, forfeited-benefits (dropped technically-incorrect "rolled over" wording), line 16 override, over-12-and-disabled (added under-13-answer-No guidance), and the §129(d) help text. The "Less common situations" toggle auto-expands on load when any of its fields are already populated.
- **Phase 3 — W-2 attribution panel.** Replaces the single "W-2 box 10 total" info-note with a richer per-W-2 panel showing employer name, Box 10 amount, and recipient label ("You" / "Spouse" / "Unknown") computed by comparing each W-2's `employeeSSN` against the household identification forms. Same pattern used by the Medicaid waiver picker. Helps MFJ couples see exactly which spouse's W-2 contributed which amount.
- **Phase 4 — Dependent SSN auto-link.** New `loadDependents()` calls `PersonalDataService.listDependents()` and surfaces the household's dependents in a "People you cared for" picker. Each picker row shows name, masked SSN, and an "Under 13" / "Over 12 — verify disability" age badge computed from the dependent's date of birth. Clicking "Add as person you cared for" pre-fills the qualifying-person row with first name, last name, SSN, and (when under 13) automatically sets the disability flag to No so the user doesn't need to answer.

UI changes touch `form-childcare-expenses.component.{ts,html,scss}`. New TS state: `w2BenefitAttribution[]`, `allDependents[]`, `showLessCommonSituations`, taxpayer/spouse SSN cache. New helpers: `loadHouseholdSsns`, `loadOneSsn`, `recipientLabelFor`, `normalizeSsn`, `extractEmployerName`, `maskSsn`, `formatCurrencyAmount`, `loadDependents`, `buildAvailableDependent`, `computeAgeAtYearEnd`, `availableDependents`, `pickDependent`.

E2E sweep — `line1e-dependent-care.spec.ts` (12 existing tests):
- `ensureDependentCareBenefitsYes` helper updated to wait on `#alreadyIncludedInBox1Amount` (the new primary anchor) instead of the line-16 override field (which moved into the Less common situations toggle).
- New `expandLessCommon` helper added to the spec.
- Tests that interact with `line16QualifiedExpensesForBenefits` or `studentOrDisabledMonths*` now call `expandLessCommon(page)` before touching those fields.
- `Add qualifying person` button → `Add person` (button renamed).

3 new tests added: dependent picker pre-fills qualifying-person row with name/SSN/disability default; W-2 attribution panel shows per-W-2 recipient label on Married Filing Jointly; Less common situations toggle hides edge-case questions until expanded.

Backend: zero changes. The childcare-expenses JSON shape is unchanged, and `computeDependentCareBenefits` + `finalizeForm2441PartII` read the same fields. No flag changes.

Documentation swept: `XLS/Computations/1e.md` (new §2.8 "Form layout (UI flow)"), `lines/1e.md` §9 interview question flow, `knowledge/line-1e-dependent-care-benefits.md` §7 Frontend Forms.

Type-check + `npm run build` both green throughout.


## 2026-05-20 — Medicaid waiver form: 6-section reorder + statement picker + broadened Schedule C flag

Two related changes to the Medicaid waiver form for line 1d:

### Backend — broadened Schedule C blocking flag

`MEDICAID_WAIVER_SCHEDULE_C_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}` previously required `hasAmounts` (qualified or taxable > 0) as a co-condition. Removed: the flag now fires whenever `receivedMedicaidWaiverPayments=true` AND `hasTradeOrBusinessProvidingHomeCare=true`, regardless of whether amounts have been entered yet. Moved the emission before the entries-empty early-return so the no-amounts case also blocks compute. Action-oriented message rewrite naming the section and giving both repair options (change the business answer to No, or remove the Medicaid waiver entries). New unit test `flagsMedicaidWaiverBusinessPath_firesEvenWithNoAmounts` (572/572 green).

### UI — six-section reorder + cross-statement picker

Both `form-medicaid-waiver-{taxpayer,spouse}.component.ts` now present:
1. Screening (unchanged)
2. **Classification gate (moved up)** — trade-or-business question first; when Yes, sections 3–6 are hidden and a prominent Schedule C banner explains that the return cannot be computed until the answer is changed back.
3. **Available statements picker (NEW)** — lists matching W-2, 1099-MISC, and 1099-NEC statements that are not yet linked to a row. Shows ALL recipients (taxpayer + spouse SSNs) so the user can verify before bringing a statement into the wrong person's form. Each picker row has a "Bring into form" button that creates a pre-filled entry. Auto-extract rules: W-2 without code II → Box 1 wages as taxable; 1099-MISC Box 3 / Box 6 → as taxable; 1099-NEC Box 1 → as taxable. Both Box 3 and Box 6 are surfaced as separate picker rows when populated. Default-to-taxable rule: the picker never auto-classifies an amount as Notice 2014-7 qualified — that's a legal-eligibility determination only the user can make.
4. Payment entries — pre-filled from the picker where applicable. Existing W-2 code II auto-create on `ngOnInit` continues to run as before.
5. **Program context (moved down)** — program name, care-recipient relationship, shared-home flag.
6. **Earned-income election (moved to end)** — asked LAST so the user has seen the qualified amounts above and can make an informed Yes/No choice.

5 new E2E tests added covering: (a) trade-or-business Yes with no entries still blocks compute, (b) picker brings in a 1099-MISC Box 3 entry, (c) picker brings in a 1099-NEC Box 1 entry, (d) picker shows W-2 without code II and pre-fills Box 1 as taxable, (e) picker shows spouse-recipient statements in the taxpayer form. Existing 4 tests updated to flip the question-answer order (business first, election last). Type-check + `npm run build` both green.

Documentation swept: `XLS/Computations/1d.md` §2.5 + new §2.8 (UI flow); `lines/1d.md` §9 (interview question flow); `dependencies/1d.md` Schedule C row; `knowledge/line-1d-medicaid-waiver-payments.md` §4 Schedule C + §6 Frontend Forms.


## 2026-05-20 — Save-vs-compute pivot + action-oriented messages for Medicaid waiver flags

Applied the save-vs-compute principle (memory: `feedback_save_vs_compute_validation`) to the two Medicaid waiver flags that previously refused save at the UI, and rewrote four flag messages in the action-oriented style established for the line-1c flags on 2026-05-19:

- **`MEDICAID_WAIVER_CODE_II_MISSING_QUALIFIED_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}`** — UI save block removed; advisory hint added next to the qualified-amount field whenever a Box 12 code II amount is present without a matching qualified amount. Backend message rewritten in action-oriented style naming the payer and giving the fix steps.
- **`MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_BOX1_UNSPECIFIED_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}`** — same: UI save block removed; advisory hint added; message rewritten. Explains both valid answers (0 for modern 2024+ box-12-code-II reporting; positive for legacy box-1 reporting).
- **`MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_OVERAGE_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}`** — new red `advisory-hint.warning` block surfaces the inconsistency immediately at data entry. Message rewritten with both repair options (reduce included-in-box-1, or increase total qualified amount).
- **`MEDICAID_WAIVER_ELECTION_NO_QUALIFIED_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}`** — new advisory hint shown near the election radio buttons whenever the election is on but no qualified amount is entered in any row. Message rewritten with both repair options (enter a qualified amount, or turn off the election).

UI changes touch both `form-medicaid-waiver-{taxpayer,spouse}.component.ts`. `isValid()` slimmed to keep only hard structural fields (payer name, source type, qualified-or-taxable amount present). New `.advisory-hint` and `.advisory-hint.warning` styles in both components. E2E sweep through `medicaid-waiver.spec.ts`: the old "code II requires qualified amount before save" test (which asserted the save block) was rewritten to assert the new behaviour — hint visible, save succeeds. Two new save-vs-compute tests added (one each for CODE_II_MISSING_QUALIFIED and W2_BOX1_UNSPECIFIED). Backend: 571/571 unit tests pass unchanged (existing tests assert flag codes, not message text). Documentation swept: `XLS/Computations/1d.md` §2.5, `knowledge/line-1d-medicaid-waiver-payments.md` flag inventory.


## 2026-05-20 — Tip-income form: W-2 picker + Box 14 RRTA fuzzy match + Box 3/Box 7 display-only

Three UX upgrades to `form-tip-income-{taxpayer,spouse}.component.ts` that move tip-income data entry closer to "extract from the W-2 the worker already uploaded; have them verify; don't make them re-type":

- **W-2 picker.** A new blue "Available W-2s (n)" panel surfaces at the bottom of the employer-detail area, listing matching-SSN W-2s not currently linked to any tipsByEmployer row. Each entry shows employer name + EIN + key tip numbers (Box 8 allocated, Box 7 SS tips). A "Bring into form" button calls a new `pickW2()` method that pushes a fully-prefilled row. Auto-create on `ngOnInit` continues for matching W-2s as before — the picker handles the cases where the row was removed, or the W-2 was uploaded after the form was last loaded.
- **Box 14 RRTA fuzzy match.** New `tryFillRrtaFromBox14()` helper scans the W-2's `box14Other` array for labels matching `/rrta|tier\s*[12]|railroad/i` (case-insensitive). On hit: prefills `rRTACompensationW2Box14` with the Box 14 amount, stores the matched label in a new UI-only `rRTAAutoFillSourceLabel` field, ticks `hasRrtaCompensation`, and renders an attribution banner ("From W-2 Box 14 code 'XYZ' — confirm this is RRTA compensation. Uncheck the box above to clear if it's not."). Mirrors the medicaid-waiver Box 12 code II auto-fill UX.
- **Box 3 + Box 7 display-only when W-2-sourced.** When the row was auto-created from a W-2 (`w2AutoFilledFrom` set), Box 3 / Box 7 are rendered as read-only currency-formatted values with a note pointing the user to fix the W-2 at source if wrong. This preserves the existing `line1cSsWageBaseW2IsCanonicalEvenWhenZero` contract (W-2 is canonical even when boxes are explicit zero) — no backend change needed. When the row was added manually with no W-2 backing, the editable fallback inputs continue to show.

`npx tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` both green (only pre-existing unrelated warnings). Knowledge file `line-1c-tip-income.md` §4 updated to describe the new UX. No backend changes; no flag changes.


## 2026-05-19 — Save-vs-compute pivot for Form 4137 EIN + adequate-records claim + Form 8919 firm ID

Applied the save-vs-compute principle (memory: `feedback_save_vs_compute_validation`) to three cross-form validations that previously blocked save at the UI:

- **`ADEQUATE_RECORDS_MISSING_SUBSTANTIATED_AMOUNT_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}`** — flipped from non-blocking to **blocking** at compute. UI no longer refuses save; an advisory hint appears next to the substantiated-amount field when the claim is true and the amount is missing.
- **`MISSING_EMPLOYER_EIN_FORM_4137_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}`** — same flip. UI no longer refuses save; an advisory hint appears next to the EIN field when the row would trigger Form 4137 and the EIN is blank.
- **`MISSING_FIRM_FEDERAL_ID_FORM_8919_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}`** — **new** blocking flag added in `computeForm8919ForPerson(...)`. Form 8919 firmFederalIdNumber was previously never enforced; compute would silently produce a Form 8919 that IRS e-file rejects. Save remains unblocked; literal `unknown` is accepted by the IRS.

All three blocking flags include action-oriented messages naming the section ("Tip income (taxpayer)" / "Uncollected Social Security and Medicare on wages (spouse)") and the fix steps. Unit tests updated (`TaxReturnComputeServiceTest` — 3 modified, 3 added; 571/571 green). E2E tests updated — sweep through `line1c-tip-income.spec.ts` + `line1g-uncollected-ss-medicare.spec.ts`; 2 new save-succeeds-compute-blocks specs added; `addFirm` helper now default-fills firmFederalIdNumber to keep existing UI-compute tests green. Docs swept: `XLS/Computations/1c.md` §2.5 + §3 + §3.2, `dependencies/1c.md`, `dependencies/1g.md`, `knowledge/line-1c-tip-income.md` §4, `knowledge/line-1g-form-8919.md`.


## 2026-05-17 — Input/Output forms xlsx audit complete (162 files; 2 stale corrected) — **★ FIRST META-AUDIT of XLS/input_forms + XLS/output_forms folders; ★ generator-driven approach (re-run canonical generators rather than manual file-by-file); ★ 160 of 162 files (98.8%) already in sync; ★ 2 stale files corrected via regeneration; ★ NO orphan files; ★ NO new gaps**

- **Scope**: All 118 files in `C:\us-tax\XLS\input_forms\` + all 44 files in `C:\us-tax\XLS\output_forms\` = 162 total xlsx field manifests.
- **Approach**: Used the canonical generators (`XLS/_tools/generate-form-xlsx.js` for input forms + a subset of output forms; `XLS/_tools/generate-output-xlsx.js` for tax-return output forms from IRS PDF field maps). These are the same generators that produce these xlsx files from authoritative sources (Angular forms / YAML specs / IRS PDF field maps). Establishes the META-AUDIT pattern: rather than reading 162 files individually, snapshot md5s → re-run generators → diff to identify stale files.
- **Results**:
  - **2 of 162 files were stale** (98.8% in sync):
    - `input_forms/form-interest-income-taxpayer.xlsx` — regenerated; now reflects current YAML structure (5 sections: screening / statementUploadCheck / supplementalInterestAmounts / interestAdjustments / importedStatementFields; 44 fields).
    - `input_forms/form-interest-income-spouse.xlsx` — regenerated; same structure tailored for spouse (4 sections; 32 fields).
  - **160 of 162 files unchanged** (already in sync with current code state).
  - **NO orphan files** — every xlsx in both folders corresponds to a current YAML/Angular component/PDF field map.
- **Why so few stale files**: The most recent broad refresh was 2026-05-09 (line 1g audit Issue #2 fixed the `generate-form-xlsx.js` regex for `<p-select>` / `<p-datepicker>` and regenerated all 118 input forms). Since then, only the interest-income forms received content changes (likely YAML schema additions) that hadn't been propagated back to the xlsx manifests.
- **Generator output summary**:
  - `generate-form-xlsx.js`: Processed all `us-tax-ui/src/app/forms/*` components + 90 YAML files in `yamls/`. YAML-first extraction (canonical source for sections with `multiplicity: multiple`, conditional requiredness, etc.); template-scan fallback for components without YAML.
  - `generate-output-xlsx.js`: Processed 44 tax-return forms. Skipped 1 known no-PDF form (`form-tax-return-required-attachment` — HTML view only). Total documented: 2,901 PDF fields.
- **Workflow significance**:
  - ★ **FIRST META-AUDIT of input_forms + output_forms folders** in workflow.
  - ★ **Generator-driven approach** — re-run canonical generators rather than manually auditing 162 files; establishes new META-AUDIT pattern for field-manifest folders.
  - ★ NO new outstanding.md gap entries — Path A applied.
  - ★ NO backend code changes — purely a doc/manifest regeneration.
  - ★ Backend tests: 765/765 unchanged (22nd consecutive audit with zero new tests).
- **Documentation accumulated**:
  - `XLS/input_forms/form-interest-income-taxpayer.xlsx` (regenerated; 44 fields).
  - `XLS/input_forms/form-interest-income-spouse.xlsx` (regenerated; 32 fields).
  - `XLS/_tools/inspect-xlsx.js` (NEW helper script for xlsx inspection during audits).
- **Cumulative status (META-AUDIT update)**:
  - **69 lines audited** (unchanged from line 38 closure).
  - **687 audit issues closed total** (unchanged — this META-AUDIT is doc-only, not part of the 10-issue-per-line walkthrough).
  - Backend: **765/765 tests pass** (UNCHANGED).
  - **★ NEW: 162-file XLS/input_forms + XLS/output_forms audit complete** — generator-driven approach; 2 stale files corrected.
  - **★ Path A continues** (no outstanding.md entry).
- **Audit workflow next**: User to direct next focus.

Plus subtle user-attested gates (VIN + first lien + US final assembly + new vehicle) + 5 canonical regression tests including the 13b #1 lock-in test. |
| 8 | VERIFIED CORRECT — Part V senior deduction (dual-eligibility derivation + continuous 6% phaseout + AMTI add-back) | Added ~50-line VERIFIED CORRECT breadcrumb above the Part V header at TaxReturnComputeService.java:~20757. Structure: (1) statutory anchor (IRS 2025 Schedule 1-A Part V + spec §8 + OBBBA 2025 §A.V + IRC §63(f)(1)(A) day-before-birthday rule); (2) 5-stage chain; (3) **DUAL-ELIGIBILITY DERIVATION** — prefer explicit `taxpayerBornBeforeJan2_1961` flag; fallback to `dateOfBirth` string via `isBornBeforeJan2_1961` helper; consistency note with 12d #5 + 12d #6 semantic-key `_1961` alignment; (4) **★ PHASEOUT-SHAPE COMPARISON TABLE** — worked example "$500 over threshold": Part II/III FLOOR=$0, Part IV CEILING=$200, **Part V CONTINUOUS=$30** (smoothest); (5) **★ AMT INTERACTION** — Senior deduction ADDED BACK to AMTI at Form 6251 line 1w via `computeLine17 ~line 8093`; CRITICAL contrast — tips/overtime/car-loan NOT added back; rationale (OBBBA designated senior deduction as AMT preference item); future line-17 audit cross-reference. Plus worked example (MFJ both eligible + MAGI $180k → $10,200) + 3 canonical regression tests. |
| 9 | OBSERVATION — Multi-employer tip-cap worksheet vs aggregate-and-cap (mathematically equivalent) | IRS Schedule 1-A Part II instructions specify a multi-employer allocation worksheet when total tips from >1 employer exceed $25k cap; backend aggregates totalRawTips then caps once. **Mathematical equivalence proven**: when total ≤ $25k, both return total_tips; when total > $25k, worksheet allocates proportionally → Σ = $25k while backend returns min(total, $25k) = $25k. Worksheet exists for IRS audit transparency, not for arithmetic differentiation. UI surface `taxpayerHasMultipleTipEmployers` informational only. **3-source coverage adequate** (spec §5.3 + knowledge §5.2 + existing 13b #5 breadcrumb at ~line 20461). **NO new breadcrumb** (anti-fragmentation policy; would create 4th-source duplication). **10th Path A application** in workflow. NO code change. |

Explicit contrast with `fullyTaxableOverall ? null : ...` pattern at lines 5372 (line 4a) + 5868 (line 5a) per 4a #3 / 5a #3. Quote from IRS 2025 line 6a instructions ("Even if none of your benefits are taxable, you still need to enter the total amount on line 6a"). Rationale: Pub. 915 worksheet uses full gross box 5 as input to IRC §86 provisional-income tiers — IRS requires gross visibility even below tier thresholds. Boundary clarification: "blank line 6a" still happens at `hasOutput=false` (no concept applies); within concept-applies branch, line 6a always populated. Prevents future "harmonization" attempts. |

- **"Three protections in one expression" pattern** (Issue #6) — same shape as 3b #7 nominee subtraction. When a single line of code carries multiple safety properties, enumerate them in the breadcrumb so future readers don't see "single line, single concern, can be simplified."
- **Pre-launch code cleanup as part of audit** (Issue #8) — when an audit identifies stylistically-redundant code (functionally equivalent but unclear), simplify rather than just document. Pre-launch context makes this low-risk; backend regression confirms equivalence.
- **Pure xlsx-flip closures as documentation pattern** (Issues #2, #9, #10) — 3/10 issues this audit were pure xlsx-flips. When a verification is fully covered by prior breadcrumbs in the same walkthrough, the xlsx audit-trail entry is the record without adding redundant code comments.

## 2026-05-11 — Line 3c (Child Dividend Disclosure Checkboxes) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\3c.xlsx` (7 sheets). Walked through all 10 Code Validation issues. **Line 3c is NEW for 2025** — it is two CHECKBOXES (not a numeric value) disclosing that Form 8814 child amounts are included in parent's lines 3a and/or 3b. Closure profile: **5 pure xlsx-flip cross-references + 2 spec/doc reconciliation + 3 verified-correct with new breadcrumbs**. Net: 751 → 751 tests (no new lock-ins — every verification covered by existing 5 form8814 tests).

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | CROSS-REFERENCE — MFS guard indirectly protects line 3c via upstream Form 8814 architecture | Pure xlsx-flip verification. Form 8814 is parent-level (not spouse-attributed) — aggregated UPSTREAM of the MFS guard at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:385-388`. The MFS guard's 5-output cascade does NOT include line 3c (line 3c is metadata, not a value). Each MFS return is its own filing entity → electing parent's line 3c correctly fires per IRS Form 8814 rules. |
| 2 | **SPEC FILE DUPLICATION ELIMINATED** — `lines/3ab.md` consolidated into `lines/3abc.md` | **Self-correcting closure**: I introduced the duplication during 3a #3 today by writing §14 verification log to the narrower `lines/3ab.md` instead of the comprehensive `lines/3abc.md`. Fixed during 3c by: (a) appending §15 "Verification log" to `lines/3abc.md` with both 3a + 3b walkthrough rows migrated; (b) updating 2 code breadcrumbs at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:4792 + 4827` from `lines/3ab.md` → `lines/3abc.md` (line 4792: `§5.3` precise reference); (c) deleting `lines/3ab.md`. Single source of truth restored. |
| 3 | **SPEC INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY FIXED** — `lines/3abc.md` §5.1 rewritten to match §5.3 | §5.1 incorrectly implied the line 3b checkbox fires on `line2aOrdinaryDividends > 0` (child has any ordinary dividends); §5.3 correctly says both checkboxes fire on `line9 > 0` (qualified portion only). Rewrote §5.1 table to show same condition for both boxes + added a clarifying paragraph explaining "conceptually independent but practically linked" via Form 8814 architecture + lock-in test reference. The §5.1 misreading would have caused future contributors to misimplement the line 3b checkbox. |
| 4 | VERIFIED CORRECT — Both line 3c checkboxes share `hasChildQualifiedDividends` trigger | 13-line breadcrumb at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:4937-4955` documenting: symmetric-trigger design (intentional, not a DRY violation); Form 8814 architecture rationale (line 9 is the only child dividend amount that flows to BOTH 3a AND 3b); cross-reference to 3a #7 + 3b #8 25-line 3-way split breadcrumb at lines 4792-4819; TRUE-or-null PDF rendering semantic. All 20 form8814* tests pass. |
| 5 | VERIFIED CORRECT — Checkbox semantic: Boolean.TRUE or null (never false) | Pure xlsx-flip — covered by Issue #4 breadcrumb. End-to-end chain verified: producer sets TRUE-or-null → buildIncome setter gates on `Boolean.TRUE.equals(...)` → Income field is `Boolean` (boxed, null-permitting) → JSON omits null fields → PDF renderer only renders checkbox when field is `true`. Same pattern as line 7b sibling checkbox. |
| 6 | VERIFIED CORRECT — buildIncome setter uses two-layer null-safe gate | 10-line breadcrumb at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:4223-4232` documenting two null-safety layers: (1) `interest != null` defends against InterestComputation being null; (2) `Boolean.TRUE.equals(...)` is null-safe boolean equality (avoids NPE from unboxing null Boolean). End-to-end semantic chain documented (null source → field stays null → JSON omits key → PDF skips checkbox). All 20 form8814* tests pass. |
| 7 | VERIFIED CORRECT — Line 3c does NOT participate in line 9 arithmetic | Pure xlsx-flip — Boolean type is structurally incompatible with addNonNull (BigDecimal) chain; Java type system prevents arithmetic propagation at compile time. Grep verified ZERO hits for arithmetic operations on line 3c fields. Spec §2 invariant: "Line 3c is metadata; it does not change the arithmetic of lines 3a or 3b." |
| 8 | VERIFIED CORRECT — Form 8814 line 4 ≤ $2,700 simplified path correctly skips line 3c | Pure xlsx-flip — three independent layers: (1) gate at `buildForm8814` line 6401 skips line 9 setter when line 4 ≤ $2,700; (2) null-filter at aggregator (`TaxReturnComputeService.java:385-388` `.filter(v -> v != null)`); (3) inline gate comment. Multi-child mixed-threshold scenarios correctly handled by null-filter. Lock-in: `form8814NoQualifiedDividendsDoesNotSetLine3cCheckboxes`. |
| 9 | VERIFIED CORRECT — 5 existing form8814 tests cover all line 3c scenarios | Pure xlsx-flip — comprehensive coverage matrix: positive (line 9 > 0), negative (line 9 = 0), critical edge (line 2a only with line 2b = 0), aggregation (multi-child), composition (parent + child both have dividends). Every Issue #1-#8 behavioral assertion is covered by at least one existing test. All 20 form8814* tests pass. |
| 10 | OBSERVATION — Line 3c is NEW for 2025; analogous to line 7b sibling checkbox | 6-line addition to the 3c #4 breadcrumb at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:4937-4955` noting: new-for-2025 status per spec §1; analogous to line 7b `line7_includes_child_capital_gain_or_loss` (per §5.4 — same pattern for Form 8814 line 10 cap-gain portion); future line 7b audit guidance (follow the same TRUE-or-null pattern + two-layer null-safe gate). |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**

- **Self-correcting closure** (Issue #2) — when an earlier audit introduces a doc-hygiene issue (spec-file duplication from 3a #3 mis-targeting `lines/3ab.md`), a subsequent audit in the same line cluster can fix it during normal walkthrough. Pre-launch context makes this approach low-risk.
- **Spec-code alignment closure** (Issue #3) — when a spec has internal inconsistency and the code matches one section but not the other, rewrite the misleading section to align with both the authoritative section AND the code. The §5.1 → §5.3 alignment removes a footgun for future contributors.
- **Three-layer structural protection pattern** (Issue #8) — when a feature has multiple independent protections (gate + null-filter + inline comment), document the layers explicitly so future readers can see the defense-in-depth from any entry point.
- **Cross-line forward-reference** (Issue #10) — when a new pattern is established (TRUE-or-null + two-layer null-safe gate for child-disclosure checkboxes), the breadcrumb can explicitly position future audits to inherit the pattern. Issue #10 positions the future line 7b audit to follow the line-3c-established conventions.
- **Pure-xlsx-flip-heavy audit profile** — 5/10 issues were pure xlsx flips (vs 4/10 for 3a and 5/10 for 3b). When a line is downstream metadata (line 3c) rather than a value-producing line, the audit naturally skews toward verifying structural correctness rather than computational logic.
- **Zero new lock-in tests** — when the upstream audit (3a + 3b) was thorough, the downstream metadata audit can ride on existing test coverage. The 5 form8814 tests cover all 3c scenarios.

- ZERO new lock-in tests

- **"3 correct design choices" pattern** (Issue #9) — when a flag emission embodies multiple deliberate choices (per-person, null-safe, non-blocking), enumerate them. Protects against well-intentioned simplification (e.g., "why not silent-cap box 5? answer: would corrupt user data").
- **"3-way split" documentation pattern** (Issue #8) — when an upstream form (Form 8814) routes to multiple downstream Form 1040 lines, the breadcrumb at one of those lines should document ALL destinations so the reader sees the full picture from any entry point.

Added lock-in test `dividendsQualifiedExceedingOrdinaryCappedToLine3bViaPerPersonCap` covering the silent per-person cap (1099-DIV box 1b=$600 > box 1a=$500 → line3a=line3b=$500, NO flag). Net +1 test. |
| 7 | VERIFIED CORRECT — Form 8814 child qualified dividends added to BOTH 3a AND 3b | Expanded inline comment to 15-line breadcrumb at TaxReturnComputeService.java:4785-4805 documenting: IRS Form 8814 instructions + lines/3ab.md §5 + **3 explicit failure modes** (3a-only → invariant violation; 3b-only → user overpays via under-applied QDCG rates; neither → under-reported income) + symmetric-addition design preserves line 3a ≤ line 3b invariant + enumeration of 5 existing form8814* lock-in tests. All 20 form8814* tests pass. |

| 9 | VERIFIED CORRECT BY DESIGN — 5 disallowed-qualified-categories are manual entry only | 13-line breadcrumb at TaxReturnComputeService.java:4944-4949 documenting the manual-entry design rationale: (1) brokers already pre-classify box 1b; (2) per-lot trade ledger data isn't available to tax software; (3) IRS Pub. 550 expects manual adjustments; (4) scenarios rare for retail filers; (5) line 3a ≤ line 3b cap (3a #6) is the safety net. Matches industry practice (TurboTax, H&R Block, Drake). No code change; no outstanding.md entry — broker-history inference would require full 1099-B per-lot integration with marginal correctness gain. |
| 10 | DEFERRED — Non-treaty foreign corp manual-classification field missing | Added new outstanding.md entry "Line 3a: Non-Treaty Foreign Corporation Dividend Manual-Classification Field Missing". Affected users: direct holders of non-ADR foreign stock (rare for retail filers). ~30-45 min implementation sketch documented. Broker pre-classification + line 3a cap are the safety net. |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**

- **Mathematical-analysis breadcrumb** (Issue #6) — when documenting defense-in-depth, include an explicit proof that the secondary defense rarely fires (here: arithmetic showing aggregation preserves the per-person caps). Future readers don't see "two caps = duplicate, remove one".
- **Failure-modes documentation pattern** (Issue #7) — when a code path implements a non-obvious dual-action invariant (Form 8814 adds to BOTH 3a AND 3b), enumerate the failure modes if either action is omitted. Makes the design rationale explicit and protects against well-intentioned simplification.
- **"End-of-file verification log" convention** (Issue #3) — not literally §12; place the log at whatever the next section number is. lines/3ab.md got §14 because §12 and §13 were already taken.
- **Knowledge-file naming convergence completes line 1c → 3ab** (Issue #2) — 10 consecutive lines now use the `line-{N}-{topic}.md` convention.
- **Distinct-from-prior-issue deferrals** (Issue #10) — when a new deferred entry overlaps conceptually with an existing fix or observation, document the **distinction** explicitly. The non-treaty foreign-corp entry explicitly distinguishes itself from 3a #9 (existing-5-field design) AND from the implemented IRC §7874 surrogate field.

## 2026-05-11 — Line 2b (Taxable Interest) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\2b.xlsx` (7 sheets). Walked through all 10 Code Validation issues. Net: 749 → 750 tests. ZERO new outstanding.md entries (all deferrals were already tracked from 2a #8 + 2a #9). The 2b walkthrough is the **first cross-reference-heavy audit** — a pattern that will likely recur as we move deeper into Form 1040 lines where compute methods are shared.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | CROSS-REFERENCE — MFS guard already applied via 2a #1 | Multi-audit-trail consolidation: extended the existing MFS-guard breadcrumb at TaxReturnComputeService.java:4308-4316 from "2a.xlsx Code Validation #1" to "2a.xlsx Code Validation #1 + 2b.xlsx Code Validation #1, both verified 2026-05-11". Clarified that the single `mfsExcludesSpouseInterestFromLine2a` lock-in test covers BOTH line 2a and line 2b through the shared `computeInterestForPerson` code path. The HIGH-LEVERAGE 5-output cascade from 2a #1 already protects line 2b. |
| 2 | CROSS-REFERENCE — Spec §7.1/§7.2/§12 refresh already done via 2a #2 | The shared-spec model (one `lines/2ab.md` file covering both lines) means the §7.1 rewrite from 2a #2 already covers line 2b bug history. Appended a 4th row to §12 verification log capturing the 2b walkthrough as a separate event (audit-trail-per-walkthrough convention). |
| 3 | CROSS-REFERENCE — Knowledge file renamed via 2a #4 | `knowledge/line-2ab-interest-income.md` covers both lines. Filesystem sweep verified no stale references. Pure xlsx-flip closure. |
| 4 | CROSS-REFERENCE — 0-vs-null compliance verified via 2a #5 (extends to 2b) | Extended the existing 2a #5 breadcrumb at line ~4588 to (a) explicitly name all 3 BigDecimal fields in InterestPersonTotals (taxableInterestBeforeExclusion, taxExemptInterest, form6251Line2g) rather than only naming taxExemptInterest; (b) cite both `2a #5 + 2b #4` audit IDs via multi-audit-trail consolidation pattern. |
| 5 | CROSS-REFERENCE — Line 2b IS in line 9 (inverse of 2a #7) | Extended the line-9 breadcrumb at TaxReturnComputeService.java:4128 to cite **three audit IDs** (`1z #7 + 2a #7 + 2b #5`). Strengthened the narrative to explicitly note that line 2b INCLUSION (this 2b #5 verification) is the INVERSE confirmation of line 2a EXCLUSION (2a #7). Lock-in test `line9EqualsLine1zPlusOtherIncomeLines` covers the invariant for both cases. |
| 6 | **NEW backend bug fix (Option A applied)** — Premium double-count when broker pre-netted | computeInterestForPerson previously subtracted manual premium adjustments unconditionally, even when the user attested the broker had already netted premium on the 1099 (`payerAlreadyReportedNetInterestOrOid = true`). If a user entered both 1099-INT box 11 = $100 AND manual taxableBondPremiumAdjustmentNotInStatements = $100, the same $100 was subtracted twice → line 2b $100 too low. Fix (Option A): added `boolean payerAlreadyNetted` parameter to computeInterestForPerson; orchestrator reads the boolean from `interest-income-taxpayer` (return-level form) and passes to both taxpayer + spouse call sites; inside the method, the 4 manual premium-adjustment fields (3 taxable for line 2b + 1 tax-exempt for line 2a) use `payerAlreadyNetted ? null : getAmount(...)`. **Single fix protects BOTH lines 2a AND 2b** — the gate also suppresses the tax-exempt premium adjustment. Accrued/nominee adjustments are NOT premium-related and remain unconditional. Lock-in test `interestPremiumAdjustmentsSuppressedWhenBrokerPreNetted`: 1099-INT $1,000 + box 11 = $100 + boolean=true + manual=$100 → line 2b = $900 (NOT $800 double-counted). Net +1 test. |
| 7 | VERIFIED CORRECT — multi-stage zero-floor | Line 2b is floored at zero at THREE separate stages: per-entry box 11/12 subtraction; person-level adjustments (accrued/nominee/premium); return-level savings bond exclusion. Per spec §9 invariant. 6-line breadcrumb at TaxReturnComputeService.java:4101 (line-2b storage site) documenting the 3-stage floor and contrasting with line 1f/1z which CAN be negative (IRC §137(a)(3) special-needs adoption — already breadcrumbed during 1z #8). **Complementary breadcrumb pattern** — future readers see both invariants documented at their respective line storage sites. |
| 8 | VERIFIED CORRECT — Schedule B Part I per-payer attribution | 5 emission categories per person (per-1099-INT, per-1099-OID, OID box 2 portion, manual, negated adjustments). Aggregation order: per-1099-source within each person, then taxpayer then spouse. 11-line breadcrumb at line 4464 (aggregation site) documenting the 5 categories + ordering contract + cross-reference to deferred seller-financed-first ordering (2a #9). |
| 9 | DEFERRED — Form 8815 already tracked | outstanding.md (line ~1295, shifted from 1253 by 2a #9 + 2b #6 entries) — same canonical entry covers both 2a and 2b. Pure xlsx-flip closure. |
| 10 | DEFERRED — Seller-financed mortgage already tracked | outstanding.md:9 (top-of-file, added during 2a #9 closure earlier today). Pure xlsx-flip closure. |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**

- **Multi-audit-trail consolidation as dominant closure type** for shared-orchestrator audits — when a downstream line audit (2b) revisits a method already protected by an upstream line audit (2a), the dominant closure shape is to EXTEND existing breadcrumbs to cite both audit IDs rather than create new breadcrumbs. The line-9 formula site now cites three audits (1z #7 + 2a #7 + 2b #5); the MFS-guard site cites two (2a #1 + 2b #1); the canonical 0-vs-null breadcrumb covers all 3 BigDecimal fields of InterestPersonTotals.
- **Backend enforcement of UI gates** (Issue #6) — when the UI presents a conditional question that suppresses other fields, the backend should enforce the same gate. UI-only protection is a defensive gap; direct-API submissions bypass it.
- **One-fix-protects-multiple-lines** (Issue #6) — the `payerAlreadyReportedNetInterestOrOid` gate suppresses both line-2b premium fields (3 of them: taxableBondPremium, treasuryBondPremium, oidAcquisitionPremium) AND the line-2a tax-exempt premium field. Single backend change at the orchestrator + per-person aggregator cascades to BOTH interest lines.
- **Zero-new-deferred-enhancements audit** — first audit where every deferral was already tracked from upstream audits. Indicates the cross-line cleanup work done during 2a closure was thorough. Future shared-orchestrator audits may follow this pattern.
- **Complementary breadcrumb pattern** (Issue #7) — line-2b zero-floor breadcrumb complements the line-1z negative-pass-through breadcrumb. Future readers see both invariants explained at their respective line storage sites.
- **Audit-trail-per-walkthrough convention extended** — `lines/2ab.md` §12 verification log now has 4 rows (knowledge-audit 2026-04-13, 2a #2/#3/#6 bug-fix re-verification, 2a #1 MFS guard, 2b walkthrough).

- `lines/2ab.md` §12 — appended 4th verification-log row for 2b walkthrough
- 1 new unit test: `interestPremiumAdjustmentsSuppressedWhenBrokerPreNetted` (Issue #6)
- **ZERO new outstanding.md entries** — every deferral was already tracked from 2a closures
