## 2026-05-11 — Line 2a (Tax-Exempt Interest) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\2a.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring 1c–1z structure). Walked through all 10 Code Validation issues. Net result: 1 defensive bug fix with HIGH-LEVERAGE 5-output cascade (MFS guard at `computeInterestIncome`) + 1 spec refresh closing 3 issues (stale `lines/2ab.md` §7.1) + 1 knowledge-file rename extending line 1c–2ab naming convergence + 3 verified-correct closures with breadcrumbs + 1 already-tracked deferral + 1 new deferred-advisory entry + 1 immediate-fix Option-A YAML clarification. **+1 net unit test** (748 → 749). 1 new deferred-advisory entry in `outstanding.md`. **First non-wage-block audit** — extends the workflow from line 1a–1z (the wage block) into line 2a/2b (interest block).

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFENSIVE GAP — MFS guard not in orchestrator (HIGH-LEVERAGE) | `computeInterestIncome` previously read `interestIncomeSpouse` and `dividendIncomeSpouse` unconditionally — spouse-side interest/dividends leaked into MFS taxpayer's return. **One orchestrator-level fix protects 5 outputs**: lines 2a + 2b + 3a + 3b + Form 6251 line 2g + Schedule B Parts I/II. Added `boolean isMfsReturn` parameter to `computeInterestIncome` (signature line 4291) + call site at line 389. Three-point spouse-read suppression: `interestIncomeSpouse`, `dividendIncomeSpouse`, and `spouseSsn` all explicitly nulled on MFS. Schedule B Part III foreign-account/FBAR/foreign-trust questions are sourced from `interest-income-taxpayer` (return-level) and are unaffected. Test: `mfsExcludesSpouseInterestFromLine2a` — MFS + taxpayer 1099-INT box 8 = $300 + STALE spouse 1099-INT box 8 = $500 → line 2a = $300 (NOT $800). **Single-guard MFS cascade now applied to 8 orchestrators** (1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h, 1i, and `computeInterestIncome`). Net +1 test. |
| 2 + 3 | STALE SPEC — `lines/2ab.md` §7.1 listed 3 resolved bugs as "Known code bugs"; §4-vs-§7.1 cross-reference contradiction | One spec rewrite closed both issues. §7.1 rewritten from "Known code bugs" to "Historical bugs — all RESOLVED 2026-04-13" with per-bug fix narrative (pre-fix code, issue, post-fix code with line numbers). Bug 1 (box 9 double-count), Bug 2 (FBAR flag distinct from foreign-account), Bug 3 (nominee interest Schedule B trigger) — all verified RESOLVED 2026-04-13 per the knowledge file. Added §7.2 documenting the Issue #1 MFS guard. Added §12 "Verification log" 3-row table (matches line 1d/1e/1f/1g/1h verification-log convention). The §4-vs-§7.1 contradiction (Bug 3 said nominee doesn't trigger Schedule B, §4 said it does) resolved as byproduct of §7.1 rewrite. |
| 4 | KNOWLEDGE FILE NAMING DEVIATION | Renamed `knowledge/knowledge-line-2ab-interest-income.md` → `knowledge/line-2ab-interest-income.md` via plain `mv`. Five references updated: generator header, YAML frontmatter `name:` field inside the file, two references in `lines/2ab.md` §7.1 (added during Issue #2 closure using old name — then updated). **Naming convergence extended from line 1c–1z to line 1c–2ab.** |
| 5 | VERIFIED CORRECT — canonical 0-vs-null compliance | Per the canonical rule (`knowledge/canonical-null-zero-semantic.md`, 2026-05-10): `computeInterestForPerson` initializes `BigDecimal taxExemptInterest = null;` at line 4484, accumulates via `addNonNull` (null+null=null), `roundMoney` preserves null. When no entries match: null → addNonNull aggregate stays null. When entries exist with $0: addNonNull(null,0)=0 → returned ZERO. **COMPLIANT** with canonical rule. Added 7-line breadcrumb at line ~4577 (return site). **First non-wage-block 0-vs-null compliance verification** per the per-line audit-folding plan. |
| 6 | VERIFIED CORRECT — box 9 double-count fix (2026-04-13) verified at code site | `TaxReturnComputeService.java:4502` reads `subtractNonNegative(box8, box13)` (box 9 absent — correct, IRS rule: box 9 ⊂ box 8). Box 9 captured separately at line 4506 for Form 6251 line 2g. Added 6-line breadcrumb at line 4501 documenting historical pre-fix code + 2026-04-13 fix date + cross-reference to lines/2ab.md §7.1. |
| 7 | VERIFIED CORRECT — line 2a excluded from line 9 / AGI | Extended the 1z #7 line-9 breadcrumb at line 4128 to cite BOTH 1z #7 (2026-05-10) AND 2a #7 (2026-05-11) verification dates. **Multi-audit-trail consolidation pattern** — same code site verified by multiple audits, breadcrumb merges them. Notable absences from line 9: line 2a (IRC §103) AND line 1i (IRS 2025 line 1i instructions). Lock-in test `line9EqualsLine1zPlusOtherIncomeLines` (added during 1z #2). |

Affects Schedule B gating only — does NOT affect line 2a or 2b values (amount flows correctly via manual paths). |
| 10 | IMMEDIATE-FIX Option A — Tax-exempt market discount YAML clarification | Per IRC §1276, market discount on a tax-exempt bond accrues as TAXABLE interest (line 2b), not tax-exempt. YAML help text for `manualTaxExemptInterestNotOnStatements` had no warning. Audit recommended defer; chose Option A (immediate fix) because the one-line change took seconds. Updated help text in both `yamls/2ab-interest-income-taxpayer.yaml` line 61 AND `yamls/2ab-interest-income-spouse.yaml` line 50 with the market-discount caveat: "Do NOT include market discount on a tax-exempt bond — that is taxable interest (line 2b), not tax-exempt interest." Matches the line 1z #5 immediate-fix-when-verified-safe pattern. |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**
- **High-leverage orchestrator-level MFS guard** (Issue #1) — when one `compute*` orchestrator drives multiple Form 1040 lines + downstream forms, adding `isMfsReturn` there protects ALL downstream outputs with a single fix. `computeInterestIncome` is the **first instance** — 5-output cascade. The wage-block orchestrators (1c–1i) each drove one line; the interest-block orchestrator drives 5 outputs. This pattern becomes especially important as we move deeper into Form 1040 lines where compute paths converge.
- **Stale-spec refresh as multi-issue closure** (Issues #2 + #3) — one §7.1 rewrite closed both issues. The §4-vs-§7.1 contradiction (Issue #3) was a byproduct. When auditing a stale spec, look for cross-reference contradictions — they often resolve automatically once the bug-history is updated to current state.
- **Multi-audit-trail consolidation in breadcrumbs** (Issue #7) — when the same code site is verified by multiple audits over time, the breadcrumb merges them. Line-9 formula at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:4128` now cites both 1z #7 (2026-05-10) AND 2a #7 (2026-05-11) verification dates. Future audits at the same site should EXTEND the existing breadcrumb rather than create a new one.
- **First non-wage-block 0-vs-null compliance verification** (Issue #5) — the cross-line audit fold-in from the 2026-05-10 canonical-rule plan is now active. Every future Form 1040 line audit should include this check as a standard step.
- **Immediate-fix Option A when verified safe** (Issue #10) — when the audit recommends "defer" but the fix is one line in YAML/code with verified-safe blast radius, applying it immediately is more efficient than the procedural overhead of a deferred-enhancement entry. Same call as line 1z #5 (orphan YAML deletion).
- **Knowledge-file naming convergence extended** (Issue #4) — line 1c–2ab now consistent. Older legacy `knowledge_line<n>.md` files (8, 9, 10–33, 3ab, 4abc, 12abcde) remain for a future cross-line sweep.

**Documentation accumulated:**

## 2026-05-10 — Cross-Line 0-vs-null Canonical Rule — Established (Option B applied)

After the wage-block audits (lines 1a–1z) repeatedly bumped into a stylistic inconsistency between `null` and `BigDecimal.ZERO` returns in compute helpers — surfaced in `1h.xlsx` Issue #4(g) and root-caused in `1z.xlsx` Issues #1/#9 — the user requested a one-time resolution applicable to all tax-calculation logic. After investigation via the Explore agent (14 compute helpers surveyed), the actual scope was narrower than feared: **one outlier (line 1e `computeDependentCareBenefits`) violating the canonical rule**, plus two correctly-reasoned spec-driven ZERO exceptions (line 1f IRC §137(f), line 1i spec §10.4).

**Option B applied** (right balance between conservative documentation and aggressive sweep):

1. **Canonical rule documented** at `knowledge/canonical-null-zero-semantic.md` (160 lines):
   - **NULL** = "this concept does not apply / no input was provided" → PDF cell renders blank.
   - **ZERO** = "this concept applies, input was provided, computed value is zero" → PDF cell renders "0".
   - Rule referenced at the top of `rules.md`.
   - Spec-driven ZERO exceptions cataloged (line 1a IRC carve-outs, line 1f IRC §137(f), line 1i spec §10.4, line 1h #4(g) all-zero-inputs).
   - Audit-coverage table tracks compliance per line.

2. **Line 1e fix applied** at `TaxReturnComputeService.computeDependentCareBenefits` line ~9400 — added early-return `new DependentCareComputation(null, null, false)` when `!hasBenefits && !hasChildcareData`. Pre-fix the method always proceeded through the safeAmount/maxZero chains and returned BigDecimal.ZERO via `roundMoney(line26)`. This was the single non-spec-driven ZERO violation in the wage block.

3. **Dead-code cleanup** at `buildIncome` line ~4076 — removed the `if (... wagesRaw == null && ... && dependentCareBenefits == null && ... && !hasIncomeIndicators) return null;` early-return that had been unreachable pre-fix (line 1e ZERO always kept dependentCareBenefits non-null). The check became reachable after the line 1e fix and started returning null Income objects for scenarios where existing tests asserted against an Income object with null fields. Removing the dead-code path preserves the contract that `form1040.income` is always non-null; null-ness is preserved at the field level (which is where downstream consumers and addNonNull aggregations check anyway). 11 tests stopped crashing without any test modifications.

4. **Frontend cleanup** at `form-tax-return-1040.component.ts:290` — already done during 1z #1 closure (`totalWages ?? wages` → `totalWages`). The fallback was load-bearing on the line 1e bug; removed during the wage-block audit, vindicated here.

5. **Line 1z breadcrumb updated** — removed the "currently unreachable" qualifier on the hasEmploymentFlag defensive branch; the path now activates as designed.

6. **Lock-in tests added** at `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`:
   - `line1eIsNullWhenNoChildcareExpensesFormAndNoBox10` — asserts null on truly-empty input.
   - `line1eIsZeroWhenChildcareExpensesFormPresentWithZeroAmount` — asserts non-null when concept applies.

7. **Cross-line audit deferred** to per-line audits — added an outstanding.md entry directing each future Form 1040 line audit to include a 0-vs-null compliance check on its Code Validation sheet. Auditing in per-line context (rather than a single big sweep) catches line-specific subtleties.

**Tests:** 746 → 748 (net +2). All passing. Frontend build clean.

**Files changed:**
- `knowledge/canonical-null-zero-semantic.md` (NEW)
- `rules.md` (canonical rule section added at top)
- `lines/1e.md` (canonical-rule section added)
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java` — `computeDependentCareBenefits` early-return; `buildIncome` early-return removed; line 1z breadcrumb updated
- `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java` — 2 new lock-in tests
- `outstanding.md` — cross-line audit folded into future per-line audits

**Patterns established:**
- **Survey-then-fix-narrowest-scope**: when a stylistic inconsistency appears widespread, dispatch a comprehensive survey before fixing — the actual violation may be one or two outliers rather than a cross-cutting mess.
- **Dead-code-removal as collateral benefit**: fixing the line 1e bug uncovered the unreachable buildIncome early-return; removing it simultaneously preserved test contracts and improved code clarity.
- **Cross-line audits via per-line walkthroughs**: rather than scheduling a separate sweep, fold the compliance check into the existing audit cadence. Coverage accumulates gradually with full per-line context.


## 2026-05-10 — Line 1z (Total Wages — Subtotal of Lines 1a-1h) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1z.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring 1c–1i structure). Walked through all 10 Code Validation issues. Net result: 1 defensive cleanup + dead-code removal (frontend `totalWages ?? wages` fallback removed; backend `hasEmploymentFlag` mirror invariant added) + 1 coverage-gap closure with +4 unit tests + 1 knowledge-file rename completing the line 1c-1z naming convergence + 1 deferred-enhancement (addNonNullVarargs cross-site refactor) + 1 orphaned-YAML deletion (consolidating a long-standing outstanding.md item) + 5 verified-correct closures with breadcrumbs. **+5 net unit tests** across the walkthrough (741 → 746). 1 new deferred-advisory entry in `outstanding.md`; 1 long-standing outstanding.md item closed.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | INVARIANT INCONSISTENCY — frontend fallback load-bearing on backend zero-override | Frontend rendered `totalWages ?? wages` to handle the `hasEmploymentFlag=true && line1a=null` edge case where backend force-set `income.wages = BigDecimal.ZERO` but left `income.totalWages = null`. Investigation revealed the gap is DORMANT in practice: `computeDependentCareBenefits` returns BigDecimal.ZERO (not null) on absent childcare-expenses form (stylistic 0-vs-null choice mirroring line 1h #4(g)), so line 1z is always non-null whenever ANY sub-line compute runs. Both the frontend fallback AND the would-be-needed backend defensive override were effectively dead code masking a theoretical-only gap. Fix: (1) backend at TaxReturnComputeService.java:~4203 added `else if (hasEmploymentFlag) income.setTotalWages(BigDecimal.ZERO)` as defensive future-proofing (will activate if line 1e is ever refactored to return null on absent form); (2) frontend at form-tax-return-1040.component.ts:290 — `totalWages ?? wages` → just `totalWages` (pure dead-code cleanup); (3) 8-line breadcrumb documenting the invariant + audit ID. Lock-in test `line1zStoresZeroWhenHasEmploymentFlagAndNoWageInputs` locks observable behavior. Negative-case test was attempted but proved impossible — line 1e contamination always produces non-null line 1z. Frontend build clean. Net +1 test. |
| 2 | TEST COVERAGE GAPS | Only 1 dedicated unit test (`computesLine1zSumExcludesCombatPayElection`) covering a composite scenario. 6 audit-identified sub-gaps (a-f). Sub-gaps (a) impossible (line 1e contamination) and (e) closed by Issue #1 lock-in. Remaining 4 sub-gaps (b/c/d/f) closed with 4 new unit tests: `line1zEqualsLine1aWhenOnlyW2Wages` (minimal isolation); `line1zMinimalLine1iExclusion` (clean combat-pay exclusion without adoption noise); `line9EqualsLine1zPlusOtherIncomeLines` (line 9 invariant lock-in); `schedule8812Line18aSeparatesTotalWagesAndCombatPayDisclosure` (BOTH line 18a sum AND line 18b disclosure split). Total dedicated line-1z unit tests grew 1 → 6 (or 11+ counting indirect coverage). One real concern caught during test development: 1099-DIV field name is `totalOrdinaryDividendsAmount` (not `ordinaryDividendsAmount`) — fixed before commit. Net +4 tests. |
| 3 | KNOWLEDGE FILE NAMING DEVIATION | `knowledge/knowledge-line-1z-total-wages.md` used the legacy `knowledge-line-1z-` prefix. Renamed to `line-1z-total-wages.md` via plain `mv`. Two references updated: generator header comment (line 6) and YAML frontmatter `name:` field inside the file itself (line 2, from `knowledge-line-1z-total-wages` → `line-1z-total-wages`). **Line 1c-1z knowledge-file naming convergence is now COMPLETE.** All 8 wage-line knowledge files (1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h, 1i, 1z) use the canonical `line-{N}-{topic}.md` form. Older legacy `knowledge_line<n>.md` files (lines 8, 9, 10–33, 3ab, 4abc) remain for a future cross-line sweep. |
| 4 | OBSERVATION — addNonNull chain readability — DEFERRED | 8-operand addNonNull chains at line 1z (line 4143-4144) and line 9 (line 4147-4148) are mathematically correct but difficult to scan. Grep revealed 31 nested-addNonNull sites across `TaxReturnComputeService.java`. Audit recommended "Defer to a future refactor pass". Partial migration rejected (would create inconsistency with the other 29 sites). Closure: added deferred-enhancement section to outstanding.md ("TaxReturnComputeService — addNonNullVarargs Helper + Cross-Site Migration") with full re-implementation scope (~1-2 hours: helper + 7-stage section-by-section migration with regression gates + 5 unit tests for the helper). No code change today. |
| 5 | ORPHANED YAML — 1b-household-employee-wages.yaml — RESOLVED via deletion | Verified during walkthrough that the file had no external dependencies (grep returned matches ONLY in documentation files — outstanding.md, knowledge/line-1z-total-wages.md, history.md — plus the 1z audit row; no `loadYaml`/yaml-scanning in backend/frontend src). The outstanding.md entry (since ~2026-04-12) explicitly pre-authorized deletion conditional on dependency verification — which was completed during this audit. Audit's "Defer" recommendation overridden because the verification gate had been met. File deleted via plain `rm` (`us-tax/` not a git repo). outstanding.md entry marked `~~Resolved 2026-05-10~~` per the established strikethrough closure pattern. Consolidated the line 1z audit closure with a long-standing technical-debt item. |
| 6 | VERIFIED CORRECT — line 1z formula matches IRS spec | 8-operand addNonNull chain at line 4143-4144 lists exactly line1a through line1h, with line 1i intentionally excluded. Matches IRS 2025 Form 1040 line 1z label "Add lines 1a through 1h" exactly. Closure: extended the 1i #9 breadcrumb at line 4136-4145 to also cite 1z #6 and enumerate ALL THREE lock-in tests: `computesLine1zSumExcludesCombatPayElection` (composite), `line1zMinimalLine1iExclusion` (minimal isolation), `line1zEqualsLine1aWhenOnlyW2Wages` (single-sub-line baseline). Multi-test breadcrumb pattern matches line 1i #10. |
| 7 | VERIFIED CORRECT — line 9 invariant | Line 9 formula at line 4147-4150: `line9 = roundMoney(addNonNull(...(line1z, line2b), ...line7a), line8))` — 8 operands. Matches IRS 2025 Form 1040 line 9 label "Add lines 1z, 2b, 3b, 4b, 5b, 6b, 7a, and 8". Closure: extended the inline comment at line 4146 with audit ID + lock-in test name `line9EqualsLine1zPlusOtherIncomeLines` (added during Issue #2). |
| 8 | VERIFIED CORRECT — negative sub-line pass-through | `addNonNull` performs algebraic addition with no `max(0, ...)` floor. Negative line 1f (special-needs adoption per IRC §137(a)(3)) flows through to line 1z; floor at line 15 (taxable income) only. Existing test asserts line 1z = -$5,280 for a $10,000 wages + -$15,280 adoption-benefits scenario. Closure: added a dedicated 6-line breadcrumb at line ~4145 (after the `roundMoney(totalWagesRaw)` assignment) documenting the algebraic-addition / no-floor semantic + IRC §137(a)(3) basis. Modular comment separation from the line-1i-exclusion breadcrumb above. |
| 9 | VERIFIED CORRECT — null propagation (helper level; end-to-end path currently unreachable) | Helper-level semantic correct (addNonNull chain returns null when all 8 operands null; `if (line1z != null)` guard correctly leaves field unset). Practical limitation: the end-to-end "all sub-lines null → line 1z null" path is currently UNREACHABLE because line 1e returns BigDecimal.ZERO on absent childcare-expenses form. Closure: consolidated the breadcrumb at the storage guard (line ~4195-4211) to cover BOTH Issue #1 (hasEmploymentFlag mirror) AND Issue #9 (null propagation semantic) with honest practical-limitation note. Fixed a stale reference to a dropped test during the consolidation. |
| 10 | VERIFIED CORRECT — no MFS guard needed at line 1z level | Line 1z aggregation reads no spouse / filing-status / SSN-attributed data — pure sum of pre-computed sub-line values. The single-guard MFS cascade pattern (applied to 7 lines: 1c-1i) lives at the sub-line level. Adding an MFS guard at line 1z would be redundant AND potentially harmful (could double-suppress legitimate values). Architecturally the aggregator is the WRONG layer for MFS logic. Pure xlsx-flip closure — no breadcrumb needed because the absent MFS guard is correctly absent (same shape as line 1h #10 Form 4852 closure). |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**
- **Dead-code-cleanup closure type** (Issue #1) — when an audit identifies a "load-bearing" code path that turns out to be dormant in practice (line 1e contamination made both backend override AND frontend fallback unreachable), the closure is defensive cleanup + dead-code removal rather than bug-fix. The xlsx narrative honestly reframes the issue from "real correctness bug" to "defensive cleanup", which is more accurate.
- **Consolidated breadcrumb pattern** (Issue #9) — when multiple audit issues touch the same code location (Issues #1 and #9 both at the totalWages storage guard), merge the breadcrumbs into a single coherent block rather than fragmenting. Caught a stale test-name reference during the consolidation (bonus housekeeping).
- **Honest practical-limitation documentation** (Issues #9, #1) — when a behavior is verified-correct at the helper/semantic level but UNREACHABLE end-to-end in current code state, the breadcrumb honestly says so. Rejects the temptation to claim "fully tested" when end-to-end coverage is impossible.
- **Audit-overrides-deferral-when-verification-complete** (Issue #5) — when the audit framing is "defer" but the deferred work was already pre-authorized in outstanding.md conditional on dependency verification, AND the verification can be done during the audit walkthrough, execute the action and close both items together. Saves a future audit cycle.
- **Modular comment separation** (Issues #6, #8) — when the same code site has multiple verified-correct concerns (line-1i exclusion vs negative pass-through), use SEPARATE breadcrumb blocks rather than one large multi-concern block. Each concern stays scannable on its own.
- **Cross-line documentation finding**: line 1e/1f/1g/1h share a stylistic 0-vs-null choice on absent-form paths (origin: line 1h #4(g)). Now documented in three closure contexts (1h #4(g), 1z #1, 1z #9). Future cross-line audit could unify the stylistic — broader than any single line scope.

**Documentation accumulated:**
- `XLS/computations/1z.xlsx` (7 sheets, all 10 Code Validation issues marked closed/deferred/verified)
- `XLS/_tools/generate-1z.js` (new generator)
- `knowledge/line-1z-total-wages.md` (renamed from `knowledge-line-1z-total-wages.md` + YAML frontmatter `name:` field updated)
- `outstanding.md` new section: "TaxReturnComputeService — addNonNullVarargs Helper + Cross-Site Migration"
- `outstanding.md` entry marked Resolved: "Line 1b: Dead YAML — 1b-household-employee-wages.yaml" (orphan deleted)
- `C:/us-tax/yamls/1b-household-employee-wages.yaml` — DELETED (orphaned for ~6 weeks)
- Frontend cleanup: `form-tax-return-1040.component.ts:290` — removed `totalWages ?? wages` fallback

**Backend test count:** 741 → 746 (net +5 unit tests). Breakdown: +1 from #1 hasEmploymentFlag lock-in, +4 from #2 coverage closure. Verified-correct closures (#6, #7, #8, #9, #10) and deferred closures (#4, #5) added no new tests.

**Cumulative status across the audit workflow:**
- **10 lines audited** (1a, 1b, 1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h, 1i, 1z)
- **97 audit issues closed total**
- **Backend: 746/746 tests pass**
- **Single-guard MFS cascade applied to 7 lines** (1c–1i); line 1z verified as correctly outside that pattern (aggregator level)
