## 2026-05-10 — Line 1i (Nontaxable Combat Pay Election) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1i.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring 1c–1h structure). Walked through all 10 Code Validation issues. Net result: 1 backend defensive fix (MFS guard — single-guard cascade now applied to **7 lines**) + 1 correctness/source-of-truth refactor (EIC unified to read line 1i value) + 2 audit-false-positive verifications with lock-in tests + 1 defensive Option-A guard (null-SSN protection) + 1 coverage-gap closure with +7 unit tests + 1 knowledge-file rename + 2 deferred-advisory closures + 1 verified-correct closure with multi-test breadcrumb. **+11 net unit tests** across the walkthrough (730 → 741). 4 new deferred-advisory entries in `outstanding.md`.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFENSIVE GAP — MFS guard not in orchestrator | computeCombatPay() read spouseSsn unconditionally and processed both `combat-pay-spouse` form data and spouse-attributed code Q W-2s regardless of filing status. On MFS with stale `combat-pay-spouse` data (from prior MFJ year), spouse contribution leaked into the taxpayer's line 1i. Same pattern as line 1c #1, 1d #1, 1e #2, 1f #2, 1g #1, 1h #1. Fix: added `boolean isMfsReturn` parameter + three-point spouse gate inside the method (`spouseSsn`, `electSpouse`, `spouseTotal` all explicitly `isMfsReturn ? null : ...`). Three-point gate vs the line-1h two-point gate because `sumW2Box12ByCodes(_, _, null)` sums ALL entries (Issue #4 — a separate defensive concern); gating only spouseSsn would not have prevented the leak under the helper's current semantic. Test: `mfsExcludesSpouseCombatPayElectionFromLine1i` (MFS + taxpayer Q $1,500 + STALE spouse Q $1,200 + STALE spouse election=true → line 1i = $1,500, not $2,700). **Single-guard MFS cascade now applied to 7 lines** (1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h, 1i). Net +1 test. |
| 2 | CORRECTNESS / DUPLICATION — EIC has its own combat-pay election flag | computeLine27aEIC() at lines 16604-16617 read its own `electNontaxableCombatPay` and `spouseElectNontaxableCombatPay` flags from the EIC personal form AND re-summed W-2 box 12 code Q — instead of using `form1040.income.nontaxableCombatPayElection`. Two separate user-facing election questions created a divergence risk: a user could elect "Yes" on the line-1i form but "No" on the EIC form (or vice versa), producing inconsistent earned-income across line 1i, ACTC (Schedule 8812 line 18a/18b — already correct), and EIC (line 27a — was broken). Fix: refactored computeLine27aEIC to read `form1040.getIncome().getNontaxableCombatPayElection()` as the single source of truth. 14-line duplicate combat-pay block replaced with 3-line read. Result: one election question (line-1i form), one computed value, three consumers (line 1i display, Schedule 8812 line 18, EIC line 27a) all reading the same field — no divergence possible. Updated existing test `line27aCombatPayElection_addsToEarnedIncome` to set the election via `combat-pay-taxpayer.electCombatPay`; new test `line27aEicReadsCombatPayFromLine1iSource_notEicFormFlag` (two-part: Part A asserts line-1i election alone produces $649 EIC; Part B asserts legacy EIC-form flag alone with line-1i election OFF produces $306 — proves legacy flag has NO effect post-refactor). Legacy YAML/UI cleanup (deprecate `electNontaxableCombatPay` / `spouseElectNontaxableCombatPay` fields in `27a-earned-income-credit-{taxpayer,spouse}.yaml` + corresponding Angular controls) deferred to outstanding.md. Net +1 test. |
| 3 | POTENTIAL DATA-MISS — spouse SSN read from legacy form — **AUDIT FALSE POSITIVE** | Audit framed `getString(spouse, "ssn")` as a potential data-miss when the modern UI populates `identification-spouse.spouseSsn`. Verification: the `spouse` map passed to computeCombatPay is NOT the raw `personalForms.get("spouse")` — it is the RESOLVED output of `resolveSpouseIdentification(legacySpouse, identificationSpouse)` at line 151, which calls `overlayResolvedField(resolved, identificationSpouse, "ssn", "spouseSsn")` at line 3902. The modern field is merged into the legacy slot before computeCombatPay runs. Audit author missed the implicit overlay. Closure: 8-line breadcrumb comment added at computeCombatPay naming the resolver + line 3902 overlay; new lock-in test `combatPayUsesSpouseSsnFromIdentificationSpouseFormViaOverlay` — MFJ with spouse SSN ONLY in identification-spouse.spouseSsn (no legacy spouse.ssn) + spouse code Q $1,200 + electCombatPay=true → asserts line 1i = $1,200. The lock-in test catches accidental future removal of the resolver, since the call site has NO visible dependency on it. Net +1 test. |
| 4 | DEFENSIVE GAP — sumW2Box12ByCodes with null SSN sums all entries (Option A scope) | Helper skips the SSN filter when ssn is null/empty (line ~10897 — `if (hasText(normalizedSsn))` skip-pattern). So `sumW2Box12ByCodes(w2Entries, COMBAT_PAY_CODES, null)` returned sum of ALL code Q entries regardless of attribution. If `you.ssn` were null in production (incomplete onboarding), taxpayer election would absorb every code Q W-2 on the return. Fix (Option A — defensive at caller, scoped to line 1i): added `hasText(ssn)` guards at both combat-pay sum sites. New pattern: `taxpayerTotal = hasText(taxpayerSsn) ? sumW2Box12ByCodes(...) : null` and `spouseTotal = (isMfsReturn || !hasText(spouseSsn)) ? null : sumW2Box12ByCodes(...)`. Lock-in test `combatPayWithNullTaxpayerSsnDoesNotAbsorbCodeQFromOtherW2s` — Single filer with `you` form lacking ssn + electCombatPay=true + unrelated W-2 (employeeSSN=333-33-3333) with code Q $5,000 → asserts line 1i = $0 (NOT $5,000 absorbed). Broader Option C cleanup (change sumW2Box12ByCodes helper itself to return null on null SSN, audit Saver's Credit at lines 19687/19730) deferred to outstanding.md. Net +1 test. |
| 5 | TEST COVERAGE GAPS | 7 audit-identified sub-gaps; (a) MFS guard and (h) null taxpayer SSN already closed by Issues #1 and #4 lock-ins. Remaining 6 sub-gaps closed with 7 new unit tests (b split into both-elect and neither-elect): `combatPayMfjBothSpousesElect_aggregatesToLine1i` (T $2,000 + S $1,500 → $3,500); `combatPayMfjNeitherSpouseElects_line1iIsNull` (both have Q but neither elects → null); `combatPayMultipleW2sForSamePersonAreSummed` ($800 + $200 → $1,000); `combatPaySpouseCodeQNotAbsorbedWhenOnlyTaxpayerElects` (T $500, S $1,200, only T elects → $500); `combatPaySumsOnlyCodeQNotOtherBox12Codes` (Q $1,000 + D $5,000 → $1,000); `combatPayElectionNullTreatedAsNoElection` (electCombatPay=null → null); `combatPayElectsWithZeroCodeQAmount_returnsZeroNotNull` (Q $0 + elected=true → BigDecimal.ZERO). Total line-1i unit-test count grew 4 → 11 (or 13 counting Issue #1/#3/#4 lock-ins). Net +7 tests. |
| 6 | KNOWLEDGE FILE NAMING DEVIATION | `knowledge/knowledge-line-1i-combat-pay.md` used the legacy `knowledge-line-1i-` prefix while line 1c–1h had converged on `line-{N}-{topic}.md`. Renamed to `knowledge/line-1i-combat-pay.md` via plain `mv`. Generator header comment updated. **Naming convergence complete across line 1c–1i.** Older legacy `knowledge_line<n>.md` files (lines 8, 9, 10–33, 3ab, 4abc) remain — future sweep, out of scope. No content change. |
| 7 | OBSERVATION — IRA compensation not wired to combat pay — DEFERRED | Per IRC §219(f)(7) and Pub. 590-A §2, nontaxable combat pay counts as compensation for IRA contribution purposes (statutory exception). Independent of the line-1i credit election. Verification: only `computeIraDistributions` exists in backend (handles lines 4a/4b); IRA contribution deduction (Schedule 1 line 20) is user-entered with no compensation-cap validation anywhere. So this is not specifically a line-1i gap — it's one of several IRC §219 compensation components that would be needed once IRA-contribution-cap validation is implemented at all. Closure (per audit "Defer"): added deferred-enhancement section to outstanding.md ("Line 1i — Nontaxable Combat Pay as IRA Contribution Compensation") with full re-implementation scope. Re-implementation must use the UNDERLYING `sumW2Box12ByCodes(..., personSsn)` (NOT `nontaxableCombatPayElection`) because §219(f)(7) is election-independent. No code change today. |
| 8 | OBSERVATION — Top-of-form combat_zone checkbox not wired — DEFERRED | Form 1040 PDF field `topmostSubform[0].Page1[0].c1_2[0]` / semantic name `top_combat_zone` exists in pdfs/f1040_field_mapping_semantic.csv but has NO backend or UI wiring (grep confirmed). Checkbox is filing-deadline-extension marker per Pub. 3 §7 (180 days after combat zone + days remaining in filing period). Distinct from line 1i math — purely administrative, no tax effect. Closure (per audit "Defer"): added deferred-enhancement section to outstanding.md ("Form 1040 Top-of-Form combat_zone Checkbox — Filing Deadline Extension Marker") with re-implementation scope (~30-45 min). Nuance documented: auto-derivation from code Q is INSUFFICIENT — combat-zone service is broader than nontaxable combat pay (brief rotations, officer-cap amounts not in code Q). Correct implementation needs an explicit user-facing checkbox. Suggested bundling with Issue #7 deferral as a "military filer enhancements" track. No code change today. |
| 9 | VERIFIED CORRECT — line 1z explicitly excludes line 1i | Line 1z formula at TaxReturnComputeService.java:4137 sums exactly 8 components (1a-1h) — line 1i intentionally absent. Inline comment explicitly states the exclusion. Same exclusion holds for line 9, AGI, and taxable income. Existing test `computesLine1zSumExcludesCombatPayElection` (line 6291) asserts: W-2 wages $5,000 + code Q $1,500 + electCombatPay=true → line 1z = $5,000 (not $6,500). Closure: extended inline comment with audit ID + verification date + lock-in test name + clarifying sentence on the credit-only nature of line 1i. No code-behavior change. |
| 10 | VERIFIED CORRECT — all-or-none per person enforced | computeCombatPay ternary `Boolean.TRUE.equals(electTaxpayer) ? (taxpayerTotal == null ? BigDecimal.ZERO : taxpayerTotal) : null` is a pure boolean gate — no partial-amount path. UI offers no partial-amount input. Matches spec §10.2 ("all-or-none per person") and §10.4 ("elected with no code Q → 0, not null"). The BigDecimal.ZERO carve-out distinguishes "user elected but no Q found" from "user did not elect" — needed by downstream consumers. Protected by FOUR lock-in tests: `combatPayElectionWithoutCodeQIsZero`, `combatPayElectsWithZeroCodeQAmount_returnsZeroNotNull`, `combatPayElectionNullTreatedAsNoElection`, `combatPayMfjBothSpousesElect_aggregatesToLine1i`. Closure: 8-line breadcrumb comment added above the ternary enumerating all four lock-in tests by name. **Multi-test breadcrumb pattern** — when a verified-correct behavior is protected by several tests (not just one), enumerate them all in the comment. No code-behavior change. |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**
- **Single-guard MFS cascade now applied to 7 lines** (1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h, 1i) — fully matured pattern. Line 1i adds the **three-point gate variant** (spouseSsn / electSpouse / spouseTotal each explicitly nulled on MFS) when the underlying helper lacks defensive null-SSN handling. Pattern note: choose two-point vs three-point based on helper semantics.
- **Source-of-truth unification as a closure type** (Issue #2) — when a credit-worksheet consumer (EIC) reads its own duplicate election flag instead of the line's computed value (Schedule 8812 pattern), refactor to single source of truth + deprecate the duplicate via outstanding.md. Pre-launch app made the clean migration straightforward.
- **Implicit-overlay lock-in test pattern** (Issue #3) — when a call site relies on an invisible field-merger (`resolveSpouseIdentification` → `overlayResolvedField`), add a lock-in test exercising ONLY the modern UI path (no legacy form data). Catches accidental future removal of the resolver, which would otherwise silently break attribution.
- **Option-A vs Option-C scoped fix** (Issue #4) — when a helper has dangerous "null = no filter" semantic, choose between (A) defensive guards at each caller (tight scope; this audit) or (C) helper-side change (broader; cross-cutting refactor). Document Option C as deferred-enhancement with full verification scope so future implementer doesn't need to re-derive.
- **Multi-test breadcrumb** (Issue #10) — when an audit-verified-correct behavior is protected by SEVERAL tests, enumerate all of them in the breadcrumb comment so future readers see the full safety net.
- **Defer-with-comprehensive-sketch pattern** continues (Issues #7, #8) — each deferred entry in outstanding.md includes statutory basis, current-state verification, re-implementation scope estimate, "why deferred" rationale, and priority.

**Documentation accumulated:**
- `XLS/computations/1i.xlsx` (7 sheets, all 10 Code Validation issues marked closed/deferred/verified)
- `XLS/_tools/generate-1i.js` (new generator)
- `knowledge/line-1i-combat-pay.md` (renamed from `knowledge-line-1i-combat-pay.md`)
- `outstanding.md` four new sections: "Form 1040 Top-of-Form combat_zone Checkbox", "Line 1i — Nontaxable Combat Pay as IRA Contribution Compensation (IRC §219(f)(7))", "Helper sumW2Box12ByCodes — Defensive Null-SSN Handling (Option C Broader Cleanup)", "Line 27a EIC — Deprecate Legacy electNontaxableCombatPay YAML/UI Fields"

**Backend test count:** 730 → 741 (net +11 unit tests). Breakdown: +1 from #1 MFS guard, +1 from #2 EIC source-of-truth, +1 from #3 overlay lock-in, +1 from #4 null-SSN guard, +7 from #5 coverage closure. Verified-correct closures (#9, #10) and deferred closures (#7, #8) added no new tests but added multi-test breadcrumb references.


## 2026-05-10 — Line 1h (Other Earned Income — Cat 1 strike + Cat 2 excess deferrals + Cat 3 disability + Cat 4 corrective) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1h.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring 1c–1g structure). Walked through all 10 Code Validation issues. Net result: 1 backend defensive fix (MFS guard — single-guard cascade now applied to **6 lines**) + 1 generator rewrite (YAML-first xlsx generation, 118 forms regenerated) + 3 verified-correct closures with audit-ID breadcrumbs (incl. 1 self-reverted false positive) + 1 coverage-gap closure with +10 unit tests + 1 knowledge-file rename + 2 deferred-advisory closures with breadcrumbs + 1 lock-in test for Cat-3-vs-Cat-4 asymmetry + 1 pure-xlsx verified-correct closure. **+11 net unit tests** across the walkthrough (719 → 730). 2 new deferred-advisory entries in `outstanding.md`.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFENSIVE GAP — MFS guard not in orchestrator | `computeOtherEarnedIncome` read spouseSsn unconditionally and processed spouse-side excess deferrals + spouse-attributed 1099-R entries regardless of filing status. On MFS, spouse contributions could leak into the taxpayer's line 1h via two paths: (a) Cat 2 W-2 box 12 deferrals via spouse SSN attribution; (b) Cat 3/4 1099-R entries auto-resolved to owner="spouse" by recipientTIN. Same pattern as line 1c #1, 1d #1, 1e #2, 1f #2, 1g #1. Fix: (1) added `boolean isMfsReturn` parameter; (2) on MFS, null out spouseSsn and spouseAge — closes Cat 2 leak via existing computeDeferralTotalsForSsn null-check early-return; (3) added explicit `continue` filter at top of 1099-R loop on MFS when recipientTIN equals spouseSsnRaw — closes Cat 3/Cat 4 leaks (the selection-based `isBelowPlanMinimumRetirementAge` fallback would otherwise bypass owner attribution). Test: `line1hMfsGuard_spouseW2DeferralsAndDisability1099RNotAggregatedOnMfs` (verifies $1,000 taxpayer excess only, NOT $12,500 aggregated). **Single-guard MFS cascade now applied to 6 lines** (1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h) — established as the canonical multi-line orchestrator-MFS-guard pattern. Net +1 test. |
| 2 | DOC GAP — input xlsx missing dynamic array fields | `form-other-earned-income.xlsx` exposed only 4 scalar rows. Three root causes: (a) `generate-form-xlsx.js` walked only [(ngModel)] bindings — `*ngFor` blocks were structurally invisible; (b) read-only displayed values (`{{ record.payerName }}` etc.) were not extracted; (c) xlsx schema had no column for "this field repeats per array entry". Rewrote generator YAML-first: when a matching YAML exists in `C:/us-tax/yamls/` (indexed by top-level `name` field), sections are walked directly with `multiplicity:multiple` emitting per-entry rows. Schema expanded from 5 to 9 columns. All 118 input xlsx files regenerated; 29 switched from template-scan to YAML-driven, 75 still use template-scan (their YAMLs use a nested-root layout deferred to a future audit). Line 1h xlsx now has 33 fields across 6 sections. Side benefit: 28 other forms with matching flat-style YAMLs also produce more complete xlsx output. No code/test changes for line 1h compute specifically. |
| 3 | OBSERVATION — ROTH DEFERRAL EXCESS UNDETECTED — **AUDIT FALSE POSITIVE; SELF-REVERTED** | Audit observation asserted that Roth excess deferrals (codes AA/BB/EE) should be added to line 1h via a combined §402(g) pool. Initial fix applied combined-pool semantics with 5-field DeferralTotals record + 5 new tests + null-vs-ZERO adjustment in `computeExcessAmount` — but on re-reading `lines/1h.md` §11 Discrepancy 3, IRS 2025 line 1h instructions, and Pub. 525 ("Excess deferrals"), the IRS rule is unambiguous: only the PRE-TAX portion of any §402(g) excess is the line 1h adjustment because the line 1h adjustment puts back into wages what was excluded from W-2 box 1 — Roth amounts are AFTER-TAX and ALREADY in box 1, so adding any Roth excess would DOUBLE-COUNT. The audit author had mischaracterized §402(g) ("deferred, not deducted" — wrong: Roth IS deducted from take-home but IS taxed in box 1). Reverted same-day: restored 3-field `DeferralTotals` + pre-tax-only constants + original `computeExcessAmount` semantics; deleted the 5 wrong tests; restored `computesLine1hRothDeferrals_AA_BB_notCountedAsExcess` from git baseline ($30k AA → null). xlsx Issue #3 reframed from "RESOLVED" to "AUDIT FALSE POSITIVE — VERIFIED CORRECT 2026-05-10" with closure narrative explaining the misread. **First instance of self-reverted audit issue** — new pattern: when an audit's recommended fix conflicts with the authoritative spec (lines/<n>.md or IRS instructions), STOP and re-verify before applying — the spec is more authoritative than the audit observation. Net 0 code changes, 0 net test changes (tests removed from intermediate state). |
| 4 | TEST COVERAGE GAPS | Audit identified 7 sub-bullets: (a) age 49 vs 50 boundary; (b) age 63 vs 64 boundary; (c) §457(b) excess detection; (d) MFJ both-spouses excess in different pools; (e) 1099-MISC strike flag false → not included; (f) 1099-R box 2a empty AND box 1 present (Cat 3 fallback); (g) zero-amount silent paths. Added 10 unit tests (boundaries split into positive/negative pairs): `_age49_noCatchup` ($24,500 D → $1,000 excess) + `_age50_regularCatchup_noExcess` ($30,000 D → null at limit $31,000); `_age63_lastEnhancedCatchup_noExcess` ($34,000 D → null at limit $34,750) + `_age64_revertsToRegularCatchup` ($32,000 D → $1,000 excess); `_codeG` ($25,000 G → $1,500) + `_codeS` ($18,000 S → $1,500); `_MfjBothSpousesExcessInDifferentPools` (D $25k + G $25.5k → $3,500 = $1,500 + $2,000); `_box3FlagFalse_notIncluded` ($500 with flag false → null); `_box2aEmpty_fallsBackToBox1` ($1,500 gross used when taxable absent); `_storesZeroAndEmptyStatements` (locks in current 0-not-null storage with empty statements list). Side observation locked in by test (g): when ALL Cat paths receive present-but-zero amounts, line 1h is stored as `BigDecimal.ZERO` (not null); statements list still correctly empty via `hasNonZeroAmount` filter. Documented as a stylistic 0-vs-null choice, not a behavior change. Net +10 tests. |
| 5 | KNOWLEDGE FILE NAMING DEVIATION | `knowledge/knowledge_line1h_other_earned_income.md` used the legacy `knowledge_line1h_` prefix while line 1c–1g converged on `line-{N}-{topic}.md` (hyphen form, no `knowledge_` prefix). Renamed via plain `mv` (`knowledge/` lives outside the `us-tax-be/` git repo). Updated the single Sheet 2 reference in `generate-1h.js`. Naming now consistent across line 1c–1h. Older legacy `knowledge_line<n>.md` files (lines 8, 9, 10–33, 3ab, 4abc) remain — future sweep, out of scope. No content change. |
| 6 | CODE 8 + CODE P COMBINED — POTENTIAL NULL AMOUNT — DEFERRED PER AUDIT + BREADCRUMB ADDED | When a 1099-R has only code P (no code 8), the Cat 4 outer guard `(hasCurrentCorrective || hasPriorCorrective)` enters but does nothing — no owner attribution, no amount added, no flag emitted. Behavior is mathematically correct (code P represents excess deferrals taxable in a PRIOR year, not the current line 1h), but a confused user might wonder why their uploaded 1099-R produced nothing. Audit recommended OPTIONAL `LINE1H_CODE_P_PRIOR_YEAR_INFO` advisory and explicitly said "Defer". Layered closure: (1) Six-line breadcrumb comment at `computeOtherEarnedIncome` line 10132 explaining the intentional `||`, citing audit ID, warning future readers not to "fix" by adding code-P amounts (real bug); (2) Deferred-enhancement section in `outstanding.md` ("Line 1h — Code-P-Only 1099-R Advisory Flag (Deferred 2026-05-10)") with full re-implementation scope (~15-20 min). No advisory flag emitted today. No code-behavior change, no tests. |
| 7 | CODE 3 + CODE 8 COMBINATION — DISABILITY WINS — VERIFIED CORRECT | When a 1099-R has codes "3,8", Cat 3 (disability) at line 10108 processes via `if (codes.contains("3"))`, and Cat 4 outer guard at line 10138 includes `&& !codes.contains("3")` — corrective path skipped exclusively. Behavior matches `lines/1h.md` §7/§8 and the existing comment block. Lock-in test `prioritizesDisabilityWhenCodesInclude3And8` already covered: taxable=$1,200 on a 1099-R coded "3 8" → line 1h = $1,200 with statement "Disability pension wages" only. Audit-revisited-as-verified-and-documented closure: extended breadcrumb comment at line 10132 to cite audit ID + verification date. No code change, no test change. |
| 8 | AGE BOUNDARY — REGULAR vs ENHANCED CATCH-UP — VERIFIED CORRECT | `catchupEligible = ageAtYearEnd != null && ageAtYearEnd >= 50` (line 11053) and `enhancedCatchupEligible = ageAtYearEnd != null && ageAtYearEnd >= 60 && ageAtYearEnd <= 63` (line 11055) — both inclusive boundaries correct per SECURE 2.0 §603. Five lock-in tests now in place (four added in Issue #4 plus the pre-existing age-62 mid-range test). Audit-revisited-as-verified-and-documented closure: extended breadcrumb comment at line 11054 to cite audit ID + verification date and enumerate the four boundary lock-in tests. No code change. |
| 9 | TAXABLE AMOUNT FALLBACK — Cat 3 vs Cat 4 ASYMMETRY — DEFERRED PER AUDIT + BREADCRUMBS + LOCK-IN TEST | Cat 3 (disability) falls back to box 1 (gross) when box 2a is null; Cat 4 (corrective) does NOT. The asymmetry is intentional — per IRS 1099-R instructions, issuers MUST populate box 2a for code-8 corrective distributions because the corrective amount must be split from any non-taxable basis. But the design was undocumented and a future reader could "fix" Cat 4 by mirroring Cat 3, introducing a real bug. Layered closure: (1) Three-line breadcrumb comment at Cat 3 (line 10122) explaining the deliberate box-1 fallback; (2) Eight-line breadcrumb comment at Cat 4 (line 10149) citing IRS instructions, warning future readers NOT to mirror Cat 3, pointing at the deferred advisory; (3) New lock-in unit test `computesLine1hCorrectiveDistribution_box2aEmpty_excludedNoFallback` — code-8 1099-R with null taxableAmount + gross=$1,500 → line 1h null (proves no fallback); (4) Deferred-enhancement section in `outstanding.md` ("Line 1h — Corrective Distribution Box 2a Missing Advisory (Deferred 2026-05-10)") with `LINE1H_CORRECTIVE_BOX2A_MISSING` re-implementation scope. Net +1 test. |
| 10 | VERIFICATION — FORM 4852 SUBSTITUTE WAGES NOT WIRED TO LINE 1H — VERIFIED CORRECT | Two-part verification: (1) grep of `TaxReturnComputeService.java` for "4852" returned matches only in W-2 substitute path — Form 4852 has ZERO references in `computeOtherEarnedIncome`. (2) grep of `lines/1h.md` for "4852" returned no matches — the spec doesn't actually mention Form 4852 (the original audit framing implied otherwise but was incorrect). Form 4852 functions as a W-2 substitute — its amounts populate the same box 1 / box 12 fields the missing W-2 would have, flowing to line 1h via standard W-2 box-12 ingest paths. **Pure xlsx-flip closure** (no breadcrumb, no test, no outstanding.md entry — first instance of this closure type, used when verifying an absence). The audit row itself serves as the audit trail. |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**
- **Single-guard MFS cascade pattern now applied to 6 lines** (1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h) — fully matured canonical multi-line orchestrator-MFS-guard pattern. Line 1h adds the wrinkle that the spouseSsn-null cascade closes Cat 2 (W-2 box 12 deferrals) but NOT Cat 3/4 (1099-R entries with selection-based fallback) — required adding an explicit recipientTIN-spouse `continue` filter at the top of the 1099-R loop. Two-path MFS cascade pattern documented for future lines that combine W-2 + 1099-R sources.
- **Audit observations can themselves be wrong** (Issue #3) — first instance of self-reverted audit issue. New rule: when an audit issue's recommended fix conflicts with the authoritative spec (`lines/<n>.md` or IRS instructions), STOP and re-verify before applying — even if the audit framing seems internally coherent. The lines/<n>.md spec is more authoritative than the audit observation. **Self-revert protocol** validated: revert in same session via clear narrative ("the original code was IRS-correct"), update the audit row to "AUDIT FALSE POSITIVE — VERIFIED CORRECT" (not "RESOLVED"), restore exact pre-fix state from git baseline.
- **Asymmetric breadcrumb pattern** (Issue #9) — full triple-protection of an intentional design asymmetry: inline comment at BOTH call sites + lock-in negative test + deferred-advisory entry in outstanding.md. Mirrors but extends the false-positive-breadcrumb convention from line 1b #7 / 1c #2 / 1d #2 / 1g #7+#8 — when the design itself is asymmetric (vs verified-correct symmetric code), comments at both sides are needed.
- **Pure xlsx-flip closure type** added (Issue #10) — when verifying an *absence* (correct code is missing from a path where it shouldn't be), the absent code is correct, no breadcrumb has a target line, and the xlsx audit trail itself is sufficient documentation. First instance.
- **Generator rewrite as audit root cause** (Issue #2) — extends the line 1g #2 "regex out of sync" pattern. Where line 1g found a missing tag-name match, line 1h found a deeper structural blindness (template-scan can't see *ngFor blocks or read-only display interpolations). Solution: switch to YAML-first generation when YAML is available. Same root-cause-vs-symptom verification rule: when audit recommendation is "re-run generator", verify the generator actually produces the expected output before re-running.
- **Defer-with-comprehensive-sketch pattern** continues (Issues #6 and #9) — ~15-20 min implementation sketch in outstanding.md per deferred advisory; mirrors the line 1f deferrals + line 1g #10.

**Documentation accumulated:**
- `XLS/computations/1h.xlsx` (7 sheets, all 10 Code Validation issues marked closed/deferred/verified)
- `XLS/_tools/generate-1h.js` (new generator)
- `XLS/_tools/generate-form-xlsx.js` (rewritten YAML-first; serves all line-input xlsx generators)
- `XLS/input_forms/*.xlsx` (118 regenerated)
- `knowledge/line-1h-other-earned-income.md` (renamed from `knowledge_line1h_other_earned_income.md`)
- `outstanding.md` two new sections: "Line 1h — Corrective Distribution Box 2a Missing Advisory (Deferred 2026-05-10)" and "Line 1h — Code-P-Only 1099-R Advisory Flag (Deferred 2026-05-10)"

**Backend test count:** 719 → 730 (net +11 unit tests). Throughout the day there was a brief intermediate state of 724 (after Issue #3 wrong fix added 5 Roth-pool tests); the +5 wrongly-added tests were removed during the #3 self-revert. Net day's work is +11 (= +1 from #1 MFS guard + +10 from #4 coverage + +1 from #9 Cat-4 no-fallback − 1 overlap because the original `computesLine1hRothDeferrals_AA_BB_notCountedAsExcess` was renamed and restored without being net-new).


## 2026-05-09 — Line 1g (Wages from Form 8919 — Uncollected SS/Medicare Tax) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1g.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring 1a/1b/1c/1d/1e/1f structure). Walked through all 10 Code Validation issues. Net result: 1 backend defensive fix (MFS guard — single-guard cascade now applied to 5 lines) + 1 generator regex fix (wider impact: 42+ form components benefited) + 1 cross-line collision advisory + 6 unit-test additions + 1 E2E test + 1 knowledge-file rename + content refresh + 2 verified-false-positive breadcrumb comments + 1 deferred enhancement (Schedule 2 line 13 codes A/B/M/N). **10 new unit tests + 1 new E2E test** across the walkthrough.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFENSIVE GAP — MFS guard not in orchestrator | `computeUncollectedSSTax` always processed both spouses regardless of filing status. On MFS with stale `uncollected-ss-medicare-spouse` data (e.g., from a prior MFJ year), spouse contribution leaked into taxpayer's line 1g + Schedule 2 line 6. Same pattern as line 1c #1, 1d #1, 1e #2, 1f #2 — single-guard cascade now established across 5 lines. Added `boolean isMfsReturn` parameter to `computeUncollectedSSTax`; on MFS, spouse's `computeForm8919ForPerson` call receives null → `getList(null, "firms")` returns empty list → `hasAnyForm8919Inputs` false → early-return `Form8919Result(null, null, null)` → spouse contributes null → addNonNull skips spouse → line 1g + Schedule 2 line 6 are taxpayer-only. Form 8959 line 3 path (in `computeAdditionalMedicareTax`) already gated by `mfj`. Test: `mfsExcludesSpouseForm8919FromLine1g`. Net +1 test. |
| 2 | DOC GAP — input xlsx out of sync with YAML | `form-uncollected-ss-medicare-taxpayer.xlsx` was missing the `reasonCode` field. Audit framed as "re-run the generator" but tracing revealed the actual root cause: `generate-form-xlsx.js` regex (line 112) only listed legacy PrimeNG tag names (`p-dropdown`, `p-calendar`) — modern `<p-select>` (used by `reasonCode` in line 1g and 41 other forms) and `<p-datepicker>` were never matched, silently dropping fields from the export. Added both modern names to the alternation list (kept legacy for backward compat). Re-ran generator → 118 input xlsx files refreshed (3,188 fields total). Verified: `form-uncollected-ss-medicare-taxpayer.xlsx` now contains `reasonCode` at row 3. Free upgrade to all 42+ p-select-using forms. No code/test changes for line 1g specifically. |
| 3 | COLLISION GUARD INCOMPLETE — line 1g vs line 1c (Form 4137 tips) | Per `lines/1g.md` §10.3, the same wages should not be counted on both Form 4137 (tips → line 1c) AND Form 8919 (misclassified wages → line 1g). The line 1b household-employee guard exists; the line 1c guard did not. Added per-person non-blocking `FORM8919_LINE1C_COLLISION_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}` advisory inside `computeForm8919ForPerson` after the form8919 result is built, when `form4137 != null` for that person. Trusts user judgment but flags the cross-form scenario without false-positive blocking. Auto-detection (per-row fuzzy payer-name + dollar-overlap matching) rejected as over-engineering. Tests: `emitsLineCollisionAdvisoryWhenBothForm4137AndForm8919GeneratedForTaxpayer`, `noLineCollisionAdvisoryWhenOnlyForm8919Generated`, `mfsSuppressesSpouseLineCollisionAdvisory` (locks the cascade interaction with Issue #1). Net +3 tests. |
| 4 | TEST COVERAGE GAPS | 7 sub-bullets identified; sub-bullet (a) MFS guard already closed by Issue #1. Added 6 new unit tests for the remaining gaps: `form8919RrtaCompensationContributesToLine8` (b — RRTA tier-1 via W-2 box 14 `rRTACompensationW2Box14`), `form8919Form4137Line10ContributesToLine8` (c — locks `computeTips → computeUncollectedSSTax` execution-order dependency), `form8919CodeHWithout1099EmitsSoftWarning` (d — non-blocking warning), `form8919CodeGEmitsSS8Advisory` (e — non-blocking advisory; no IRS date for code G), `form8919SsWageBaseCapZerosLine10` (f — when prior wages reach $176,100, line 9/10/11 = 0 but Medicare line 12 still applies — no annual cap), `form8919MfjBothSpousesAggregateLine1gAndSchedule2Line6` (g — complement to Issue #1's MFS test). Net +6 tests; total Form 8919 unit-test count grew from 4 to 14. |
| 5 | KNOWLEDGE FILE NAMING DEVIATION | `knowledge/knowledge-line-1g-form-8919.md` used the `knowledge-line-1g-` prefix (word "knowledge" duplicated in filename since folder is `knowledge/`) instead of the established `line-{N}-{topic}.md` pattern (cf. `line-1c-tip-income.md`, `line-1d-medicaid-waiver-payments.md`, `line-1e-dependent-care-benefits.md`, `line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md`). Renamed via plain `mv` (project root not a git repo). Glob `knowledge/line-*.md` now discovers all line-knowledge files. No content change. Verified no broken cross-references. |
| 6 | KNOWLEDGE FILE OUTDATED — verification log + closed-items table | File dated 2026-04-11 with stale entries (e.g., "1 Form 8919 unit test" undercount; missing isMfsReturn parameter; missing `FORM8919_LINE1C_COLLISION_*` flag; no closure tables for any audit issues). Refresh: bumped header to 2026-05-09; updated §6 entry-point signature with `isMfsReturn` + new MFS gating cascade table (mirrors line 1d/1e/1f convention); added `FORM8919_LINE1C_COLLISION_*` rows to flag inventory; replaced §10 1-test undercount with full 14-test inventory each annotated with closing Issue; updated §11 "Not covered by E2E" to mark closed-at-unit-layer entries; restructured §13 into Open + Closed sub-tables; added new §14 Verification log mirroring line 1d/1e/1f pattern. File grew 287 → 339 lines. |
| 7 | LINE 8 CONTRACT — null-safety on form4137 — VERIFIED FALSE POSITIVE | `form4137 == null ? null : form4137.getLine10UnreportedTipsSubjectSocialSecurity()` is correct; `addNonNull` semantics handle null gracefully. Added one-line breadcrumb comment near the read citing `1g.xlsx Issue #7 verified-OK (2026-05-09)` so future audits don't re-flag. Mirrors the false-positive-breadcrumb convention from line 1b #7 / line 1c #2 / line 1d #2. No behavior change. |
| 8 | FORM 8919 LINE 7 NOT SET WHEN form8919=null — VERIFIED FALSE POSITIVE | When firmEntries empty, no Form 8919 instantiated → no line 7 set → spec §11 display correctly absent. Added 4-line breadcrumb comment immediately above `new Form8919()` instantiation citing `1g.xlsx Issue #8 verified-OK (2026-05-09)`. No behavior change. |
| 9 | NO E2E TEST FOR MFS GUARD | E2E counterpart to Issue #1's unit test. Added `MFS: stale spouse Form 8919 data is excluded from line 1g (1g.xlsx Issue #1 lock-in)` to `e2e/tests/line1g-uncollected-ss-medicare.spec.ts`. Setup: clearUserData + seedRequiredDob (taxpayer 111-11-1111) + seedSpouseApi (spouse 222-22-2222) + setFilingStatusApi(MFS) + save taxpayer Form 8919 ($7k) + save STALE spouse Form 8919 ($5k) + computeReturnApi. Asserts: form8919Spouse = null; line 1g = $7k (not $12k); Schedule 2 line 6 = $536 (taxpayer line 13 only). Imports updated to add `setFilingStatusApi`. Net +1 E2E test. |
| 10 | OBSERVATION — W-2 BOX 12 CODES M/N → SCHEDULE 2 LINE 13 — DEFERRED | Audit asked to "verify with grep before logging". Verified: `Schedule2OtherTaxes.uncollectedSocialSecurityMedicareRrtaTax` field exists; line 18 total formula references it (line 11809) explicitly commented as "line 13 (deferred — null today)". But no compute path scans W-2 box 12 for codes A/B/M/N. New `outstanding.md` entry "Schedule 2 Line 13 — Uncollected SS/Medicare or RRTA Tax on Tips and Group-Term Life Insurance (W-2 Box 12 Codes A, B, M, N)" with full implementation sketch (~30-45 min): new `sumW2Box12CodesABMN` SSN-filtered helper + `isMfsReturn` cascade + wire to existing field + 4 tests. Code 11809 comment expanded to cross-reference the deferral entry. No code change. Same defer-with-comprehensive-sketch pattern as the 5 line 1f deferrals + line 1d §131(c) per-individual cap. |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**
- **Single-guard MFS cascade pattern now applied to 5 lines** (1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g) — established as the canonical multi-line orchestrator-MFS-guard pattern. Each line has its own `isMfsReturn` parameter wired from `prepare()`; passing null/empty as spouse data triggers the per-line empty-result early-return; addNonNull aggregations cascade through to line outputs + downstream Schedule 2 / Form 8959 lines.
- **Generator regex out-of-sync as audit root cause** — Issue #2 framed as "re-run the generator" but tracing revealed a wider scope: legacy PrimeNG tag names (`p-dropdown`, `p-calendar`) in the regex meant 42+ forms using modern names (`p-select`, `p-datepicker`) silently dropped fields from xlsx exports. Audit closure regenerated all 118 input xlsx files. Pattern: when audit recommendation is "re-run X", verify X actually does what the recommendation assumes before re-running.
- **Per-person collision-detection advisory** (Issue #3) — first cross-line collision flag; trusts user judgment; mirrors soft-defense pattern from line 1d #9, 1e #7, 1f #1, 1f #8.
- **False-positive breadcrumb comments** (Issues #7, #8) — established codification across line 1b #7, 1c #2, 1d #2, and now line 1g #7+#8. Add a one-line comment near verified-OK code citing the audit ID + verification date so future audit walkthroughs don't re-flag.
- **Defer-with-comprehensive-sketch pattern** continues (Issue #10) — ~30-45 min implementation sketch in outstanding.md per deferred enhancement; mirrors the 5 line 1f deferrals.

**Documentation accumulated:**
- `XLS/computations/1g.xlsx` (7 sheets)
- `XLS/_tools/generate-1g.js` (new generator)
- `XLS/_tools/generate-form-xlsx.js` — regex fix (Issue #2 free upgrade for 42+ forms)
- `knowledge/line-1g-form-8919.md` (renamed + refreshed; 339 lines after content updates)
- `outstanding.md` — 1 new deferred entry (Schedule 2 line 13 codes A/B/M/N)
- 2 false-positive breadcrumb code comments in `TaxReturnComputeService.java` (Issues #7, #8)

**718/718 backend tests pass** at end of day (started at 708 from end of 1f closeout; +10 net for 1g Issues #1+#3+#4 = 1+3+6). Angular build clean throughout. **All 10 issues from `1g.xlsx` Code Validation closed.** 1 new E2E test added (Playwright; not in backend test count).

**Side fix during the audit** (post-Issue #2): re-running `generate-form-xlsx.js` benefited 42+ form components using `<p-select>` and any using `<p-datepicker>`. The line 1g `reasonCode` field was the trigger but the fix was wider — silent xlsx gaps closed across the entire form catalogue.


## 2026-05-08 — Line 1f (Taxable Adoption Benefits — Form 8839 Part III line 31) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1f.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring 1a/1b/1c/1d/1e structure). Walked through all 12 Code Validation issues. Net result: 1 BUG fix (MFS spouse W-2 box T leak) + 1 doc/code reconciliation (MFS-disallowed flag severity) + 1 over-blocking → per-child gate refactor + 1 backend smart-default + 1 audit-revisited-as-verified + 4 deferred items + 1 new knowledge file + non-blocking advisories for 2 edge cases + multiple test additions. **20 new unit tests** across the walkthrough.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | NOT IMPLEMENTED (audit re-scoped from "test gap") | Foreign-child finality-year retroactive exclusion. Audit framed as test-gap; tracing revealed the spec's negative-L31 mechanism (lines/1f.md §8) requires a missing branch — not just a missing test. The non-special-needs `L24 = min(L21, L22)` formula caps `L29 ≤ L22`, so `L30 ≤ L23` and `L31 ≥ 0` always; only the special-needs override produces negative L31. Closure: non-blocking `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_FOREIGN_CHILD_FINALITY_RETRO_PARTIAL` advisory inside per-child Part III block when `foreignChild && adoptionFinal && priorYearEmployerBenefitsByChild[N] > 0 && !specialNeeds`. Suppressed for special-needs cases (already handled). Full implementation deferred to `outstanding.md` "Form 8839 Foreign-Child Finality-Year Retroactive Exclusion" — per-child `deferredPriorYearForeignBenefits` UI input + L22 enrichment + allocation-flag relaxation. Tests: `emitsForeignChildFinalityRetroAdvisoryWhenPriorYearBenefitsPresent`, `noForeignChildFinalityRetroAdvisoryWhenSpecialNeeds`. +2 tests. |
| 2 | BUG — REAL CORRECTNESS GAP | SSN filter on W-2 box 12 code T sum. `sumW2AdoptionBenefits(w2Entries)` aggregated ALL W-2s without SSN filtering — on MFS the spouse's W-2 box T leaked into the taxpayer's line 1f. Same pattern as line 1e Issue #2 (which we closed earlier). Created new helper `sumW2AdoptionBenefitsForSsns(w2Entries, ssns...)`. `computeAdoptionBenefits` signature gained `you`, `spouse`, `isMfsReturn` parameters (mirrors line 1c/1d/1e). SSN-filtered sum: taxpayer-only on MFS, taxpayer + spouse on MFJ; legacy unfiltered fallback when neither SSN populated. Test: `mfsExcludesSpouseW2BoxTFromLine1f`. +1 test. |
| 3 | DOC vs CODE INCONSISTENCY | `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_MFS_EXCLUSION_DISALLOWED` was emitted as blocking (code) but documented as non-blocking (`dependencies/1f.md`). Reconciled to non-blocking: the MFS-no-exception return — full W-2 box T as taxable wages on line 1f — is a valid IRS return; blocking would prevent the user from filing correctly. Mirrors line 1e Issue #1 pattern (silently skip §21 credit, not block). Updated 2 existing tests (`adoptionBenefitsMfsWithoutExceptionIsFullyTaxable`, `adoptionCreditMfsWithoutExceptionEmitsBlockingFlag` → renamed to `...EmitsAdvisoryAndSuppressesForm`); message expanded to tell the user "the full W-2 box 12 code T amount has been treated as taxable wages on Form 1040 line 1f". 0 net tests (assertion flips). |
| 4 | KNOWLEDGE FILE MISSING | Created `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` (336 lines) mirroring line 1e structure. 11 sections + verification log. Function-name convention applied throughout. Notably documents the **two-path MAGI architecture** (Phase 1 imports + Phase 2 G1 fallback — see Issue #9). |
| 5 | TEST COVERAGE GAP — MAGI PHASEOUT BOUNDARIES | Added 3 boundary tests at the three critical MAGI thresholds: `line1fAtPhaseoutStartNoExclusionReduction` (MAGI = $259,190 → fraction=0); `line1fAtFiftyPercentPhaseoutHalfTaxable` (MAGI = $279,190 → fraction=0.5000); `line1fAtPhaseoutCeilingFullyTaxable` (MAGI = $299,190 → fraction=1.0000). Locks all three branches of `computeAdoptionPhaseoutFraction`. New helper `buildPart3PhaseoutFixture(magi)` distinct from the existing Part-II-credit-focused `buildAdoptionFormWithMagi`. +3 tests. |
| 6 | NOT IMPLEMENTED — Shared-adoption $17,280 split with non-spouse | Deferred per-issue documentation pattern (mirrors §1372 deferral, foreign-finality retro deferral). Joint adoption with a non-spouse (e.g., domestic partners pre-marriage) requires the $17,280 max to be split between parties; current code unconditionally assigns full $17,280 → joint over-claim by up to $17,280 per shared child. Full implementation sketch (~45-60 min) in `outstanding.md` "Form 8839 Shared-Adoption L19 Split with Non-Spouse"; per-child boolean + per-child allocation input + backend `min(maxPerChild, allocation)` + validation flag + 2 tests. `dependencies/1f.md` cross-reference enriched. No code change. |
| 7 | NOT IMPLEMENTED — IRC §1372 over-2% S corp owner | Deferred. >2% S corp shareholder-employees treated as partners for fringe-benefit purposes per IRC §1372 — §137 exclusion disallowed for them. Currently no UI gate or backend check → silent over-claim by up to $17,280/child/year. Full implementation sketch (~30-45 min) in `outstanding.md` "Form 8839 §1372 Over-2% S Corporation Owner Exclusion Disallowance"; per-W-2 boolean preferred over per-return for accuracy + dual-path backend + BLOCKING flag (hard rule, no exception list). `dependencies/1f.md` cross-reference enriched. No code change. |
| 8 | NOT ENFORCED — IRC §137(d) same-expense coordination | Per-child non-blocking advisory `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_VERIFY_NO_DOUBLE_DIP_CHILD_N` added when both Part II expenses + Part III benefits are populated for the same child. Trusts the user (per Form 8839 line 5 instruction "don't include amounts reimbursed by your employer"); doesn't auto-subtract (would risk under-claim for users who already followed instructions). Auto-subtraction (Option B) deferred to when nonrefundable Part II credit lands — sketch in `outstanding.md` "Form 8839 §137(d) Same-Expense Coordination — Auto-Subtraction" (~30-45 min when revisited). 3 new tests: `emitsDoubleDipAdvisoryWhenBothCreditAndBenefitsForSameChild`, `noDoubleDipAdvisoryWhenOnlyEmployerBenefits`, `noDoubleDipAdvisoryWhenOnlyQualifiedExpenses`. +3 tests. |
| 9 | AUDIT REVISITED — VERIFIED-AND-DOCUMENTED | Original 1f.xlsx claim ("no compute path populates MAGI" → silent over-claim, HIGH severity) was **wrong**. Tracing revealed the `applyAdoptionCredit` G1 fallback (called from `prepare()` after Form 2555 + Schedule 3 lines 1-6b) auto-derives MAGI = AGI + Form 2555 lines 45/50 + Puerto Rico + Form 4563 line 15 when Phase 1 had no manual entry, then re-runs Part II + Part III with corrected phaseout, AND propagates the corrected line 1f back to `form1040.income.adoptionBenefits`. Closure: VERIFIED-AND-DOCUMENTED. Added 3 G1 lock-in tests: `g1AutoDerivesMagiAndAppliesPhaseoutWhenMagiAbsent`, `g1DoesNotFireWhenMagiManuallyProvided`, `g1IncludesPuertoRicoAddBackInAutoDerivedMagi`. Knowledge file §5 expanded from "Single-Pass Architecture" to "Two-Path MAGI Architecture" with full Phase 1 + G1 description; iterative-AGI approximation note. `dependencies/1f.md` imports row enriched with G1 cross-reference + full add-back formula. Issue #12 (MAGI null silent over-claim) closed as MOOT (G1 prevents the silent-exclusion case). +3 tests. |
| 10 | UX SHARP EDGE — Allocation mismatch | Backend smart default + ±$1 tolerance. When `currentYearBenefitsAllocation` is empty across all child slots AND `hasBenefits=true`, auto-distribute `w2TotalBenefits` evenly with last-child-absorbs-rounding-remainder so the sum is exact. Plus ±$1 tolerance on explicit user allocation (handles `$5,001 vs $5,000` rounding artifact from `$5,000 / 3 = $1,667 × 3`). Strict-match preserved for partial allocation (mixed state — user took control of some children) and for diffs > $1 (genuine errors). 5 new tests: `autoDistributesAllocationWhenSingleChildAndNoAllocation`, `autoDistributesAllocationEvenlyAcrossThreeChildrenWithRoundingRemainder`, `allocationToleranceAcceptsOneDollarRoundingVariance`, `allocationMismatchStillFiresOnLargeDifference`, `allocationMismatchStillFiresOnPartialAllocation`. +5 tests. |
| 11 | OVER-BLOCKING — `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_W2_REQUIRED` (Option C — partial closure) | Function-level gate fired blocking when ANY Part III input present without W-2 box T — over-blocked legitimate IRC §137(a)(3) special-needs off-W-2 path. Removed the function-level flag; replaced with per-child `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_OFF_W2_NOT_SPECIAL_NEEDS_CHILD_N` blocking flag that fires only for non-special-needs children with off-W-2 amounts and no W-2 box T. Special-needs final children pass the gate (legitimate per §4.2). 1 existing test updated to assert the per-child code; 3 new tests: `specialNeedsOffW2BenefitsDoNotBlockButPartIIINotEnabled`, `mixedSpecialAndRegularChildrenBlocksOnlyTheRegularChild`, `priorYearOffW2BenefitsAlsoBlockNonSpecialNeedsChild`. **DEFERRED:** full §4.2 enablement (allowPartIII relaxation + L23 source change → spec-mandated negative line 1f for pure off-W-2 special-needs case). See `outstanding.md` "Form 8839 Special-Needs Off-W-2 Part III Enablement" for ~30-45 min full-implementation sketch. +3 tests. |
| 12 | MAGI = null SILENT FULL EXCLUSION | Closed as MOOT — Issue #9's G1 fallback prevents the silent-exclusion case. No code change; documentation updated to note the moot closure. |

**Patterns established / reinforced:**
- **SSN-filter at W-2-aggregation layer now standard** across lines 1c/1d/1e/1f (each line has a dedicated `sumW2*ForSsns` helper that mirrors the pattern); the line 1f signature change (gained `you`, `spouse`, `isMfsReturn` parameters) reaches feature-parity with line 1e.
- **Per-child blocking flags** as a new pattern for the adoption path (vs the per-spouse cascade pattern from lines 1c/1d/1e). Per-child flags carry child-index suffixes (`_CHILD_1`, `_CHILD_2`, ...) so the UI can surface which child needs correction.
- **Audit-revisited-as-verified-and-documented** (Issue #9) — when an audit's severity is overstated, the closure can be "verify the actual code, write tests to lock the existing behavior, expand documentation". Original audit author misses the G1 path; walkthrough catches it. Same pattern surfaced earlier in line 1c Issue #2 (false-positive verified).
- **Backend smart defaults + tolerance** (Issue #10) — auto-distribute when user didn't allocate; ±$1 absorbs rounding artifacts. UI is unchanged; backend defaults to sensible behavior.
- **Partial closure with deferred enhancement** (Issue #11 Option C) — fix the immediate concern (over-blocking) with a per-child gate; track the full spec compliance (negative-line-1f for off-W-2 special-needs) as a separate deferred item. Mirror this where audit issues bundle a primary concern with a related but distinct enhancement.
- **Audit refinement during walkthrough** — multiple issues required re-scoping when tracing the actual code (#1 was test-gap → not-implemented; #9 was HIGH → false-positive). The xlsx Code Validation sheet is a starting hypothesis, not a final verdict; verify-then-decide.
- **G1 MAGI auto-derive pattern** (line 1f) — analogous to line 1e's two-pass architecture but for a single field. Phase 1 reads a manual entry; Phase 2 (after AGI + Form 2555 are computed) auto-derives via inline formula and re-runs the dependent computation. Knowledge-file §5 documents this for line 1f; could become a reusable architecture pattern for any tax-year-spanning MAGI-driven phaseout.

**Documentation accumulated:**
- `XLS/computations/1f.xlsx` (7 sheets)
- `XLS/_tools/generate-1f.js` (new generator)
- `knowledge/line-1f-employer-adoption-benefits.md` (now 314 lines after §5 expansion + verification log entries)
- `outstanding.md` — 5 new deferred entries (foreign-child finality retro; shared-adoption split; §1372 S-corp; §137(d) auto-subtract; special-needs off-W-2 Part III enablement)
- `lines/1f.md` cross-references (no edits needed for this audit; spec was already well-aligned)
- `dependencies/1f.md` — multiple row enrichments (`Shared-adoption L19 split`, `Over-2% S corp owner`, `imports.magiForAdoptionBenefitsExclusion`, flag table updated to add `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_OFF_W2_NOT_SPECIAL_NEEDS_CHILD_N`)

**708/708 backend tests pass** at end of day (started at 688 from end of 1e; +20 net). Angular build clean throughout. All 12 issues from `1f.xlsx` Code Validation closed (some via deferral with implementation sketch; rest in-code).

**Side discovery during the walkthrough:** the `applyAdoptionCredit` G1 fallback (Issue #9) is more sophisticated than the original 1f.xlsx audit gave it credit for — it not only auto-derives MAGI but ALSO re-runs both Part II AND Part III with the corrected phaseout AND propagates the corrected line 1f back to Form 1040 income. This is a one-shot iterative-AGI approximation (G1 uses Phase-1-AGI, doesn't re-iterate when corrected line 1f bumps AGI), but the math is correct under the IRS form's single-pass MAGI assumption.


## 2026-05-07 — Line 1e (Taxable Dependent Care Benefits — Form 2441 line 26) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1e.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring the 1a/1b/1c/1d structure: Main Computation, Inputs, Reference Data, Side-Effect Outputs, Validation Flags, Code Validation, Output Flow). Walked through all 9 Code Validation issues one-by-one. Net result: 1 BUG fix (MFS-no-exception §21 credit) + 1 real correctness gap fix (MFS spouse W-2 box 10 leak) + 1 spec-compliance fix (DCB write-in PDF label) + 4 test additions + 1 new knowledge file + 2 backend defensive gates + 2 non-blocking user advisories. **17 new unit tests** across the walkthrough.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | BUG — MFS NO-EXCEPTION CREDIT NOT BLOCKED | IRC §21(e)(2) disallows the §21 credit for MFS filers without the separation exception. Code applied the $2,500 §129 exclusion cap correctly (Step 12) but did not block Part II credit. Added `boolean creditDisallowed` field to `DependentCareComputation` record (initially `mfsCreditDisallowed`; renamed in Issue #8 to cover broader causes). Phase 1 sets it via `isMfs && !mfsException`; `finalizeForm2441PartII` early-returns when true; `applyChildDependentCareCredit` no-op-on-null cascade preserves Schedule 3 wire-up gating automatically. Two new tests: `computesPartIICreditNotAllowedOnMfsWithoutException` (lock-in), `computesPartIICreditAllowedOnMfsWithSeparationException` (separation-exception sanity). |
| 2 | DEFENSIVE GAP + REAL CORRECTNESS BUG | Audit framed as "MFS guard not in orchestrator" but tracing revealed `sumW2DependentCareBenefits(w2Entries)` lacked SSN filtering — on MFS the spouse's W-2 box 10 leaks into the taxpayer's return when the database holds both W-2s. Created new helper `sumW2DependentCareBenefitsForSsns(w2Entries, ssns...)` mirroring `sumW2WagesForSsn`. `computeDependentCareBenefits` now takes `boolean isMfsReturn` parameter; SSN-filtered sum is taxpayer-only on MFS, taxpayer + spouse on MFJ. Falls back to legacy unfiltered sum when neither SSN populated (test data / partial setup). As a free upgrade: spouse-side defensive reads (`spouseSsn`, `spouseEarned`, `spouseInmateWages`, `additionalEarnedSpouse`, `deemedSpouse`) gated on `isMfsReturn` — single-guard cascade matching line 1c/1d. New test `mfsExcludesSpouseW2Box10FromLine1e`. |
| 3 | SPEC NON-COMPLIANCE — "DCB" WRITE-IN MISSING | 2025 Form 1040 instructions require writing "DCB" on the dotted line next to line 1e when the amount is positive. The IRS PDF has no fillable field for this label; pre-fix the UI rendered only the amount cell. Built a generic `extraTextOverlays` mechanism on `<app-pdf-readonly-preview>` (`ExtraTextOverlay` interface exported); `applyExtraOverlays` runs after `applyValues` in `buildFilledPdf` (covers preview + Save-as-PDF); disabled in debug mode. Form 1040 component populates a single `{ page: 1, x: 475, y: 284, text: 'DCB', size: 9 }` overlay when `dependentCareBenefits > 0`. Reusable for future write-in labels (line 8z type, line 1h type, etc.). Visual placement may need post-deploy nudging. |
| 4 | TEST COVERAGE GAPS | Added 4 new unit tests for previously uncovered branches: `computesMfsWithoutExceptionCapsExclusionAtTwoFiveHundred` (clean MFS-no-exception cap, untangled from SSN-filter), `computesPlanMaxLimitsExclusionBelowStatutory` (line 21 = min(planMax, statutoryCap)), `computesZeroEarnedIncomeMakesAllBenefitsTaxable` (earned-income-floor → all taxable; the Issue #7 UX trap), `computesTwoQualifyingPersonsUsesSixThousandExpenseLimit` (line 27 = $6,000 with ≥2 persons). Sub-bullet (e) `hasCareExpenses` outer-gate test deferred to Issue #8 closure. |
| 5 | KNOWLEDGE FILE MISSING | Created `knowledge/line-1e-dependent-care-benefits.md` (336 lines) mirroring `knowledge/line-1d-medicaid-waiver-payments.md` structure. 11 sections: What is Line 1e, Personal Form ID (single — childcare-expenses), Form Sections + YAML Fields (5 sections), Backend Compute Logic (function-name convention; MFS gating cascade table; blocking flags inventory), Two-Pass Architecture (the unique feature), Schedule 3 Line 2 Wire-Up (incl. DCB overlay from Issue #3), Frontend Forms, Unit + E2E Test Coverage, Known Implementation Gaps (open vs closed table), Key Constants (4 ReferenceData + applicable-percentage table), Verification log footer. |
| 6 | OVER-BROAD GATE — `hasAnyChildcareData` | Pre-fix returned true on any non-empty sub-map — even a single boolean answer like `hasCareExpenses=No`. Caused Form 2441 to be instantiated with all 31 lines populated (mostly zeros) on returns with nothing to file. Tightened to require concrete data: care providers with name/EIN/positive `amountPaidToProvider`; qualifying persons with name/SSN/positive `qualifiedExpensesForPerson`; positive `dependentCareBenefits` field; `employerPlanFailed129dTest=true`; or positive `priorYearQualifiedExpensesPaidThisYear`. Booleans on `generalQuestions` no longer trigger Form 2441 instantiation on their own (W-2 box 10 still triggers via the OR'd `hasBenefits` gate). Mirrors `hasAnyAdoptionInputs`. Two new tests: `formNotGeneratedWhenOnlyHasCareExpensesAnswered` (locks the new bar), `formGeneratedWhenQualifyingPersonHasSsnOnly` (sanity that minimal real data still triggers). **Side discovery during implementation**: tried also nulling line 1e when `form2441 == null` (so PDF shows blank not "0"), but it cascaded into `buildIncome`'s "all-null → return null Income" early-return and broke 5 unrelated tests. Reverted; line 1e remains `BigDecimal.ZERO` when no Form 2441 — consistent with existing tests. |
| 7 | EARNED INCOME — UX TRAP | On MFJ when spouse has $0 earned income (no W-2, no `additionalEarnedIncomeSpouse`, not student/disabled), §21 earned-income limit forces `line 20 = 0` → `line 25 = 0` → entire DCFSA taxable on line 1e. Mathematically correct (Pub 503), but a common UX surprise ("Why is all my DCFSA suddenly taxable?"). Added non-blocking advisory flag `DEPENDENT_CARE_SPOUSE_EARNED_INCOME_ZERO` fired when `isMfj && hasBenefits && spouseEarned == 0 && line26 > 0`. Message explains the Pub 503 §21 limit + points to the student/disabled exit door (deemed income substitution). Three new tests: trigger, suppression with spouse W-2 wages, suppression with student/disabled deemed income. |
| 8 | OUTER GATE — `hasCareExpenses` NOT READ | UI form has `hasCareExpenses` as the FIRST question; backend never read it. Stale `qualifyingPersons` rows from prior sessions could drive a credit even when the user marked "no care expenses this year". Added backend SOFT gate inside `computeDependentCareBenefits`: `boolean noCareExpenses = Boolean.FALSE.equals(hasCareExpenses)`. When true: override `qualifyingPersons = Map.of()` (cascades to `countQualifyingPersons=0`, line 30 = 0, Form 2441 qualifying-persons section empty); override `line16Input = null`; OR into `creditDisallowed` (skips Phase 2 entirely). Renamed record field `mfsCreditDisallowed` → `creditDisallowed` to reflect broader cause coverage. **UI normalizeForSave already had the matching clearing logic** (form-childcare-expenses.component.ts:272-298) since prior development — confirmed dual-layer defense. New test `noCareExpensesOverridesStaleQualifyingPersonsAndSkipsCredit` simulates direct-API-save bypass + asserts line 30=0, line 16=0, line 26=full, line 11=null, Schedule 3 line 2=null. |
| 9 | WORKSHEET A LINE 9 PARTIAL | Pub 503 Worksheet A caps current-year credit on prior-year expenses by prior year's UNUSED expense limit + recomputes prior-year MAGI. Backend uses `priorYearQualifiedExpensesPaidThisYear` directly as line 9b (correct only when prior year had full unused capacity). Added non-blocking advisory `DEPENDENT_CARE_WORKSHEET_A_PARTIAL` fired when `line9b > 0`. Message acknowledges the simple-case treatment + names what Worksheet A would additionally check + points to Pub 503 for complex cases. Two new tests: trigger + suppression on absent value. |

**Patterns established / re-used:**
- **Single-guard cascade** for MFS spouse exclusion now applied to line 1e (previously line 1c/1d). One `isMfsReturn` boolean derived in `prepare()`; passed to `computeDependentCareBenefits`; null-gates spouse SSN/wages/inmate-wages/additional/deemed → all downstream paths exclude spouse automatically.
- **`creditDisallowed` boolean as broad gate** — single field on `DependentCareComputation` record cleanly OR's multiple disallow causes (IRC §21(e)(2) MFS-no-exception, `hasCareExpenses=false`). Phase 2 gating becomes one-liner.
- **Generic `extraTextOverlays` mechanism on `pdf-readonly-preview`** — first place needing pdf-lib `drawText` for IRS write-in labels. Reusable for future free-text overlays (line 8z type, line 1h type).
- **Non-blocking advisory flags as user-facing protection** — when math is correct but the result is surprising (Issue #7) OR when implementation is best-effort (Issue #9), a non-blocking flag explains the situation without breaking the return.
- **SSN filter beyond per-spouse computation** — line 1c/1d filter at the per-spouse aggregation layer; line 1e needed it at the W-2-box-10 sum layer (different shape, same intent — exclude spouse W-2s on MFS).
- **Side-discovery scoping** — Issue #6 implementation revealed the `buildIncome` "all-null → null Income" early-return is brittle (5 unrelated tests depend on line 1e = ZERO to keep Income alive). Documented; reverted attempted line 1e null change; kept Issue #6's primary goal (Form 2441 not attached) intact.

**Documentation accumulated:**
- `XLS/computations/1e.xlsx` (7 sheets, ~190 rows total)
- `knowledge/line-1e-dependent-care-benefits.md` — new file (336 lines)
- `XLS/_tools/generate-1e.js` — new generator

**688/688 backend tests pass** at end of day (started at 671 from end of 1d. +17 net: +2 (#1) +1 (#2) +0 (#3) +4 (#4) +0 (#5) +2 (#6) +3 (#7) +1 (#8) +2 (#9) plus +2 carried over from line 1d Issue #4 W-2 lookup tests). Angular build clean throughout. All 9 issues from `1e.xlsx` Code Validation closed.

**Side fix during the audit** (between Issues #2 and #3): UI E2E regression introduced by line 1d Issue #5 `entryNeedsIncludedInW2` per-entry required-field rule blocked the medicaid-waiver `W-2 box 12 code II auto-fill` test from saving (the new 2024+ box-1=0 reporting pattern). Fixed `fillFromW2` in both `form-medicaid-waiver-{taxpayer,spouse}.component.ts` to auto-set `qualifiedAmountIncludedInW2Box1 = 0` when the matched W-2 has `wagesTipsOtherCompAmount ≤ 0` — mirrors backend `computeMedicaidForPerson` auto-set. Manual W-2 entries (no matching W-2) still show the required-field warning, which is correct (user must declare overlap). Inverse path (`code II requires qualified amount before save` test) preserved.


## 2026-05-06 (evening) — Line 1d (Medicaid Waiver Payments + Schedule 1 line 8s) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1d.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring 1a/1b/1c structure: Main Computation, Inputs, Reference Data, Side-Effect Outputs, Validation Flags, Code Validation, Output Flow). Walked through all 9 Code Validation issues. Net result: 1 backend defensive fix + 1 UI required-field enforcement + 1 UI advisory callout + 5 new unit tests (1 tightened existing) + 1 new knowledge file (the meta-fix that captures the audit-walkthrough trail). Phone-auth UX side-fix added partway through.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFENSIVE GAP | Backend MFS guard added to `computeMedicaidWaiverPayments(...)` — accepts `boolean isMfs`, skips spouse `computeMedicaidForPerson` call when MFS via `MedicaidResult(null, null)`. **Single guard cascades to BOTH outputs** (line 1d AND Schedule 1 line 8s) automatically via `addNonNull`. New unit test `line1dMfsGuard_spouseMedicaidWaiverNotAggregatedOnMfs` (taxpayer $500 taxable + $1,000 qualified; spouse stale $2,000 + $3,000; MFS → line 1d = $1,500 not $6,500, line 8s = -$1,000 not -$4,000). Mirrors line 1c `computeTips` MFS pattern. Renamed shared local `tipsIsMfs` → `isMfsReturn` for clarity. |
| 2 | DEFENSIVE GAP — verified OK | `computeMedicaidForPerson` HARD gate on `receivedMedicaidWaiverPayments` is correct (no auto-fill convenience to preserve, unlike line 1c's soft gate). No code change; documented contract in `knowledge/line-1d-medicaid-waiver-payments.md` §4 with the SOFT-vs-HARD decision-criteria reference from 1c.xlsx. |
| 3 | DOC vs CODE INCONSISTENCY | RESOLVED by Issue #1 doc edits (already applied during the #1 fix). `dependencies/1d.md` rows for spouse form, filing status, "Spouse form present on MFS (stale data)", "Filing status not saved", and Cross-line consistency MFS bullet all updated. |
| 4 | TEST COVERAGE GAP | Investigation showed most sub-bullets (a, b, c, d, h) were already covered by existing tests. Tightened `flagsWhenLine8sExceedsQualifiedTotal` to also assert `MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_OVERAGE_TAXPAYER` (sub-bullet e). Added 4 new tests: `line1dMfj_bothSpousesElect` (sub-bullet f), `line1dMfj_taxpayerElectsSpouseDoesNot_perSpouseElectionRule` (sub-bullet g — **most important**, locks in all-or-none-PER-SPOUSE election rule), `line1dW2LookupNoMatch_includedInW2NullTreatedAsZero` (sub-bullet i half 1), `line1dW2LookupMatchedBox1Zero_autoSetsIncludedInW2Zero` (sub-bullet i half 2). 467 → 471 tests. |
| 5 | UI ENFORCEMENT GAP | Per-entry helper `entryNeedsIncludedInW2(entry)` added to both medicaid-waiver forms (taxpayer + spouse). Returns true when `sourceType==='W-2'` AND positive `qualifiedNotice2014_7Amount`. Drives conditional `*` + `[required]` + inline error on `qualifiedAmountIncludedInW2Box1` input. Inline error explicitly explains the two valid answers (0 for modern 2024+ box-12-code-II reporting; positive for legacy box-1 inclusion). `isValid()` per-entry check added to both forms. Mirrors line 1c Issue #6 EIN-required pattern. No backend change — backend `MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_BOX1_UNSPECIFIED_*` blocking flag already in place. |
| 6 | NOT IMPLEMENTED — IRC §131(c) per-individual cap | Verified safe to defer (HCBS waiver programs structurally one recipient per household). Created broken cross-reference fix: added "IRC §131(c) Per-Individual Cap" entry to `outstanding.md` (the dependencies file referenced it but it didn't exist) with full implementation sketch + effort estimate (~2-2.5 hours basic pro-rata, ~5-5.5 hours with per-recipient breakdown). Added verification footnote to `lines/1d.md` §4.2. No code change. |
| 7 | POTENTIAL DOUBLE COUNT — Schedule 1 line 8s | Algebraic analysis surfaced more than the xlsx noted: `line 8s = −qualifiedTotal` always under the per-entry `MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_OVERAGE` constraint, so cross-entry duplication does NOT affect AGI (offset cancels). However, EIC/ACTC earned-income basis IS inflated by duplicates — a subtler risk. Added new §8.6 "Cross-entry duplication invariant" to `lines/1d.md` with the algebraic proof and the residual EIC/ACTC risk note. Added §5.2 footnote pointing to §8.6. New `outstanding.md` entry for the deferred UI heuristic (cross-entry duplicate detection). No code change. |
| 8 | KNOWLEDGE FILE MISSING | Created `knowledge/line-1d-medicaid-waiver-payments.md` (~270 lines) mirroring the `line-1c-tip-income.md` structure. 10 sections: What is line 1d, Personal Form IDs, YAML Fields, Backend Compute Logic (with SOFT-vs-HARD-gate decision callout + function-name-not-line-number convention from 1c.xlsx Issue #9), Downstream integrations, Frontend Forms, Unit Test Coverage (12-test inventory), E2E Test Coverage (5 tests in `medicaid-waiver.spec.ts`), Known Implementation Gaps, Key Constants ("None — pure aggregation"). Verification log footer with bullet entry per Issue #1–#9. |
| 9 | POTENTIAL UX ISSUE — `livesWithCareRecipient` not wired | Two-part fix: (a) ADVISORY CALLOUT applied — yellow `.notice2014-warning` callout in both forms when `livesWithCareRecipient === false` AND `hasAnyQualifiedAmount()` returns true (any entry has positive qualified amount). Help text on `livesWithCareRecipient` rewritten with explicit Notice 2014-7 home-sharing rule + IRC §131 citation + exception list. CSS class added (orange/amber consistent with 1c/1d Issue #8 callouts). (b) BLOCKING-FLAG ESCALATION deferred to `outstanding.md` for future evaluation — soft advisory chosen first because Notice 2014-7 has documented exceptions (shared home with no separate provider home; some non-§1915(c) state programs); blocking would risk preventing legitimate filings. |

**Side fix during the audit** (between Issues #3 and #4): phone-auth UX improvement. Added a visible "Sending verification code to your phone…" indicator (PrimeNG `[loading]` spinner button + blue `.auth-info` status banner with `aria-live="polite"`) to both `auth-sign-in.component.ts` and `auth-sign-up.component.ts`. Replaces the silent gap between submitting the phone number and the verify-code page navigating in. Auto-dismisses on success (route navigation) or failure (error message takes its place). No backend change — repurposed the existing `loading` flag.

**Discovered side issues** logged to `outstanding.md`:
- **Notice 2014-7 home-sharing — escalate to blocking flag** — soft-advisory-first decision documented with full revisit scope (~1-2 hours)
- **UI heuristic for cross-entry duplicate detection** — algebraic AGI invariant proves it benign; EIC/ACTC residual risk noted; UI heuristic deferred (~30 min if revisited)
- **IRC §131(c) per-individual cap** — re-verified deferred; full implementation scope documented for future revisit (~2-2.5 hours basic, ~5-5.5 with per-recipient)

**Documentation accumulated:**

## 2026-05-06 — Line 1c (Tip Income + Form 4137) xlsx audit complete

Generated `C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1c.xlsx` (7 sheets, mirroring the 1a/1b structure: Main Computation, Inputs, Reference Data, Side-Effect Outputs, Validation Flags, Code Validation, Output Flow). Walked through all 9 Code Validation issues one-by-one. Net result: 6 real fixes shipped (3 backend defensive gates + 1 UI required-field enforcement + 1 deferred-W-2-canonical contract pin + 1 Pub 531 audit-trail guidance), 2 verified false positives (knowledge-file outdated claims), 1 doc-convention pin. 14 new unit tests added across the walkthrough.

| # | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFENSIVE GAP | Backend MFS guard added to `computeTips(...)` — accepts `boolean isMfs` and skips spouse `computeTipsForPerson` call when MFS. Single guard cascades to four downstream effects (line 1c excludes spouse, `form4137Spouse=null`, Schedule 2 line 5 excludes spouse, Form 8959 line 6 base excludes spouse). New test `line1cMfsGuard_spouseTipIncomeAndForm4137NotAggregatedOnMfs`. Mirrors the line 1b `sumHouseholdEmployeeWages` pattern from 2026-05-05. |
| 2 | DEFENSIVE GAP | SOFT gate at top of `computeTipsForPerson` — `if Boolean.FALSE.equals(hasUnreportedTips) return null`. Soft (not hard) because of the W-2 box 8 auto-fill convenience for new users — null falls through, only explicit `false` blocks. UI's `normalizeForSave` already clears `tipsByEmployer` when not exactly true (verified pre-existing — no UI changes needed). 2 new tests: `line1cDefensiveGate_returnsNullWhenHasUnreportedTipsExplicitlyFalse` + `line1cAutoFill_stillWorksWhenHasUnreportedTipsNotAnswered`. |
| 3 | TEST COVERAGE | Investigation showed 4 of 5 "missing" sub-tests were in fact already in place after Issues #1 + #2. Added the genuine gap: noncash-only path, all-under-$20 path, MFJ separate Form 4137, RRTA contribution to line 8. 4 new unit tests. Knowledge file §7 unit-test inventory refreshed from "Two unit tests" to 9 with one row per scenario. |
| 4 | DATA INTEGRITY EDGE CASE | Dual-layer fix for adequate-records claim with missing substantiated amount. UI: `*` + `[required]` on the substantiated input + `isValid()` block (already in place — verified). Backend: new non-blocking flag `ADEQUATE_RECORDS_MISSING_SUBSTANTIATED_AMOUNT_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}` emitted when any employer in the loop has the silent fallthrough state. 3 new tests. Skipped enhancements documented in `outstanding.md` (3 sub-options: blocking flag, auto-clear normalizer, ngModel always-required). |
| 5 | SS DATA SOURCING SUBTLETY | Verified the `firstNonNull(ssWagesTipsFromW2, ssWagesTipsFromInput)` semantics + documented the W-2-canonical design. Code comment added at the call site explaining the $0 edge case (matching W-2 with explicit $0 boxes 3+7 → W-2 wins, fallback ignored). UI help text rewritten on both fallback fields — "FALLBACK ONLY" suffix in title + body explicitly states the value will be silently ignored when a W-2 is on file. 1 new contract-locking test. Deferred enhancement: hide fallback fields when matching W-2 detected. |
| 6 | MISSING UI ENFORCEMENT | Dual-layer EIN-required enforcement when an employer row contributes to Form 4137. UI: per-row helper `rowNeedsEinForForm4137(employer)` (true when received > reported OR allocated > 0); conditional `*` + `[required]` + inline error citing IRS rejection consequence; `isValid()` blocks save. Backend: `MISSING_EMPLOYER_EIN_FORM_4137_{TAXPAYER,SPOUSE}` non-blocking flag streams over `employers` after Form 4137 is built. 3 new tests. |
| 7 | TEST COVERAGE GAP | **VERIFIED — false positive.** xlsx claim "E2E covers 7 of 12 spec scenarios" was generated against an older snapshot. Reality: 13 E2E tests (11 main + 2 overflow) cover all 12 scenarios. Knowledge file §8 refreshed with full test inventory + line-number references; §9 gaps stripped of stale entries. New header comment on `line1c-tip-income.spec.ts` provides scenario-coverage map for future audits. |
| 8 | NOT IMPLEMENTED — adequate-records audit trail | Option B applied (guidance-only, no new storage). Help text for `hasAdequateRecordsUnreportedLessThanAllocated` rewritten to be explicit about IRS expectation of CONTEMPORANEOUS daily records (Form 4070A or equivalent per Pub 531) — lists acceptable evidence, warns IRS may DISALLOW the reduction without proof on audit. New inline `.form4852-guidance` yellow callout under the substantiated input when adequate-records is Yes. CSS class added to both forms. Option C (records-notes-field per employer) deferred to `outstanding.md` with implementation sketch. |
| 9 | KNOWLEDGE FILE OUTDATED LINE NUMBERS | **VERIFIED — already resolved organically through Issues #1–#8.** All edits today used function-name references; zero source-code line numbers remain. Header timestamp bumped; new "Convention" callout in §4 captures the discipline; new "Verification log" footer documents the grep-zero-match verification. Sweep across other knowledge files (e.g., `line-1b-household-wages.md`) deferred as a follow-up in `outstanding.md`. |

**Discovered side issues** logged to `outstanding.md`:
- `buildTipEntriesFromW2` null-SSN fallthrough (Medium priority) — function uses `hasSsn = hasText(normalizedSsn)` to gate filtering; when `ssn` is null/empty, ALL W-2s are included unfiltered, causing potential double-count on the spouse path. Mitigated in practice by Issue #1's MFS guard; worth a future fix.
- Hide SS wage-base fallback fields when a matching W-2 is detected by the system (Low — UX polish).
- Adequate-records audit-trail storage (Low — Option C from Issue #8).
- Knowledge-file source-code line-number sweep across all files (Low — convention discipline).

**Skipped enhancements documented** for completeness:
- Adequate-records: blocking flag (rejected as too aggressive); auto-clear normalizer (silent data mutation); always-required vs. conditional `[required]` (chose conditional).

668/668 backend tests pass at end of day (started at 655 from the 2026-05-05 line 1a/1b closeout). Angular build clean throughout.


## 2026-05-05 — Form-field documentation arc, SSN/ITIN/EIN validation, line 1a + line 1b xlsx audits

### Form-field documentation (XLS spreadsheets per form)

Generated 162 Excel files documenting the field schema of every Angular form component in the application:
- `C:\us-tax\XLS\input_forms\` — 118 .xlsx files for forms in Personal, Statements, Incomes, Deductions, and Applications sidebar sections.
- `C:\us-tax\XLS\output_forms\` — 44 .xlsx files for forms in the Tax Return section (PDF-backed).

Each file lists every field in template order with: Order, Field Name (`ngModel` binding for input forms; PDF field id for output forms), Label (visible label or PDF tooltip), Type (text/checkbox/radio/textarea/select/etc.), and a Purpose column intentionally left blank for later authoring. Generator scripts at `C:\us-tax\XLS\_tools\` (`generate-form-xlsx.js`, `generate-output-xlsx.js`) so the docs can be regenerated whenever forms change. Two forms intentionally skipped: `form-statements-select` (multi-statement picker, not a form) and `form-tax-return-required-attachment` (no PDF backing).

### Computation map for line 1a

`C:\us-tax\XLS\computations\1a.xlsx` — 7-sheet workbook documenting the full computation map for Form 1040 line 1a:
- Main Computation: 8-step formula + carve-out rules (statutory employee, Box 11 NQDC, inmate wages).
- Inputs: 15 cross-referenced source fields with pointers to the input-form xlsx files.
- Reference Data: explicit "no constants" note (line 1a is pure aggregation).
- Side-Effect Outputs: Schedule 1 lines 8t/8u with cross-line invariant.
- Validation Flags: 3 blocking flags (`MISSING_W2_EMPLOYMENT_INCOME_*`, `STATUTORY_EMPLOYEE_W2_OUT_OF_SCOPE`).
- Code Validation: 9 issues uncovered with severity ratings.
- Output Flow: where line 1a feeds (line 1z → line 9 → AGI → downstream credits).

### TIN validation (defense-in-depth at UI + API)

Added unified validator for SSN, ITIN, and EIN with graceful handling of incomplete values:

Frontend:
- `src/app/utils/tin-validator.ts` — pure helpers: `isValidSsn`, `isValidItin`, `isValidEin`, `isValidIndividualTin`, `isValidAnyTin`, plus `tinValidator` ValidatorFn. Plus Jasmine spec.
- `src/app/directives/tin.directive.ts` — `[appTin]` directive accepting kind `'ssn' | 'individual' | 'ein' | 'any'`.
- Applied to 24 form components (~42 field bindings): individual `*SSN` fields → `individual`; `*EIN` → `ein`; `*TIN` payer/filer fields → `any`. Also covers `pdfRaw['payer_tin']` style PDF-overlay bindings on statement forms.

Backend:
- `TaxReturnComputeService` mirror in `TinValidator.java` — same rules + a payload-walker that recurses through nested maps/lists looking for TIN-bearing field names (suffix-based: `*SSN` → INDIVIDUAL, `*EIN` → EIN, `*TIN` → ANY, with explicit overrides). 26 unit tests in `TinValidatorTest.java`.
- Wired into `PersonalResource.saveForm` / `createDependent` / `updateDependent` and `StatementResource.createEntry` / `saveEntry`. Returns 400 with field paths for any violation.

Save-time policy is lenient on incomplete values (less than 9 digits, mixed letters, OCR partials) — only rejects "completed-looking" 9-digit attempts that fail SSA/IRS rules (000/666 areas, ITIN groups outside 50-65/70-88/90-92/94-99, all-zero / 00-prefix EINs). Required because Azure document extraction often returns partial digits and users save iteratively. Compute-time enforcement to be added separately as a blocking-flag check.

ITIN format enforced strictly per IRS rules (9XX area, group must be in `[50-65, 70-88, 90-92, 94-99]`). Replaced 7 invalid test SSNs that previously slipped through but would have been rejected by the new validator: `666-66-6666` (13×) → `555-66-6666`; `912-34-5678` (7×) → `912-71-5678`; `100-00-0001` (3×) → `100-71-0001`; `555-00-0001` (2×) → `555-71-0001`; `912-11-1111` (2×) → `912-71-1111`; `934-22-2222` (1×) → `934-72-2222`; `912-22-2222` (1×) → `912-72-2222`. All 4 affected files (`TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`, `line13b-additional-deductions.spec.ts`, `line1e-dependent-care.spec.ts`, `line1f-adoption-benefits.spec.ts`) plus all 642 backend tests still pass.

### Line 1a documentation + code cleanup (audit issues #1, #2, #3)

From the line 1a code-validation audit (in `XLS\computations\1a.xlsx` "Code Validation" sheet):

**Issue #1 — `dependencies/1a.md` falsely claimed SSN-based filtering for line 1a.** Verified `computeLine1aWages()` aggregates ALL non-statutory W-2 entries with no SSN matching; SSN is only consumed by `hasW2ForSsn()` in the missing-W-2 validation gate.
