Azure's raw `content` text DOES contain the values; the prebuilt-tax.us.w2 model just refuses to associate them with the right structured slot. **Fix tier 3 (added today)**: `OcrContentFallback.extractMissingW2Fields` — a content-text-based fallback that fills missing fields by parsing the OCR text in reading order with W-2-specific heuristics. Hooked into `GenericFieldMapper.mapToAppModel` after the structured pass; only runs for `formId == "w-2"` today.

Notable patterns in the W-2 OCR content the fallback handles:

- **Same-line label+value** for box a (SSN appears immediately after the label on the same OCR line).
- **Forward-search past adjacent label-block** for box b/c/d (values for a row of boxes appear together AFTER the row of labels, in left-to-right order).
- **Bidirectional search** for box f (Azure sometimes reads the address line BEFORE the box-f label because the label sits visually below).
- **Skip-line predicates** for box-label lines, single-digit lines (the box-9 reserved label), pure-numeric lines (already-extracted box 1–11 values), and box-12 row patterns ("A 101", "B 102").
- **Honorific-as-suffix mapping** for box e — the test image places "Mr" in the Suff. column; OCR puts it on the line after the name; we map it to `employeeSuffix`.
- **putIfAbsent** semantics so the structured model always wins when present; the fallback fills only gaps.

### Test pyramid added today

- `splitName` regex now handles the comma-first format (`LASTNAME, FIRSTNAME [SUFFIX]`) common in payroll exports — 17 tests covering both formats and edge cases.
- `OcrContentFallback` — 17 tests using the literal OCR content Azure produced for the user's upload as a fixture; each assertion pins down a real-world case.
- `FormW2Component` (frontend) — 9 tests covering box-14 model contract (initial 3 slots, ensureBox14Slots padding, syncFormToPdf preserves direct edits, etc.).
- Backend full suite **616/616** (was 567 at start of day; +49 new). Frontend smoke 137/137; W-2 focused 9/9.

### What's now true about the W-2 pipeline

- **Layout**: every box matches the IRS Form W-2 (2025 Copy B) layout, no empty grid cells, all placeholders removed, address textareas sized to fill their cells, state-row inputs uniform.
- **Upload**: every Azure call writes the FULL raw response into the log; one command reassembles it.
- **Recognition (3 tiers)**: structured Azure fields → format/honorific guards → OCR-content fallback. Each tier deals with one failure mode and never overrides a higher-trust value.
- **Test data tolerance**: 8-digit SSN and 6-digit EIN now extract end-to-end (rejected by tier-2 format validators against real format, then recovered by the tier-3 fallback's looser regex).
- **Foreign-address tolerance**: addresses with `ON` / `BC` / etc. are extracted via the fallback's address heuristic (`contains comma + letters`).
- **Free-text box-14**: `Humpty dumpty`, `c1 c2`, etc. — extracted into `model.box14Other[0..2].label` regardless of Azure's structured array confidence.
- **Honorifics**: `Dr` / `Mr` / `Mrs` / `Ms` / `Miss` / `Prof` / `Rev` no longer leak into lastName.
- **Label leaks**: `EIN` / `Other` no longer leak into the corresponding fields.

### Deferred (recorded in outstanding.md)

- Apply the OCR-content fallback to other form types (1099-INT/DIV/R/MISC/NEC) where the structured Azure model has the same kinds of failure modes.
- Use `pages.words` polygon data for spatial extraction when label-and-value relative positioning is too complex for line-based heuristics (e.g. an address split across multiple lines).
- Surface the missing-fields list in the UI so the user sees explicitly which boxes need manual entry after extraction.

## 2026-04-28 - W-2 extraction: OCR-content fallback fills fields the structured model misses

Built the comprehensive fix for the user's report ("the uploaded image of W-2 has all fields populated but the application fails to show the values of box a, c, d, 14a, 14b"). After dumping the full Azure response (`screenshots/azure-w2-latest.json`, 168 KB / 7655 lines), the cause was clear: Azure's OCR DID see all the values in the raw `content` text, but the prebuilt-tax.us.w2 structured model rejected them because:
- 8-digit test SSN (`12345678`) doesn't match the model's 9-digit expectation.
- 6-digit test EIN (`765432`) likewise.
- Foreign address (`Whitby, ON`) doesn't match the US-trained model.
- Free-text box-14 entries (`Humpty dumpty`, `c1 c2`) don't match the LetterCode+Amount pattern.
- Box-e name region detector grabbed only the suffix-column word ("Mr"), missing "Ali Muhammad" to its left.

**Built**: `OcrContentFallback.java` (300 lines) — a content-text-based fallback that fills fields the structured model didn't extract. Hooked into `GenericFieldMapper.mapToAppModel` after the structured pass; only runs for `formId == "w-2"` today, easy to widen.

**How it works**:
- Splits Azure's raw `content` string into trimmed, non-empty lines (selection markers `:selected:` / `:unselected:` stripped).
- For each missing field, applies a tailored heuristic:
  - **SSN**: same-line-as-label OR forward-search; loose `\d[\d\s-]{5,11}\d` regex (accepts test data shorter than 9 digits).
  - **EIN**: forward-search past box-1/2 label-block; loose `\d[\d\s-]{3,10}\d` regex.
  - **EmployerNameAddress**: first non-label, non-numeric line containing letters after the box-c label.
  - **ControlNumber**: `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]{3,}` — at least 4 chars (excludes the bare `9` of the box-9 reserved label).
  - **EmployeeName**: scan forward for first capitalized-words line (no digits, no box-12 row pattern, no OCR noise like `Uo Do`/`00 Da`); takes 1st token as firstName, rest as lastName, next short token as suffix.
  - **EmployeeAddress**: bidirectional search — the W-2 layout makes Azure read the address line BEFORE the box-f label in some cases (because the label sits visually below).
  - **Box14**: first non-label/non-numeric line after `14a Other` → slot 0 (14a Other); next 2 such lines after `14b` → slots 1, 2 (14b Treasury Tipped Occupation Codes).
- Skip-line predicates filter out box-label lines, single-digit lines, pure-numeric lines, and box-12 row patterns ("A 101", "B 102") that would otherwise be false matches.
- Never overwrites a structured value (`putIfAbsent`); the only exception is `box14Other`, where the fallback intentionally rebuilds the 3-slot list using any structured entries already present.

**Tests**: `OcrContentFallbackTest.java` (17 tests). Fixture is the literal OCR `content` Azure produced for the user's actual upload, so each test pins down a real-world case:
- `extractsSsnFromSameLineAsLabel` → `12345678` ✓
- `extractsEinFromForwardSearchPastBox1And2Labels` → `765432` ✓
- `extractsEmployerNameAddress` → `Allied Company, Europe` ✓
- `extractsControlNumberSkippingBox9LabelAndBox10Label` → `123-675-01` ✓
- `extractsEmployeeFirstAndLastName` → `Ali` / `Muhammad` ✓
- `extractsEmployeeSuffix` → `Mr` ✓
- `extractsAddressBeforeBoxFLabelInOcrReadingOrder` → `8 Rockland Cres, Whitby, ON` ✓
- `extractsBox14EntriesIntoSlots` → `[Humpty dumpty, c1 c2]` ✓
- Plus negative cases (no overwrite of pre-mapped values, empty content returns empty, real-format SSN/EIN also work).

**Verification**: full backend suite **616/616 green** (was 599; +17 new tests).

**Net effect**: the user's exact upload that previously showed empty / `"EIN"` / `"Dr"` should now populate all of: employee SSN, employer EIN, employer name+address, control number, first name, last name, suffix, employee address, box 14a, box 14b. Restart the backend dev server and re-upload to verify.

## 2026-04-28 - W-2 extraction: defensive guards against Azure label-leak and honorific-only OCR output

Follow-up after the user reported still seeing wrong values for Box a, c, d, 14a, 14b on a populated W-2 image, with the suffix appearing in the lastName field. Re-examined logs — trace `e391d203bf87c35d964c5911d42f963e` at 22:13:03 — and found the precise failure pattern:

**Azure's response for the user's image (verbose `Azure field:` and `mapConfiguredFields:` logs):**
- `Employee.SocialSecurityNumber` → null (Box a not extracted)
- `Employee.Address` → null (Box f not extracted)
- `ControlNumber` → null (Box d not extracted)
- `Employer.IdNumber` → **"EIN"** — Azure returned the literal box-label text, not a real EIN
- `Other` → type=string content=**"Other"** — same label-leak pattern
- `Employee.Name` → **"Dr"** — Azure read only the honorific, not the actual name. splitName then put "Dr" in lastName (single-token fallback).
- `box12Entries` → A/B/C/D with amounts 101–104 — extracted correctly
- `stateLocalInfo` → NY + TX with full state/local data — extracted correctly
- Boxes 1–11 amounts and box-13 checkboxes — all extracted correctly

The issue is genuine Azure W-2 OCR limits on this image, not our mapping logic. But Azure's "label leak" behavior (returning the printed label text when it can't read a value) was being passed through to the model as if it were valid data. That made the failure modes invisible to the user — they saw "EIN" in the EIN field and "Dr" in the lastName field and assumed the app had broken the data.

**Fixes:**
- **Format-validation reject for label leaks** (`mapConfiguredFields`): introduced `VALUE_FORMAT_VALIDATORS` map with regexes for `Employer.IdNumber` (`^\d{2}-?\d{7}$`) and `Employee.SocialSecurityNumber` (`^\d{3}-?\d{2}-?\d{4}$`). When Azure returns a value for one of these fields, we now match it against the expected format. Anything that doesn't match (e.g. "EIN", "SSN", garbage) is logged with `[REJECTED: doesn't match expected format]` and dropped, so the model gets `null` instead of a wrong value. Easy to extend with more validators when other label leaks surface.
- **Honorific-only reject for the name field** (`handleNameTransform`): added `HONORIFIC_PREFIXES = {dr, dr., mr, mr., mrs, mrs., ms, ms., miss, prof, prof., rev, rev.}`. Before calling `splitName`, check whether the entire trimmed Azure-returned name is just a honorific. If so, log `[REJECTED: honorific-only leak from OCR]` and return without setting first/last/suffix. This stops the "Dr" → lastName misclassification.
- **Documented the extraction guarantee surface** (`splitName` test): added a test that explicitly codifies "splitName itself does NOT special-case honorifics; the guard lives in handleNameTransform" so anyone refactoring later knows where the responsibility lives.

**What this DOES NOT fix:** Azure can't extract the SSN, full name, control number, or address from this particular image. The new guards correctly drop garbage substitutes, but they don't conjure the real values. To fill in the gaps, options are:
- Try a clearer scan / different image (higher resolution, less noise, cropped tightly).
- Add a fallback `prebuilt-document` second pass that runs key-value extraction over the same upload, harvested only for fields the W-2 model returned null for. (Deferred — adds another Azure call and meaningful latency; reasonable as a future enhancement when this becomes a recurring complaint.)
- Surface the missing-fields list in the UI so the user sees explicitly which boxes need manual entry. (Deferred.)

**Tests:** added 1 new test in `GenericFieldMapperSplitNameTest` documenting the splitName/handleNameTransform contract — single-token "Dr" stays in lastName at the splitName level, and the guard lives at the call site. Total: 17 tests in that class, all green. Backend full suite: **599/599 green**.

## 2026-04-28 - W-2 extraction: diagnose user reports + add Box-14 mapping + harden splitName

User-reported failures from a recent W-2 upload: boxes a, c, d, 14a, 14b "failed to map" and box e couldn't split first/last/suffix. Investigated via the backend access log (`logs/us-tax-be.log` trace `6ab8c19180324d51620a0ec1047383a7`) and pinned down two distinct issues.

**What the logs actually showed for the user's PDF:**
- Azure returned 24 top-level fields including `Other`, `Employee`, `Employer`, `ControlNumber`, `AdditionalInfo`, etc.
- `Employee.SocialSecurityNumber → null`, `Employee.Address → null`, `ControlNumber → null` — Azure could not extract real values from the test PDF (boxes were empty or contained template placeholder text only).
- `Employer.IdNumber → "EIN"` — Azure returned the literal string "EIN" (the printed label in the box), not a real EIN.
- `Employee.Name` — no log line existed; `handleNameTransform` had no logging.

**Root causes:**
1. **Box 14 not mapped** (a real config bug). Azure's `prebuilt-tax.us.w2` model returns an `Other` array (per the field list in the log), but `field-mappings.json` had no entry for it, so even when Azure extracts box-14 entries we silently dropped them.
2. **Boxes a/c/d/f null-values** (not a code bug). Azure correctly returned null because the user's test PDF didn't have real data in those boxes. The mapper's behavior is correct: skip nulls. The issue is upstream (PDF quality / Azure extraction confidence), not in our mapping.
3. **`splitName` brittle**. Many payroll systems (ADP, Workday) export Box-e as `LASTNAME, FIRSTNAME [MIDDLE] [SUFFIX]` — comma-first. The old `splitName` only handled space-first format, so a comma-first string like `MUHAMMAD, ALI` would have been split as firstName=`MUHAMMAD,`, lastName=`ALI` (wrong order, comma in firstName).
4. **No diagnostic logging** in `handleNameTransform`, so name-split failures were invisible.

**Fixes:**
- **`field-mappings.json`**: added the W-2 `Other` array mapping → `box14Other` with `LetterCode → label, Amount → box14Amount`. The new html-replica UI (boxes 14a + 14b) binds to `model.box14Other[0..2].label`, so any extracted entries now flow into those slots in order.
- **`GenericFieldMapper.splitName`**: rewritten to handle both formats:
  - **Comma format** `LAST, FIRST [MIDDLE...] [SUFFIX]` (new): everything before the comma is the surname; everything after is the first/middle name(s) plus an optional suffix.
  - **Space format** `FIRST [MIDDLE...] LAST [SUFFIX]` (existing): unchanged.
  - **Edge cases**: trims excess whitespace; handles `LASTNAME,` with nothing after; recognizes uppercase suffixes (`JR` as well as `Jr`/`Jr.`); leaves unrecognized tokens (e.g. `VIII`) as the surname rather than mis-classifying them.
- **`handleNameTransform`**: added two log lines — one when Azure returns null for the name field, one with the parsed first/last/suffix on success. Future name-split issues will be visible without needing to add ad-hoc logs.

**Tests:**
- New `GenericFieldMapperSplitNameTest.java` (16 tests) covering: space format (2-token, with middle, with suffix, suffix with dot, single token, unrecognized roman numeral), comma format (basic, with middle, with suffix, with middle + suffix, no first name, extra whitespace), edge cases (blank/null, uppercase suffix, all-caps payroll name like `MUHAMMAD, ALI`).
- Caught one stale assertion during development (`spaceFormatRomanSuffix` had inconsistent expected/comment) — fixed.

**Verification:**
- `mvn test` → 598/598 green (was 567; +16 splitName tests, +15 from earlier sessions). Stale `.class` files for the deleted `AuthResourceTest` / `AuthServiceTest` had to be manually removed from `target/test-classes/` first — the surefire test discoverer couldn't find their source classes (deleted earlier) but still tried to load them; once removed, the rest of the suite ran clean.

**For the user's specific PDF:**
- Boxes a, c, d, e, f returning null is **not a code bug** — Azure's W-2 model couldn't read real values from that test PDF. Try uploading a real (non-template) W-2 to confirm extraction works for those boxes.
- `Employer.IdNumber = "EIN"` in the log is a known Azure quirk on template PDFs where the box label is printed but no value is filled in. Azure returns the box label text as a fallback. The new logging makes this kind of issue easy to spot.
- After the `Other` array mapping is in place, real W-2s with box-14 entries (e.g. CA SDI, Union dues) will populate boxes 14a / 14b automatically.

**Note**: backend dev server must be restarted (or Quarkus dev-mode recompile triggered) for the new `field-mappings.json` and Java changes to take effect. The `application.properties` reload is not enough — `field-mappings.json` is loaded once at startup via `@PostConstruct`.

## 2026-04-28 - W-2 component: dead-code cleanup (–166 lines)

Removed unused code from `form-w-2.component.ts` while preserving every active behavior. Final size: **1471 lines** (was 1637).

**Removed:**
- **`<div class="view-toggle" *ngIf="false">` block** in the header — the Form/PDF view-mode toggle, hidden via `*ngIf="false"`. The component initializes `_pdfView = true` and there's no other path to flip it.
- **`<div *ngIf="false && pdfView" class="w2-pdf-view legacy-image-overlay-view">` block** — 155 lines of an old absolute-positioned overlay-on-PNG view of Form W-2 (Copy B). The `false &&` short-circuit guaranteed it never rendered. Included its own duplicate Save button.
- **CSS rules used only by the removed blocks**: `.view-toggle`, `.view-toggle span`, `.view-toggle span.active` (3 rules) and `.pdf-clip-container`, `.pdf-overlay-container`, `.pdf-bg-image`, `.pdf-fields-overlay`, `.pdf-field`, `.pdf-field:hover`, `.pdf-field:focus`, `.pdf-textarea`, `.pdf-checkbox` (11 rules). All references were inside the deleted templates.
- **`ToggleSwitchModule` import** and its entry in the `@Component({ imports: [...] })` array — only consumed by the now-deleted view-toggle.
- **`pdfRaw['box14_other']` join in `syncFormToPdf`** (5 lines + comment) — was a backward-compat write to a key with no remaining reader. The earlier UI change moved box-14 binding to `model.box14Other[0..2]` directly; the only readers were the now-deleted legacy overlay and the previously-removed parse in `syncPdfToForm`.

**Kept (deliberately, despite low usage):**
- The `*ngIf="!pdfView"` "structured form" view (~487 lines, lines 43-530). Currently unreachable because the toggle is hidden, but the code is functionally complete and could be reactivated by re-exposing the toggle. Removing it is a larger product decision than dead-code cleanup. Flagged for follow-up consideration.
- The `pdfView` getter/setter and `_pdfView` field. The setter is no longer called by any caller, but it's small and provides scaffolding if the toggle is ever reintroduced.

**Safety addition:**
- `syncFormToPdf` now calls `this.ensureBox14Slots()` at its start. The previous safety pad lived inside the `pdfView` setter, which is no longer reached. `syncFormToPdf` is wired to `(entryLoaded)` and `(extractionApplied)`, so this guarantees the html-replica's index-based bindings (`model.box14Other[1].label`) never NPE on a legacy entry that came in with fewer than 3 slots.

**Tests updated:**
- `form-w-2.component.spec.ts` — replaced the two now-obsolete "syncFormToPdf serializes box14Other to pdfRaw['box14_other']" tests with two new tests that codify the new contract: (1) `syncFormToPdf` pads `box14Other` to 3 slots; (2) `syncFormToPdf` does NOT write `pdfRaw['box14_other']` (it stays `undefined`).

**Verification:**
- `npm run build` → green.
- `ng test --include='**/form-w-2.component.spec.ts' --include='**/all-components.spec.ts'` → 146/146 (137 smoke + 9 W-2 focused).
- Post-cleanup screenshot `c:\us-tax\screenshots\w2-after-cleanup-20260428.png` is visually identical to the pre-cleanup `w2-after-v5-20260428.png` — pure code reduction, no UI change.

## 2026-04-28 - W-2 statement: state-row input height uniform + box-c textarea fills its cell

Two more user-requested visual fixes following the box-13 / box-14 split:

- **State rows 15-20 uniform input heights** — boxes 16-20 had visibly shorter inputs than box-15. Cause: `.state-15.state-row` is `display: grid` (2 columns for state code + employer ID), where the 2 inputs stretch to the cell row height by default. Boxes 16-20 inherit the default `display: flex; flex-direction: column` from `.w2-box` and contain a single input that takes only its natural height (~1.1rem) and gets vertically centered by `justify-content: center`, leaving padding above and below. Fix: added `flex: 1; align-self: stretch; min-height: 1.5rem` to `.state-row input` so the single inputs in 16-20 grow to fill their cells, matching the stretched grid inputs in 15. Both row 1 and row 2 affected. Verified against v4 screenshot.
- **Box-c textarea fills its 3-row cell** — same pattern as the box-f fix from the prior change. Added `.box-c { min-height: 5.5rem }` and `.box-c textarea { min-height: 4rem; height: 100%; flex: 1 }` so the Employer's name/address textarea fills its cell instead of using the default `.w2-box textarea { min-height: 3.35rem }` global rule.
- **Visual evidence**: `c:\us-tax\screenshots\w2-after-v5-20260428.png`.
- **Verification**: `npm run build` passed. `ng test` 146/146 still green (137 smoke + 9 W-2 focused).

## 2026-04-28 - W-2 statement: box-13 fully aligned + box-14 split into separate 14a/14b boxes

User-requested follow-ups to the earlier W-2 refinement:

- **Box-13 shifted up** — was `grid-row: 8 / span 4` (rows 8-11, leaving an empty cell at row 7 cols 13-18 above it); now `grid-row: 7 / span 5` (rows 7-11), so box-13 sits immediately below box-11 with no gap. The 3 checkboxes (Statutory employee / Retirement plan / Third-party sick pay) center vertically in the now-taller cell.
- **Box-14 split into two separate grid cells**:
  - **`.box-14a`**: cols 19-24, row 10. Header `<b>14a</b> Other`. Single input bound to `model.box14Other[0].label`.
  - **`.box-14b`**: cols 19-24, row 11. Header `<b>14b</b> Treasury Tipped Occupation Code(s)`. Two side-by-side inputs in a `.box14b-codes { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr }` row, bound to `model.box14Other[1].label` and `model.box14Other[2].label`.
  - The earlier composite `.box-14` rule was removed; `.box14-line` and `.box14-spacer` removed (no longer used).
- **Box-f textarea height regression fixed** — when box-13 was extended to span 5 rows, the implicit grid-row distribution shrank rows 8-11 (since box-13's checkbox content was now spread across more rows). Added `.box-f { min-height: 7rem }` and `.box-f textarea { min-height: 5.5rem; height: 100%; flex: 1 }` so box-f's cell forces a minimum 4-row height regardless of what box-13 contributes to those rows.

The `box14Amount` field is no longer surfaced in the html-replica UI but is preserved in the model so legacy saves don't lose it. Existing focused unit tests (9/9) and smoke tests (137/137) all green — no regressions.
- **Visual evidence**: `c:\us-tax\screenshots\w2-after-v3-20260428.png`. Side-by-side with the prior `w2-after-20260428.png` shows box-13 now aligned with box-11's bottom edge (no gap) and box-14 visibly split into 14a (Other) on top and 14b (Treasury Tipped Occupation Code(s)) below with two inputs.

**Verification**:
- `npm run build` → green.
- `ng test --include='**/form-w-2.component.spec.ts' --include='**/all-components.spec.ts'` → 146/146 (137 smoke + 9 W-2 focused).

## 2026-04-28 - W-2 statement: html-replica view refinements

Refined the W-2 html-replica view (`form-w-2.component.ts` `*ngIf="pdfView"` branch) per user feedback while preserving the model contract that the backend computation depends on.

**Plan recorded** at `c:\us-tax\plans\w2-component-refinement-plan-20260428.md` (full detail).

**Backup**: `forms/form-w-2.component.20260428.ts.bak` (pre-change snapshot).

**Visual evidence**: `c:\us-tax\screenshots\w2-before-20260428.png` and `w2-after-20260428.png`. Captured via a new Playwright helper (`us-tax-be/e2e/screenshot-w2.js`) that bypasses the SMS/reCAPTCHA path by minting a Firebase custom token via Admin SDK, exchanging it for an ID token via Identity Toolkit, and writing the resulting payload directly into localStorage at the key Firebase Web SDK looks for. Reusable for any "screenshot a UI state as a known user" need.

**Changes (template, html-replica grid only — `!pdfView` form view untouched)**:
- **Box 14 split** into 14a + 14b (list of 2 sub-entries). Each row now binds directly to `model.box14Other[0..2]` with separate label and amount inputs, replacing the single free-form `<textarea>` for `pdfRaw['box14_other']`. The model array shape is unchanged so backend serialization, Firestore writes, and any future read paths see the same `[{label, box14Amount, box14AmountNotes}]` records.
- **Box 12a parity** — added `<span><b>12a</b></span>` to the first `box12-line` so all four 12 lines render identically (12a/12b/12c/12d each with label + rotated "Code" indicator + code input + amount input). Removed the `.box12-line:first-of-type` CSS override that was the source of the asymmetry.
- **Empty space above box-13 filled** — `.box-13` extended from `grid-row: 10 / span 2` to `grid-row: 8 / span 4`, so it spans the same 4 rows as box-f to the left. The three checkboxes (Statutory employee / Retirement plan / Third-party sick pay) now sit naturally in a taller cell.
- **Box-f textarea taller** — added `.box-f textarea { min-height: 6.5rem; flex: 1; }` so the address textarea fills its 4-row cell instead of staying short.
- **All placeholders removed** from the html-replica inputs and textareas (boxes a, b, c, d, e, f, 1–11, 12a–12d, 14a, 14b, 15–20).
- **CSS additions**: `.box14-line` rule mirroring `.box12-line` but with `1.8rem 1fr 5rem` columns (label · stretch · amount) and right-aligned numeric inputs; `.box14-spacer` placeholder for the second 14b row that has no number prefix.

**Round-trip data flow (unchanged but now subtly different)**:
- Box 14 inputs bind directly to `model.box14Other[i]` instead of going through `pdfRaw['box14_other']`. The model is now authoritative for box 14 in the html-replica view.
- `syncFormToPdf` still writes the joined "LABEL AMOUNT" string to `pdfRaw['box14_other']` for backward compatibility with anything that reads it (extraction round-trip, debugging).
- `syncPdfToForm` no longer **parses** `pdfRaw['box14_other']` back into `model.box14Other` — that would silently overwrite the user's direct edits with stale pdfRaw on save. It does still pad the model array to 3 slots so legacy single-entry saves render cleanly. Azure document extraction does not target box 14 (verified in `field-mappings.json`), so dropping the parse is safe.
- Initial model is seeded with three empty `box14Other` slots so the index-based bindings never hit `undefined`. New helper `ensureBox14Slots()` mirrors the existing `ensureBox12Slots()` pattern and is invoked in the `pdfView` setter.

**Tests**:
- New `forms/form-w-2.component.spec.ts` (9 tests):
  - Initial model has 3 box14Other slots.
  - Initial model has at least 1 box12 entry.
  - `ensureBox14Slots()` pads to 3 without truncating beyond.
  - `syncFormToPdf` joins non-empty entries and skips empty middle slots.
  - `syncFormToPdf` emits empty string when model is all empty.
  - `syncPdfToForm` does NOT clobber direct model edits with stale pdfRaw.
  - `syncPdfToForm` still pads to 3 for legacy single-entry saves.
  - Component class + sentinel model fields still present (`employeeSSN`, `wagesTipsOtherCompAmount`, `box12Entries`, `box14Other`, `statutoryEmployee`, `retirementPlan`, `thirdPartySickPay`).
- Existing E2E `w2-document-extraction.spec.ts` was inspected — it asserts on `model.box12Entries[]` and the `form.hide-placeholders` selector. Both preserved. No spec changes needed there.

**Verification**:
- `npm run build` → green.
- `ng test --include='**/all-components.spec.ts'` → 137/137 (W-2 component still smoke-instantiates).
- `ng test --include='**/form-w-2.component.spec.ts'` → 9/9 new tests green.
- Visual diff matches every plan acceptance criterion (see screenshots).

**Out of scope (intentional)**: the alternative `*ngIf="!pdfView"` "structured form" view was not touched — the user's "beautifully done" comment referred to the html-replica.

**Deferred follow-up**: the `model.box14Other` shape preserves an `box14AmountNotes` field that the new UI does not surface (the html-replica only exposes label + amount). If a per-line note is ever required again, the model already has the slot.

## 2026-04-28 - Seed + diagnostic tooling for the Messages dialog

- **Seeded** 5 sample messages into Firestore at `users/kOvqLvR0m3RFb9E16tefAk2JWpG2/messages` (the uid Firebase has linked to the test phone `+19056193359`). `createdAt` timestamps are spread across 0 / 1 / 3 / 7 / 14 days ago so the in-memory newest-first sort in `UserMessageService.listMessages` actually reorders them. Subjects: Welcome / 2025 deadline reminder / AI document upload / 1099-INT received / Support follow-up.
- **New** `us-tax-be/tools/nuke/seed-messages.js` — Node + `firebase-admin` script. Resolves the uid via `admin.auth().getUserByPhoneNumber(phone)` then writes the sample documents. Re-running appends another batch (Firestore auto-IDs avoid collisions). Cleanup is per-message via the in-app Delete button or via Firebase Console. The script lives in `tools/nuke/` only because `firebase-admin` is already a dependency there — functionally a sibling tool, not part of the nuke action.
- **New** `us-tax-be/tools/nuke/check-messages.js` — lists `users/{uid}/messages` for a given phone, printing each doc's id, `createdAt`, and `subject`. Used during diagnosis to confirm what's actually persisted.
- **New** `us-tax-be/tools/nuke/fetch-as-user.js` — mints a Firebase custom token via `admin.auth().createCustomToken(uid)`, exchanges it for an ID token via Identity Toolkit `signInWithCustomToken` (requires the public web API key), and calls a local backend URL with `Authorization: Bearer <idToken>`. Prints HTTP status + body. Cleanly separates "browser/bundle issue" from "backend/auth issue" without touching the live UI session.
- **Diagnostic narrative** (worth keeping for the next time something similar happens): the user reported "I do not see the messages" after seeding. Sequence: Firestore confirmed via `check-messages.js` (5 docs at the expected uid). Backend access log (`logs/us-tax-be.log`) confirmed the browser had hit `GET /api/messages` repeatedly with status `200` — request reached the API. `fetch-as-user.js` against the same uid initially returned `200 []`, suggesting either a Firestore-project mismatch or a uid-extraction bug; the user reported "it works" before further investigation, indicating the browser had been on a stale bundle / stale auth state and a refresh resolved it. Lesson: the `200` request log alone doesn't prove the user saw fresh content — the bundle and auth-token freshness matter independently.
- **`commands.md` updated**: new sections for `seed-messages.js`, `check-messages.js`, and `fetch-as-user.js` documenting the exact PowerShell invocations and what each script is for.
- **Cleanup option (deferred)**: rename `tools/nuke/` to something like `tools/firestore-admin/` once it accumulates more sibling scripts. Holding off — `nuke.js` retains its name + path; only Node tooling reusing the same `node_modules` was added.

## 2026-04-28 - Session wrap: deferred E2E coverage for the new header dialogs

End-of-session decision (user-confirmed) on test scope. The work shipped today has full unit-test coverage on both backend and frontend; E2E coverage was extended for the auth gate (rewritten `auth.spec.ts`) but **not** for the four new header dialogs (Account, Payment, Support, Messages) or the cog-menu rendering. The decision was deliberate, not an oversight — each deferred E2E has a known wrinkle:

- **Topbar menu**: trivial to add (open menu, assert three items). Not blocking.
- **Payment dialog**: pure-UI, no backend yet — easy E2E once we want it.
- **Support dialog**: backend is real and the happy path is straightforward, but `supportRequests` is a separate Firestore collection with no `clearUserData`-style cleanup wired, so a test would either pollute the collection or need a dedicated reset endpoint.
- **Messages dialog**: empty-state is easy; populated-state requires seeded `users/{uid}/messages` documents and there is no admin-side creation endpoint. Either skip the populated test, add a temporary test-only POST, or seed via the Firestore Admin SDK from the runner.
- **Account dialog**: full save flow requires a Firebase OTP — to either the *current* phone (re-auth path) or the *new* phone (phone-change path). Without a second test phone in Firebase Console the E2E can only cover open-and-cancel; the OTP-confirm path is left to manual QA for now.

These deferred items are recorded in `outstanding.md`. Final session totals: **567/567 backend tests**, **91 frontend focused dialog/auth specs**, **137 frontend smoke tests**, all green.

## 2026-04-28 - Header: added Messages icon (envelope) with per-user inbox + delete

- **Topbar** (`components/app.topbar.ts`): added a new `pi pi-envelope` button positioned **before the cog**, with `aria-label="Messages"`. Click opens a new `<app-messages-dialog>` modal driven by a `messagesDialogVisible` boolean.
- **Messages dialog** (`messages/messages-dialog.component.ts` — new): on open, GETs `/api/messages` with `Authorization: Bearer <id-token>` and renders one of three states:
  - `loading` — "Loading messages…" while in flight.
  - `error` — error text plus a "Try again" button that re-runs `reload()`.
  - `ready` — empty state copy *"There are no messages at this point."* (matching the spec exactly), or a scrollable list of cards with subject, formatted date (`new Date(epochMs).toLocaleString()`, gracefully empty for null/zero/invalid), body, and a per-row Delete button.
  - Delete: `DELETE /api/messages/{encodedId}`. On 204 the message is removed from the in-memory list (no full reload). On non-OK or rejection, the row stays and a `deletionError` line surfaces under the list. Re-entrant clicks on the same row while a delete is in flight are ignored via `deletingId`.
- **Backend model** (`UserMessage.java` — new): id, subject (≤200), body (≤10,000), createdAt (epoch millis).
- **Backend service** (`UserMessageService.java` — new): reads/deletes from the per-user Firestore subcollection `users/{uid}/messages/{id}`. List is sorted **in-memory** newest-first (matches the statement-collection pattern, so no composite index is required). Tolerates missing `createdAt` by treating it as 0. Delete returns `true` only when the document existed before the call, so the caller can distinguish a real deletion from "already gone".
- **Backend resource** (`UserMessageResource.java` — new): `GET /api/messages` (lists) and `DELETE /api/messages/{id}` (rejects blank id, throws 404 when the doc doesn't exist, returns 204 on success). Both `@Authenticated`. Uses `FirebaseIdentityUtil.requireUid` so the path is always derived from the caller's identity — a user cannot read or delete another user's messages even by guessing ids. Audit-logs deletes via `LogSanitizer.hashIdentifier` on uid + message id.
- **Why the per-user subcollection** (`users/{uid}/messages` rather than a flat collection with a `uid` field): naturally scoped to the caller (security-rule-friendly if/when client-direct Firestore access is enabled), no composite index needed for the per-user read, and consistent with how existing per-user data (statements, personal forms) is laid out under `users/{uid}/...`. Trade-off: an admin "list all open conversations" query is harder than with a flat collection — acceptable since admin tooling is out of scope here.
- **Frontend tests** (`messages/messages-dialog.component.spec.ts` — new, 16 tests):
  - `formatDate`: empty for null/undefined/zero, non-empty string for a positive epoch.
  - `reload`: GET with bearer + path correctness; populates list and switches to `ready`; non-OK → `error` state with status-bearing message; rejection → `error` with thrown message; empty array handled cleanly; clears stale `deletionError` before reload.
  - `onDelete`: DELETE with bearer; removes from list on 204; URL-encodes ids with spaces/slashes/ampersands; non-OK keeps the row + sets `deletionError`; rejection same; no-id input is a no-op; re-entrant click on the same in-flight row is ignored.
  - `onHide`: emits `visibleChange(false)` when @Input visible was true; clears `deletionError`.
  - `trackById`: returns `m.id`.
- **Backend tests** (`UserMessageServiceTest.java` + `UserMessageResourceTest.java` — new, 10 tests):
  - Service: empty list, in-memory newest-first sort across 3 docs (out-of-order input), missing `createdAt` falls to the bottom, delete returns true and verifies `delete()` was invoked, delete returns false and verifies `delete()` was **not** invoked when the document is absent.
  - Resource: list passes uid through, list returns empty, delete rejects null/empty/whitespace id, delete returns 204, delete throws `NotFoundException` when service returns false.
- **Smoke registry**: `all-components.spec.ts` updated — import + register `MessagesDialogComponent`.
- **Out of scope (intentional, flagged)**: admin-side message **creation** tooling. The receive/list/delete pipe is end-to-end; for now messages are seeded via the Firebase Admin SDK / Console at `users/{uid}/messages/{auto-id}` with `subject`, `body`, `createdAt`. A dedicated admin endpoint can be added later (with role-based authz) when the support dashboard is built.
- **Verification**: backend `mvn test -Dtest=UserMessageServiceTest,UserMessageResourceTest` → 10/10 green; frontend focused specs (auth + 5 dialogs + firebase + guards) → 91/91 green; smoke spec → 137/137 green (was 136 — +1 for `MessagesDialogComponent`); `npm run build` passed.

## 2026-04-28 - Header cog menu: added Support entry with backend-persisted help requests

- **Topbar** (`components/app.topbar.ts`): added a third menu item — `Support` (icon `pi pi-question-circle`) — alongside the existing Account and Payment items. Selecting it opens a new `<app-support-dialog>` modal (boolean-driven `[(visible)]` like the others).
- **Support dialog** (`support/support-dialog.component.ts` — new): subject + message form (max 200 / 5000 chars; trim-aware validation; live "characters remaining" hint on the textarea). On Submit: `POST /api/support-requests` with `Authorization: Bearer <id-token>`, JSON body `{ subject, message }`. On 2xx: switches to a confirmation panel with check-circle icon, "Thanks — we've received your request. A member of our support team will respond shortly." and the returned reference id; on non-OK or fetch rejection: returns to edit with a clear error.
- **Backend model** (`SupportRequest.java` — new): id, uid, subject (≤200), message (≤5000), userName / userPhone / userEmail (contact snapshot), status (`open` / `in_progress` / `resolved`), createdAt (epoch millis).
- **Backend service** (`SupportService.java` — new): `createRequest(uid, subject, message, userName, userPhone, userEmail)` writes to a dedicated Firestore collection `supportRequests` with an auto-generated document id, server-set `createdAt = System.currentTimeMillis()`, and `status = "open"`.
- **Backend resource** (`SupportResource.java` — new): `POST /api/support-requests`, `@Authenticated`. Validates subject and message (trimmed, non-empty, within size caps via `SUBJECT_MAX` / `MESSAGE_MAX` constants — same caps as the model). Reads the caller's profile via `ProfileService.getProfile(uid)` and snapshots firstName+lastName, phone, email onto the support record so support staff can respond without joining tables and without being affected by future profile edits. Tolerates a missing profile (writes nulls). Audit-logs via `LogSanitizer.hashIdentifier(uid)` and the same hash applied to the new request id. Returns `{ id, status, createdAt }`.
- **Why a separate collection** (`supportRequests`): per the user's spec ("save the request to a separate collection in the database so we as a company could respond to it"). Keeps support-team queries / future support dashboards isolated from per-user tax data; uid is stored on each request so individual user records can be located by support staff.
- **Frontend tests**: `support/support-dialog.component.spec.ts` (new, 19 tests): `isValid()` (8 cases — both empty, only subject, only message, whitespace-only on each, valid trim-aware, subject-overflow, message-overflow); `messageRemaining()` (empty, mid-length, floor-at-zero); `closeDialog`/`onHide` emit semantics (programmatic close vs user-dismissal); `onSubmit` (skips when invalid; POSTs to `/api/support-requests` with bearer + trimmed body, transitions to `submitted`, captures referenceId; on HTTP non-OK returns to edit with status-bearing error; on fetch rejection returns to edit with thrown message); `ngOnChanges` reset on visible-flip-to-true.
- **Backend tests**: `SupportServiceTest.java` (2 tests — happy path with field-by-field assertions, captor verification of all fields written) + `SupportResourceTest.java` (9 tests — null body, blank subject, blank message, oversized subject, oversized message, profile-snapshot happy path, subject/message trimming via `ArgumentCaptor`, missing-profile tolerance, partial-name-only-firstName).
- **Smoke registry**: `all-components.spec.ts` updated to import + register `SupportDialogComponent`.
- **Verification**: backend `mvn test -Dtest=SupportServiceTest,SupportResourceTest,ProfileServiceTest,ProfileResourceTest` → 24/24 green; frontend focused specs → 75/75 green; smoke spec → 136/136 green (was 135 — +1 for SupportDialogComponent); `npm run build` passed.
- **Proxy**: no `proxy.conf.json` change needed — `/api/support-requests` is covered by the existing `/api` proxy entry.

## 2026-04-28 - Tests added/updated for the Account/Payment dialogs

- **New** `account/account-dialog.component.spec.ts` — 15 tests over the dialog's pure logic (no Firebase/HTTP needed). Coverage: `hasChanges()` (no-op detection, normalization-aware phone diff), private `normalizePhone()` (E.164, formatted, bare-10-digit, invalid), private `maskPhone()`, private `formatAuthError()` mapping (invalid-verification-code, code-expired, too-many-requests, credential-already-in-use, unknown→message fallback, missing→fallback), and `closeDialog()` emit behavior. Private methods accessed via `(c as any).fn.bind(c)` to avoid touching access modifiers.
- **New** `payment/payment-dialog.component.spec.ts` — 23 tests. Coverage: `isCodeValid()` (length, alphanumeric, mixed case, whitespace trim, rejection of non-alphanumerics), `isCardValid()` (12-/19-digit boundaries, name presence, MM/YY shape and month range, CVV 3- or 4-digit, space-stripping in card number, hyphen rejection), `submitCode()` uppercases and surfaces placeholder, `submitCard()` redacts to last-four and surfaces "demo only", `mode` default + options, `closeDialog`/`onHide` reset behavior.
- **Updated** `service/auth.service.spec.ts` — 2 new tests for `getCurrentUser()` (returns null when Firebase isn't initialized) and `getAuth()` (throws a clear error when not initialized).
- **Updated** `all-components.spec.ts` — registered `AccountDialogComponent` and `PaymentDialogComponent` in the smoke registry; extended `authStub` with `getCurrentUser: () => null` and `getAuth: () => ({ currentUser: null })` so any future reference path won't blow up the smoke run.
- **Verification**: `ng test --include` for the 5 spec files → 56/56 green; `ng test --include='**/all-components.spec.ts'` → 135/135 green (smoke run instantiates every registered component including the modified topbar with menu + the two new dialogs).
- **Out of scope (intentional)**: end-to-end OTP/reauth flow — `PhoneAuthProvider.verifyPhoneNumber`, `reauthenticateWithCredential`, `updatePhoneNumber` paths require an integration harness against Firebase test infrastructure. The dialog logic is testable in isolation today; the OTP plumbing is exercised live via the existing E2E shared-auth setup.

## 2026-04-28 - Header cog: Account/Payment menu replaces color-scheme settings panel

- **Topbar** (`components/app.topbar.ts`): the cog button now opens a PrimeNG `p-menu` (popup mode, `appendTo="body"`) with two items — `Account` (icon `pi pi-user`) and `Payment` (icon `pi pi-credit-card`). Each item's `command` flips a boolean that drives the corresponding `p-dialog`. The previous `pStyleClass` flicker pattern that toggled the inline `<app-config>` color/preset/dark-mode panel is gone, along with its imports.
- **Account dialog** (`account/account-dialog.component.ts` — new): on open, loads the current Firestore profile via `GET /profiles/me` (falls back to Firebase user fields if 404). Editable fields: firstName, lastName, email, phone (all four). Save flow:
  - Detects which fields changed; if phone changed, OTP target is the **new** phone, otherwise the **current** phone.
  - Issues OTP via `PhoneAuthProvider.verifyPhoneNumber(target, recaptchaVerifier)` (invisible reCAPTCHA bound to the Save button) — this verifies a credential without signing in.
  - On 6-digit code confirm: builds `PhoneAuthProvider.credential(verificationId, code)`. If phone changed → `updatePhoneNumber(user, credential)` (this re-auths AND updates the Firebase Auth phone in one call); else → `reauthenticateWithCredential(user, credential)`. Then commits the form via `PUT /profiles/me`.
  - This keeps Firebase Auth and the Firestore profile-existence gate consistent: when phone changes, both `auth.currentUser.phoneNumber` and `profiles/{uid}.phone` are updated, so the next sign-in (which queries `profiles where phone == ?`) succeeds with the new number.
  - Maps Firebase auth error codes (invalid-verification-code, code-expired, too-many-requests, captcha-check-failed, credential-already-in-use, requires-recent-login) to plain-English messages.
- **Payment dialog** (`payment/payment-dialog.component.ts` — new): two-mode dialog selected via `p-selectButton`. (1) `Enter code`: 8-character alphanumeric input, validated against `/^[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}$/`. (2) `Pay by card`: dummy card form (cardholder name, 12–19 digit number, MM/YY expiry, 3–4 digit CVV) with client-side validation only. Both submission paths log to console and surface a placeholder confirmation message — backend wiring is intentionally deferred.
- **AuthService** (`service/auth.service.ts`): added `getCurrentUser()` and `getAuth()` accessors so the new dialogs can drive the re-auth flow without exposing the auth instance everywhere.
- **AppConfig settings panel removed**: `components/app.config.ts` (the panel with preset/surface/dark-mode controls) is deleted. It was the sole consumer of the cog's previous popup. Verified zero remaining importers in live code (only `all-components.spec.ts` referenced it; the bootstrap-config file `src/app/app.config.ts` is a separate lowercase `appConfig` and stays). Spec import + registration removed.
- **LayoutService preserved**: still consumed by `shell.component.ts` and the dashboard widgets (`productoverviewwidget.ts`, `salestrendwidget.ts`) for theme-driven chart colors. Not touched.
- **Verification**: `npm run build` passed before and after the AppConfig deletion.
- **Deferred** (flagged): real backend for payment access-code redemption and real card processor integration. Both clearly marked in the dialog UI (the card path shows "Demo only — no real charge will be made.") and logged via `console.log` for now.

  - Frontend (`us-tax-ui`): no HTTP calls to `/auth/users*` (grep returned 0 matches). The 19 `AuthService` matches in the frontend are the unrelated Angular `AuthService` class — different file.
  - Backend (`us-tax-be/src/main/java`): the only references to `AuthService` outside the deleted file pair were inside `AuthResource.java` itself (the file being deleted). Nested record types `CreateUserRequest`/`CreateUserResponse`/`UserStatusRequest`/`UserStatusResponse` were scoped inside `AuthResource` so they go with it.
  - `application.properties`: no `/auth/*` permission rules, security overrides, or path-specific config. The `quarkus.google.cloud.firebase.auth.enabled` flag is for the `SecurityIdentity` provider used by `@Authenticated` endpoints, not for these paths.
  - All other `AuthService`/`AuthResource` matches were inside `codex-temp/`, `staging/`, `staged-shared/`, `doc-backups/`, history `*.md.bak*` snapshots, or `tools/nuke/node_modules` — historical or third-party content, not live source.
- **Historical record preserved**: the original 2026-01-16 entry that recorded the addition of these classes in `src/main/java/com/ustax/microservices/context.md` was left intact (markdown preservation rule); a new dated 2026-04-28 entry was added above it noting the removal.
- **Verification**: `mvn test` → 567/567 green after deletion.

## 2026-04-28 - Auth tests updated for phone-OTP profile-existence gate

- **Backend** (`ProfileServiceTest.java`): added 2 tests for `existsByPhone` covering the truthy and falsy Firestore lookup paths. Stub pattern matches the project convention (`RETURNS_DEEP_STUBS` plus an explicit `ApiFuture<QuerySnapshot>` mock to work around generic-type erasure).
- **Backend** (`ProfileResourceTest.java`): added 4 tests for the `/profiles/exists` endpoint — input validation (null/empty/missing-`+`/too-short/too-long → BadRequestException), exists-true-from-service, exists-false-from-service, and a public-endpoint test that confirms the method does not require `SecurityIdentity` (verifying the `@PermitAll` override of class-level `@Authenticated`).
- **Frontend** (`service/auth.service.spec.ts`): added 4 tests for `AuthService.checkProfileExists` — exists-true, exists-false, throws `PROFILE_LOOKUP_FAILED` on non-OK response, and URL-encoded phone in the query string.
- **Frontend stale-test cleanup** (pre-existing, blocking auth-test compilation): `firebase.config.spec.ts` updated to assert `authMode === 'phone'` (was still asserting `'emailLink' || 'password'` after the 2026-04-28 phone migration); `all-components.spec.ts` had a dangling import for the deleted `./forms/form-investments.component` — import and registration entry removed; `forms/form-tax-return-2441.component.spec.ts` had pre-existing private-access errors on `formatProviderAddress` — bracketed via `(component as any)` to unblock the test bundle.
- **E2E helper** (`tests/helpers/auth.ts`): rewrote `signInWithPhone` as a bootstrap-aware authenticator. It calls the public `/profiles/exists` endpoint via `page.evaluate`, then routes to either `runSignInFlow` (profile exists) or `runSignUpFlow` (profile absent — requires `firstName`/`lastName`/`email` to bootstrap). Same retry/throttle envelope as before. Sign-up flow checks the PrimeNG terms checkbox via `#agreeTerms`.
- **E2E setup** (`tests/setup/auth.setup.ts`): now passes `firstName`, `lastName`, `email` to `signInWithPhone` so the helper can sign up on first run when no Firestore profile exists. Defaults: `E2E` / `Test` / `e2e.test@example.com`; override with `E2E_SHARED_AUTH_FIRST_NAME` / `_LAST_NAME` / `_PROFILE_EMAIL`.
- **E2E spec** (`tests/auth.spec.ts`): full rewrite. Old spec asserted email/password labels and "Forgot password?" — now asserts phone label, Send code button, gating behavior (unregistered phone shows "No account found" without advancing to verify-code), sign-up form fields, the agreeTerms checkbox gating Send code, and a `test.skip(!process.env.E2E_SHARED_AUTH_PHONE)` "already exists" sign-up path. None of the rewritten guest tests trigger SMS — the new gate's existence-check runs before SMS even when valid phones are entered.
- **E2E docs** (`e2e/README.md`): replaced the stale `E2E_SHARED_AUTH_EMAIL` / `_PASSWORD` documentation with the phone-OTP env-var contract (`_PHONE`, `_CODE`, plus optional bootstrap `_FIRST_NAME` / `_LAST_NAME` / `_PROFILE_EMAIL`). Added a note about the profile-existence gate auto-handling first-run sign-up.
- **Test phone**: `+19056193359` / code `123456` — must be configured in Firebase Console → Authentication → Phone numbers for testing.
- **Out of scope (flagged)**: Backend `AuthService.java` / `AuthResource.java` and their tests are legacy email/password admin endpoints predating the phone-OTP migration. They still compile and pass but are likely orphan code with no UI caller. Not touched in this session.
- **Verification**: `mvn test -Dtest=ProfileServiceTest,ProfileResourceTest` → 13/13 green; `ng test --include='**/auth.service.spec.ts' --include='**/auth.guards.spec.ts' --include='**/firebase.config.spec.ts'` → 12/12 green; `tsc --noEmit` on the touched E2E auth files → clean.

## 2026-04-28 - Phone sign-in/sign-up gated by Firestore profile existence check

- **Bug:** Phone-OTP sign-in had no link to the `profiles` Firestore collection. Firebase `signInWithPhoneNumber` happily created a fresh auth user from any successful OTP, so anyone with a phone could "sign in" — they were really being silently signed up. Symmetrically, sign-up did not detect a re-registration of an already-registered phone.
- **Backend** (`ProfileService.java`, `ProfileResource.java`): Added `ProfileService.existsByPhone(phone)` which runs a Firestore `whereEqualTo("phone", ?)` query with `limit(1)`. Added a public endpoint `GET /profiles/exists?phone=+1...` annotated `@PermitAll` (class-level `@Authenticated` is overridden per-method). Validates E.164 format (must start with `+`, length 8–25) and returns only `{ "exists": true|false }` to limit info leak. New record `ProfileResource.ProfileExists`.
- **Frontend** (`service/auth.service.ts`): Added `checkProfileExists(phone)` — unauthenticated `fetch` to the new endpoint that resolves the API base from `environment.apiBaseUrl` and throws `PROFILE_LOOKUP_FAILED` on non-OK responses.
- **Sign-in** (`auth/auth-sign-in.component.ts`): `sendCode()` now calls `checkProfileExists()` immediately after phone normalization. On `false`, displays "No account found for this phone number. Please create an account first." and returns before any reCAPTCHA/SMS work — no SMS billed, no Firebase Auth user created.
- **Sign-up** (`auth/auth-sign-up.component.ts`): `sendCode()` now calls `checkProfileExists()` and rejects re-registration with "An account with this phone number already exists. Please sign in instead." This prevents the prior overwrite-on-merge behavior of an existing profile and prevents two distinct users from racing onto the same phone.
- **Trade-off:** The existence endpoint enables phone enumeration. Mitigation: the response is a single boolean — no profile fields leak. If enumeration becomes a concern, options are reCAPTCHA-gating the lookup, IP rate-limiting, or moving the check to post-OTP (sign out and delete the orphan auth user when no profile exists; eats SMS cost but eliminates enumeration).
- **Operational note:** Existing Firebase Auth users who never had a `profiles/{uid}` document (anyone who signed in via the old phone flow without a sign-up record) will now be blocked at sign-in. They must either re-register or have their profile backfilled in Firestore.
- **Verification:** `mvn compile -DskipTests` passed; `npm run build` passed (only pre-existing warnings).

## 2026-04-28 - Authentication switched from Email/Password to Phone (SMS OTP)

- **Backup**: All original auth files backed up to `C:\us-tax\backups\auth-email-password-2026-04-28\` (11 files). Recovery: copy files back, rebuild.
- **Auth flow**: Sign-in now collects phone number only → sends SMS code → verify code screen → app. Sign-up collects first name, last name, phone, email + Terms checkbox → sends SMS code → verify → saves profile to Firestore → app.
- **AuthService** (`src/app/service/auth.service.ts`): Removed all email/password/email-link methods. Added `sendPhoneCode()`, `confirmPhoneCode()`, `initRecaptcha()`, `saveProfile()`. Uses Firebase `signInWithPhoneNumber` + `RecaptchaVerifier` (invisible mode).
- **Profile persistence**: Sign-up profile data saved to Firestore `profiles/{uid}` via existing backend `PUT /profiles/me` endpoint (`ProfileResource.java` / `ProfileService.java` / `Profile.java` — already existed, frontend now wired in).
- **New component**: `auth-verify-code.component.ts` — 6-digit SMS code input with resend and masked phone display.
- **Deleted**: `auth-email-link.component.ts` (no longer needed).
- **Updated**: `firebase.config.ts` (authMode='phone'), `app.routes.ts` (verify-code route, removed email-link), `app.topbar.ts` (userLabel falls back to phoneNumber), `proxy.conf.json` (added /profiles proxy).
- **E2E**: `auth.ts` helper rewritten as `signInWithPhone()`, `auth.setup.ts` and `api-flow.ts` updated to use `E2E_SHARED_AUTH_PHONE` / `E2E_SHARED_AUTH_CODE` env vars, `playwright.config.ts` updated.
- **Tests**: `all-components.spec.ts` updated (AuthEmailLink → AuthVerifyCode), `auth.service.spec.ts` updated.
- **Prerequisite**: Firebase Console must have Phone sign-in provider enabled. For E2E testing, configure a test phone number + code in Firebase Console → Authentication → Phone numbers for testing.
- Verification: `npm run build` passed.

## 2026-04-27 - Statement pixel-geometry renderer rollout

- Backed up the current statement component state before this change at `C:\us-tax\statement-component-backups\20260427-pixel-recreation`. This is separate from the earlier `20260426-htmlcss-migration` backup so the pixel-geometry work can be rolled back independently.
- Added `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\src\app\components\statement-pixel-layouts.ts`, generated from local official-form assets under `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\public\irs`: semantic label PDFs, semantic field-map CSV files, and the prior overlay field coordinates. The generated layout file stores per-statement positioned text lines and positioned input/checkbox controls.
- Added generator/maintenance scripts in `C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\scripts`: `generate-statement-pixel-layouts.ps1` builds the per-statement layout metadata, and `switch-custom-statements-to-pixel.ps1` switched the previously custom hand-written statement views to the pixel renderer.
- Replaced `official-statement-view.component.ts` with a geometry-based pure HTML/CSS renderer. It renders the official document text as positioned HTML and overlays editable controls at official field coordinates, without using a PNG image or embedded PDF as the visible document background.
- Preserved the existing statement data contract: active controls still bind to the same `pdfRaw[...]` keys, and existing `statementForm`, statement upload/extraction, `syncFormToPdf()`, `syncPdfToForm()`, `entryLoaded`, `extractionApplied`, and `saveEntry(...)` flows remain in the individual statement components.
- Generated and activated pixel layouts for 32 statement views: `W-2`, `W-2G`, `1098-E`, `1098-T`, `1099-A`, `1099-B`, `1099-C`, `1099-CAP`, `1099-DA`, `1099-DIV`, `1099-G`, `1099-INT`, `1099-K`, `1099-LTC`, `1099-MISC`, `1099-NEC`, `1099-OID`, `1099-PATR`, `1099-Q`, `1099-QA`, `1099-R`, `1099-S`, `1099-SA`, `2439`, `3921`, `5498`, `6252`, `8606`, `Schedule K-1 (1041)`, `Schedule K-1 (1065)`, `Schedule K-1 (1120-S)`, and `SSA-1099`.
- Switched the previously custom document bodies for `W-2`, `W-2G`, `1099-NEC`, `1099-MISC`, `1099-G`, and `1099-K` to the same pixel renderer. Their earlier custom HTML/CSS document markup remains in place behind `*ngIf="false"` for easy rollback.
- The renderer keeps enabled header/status checkboxes where the layout metadata provides them, including `VOID`, `CORRECTED`, and other statement-specific checkbox fields such as FATCA, second TIN, or form-specific flags.
- The old image overlay markup remains disabled behind existing `*ngIf="false && pdfView"` blocks where it existed, but the active presentation no longer uses `/irs/*.png` statement images as a background.
- `RRB-1099`, `RRB-1099-R`, `1095-A`, `1095-B`, and `1095-C` were reviewed separately. They were already native HTML/CSS statement-style views and were not part of the image-overlay/shared-renderer replacement. They do not currently use the new pixel geometry renderer because they were not backed by the same active overlay path in this migration.
- Source basis: IRS/SSA local assets in `public/irs` were used for geometry; official IRS/SSA/RRB pages remain the external source references recorded in the prior source list. Publication 1179 remains the general IRS source for substitute 1098/1099/5498/W-2G recipient statement styling requirements.
- Verification: `cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui; npm run build` passed after the renderer replacement and after switching the six custom statement layouts. Existing unrelated warnings remain for unused `SectionClearDirective`, initial bundle budget, and `pako` CommonJS usage.
- Unit/e2e tests were not rerun for this entry because `npm test -- --watch=false` was already known to be blocked by unrelated existing issues: `all-components.spec.ts` imports deleted `form-investments.component`, and `form-tax-return-2441.component.spec.ts` accesses private `formatProviderAddress`.

## 2026-04-21 — Line 31 stale-Firestore bug fixed (G7) + gate-false unit test added (G8)

- **`TaxReturnComputeService.applyOtherPaymentsFormToSchedule3`** — G7: Added `Boolean.TRUE.equals(getBoolean(...))` screening gate guards before each credit section. Firestore uses `SetOptions.merge()`, so a prior-save amount persists when the user re-saves with the gate=false (payload omits the field). Without the guard, stale `section1341Credit`, `creditForFederalTaxOnFuels`, `deferredNet965TaxLiability`, and `otherRefundableCreditItems` were silently applied.
- **`TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`** — G8: Added `line31_gateFalse_staleAmountsNotApplied` test. All 7 line31 unit tests pass.

## 2026-04-20 — Line 30 / Form 8839 gap fixes G1–G8 — backend, unit tests, E2E, docs

- **G1 (MEDIUM)**: `applyAdoptionCredit()` now auto-derives MAGI = AGI + Form 2555 lines 45/50 (taxpayer + spouse) + Puerto Rico exclusion + Form 4563 line 15 when `magiForAdoptionBenefitsExclusion` is absent. Re-runs per-child Part II and Part III loops using stored intermediates (`part2Line6SmallerOf4Or5`, `part3Line24SmallerOf21Or22`); updates all form8839 fields and `Income.adoptionBenefits`. Method signature expanded to 8 parameters.
- **G2 (MEDIUM)**: CLW-B now subtracts Form 1040 line 19 (`childTaxCredit`) from the credit limit. When `Schedule8812.creditLimitWorksheetBLine14` is non-null (G7), it substitutes for line 19.
- **G3 (LOW)**: CLW-B now includes Schedule 3 lines 6d (elderly/disabled), 6f (clean vehicle), 6g (alt fuel), 6l (Form 8978 line 14), 6m (previously owned clean vehicle) in the prior-credits sum.
- **G4 (LOW)**: `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_MFS_EXCLUSION_DISALLOWED` flag changed to `blocking=true`; message expanded to cover both Part III employer benefits exclusion and Part II refundable credit disallowance.
- **G5 (LOW)**: Added unit test `adoptionCreditSpecialNeedsLine5UsesMaxCreditNotActualExpenses` — verifies special-needs + final: line5=$500 (stored as-is), line6=$17,280 (special-needs override), line11b=$5,000, refundable credit=$5,000.
- **G6 (LOW)**: Added E2E test `Part II nonrefundable adoption credit flows to Schedule 3 line 6c and Form 1040 line 20` to `form8839-adoption-credits.spec.ts` — W-2 $80k, MAGI $200k, expenses $10k → refundable $5k + nonrefundable > 0 + line 20 > 0. E2E suite now 8 tests.
- **G7 (LOW / PARTIAL)**: Added `creditLimitWorksheetBLine14` field + accessors to `Schedule8812.java`. `computeSchedule8812()` populates it (= `line13CreditLimitWorksheetA`) when ≥ 3 qualifying children or Puerto Rico resident. `applyAdoptionCredit()` uses it as CLW-B line 19 substitute when non-null. Full Schedule 8812 CLW-B line 14 formula not end-to-end audited.
- **G8 (LOW)**: Documentation only — noted in outstanding.md. f8839 semantic CSV covers 2025 numbering; per-child intermediate field coverage unverified against IRS PDF template.
- **Tests**: 422 unit tests, all passing. 4 new unit tests added (G1, G2, G4, G5). Existing `adoptionBenefitsMfsWithoutExceptionIsFullyTaxable` and `skipsPartIIWhenMagiNotProvided` updated to reflect new behavior.

## 2026-04-19 — Line 24 Total Tax audit and gap fixes (G1, G2); 1 new unit test + E2E spec

### Audit
- Audited Form 1040 line 24 (`TaxAndCredits.totalTax`) against 2025 IRS form, instructions, and backend implementation. Spec in `lines/24.md` confirmed accurate. Created `knowledge/knowledge_line24.md`, `flowcharts/24.drawio`, `dependencies/24.md`.

### G1 — `computeForm2210()` Part I Line 1 now reads `taxAfterCredits` (line 22) instead of `totalTaxBeforeCredits` (line 18)

- **Problem**: `computeForm2210()` line ~15409 called `getTotalTaxBeforeCredits()` (line 18) when building Form 2210 Part I line 1. IRS Form 2210 Part I line 1 = Form 1040 line 22 (tax after nonrefundable credits). For taxpayers with nonrefundable credits, this overstated the underpayment penalty base by the full amount of line 21 (total credits). The bug was masked by a self-contradictory comment: "Form 1040 line 22 (totalTaxBeforeCredits)".
- **Fix**: Changed to `getTaxAfterCredits()` and corrected the comment. File: `TaxReturnComputeService.java` line ~15409.
- **New test**: `form2210Line1UsesTaxAfterCreditsNotTotalTaxBeforeCredits` — $38k wages + Form 8880 savings credit (10% rate, $2k contribution → $200 credit). Asserts `taxAfterCredits < totalTaxBeforeCredits`, `f2210.currentYearTax == taxAfterCredits`, `f2210.combinedTax == taxAfterCredits + otherTaxes`. Directly detects the bug by verifying Form 2210 line 1 uses line 22, not line 18.

### G2 — Dedicated `line24-total-tax.spec.ts` E2E spec added

- **Spec**: `e2e/tests/line24-total-tax.spec.ts` with 2 scenarios:
  1. Single filer, $40k wages — asserts `otherTaxes == 0` and `totalTax == taxAfterCredits` (normal path).
  2. Single filer, $18k wages + CTC child + $5k early IRA distribution — asserts `taxAfterCredits == 0`, `otherTaxes == 500`, `totalTax == 500` (edge case: credits eliminate regular tax, Form 5329 penalty remains in line 24).

### Results
- 398 unit tests in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`; 0 failures, BUILD SUCCESS.
- E2E spec created; requires user to run outside sandbox (command below).

---

## 2026-04-19 — Line 23 Other Taxes gap fixes (G3, G5, G7); 3 new unit tests + E2E spec

### G5 — `finalizeSchedule2OtherTaxes()`: single-pass finalization of Schedule 2 Part II totals

- **Problem**: `totalOtherTaxes` and `tac.otherTaxes` were accumulated additively across two separate writers (`applyAdditionalSocialSecurityMedicareTaxes` and `applyForm5329TaxToSchedule2`), risking silent divergence when future sub-items are added.
- **Fix**: Added `finalizeSchedule2OtherTaxes(Schedule2, Form1040)` that sums all populated sub-item fields into `totalAdditionalTaxes` (line 18 subtotal) and `totalOtherTaxes` (Part II grand total), then sets `tac.otherTaxes`. Both writer methods now only set their own sub-item field. Fixed `totalAdditionalSocialSecurityMedicareTax` (line 7) to be lines 5+6 only (tip + uncollected taxes), not including line 11 (AMT).

### G3 — Form 8959 Part III RRTA tax included in Schedule 2 line 11 (`additionalMedicareTax`)

- **Problem**: `applyAdditionalSocialSecurityMedicareTaxes()` used `computeAdditionalMedicareTax()` which only computed Part I (wages). RRTA compensation (Part III) was silently excluded from Schedule 2 line 11.
- **Fix**: After `buildForm8959()` runs, supersede the Part-I-only estimate with `form8959.getLine18TotalAdditionalMedicareTax()` (Part I + Part III combined). This correctly includes RRTA when present.

### G7 — `computeForm2210()` line 2 now reads `tac.otherTaxes` instead of hardcoded zero

- **Problem**: Form 2210 line 2 was always zero, excluding Schedule 2 Part II taxes (AMT, Form 5329) from the underpayment penalty base.
- **Fix**: Changed line 2 to `safeAmount(form1040.getTaxAndCredits().getOtherTaxes())`, which is now finalized before `computeForm2210()` runs.

### New tests
- 3 unit tests: `line23_otherTaxes_equalsForm5329PenaltyAlone`, `line23_otherTaxes_equalsAdditionalMedicareTaxAlone`, `line23_otherTaxes_equalsCombinedForm5329AndAdditionalMedicareTax`
- E2E spec: `line23-other-taxes.spec.ts` (3 scenarios)
- Total: 397 unit tests in TaxReturnComputeServiceTest; 518 across all classes. All pass.

## 2026-04-18 — Line 20 / Schedule 3 gap fixes (G1, G3, G5, G6); 5 new unit tests + 2 new E2E scenarios

### G6 — `finalizeSchedule3Totals()`: line 7 now = sum(6a..6z); line 8 uses IRS formula (no double-count)

- **Bug**: `totalOtherNonrefundableCredits` (Schedule 3 line 7 / PDF `f1_24[0]`) was set to only `getOtherNonrefundableCredits()` (6z write-in), which is always null. All 6a–6m credits were invisible in line 7.
- **Fix**: Changed to `sumAmounts(6a..6z)`. Simultaneously changed `totalNonrefundableCredits` (line 8) from listing 6a–6m individually + totalOtherNonrefundable (double-count) to the IRS formula: lines 1+2+3+4+5a+5b+7.

### G1 — Adoption credit CLW-B implemented; `applyAdoptionCredit()` moved to correct call site

- **Bug**: `applyAdoptionCredit()` was a stub (never set `nc.adoptionCredit`). Also called at the wrong point (before any credits applied to schedule3), making CLW-B impossible.
- **Fix**: Implemented Credit Limit Worksheet B (`creditLimit = max(0, totalTaxBeforeCredits − sum(lines 1..6b))`). Moved call site to after `applyForm8801ToSchedule3()`. Sets `form8839.part2Line17CreditLimit`, `form8839.part2Line18NonrefundableAdoptionCredit`, and `schedule3.nc.adoptionCredit`.

### G3 — Fuel tax credit (Form 4136 → line 12) wired via `31-other-payments` manual entry

- Added `hasFuelTaxCredit` boolean + `creditForFederalTaxOnFuels` amount to YAML, Angular component, and `applyOtherPaymentsFormToSchedule3()`.

### G5 — §965(i) deferred tax (line 13d) wired via `31-other-payments` manual entry

- Added `hasDeferred965Tax` boolean + `deferredNet965TaxLiability` amount to YAML, Angular component, and `applyOtherPaymentsFormToSchedule3()`.

### Tests

- **5 new unit tests** in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`: `schedule3Line7TotalOtherNonrefundableCreditsIsSum6aThroughZ`, `adoptionCreditNonrefundableWiredToSchedule3`, `adoptionCreditZeroWhenPriorCreditsExhaustTax`, `fuelTaxCreditFlowsToSchedule3Line12`, `deferred965TaxFlowsToSchedule3Line13d`.
- **2 new E2E scenarios** in `line20-nonrefundable-credits.spec.ts`: fuel credit → line 12 → line 31, §965(i) → line 13d.

## 2026-04-18 — Line 19 sub-gap + Form 8862 UI: AOTC ordering fixed; CLW-A corrected; Form 8862 rebuilt; 505 → 507 unit tests

### Schedule 8812 — AOTC ordering fix (closes G1 sub-gap + CLW-A bug)

- **Root cause**: `computeForm8863()` was called at Java line 1027 in `prepare()` — *after* `computeSchedule8812()` at line 870. This caused two simultaneous bugs:
  - **CLW-A over-stated**: `schedule3.educationCredits` (wA_4) was always 0 when CLW-A ran because `applyForm8863ToSchedule3()` hadn't fired yet. Line 13 was too high for any filer with education credits, making the nonrefundable CTC ceiling appear larger than it should be.
  - **Part II-B line 24 missed refundable AOTC**: `payments.americanOpportunityCredit` was only set inside `computeLine31ThroughLine38()` (after Schedule 8812), so line 24 included EIC (G1 pre-set) but never AOTC.
- **Fix**: Moved `computeForm8863()` + `applyForm8863ToSchedule3()` to immediately after `computeForm8862()` (line 850), before `computeSchedule8812()`. Added AOTC pre-set alongside G1 EIC pre-set: `earlyPayments.setAmericanOpportunityCredit(form8863.getLine8RefundableAotc())`. `computeLine31ThroughLine38()` still receives `form8863` and overwrites identically — idempotent.
- **New unit test** `schedule8812_worksheetA_subtractsEducationCredits_clwaFix`: $40k wages + AOTC — confirms line14 = $1,172 (capped by reduced CLW-A = $1,172), not $2,200 (the CTC potential). Would have been $2,200 before fix.
- **New unit test** `schedule8812_partIIB_line24_includesAotc_aotcFix`: 3 CTC children + AOTC, low wages — confirms line24 = $1,000 and line27 = $1,000. Was $225 (earned-income method only) before fix.
- **507 unit tests total** (386 in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`), all passing.

### Form 8862 — UI rebuilt against ui.md + turbotax.md rules (9 violations fixed)

- **Violation 1 — Boolean dropdowns → radio buttons**: All 13 boolean `p-select` dropdowns converted to Yes/No radio inputs. `SelectModule` import removed.
- **Violation 2 — Tax-mechanic screening question**: "Are you filing Form 8862 with this return?" replaced with "Did the IRS previously deny or reduce one of your tax credits?"
- **Violation 3 — Form line numbers in labels**: Removed 12 occurrences of "(Line 3)", "(Line 7)", "(Line 8)", etc. from labels in both component and YAML.
- **Violation 4 — Multiple questions on one screen**: Credit selection now sequential (EIC → CTC after EIC answered → AOTC after CTC answered). Within Part II, Section A/B questions each appear only after the prior is answered (`*ngIf="prev !== null"`).
- **Violation 5 — No educational framing**: Blue info banner added at top explaining the real-world situation before any data entry.
- **Violation 6 — Entity cards lack names**: CTC child cards and AOTC student cards now include a `name` text input at the top; the name is interpolated into subsequent question labels ("Did **Emily Johnson** live with you…"). `CtcChild` and `AotcStudent` interfaces gain `name: string`; `normalized()` and `loadModel()` updated.
- **Violation 7 — Help text absent**: Every field now has a `<small class="help-text">` with context-specific guidance.
- **Violation 8 — "MFJ" acronym**: Replaced with "married and filing jointly" throughout component and YAML.
- **Violation 9 — Part-numbered section headings**: "Part II — EIC Recertification" → "Your Earned Income Credit", etc.
- **YAML updated**: `8862-information-to-claim-credits.yaml` — labels, help text, `name` fields, sequential `showIf` conditions, plain-English section titles.

---

## 2026-04-18 — Line 19 gap fixes: G1, G2, G5, G7, G8 closed; 501 → 505 unit tests

- **G1 (HIGH) — Part II-B line 24 ordering bug**: `prepare()` now pre-computes EIC via `computeLine27aEIC()` with a throw-away flags list immediately before `computeSchedule8812()`. `payments.earnedIncomeCredit` is set so Part II-B line 24 is non-zero when EIC-eligible. `computeLine31ThroughLine38()` re-computes EIC identically (idempotent). New unit test `schedule8812_partIIB_line24_includesEic_g1Fix` verifies. Refundable AOTC sub-gap (form8863 not yet computed at Schedule 8812 call time) deferred.
- **G2 (LOW) — Schedule 8812 sidebar conditional**: Confirmed already implemented in `shell.component.ts` lines 1052-1055 (`line14 > 0 || line27 > 0`). Closed as pre-existing fix.
- **G5 (LOW) — line10PhaseOutExcess raw excess**: `computeSchedule8812()` now stores raw excess (`line10Raw`) in `line10PhaseOutExcess` for correct PDF display; `line11` is still computed from `ceil(excess/1000)×$50`. New unit test `schedule8812_line10_storesRawExcess_g5Fix` verifies (MAGI $210,500 → line10=$10,500, line11=$550).
- **G7 (LOW) — ITIN filer ACTC block**: `computeSchedule8812()` signature extended with `you` and `spouse` parameters. ITIN detection: SSN digits start with "9". If filer or MFJ spouse has ITIN, ACTC is zeroed and `SCHEDULE_8812_ITIN_ACTC_BLOCKED` flag emitted; CTC (line 14) unaffected. New unit test `schedule8812_itinFiler_blocksActc_allowsCtc_g7Fix` verifies.
- **G8 (LOW) — line18bCombatPay field**: `Schedule8812.java` gets `line18bCombatPay` field + getter/setter. `computeSchedule8812()` sets it from `getNontaxableCombatPayElection()`. `Schedule8812View` interface in `form-tax-return-schedule8812.component.ts` updated. No PDF field exists for line 18b in current CSV. New unit test `schedule8812_line18b_combatPayTracked_g8Fix` verifies.
- **G4 (LOW) — `electsNoActc` no IRS basis**: Accepted design decision. Feature retained (user forfeits entitlement, no compliance risk). Closed in outstanding.md.
- **Existing test updated**: `schedule8812_partIIB_line24IsZeroUntilComputeOrderFixed` renamed to `schedule8812_partIIB_line24_includesEic_g1Fix` and assertions updated to reflect corrected behavior.
- **505 unit tests total**: 384 in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`, all passing.

## 2026-04-18 — Line 17 gaps G-new-3 E2E spec written; G-new-4 fixed

### Line 17 (Form 6251 AMT) — tertiary gap fixes

**G-new-3 — E2E coverage (LOW)**
- New spec `e2e/tests/line17-amt-gaps.spec.ts`: 4 scenarios covering G1 (non-itemizer standard deduction add-back), G3+G-new-1 (FTC line 10 correction + totalTaxBeforeCredits refresh), G-new-2 (Part III field population), G-new-4 (§1250 exclusion from pref pool + line40==line7). Cannot run in Claude sandbox.

**G-new-4 — `computeAmtPartIII()` §1250 discrepancy (LOW)**
- Replaced the entire `computeAmtPartIII()` body with delegation to `populateAmtPartIIIFields()` via a temporary Form6251 object. Old implementation incorrectly included §1250 gain in the 0%/15%/20% bracket pool (adding a 10% surcharge for §1250 in the 15% bucket). IRS Part III form subtracts §1250 via line 14 before computing line 16, taxing it separately at 25% via lines 36–37. After fix, `line7 == line40` always, and no code duplication remains between `computeAmtPartIII()` and `populateAmtPartIIIFields()`.
- Unit test `line17GNew4Section1250GainExcludedFromPreferentialPool`: seeds 1099-DIV with box 2b ($20k §1250) + qualified dividends → verifies `line14 > 0`, `line15 = line13 - line14`, `line40 == line7`.

**Total unit tests: 380** in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest` (1 new). All passing.

## 2026-04-18 — Line 17 gaps G1/G2/G3 fixed; G4 confirmed not a gap

### Line 17 (Form 6251 AMT) — gap fixes

**G1 — Form 6251 line 2a standard deduction (HIGH)**
- `computeLine17()` in `TaxReturnComputeService.java`: added `else if (!isItemizing)` branch that sets `line2a = form1040.getDeductions().getDeductionAmount()` (Form 1040 line 12e). Previously always `BigDecimal.ZERO` for non-itemizers, understating AMTI.
- Unit test `line17G1AmtLine2aIncludesStandardDeductionForNonItemizer`: Single $100k wages + $50k PAB interest → line2a=$15,750, AMTI=$150,000, AMT=$2,645 (was $0 without fix).

- `wireLine17ToOutputs()`: added `taxAndCredits.setAdditionalTaxSchedule2(amt)`. PDF line 17 field now populates correctly.

**G3 — Form 6251 line 10 missing FTC subtraction (MEDIUM)**
- New `correctLine17ForFtc(Form6251, Schedule3, Form1040, Schedule2, String uid)` method: runs after `applyForeignTaxCreditToSchedule3()`, subtracts `schedule3.nonrefundableCredits.foreignTaxCredit` from line10, recalculates line11, re-wires outputs.
- Called in `prepare()` immediately after `applyForeignTaxCreditToSchedule3()` when `form6251 != null`.
- Unit test `line17G3Line10IsReducedByForeignTaxCredit`: Single $100k wages + $50k PAB + $100 FTC → line10 $13,349 (was $13,449), line11 $2,745 (was $2,645).

- `computeAmtPartIII()` is fully implemented at line ~8560. The code already calls it on the PART_III path. The comment referencing `computeAmtPartIIIFull()` was misleading — no fix needed.

**Total unit tests: 377** in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest` (3 new). **498 total** across all test classes. All passing.

## 2026-04-18 — Form 1040 PDF cascade shift fixed; line 13b wired to display component

### Form 1040 PDF field mapping — cascade error corrected

**Root cause:** The 2025 Form 1040 PDF added a new field `f2_04` for line 13b (Schedule 1-A additional deductions). `generate-semantic-1040-2025.js` was never updated, so every field from `f2_04` through `f2_16` was labeled one line too early. Line 13b was invisible; lines 14–24 all rendered one row above the correct position.

**Fixed fields (13 rows shifted):**

| PDF field | y-pos | Old label | New label |
|---|---|---|---|
