## 2026-04-17 — Line 14 gaps closed; 4 Java unit tests + 1 E2E scenario added; lines/14.md corrected

### Line 14 — test coverage gaps resolved (all 5 identified gaps)

**lines/14.md correction**
- Age/blind add-on amounts corrected: was $1,550/$1,950 (2024 values); changed to **$1,600** (MFJ/QSS/MFS) / **$2,000** (Single/HOH) — matching 2025 `ReferenceData.java` constants and the existing test suite.

**4 new Java unit tests added to `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`** (372 total in class; 493 total suite):
1. `line14_allThreeComponents_totalDeductionsIsSum` — Gaps 1+3: full-service integration asserts `getTotalDeductions()` = $29,750 when standard ($15,750) + K-1 QBI 13a ($6,000) + overtime 13b ($8,000) all present simultaneously.
2. `line14_line13aBlockedBySelfEmploymentFlag_totalDeductionsStillComputed` — Gap 2: SE flag blocks line13a to null; asserts `getTotalDeductions()` = $15,750 (line12e alone), proving null is treated as 0.
3. `line14_itemizedDeductionsFlowIntoTotalDeductions` — Gap 4: itemized path (Schedule A $20,000); asserts `getTotalDeductions()` = $20,000.
4. `line14_ageBlindAdjustmentsFlowIntoTotalDeductions` — Gap 5: MFJ 4 boxes; asserts `getTotalDeductions()` = $37,900 ($31,500 + 4 × $1,600).

**1 new E2E scenario added to `line14-total-deductions.spec.ts`** (6 scenarios total):
- Scenario 6 — Gap 3: K-1 QBI (13a > 0) + overtime (13b > 0) simultaneously; asserts `totalDeductions = line12e + line13a + line13b`.

## 2026-04-17 — Lines 13a/13b gaps 3, 7 (already done), 1, 2, 5 fixed; 4 new unit tests; 3 output forms wired to Tax Return sidebar

### Changes
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java` — Gap 3 (MEDIUM/SSTB phase-in): removed blanket `LINE13A_SSTB_THRESHOLD_UNSUPPORTED` block; `compute8995AQbiDeductionComponent()` now scales SSTB QBI/W-2/UBIA by applicable percentage `(1 − phaseInPercentage)` in phase-in band; above upper threshold contributes explicit $0. Gap 1 (MEDIUM/1099-NEC tips): added `sum1099NecTipsForSsn()` helper; calls it inside taxpayer/spouse tips block gated on `taxpayerReceivedQualifiedTips + taxpayerTippedOccupationConfirmed`. Gap 2 (MEDIUM/trade tips cap): `taxpayerTradeTipsNetIncomeCap` and `spouseTradeTipsNetIncomeCap` fields enforced via `min(necTips, cap)`. Gap 5 (LOW/refinancing): vehicle loop proportionalizes interest as `paid × (originalPrincipal / refinancedBalance)` when `vehicleWasRefinanced = true`.
- `form-1099-nec.component.ts` — added `isTipIncome: false` to model; added checkbox in Amounts section.
- `form-additional-deductions.component.ts` / `.html` — added `taxpayerTradeTipsNetIncomeCap` (taxpayer), `spouseTradeTipsNetIncomeCap` (spouse) amount fields; added `vehicleWasRefinanced`, `vehicleOriginalLoanPrincipal`, `vehicleRefinancedLoanBalance` to `VehicleEntry` interface and template.
- `form-tax-return-8995.component.ts`, `form-tax-return-8995a.component.ts`, `form-tax-return-schedule-1a.component.ts` — new output-view components wired to Tax Return sidebar; conditional sidebar entries based on `computation.form8995`, `computation.form8995A`, `computation.schedule1A`.
- `shell.component.ts` — imported and registered all 3 new output components; added 3 conditional sidebar items.
- `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java` — 4 new tests: `schedule1ANecTradeTipsAutoImport`, `schedule1ANecTradeTipsCappedByNetIncomeCap`, `schedule1ACarLoanRefinancingProportionalizesInterest`, `line13aSstbActivityInPhaseInBandGetsPartialDeduction`. Total: **367 tests, all passing**.

## 2026-04-17 — Lines 13a/13b gaps 1, 2, 6, 10, 11 fixed; 3 unit tests + 1 E2E test added

### Changes
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java` — Gap 1 (HIGH): added `BigDecimal line13b` parameter to `computeLine13a()`; updated `taxableIncomeBeforeQbi = AGI − line12e − line13b` (IRS formula); added second-pass re-invocation of `computeLine13a()` in `prepare()` after `computeSchedule1A()` so the corrected `taxableIncomeBeforeQbi` flows into the 20% limitation; first pass inside `computeLine12()` now passes `null` for `line13b`. Gap 2 (MEDIUM): removed misleading "proxy" comment on capital gain limitation (formula was already correct). Gap 6 (LOW): added `isCooperativePatronOfAgriculturalHorticulturalCooperative` boolean field; emits blocking flag `LINE13A_COOPERATIVE_PATRON_UNSUPPORTED`. Gap 10 (LOW): added `priorYearReitPtpLossCarryforward` field to `QbiPersonInputs`; computes `netQualifiedReitAndPtpAmount = qualifiedReitAndPtpAmount − priorYearReitPtpCarryforward`; uses net amount as component base; stores carryforward on Form 8995/8995A output models.
- `Form8995.java`, `Form8995A.java` — added `priorYearReitPtpLossCarryforward` field with getter/setter.
- `yamls/13a-qualified-business-income-taxpayer.yaml`, `yamls/13a-qualified-business-income-spouse.yaml` — added `isCooperativePatronOfAgriculturalHorticulturalCooperative` boolean field; added `hasPriorYearReitPtpLossCarryforward` + `priorYearReitPtpLossCarryforwardAmount` fields.

- `e2e/tests/line13b-additional-deductions.spec.ts` — Gap 11 (LOW): added Scenario 8 E2E test for tips phaseout (single filer, wages $160k, box 7 tips $20k, expected line13TipsDeduction = $19,000).

## 2026-04-17 — Standard deductions backend gap fixes (3 gaps, 9 new unit tests)

### Changes
- `ReferenceData.java`: Added 4 SALT phasedown constants — `SCHEDULE_A_SALT_PHASEOUT_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT` ($500k), `SCHEDULE_A_SALT_PHASEOUT_THRESHOLD_MFS` ($250k), `SCHEDULE_A_SALT_FLOOR_DEFAULT` ($10k), `SCHEDULE_A_SALT_FLOOR_MFS` ($5k)
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java`: (1) Renamed `saltLimitForStatus()` → `saltLimitForStatusFlat()` and added `saltLimitForStatusAndMagi(status, magi)` implementing the OBBBA 2025 phasedown worksheet ($1-for-$1 above MAGI threshold, floor applies). Updated `buildScheduleA()` to use AGI as the MAGI proxy. (2) Updated `countAgeBlindnessBoxes()` to accept `status` parameter; HOH filers no longer count spouse age/blindness boxes (spouse boxes silently accepted but not counted). The preliminary indicator count (before status is resolved) passes `null` to preserve raw counts. (3) Added `applyForm8396ReductionToScheduleA(scheduleA, form8396, form1040)` method — reduces Schedule A mortgage interest by Form 8396 line 3 when itemizing, recomputes totalInterestPaid and totalItemizedDeductions, patches `Deductions` in Form 1040 (itemizedDeductionsFromScheduleA, deductionAmount, totalDeductions, taxableIncome, line15TaxableIncome). Called in `prepare()` after `applyForm8396ToSchedule3()`. Updated Form 8396 note from "deferred" to "applied".
- `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`: Added 9 new unit tests covering: line12b forced zero, line12c forced zero, MFJ 4-box count, QSS 2-box cap, SALT base cap enforcement, SALT MAGI phasedown to floor, HOH spouse-box guard, dependent worksheet at exact threshold, AUTO election selects itemized when larger.

- `computesLine12bForcesStandardDeductionToZeroForMfsSpouseItemizes`
- `computesLine12cForcesStandardDeductionToZeroForDualStatusAlien`
- `computesLine12dFourBoxesForMfj`
- `computesLine12dForQssCapsTwoBoxes`
- `computesScheduleASaltCapEnforcedAtFortyThousand`
- `computesScheduleASaltCapPhasesDownAboveMagiThreshold`

- `computesAutoElectionChoosesItemizedWhenLarger`

## 2026-04-17 — SALT cap fix: $10,000/$5,000 → $40,000/$20,000 MFS (OBBBA 2025) + null-safety fix in buildScheduleA()

### Backend changes
- `ReferenceData.java`: Updated 5 SALT constants — `SCHEDULE_A_SALT_LIMIT_SINGLE/MFJ/QSS/HOH` from $10,000 → $40,000; `SCHEDULE_A_SALT_LIMIT_MFS` from $5,000 → $20,000 (One Big Beautiful Bill Act, 2025)
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java` `buildScheduleA()`: Fixed null-safety bug — `deductibleTaxes` was being set to `saltLimit` when no SALT was entered (because `minAmount(null, limit)` returns `limit`). Fixed to return `null` when `taxesBeforeLimit == null`, preventing phantom SALT deductions
- Comment fix in Form 6251 line 2a: removed stale "$10k cap" reference

### Test updates (reflecting correct 2025 SALT cap)
- `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java` `computesLine12UsingItemizedDeductionsAndScheduleA()`: updated 5 assertions — SALT components $13k (was $10k); itemized total $29,700 (was $26,700); taxable income $70,300 (was $73,300)
- `line12e-standard-deductions.spec.ts`: updated 3 E2E assertions — itemized total $15,300 (was $14,300; SALT $11k now uncapped under $40k limit)

### Frontend change
- `form-standard-deductions.component.ts`: updated default values `saltLimitDefault` 10,000 → 40,000; `saltLimitMfs` 5,000 → 20,000 (reference data API overrides these on load, but defaults now accurate)

### Remaining gaps for SALT
- SALT MAGI phasedown worksheet (above $500k/$250k MFS, floor $10k/$5k) — not yet implemented
- outstanding.md: "CRITICAL" gap entry updated to "RESOLVED" status will be reflected in next outstanding.md update

## 2026-04-17 — Line 10 UI violations fixed (ui.md + turbotax.md audit)

### Frontend changes
- **`form-income-adjustments-taxpayer.component.ts`** — Full rewrite:
  - Removed 8 statement-upload yes/no fields (violating rule 35–38 statement-first design). Replaced with count pills + attribution rows + sidebar guidance messages derived live from `StatementDataService.listEntries()`.
  - Added `isCoveredByWorkplaceRetirementPlanTaxpayer` to interface, model, and template (gated on IRA amount).
  - Added `filingStatus` fetch + `isMfs` getter; student loan field replaced with MFS notice when filing separately.
  - Split 13-field flat grid into 9 named `.adjustment-group` sub-sections (educator / business / HSA / moving / penalty / alimony / IRA / student loan / Archer MSA), each with header, line reference, and educational description paragraph.
  - Screening question reworded to plain English (removed form/line number references).
  - `normalizeForSave()`: clears IRA sub-fields, MFS student-loan field, and No-branch fields appropriately.
- **`form-income-adjustments-spouse.component.ts`** — Full rewrite with matching pattern:
  - Added `StatementDataService` import; statement count + derived totals; count pills + attribution + sidebar guidance.
  - Added `isCoveredByWorkplaceRetirementPlanSpouse` to interface, model, and template (gated on IRA amount).
  - Added `filingStatus` fetch + `isMfs` getter; MFS student loan gate and `normalizeForSave()` clear.

## 2026-04-16 — Line 10: all 6 gaps fixed; 14 new unit tests; 370 tests passing

### Backend (TaxReturnComputeService.java)
- **Gap 1 — Educator cap** (`computeIncomeAdjustments()`): Applied $300/person cap before summing taxpayer + spouse educator expenses (IRS §62(a)(2)(D)).
- **Gap 2 — Post-2018 alimony guard**: After computing line19a/19c, checks `isAlimonyAgreementPost2018(line19c)`; emits blocking flag `INCOME_ADJUSTMENTS_ALIMONY_POST_2018_NOT_DEDUCTIBLE` and nulls line19a for agreements dated 2019-01-01 or later (TCJA §11051).
- **Gap 3 — Student loan $2,500 cap**: Applied `min(STUDENT_LOAN_INTEREST_MAX)` to uncapped line21 (IRC §221(b)(1)).
- **Gap 4 — Student loan MAGI phaseout** (`buildAdjustments()`): Phaseout using MAGI_SL = line11a + line21Pre. MFS → fully disallowed. Single: $85k–$100k. MFJ: $170k–$200k. Mutates `adj.setStudentLoanInterestDeduction()` and line10/line11a in-place.
- **Gap 5 — IRA MAGI phaseout** (`buildAdjustments()`): Per-person phaseout using MAGI_IRA = line11a (post-SL) + SL deduction + IRA total. Separate thresholds for covered/non-covered by filing status (§219(g)). IRS rounding rules: round up to nearest $10; $200 floor. `IncomeAdjustmentsComputation` record expanded with 4 new fields (iraDeductionTaxpayer, iraDeductionSpouse, isCoveredByWorkplaceRetirementPlanTaxpayer, isCoveredByWorkplaceRetirementPlanSpouse).
- **Gap 6 — K-1 (1041) direct parse for line 24k**: Added `sumStatementAmount(entriesK11041, "excessDeductionsSection67eAmount")` as third addend to line24k (stacks with personal form field + imported field).
- **buildAdjustments() signature change**: Added `Map<String, Object> filingStatus` parameter; call site in `buildForm1040()` updated.
- **4 new helper methods**: `applyLinearPhaseout()`, `roundUpToNearest10()`, `computeIraPhaseout()`, `isAlimonyAgreementPost2018()`.
- **14 new constants in ReferenceData.java**: `EDUCATOR_EXPENSES_CAP_PER_PERSON`, `STUDENT_LOAN_INTEREST_MAX`, SL phaseout thresholds (single/MFJ), IRA phaseout thresholds (single/MFJ/MFS/MFJ-non-covered).

### YAML changes
- `10-income-adjustments-taxpayer.yaml`: Added `isCoveredByWorkplaceRetirementPlanTaxpayer` boolean field after `iraDeductionMfsLivedApartLine20`.
- `10-income-adjustments-spouse.yaml`: Added `isCoveredByWorkplaceRetirementPlanSpouse` boolean field after `iraDeductionMfsLivedApartLine20`.

### Frontend (form-schedule-k1-1041.component.ts)
- Added `excessDeductionsSection67eAmount` input field (Box 11 Code A — §67(e) excess deductions) for K-1 (1041) statement entries.

### Tests
- Fixed existing test `computesSchedule1PartIiAndForm1040Line10FromIncomeAdjustmentsForms`: alimony date changed from `"2020-01-01"` (would now be blocked) to `"2017-12-15"` (pre-2019 → passes through; all assertions unchanged).
- 14 new unit tests added (T1–T14 per plan): educator cap per-person, MFJ educator both capped, post-2018 alimony flag, pre-2019 alimony pass-through, SL cap, SL MFS disallowed, SL partial phaseout (single), SL no phaseout below threshold, IRA fully phased out (single covered), IRA no phaseout (single not covered), IRA partial phaseout (MFJ covered), IRA phased via spouse coverage, K-1 direct parse, K-1 + personal form stack.
- **370 unit tests total** (up from 356). All passing.

### outstanding.md
- Marked 5 of 6 gaps as resolved. Remaining open: statement auto-import path tests (low priority).

## 2026-04-16 — Line 8: 4 gap fixes (1099-K disclosure, Olympic line 24c auto-compute, Section 962 gate, 1099-G auto-parse)

### Gap 1 — 1099-K top-of-Schedule-1 disclosure field
- `Schedule1AdditionalIncome.java`: added `form1099KPersonalItemsDisclosureAmount` field + getter/setter.
- `computeOtherIncomes()`: reads `form1099KPersonalItemsDisclosureAmount` from both forms, stores on `additionalIncome`; does NOT add to line 9/10; added to `hasAnySchedule1Input` OR chain so Schedule 1 is still attached for disclosure-only returns.
- `8-other-incomes-taxpayer.yaml` + `8-other-incomes-spouse.yaml`: field added to `schedule1Lines1to7` section.
- Frontend: added to `OtherIncomesTaxpayerModel` interface + initializer + `sections` array for lines 1-7. Added help text entry.

### Gap 2 — Olympic exclusion auto-compute for line 24c
- `computeIncomeAdjustments()`: new `BigDecimal olympicLine8m` parameter.
- Line 24c now uses `firstNonNullAmount(userEnteredLine24c, olympicLine8m)` — user override takes precedence; auto-compute fallback.
- `prepare()`: extracts `olympicLine8m` from `otherIncome.additionalIncome().getOtherIncomeOlympicParalympicAwards()` and passes to `computeIncomeAdjustments()`.

### Gap 3 — Section 962 election out-of-scope blocker
- `8-other-incomes-taxpayer.yaml`: `hasSection962CfcElection` boolean added to `outOfScopeScreening` section.
- `computeOtherIncomes()`: Section 962 check emits `OTHER_INCOME_SECTION_962_ELECTION_OUT_OF_SCOPE` blocking flag (same pattern as Schedule C/F).
- Frontend: `hasSection962CfcElection` added to model interface, initializer, and out-of-scope HTML section with out-of-scope-blocker div and help text.

### Gap 4 — 1099-G auto-parse for line 7 unemployment
- `computeOtherIncomes()`: streams `entries1099G` for `unemploymentCompensationAmount`; `importedOrAutoLine7 = firstNonNullAmount(statementTotal, legacyImportedField)`; manual `unemploymentCompensationLine7` always adds on top.

### Tests
- 6 new unit tests in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`: T1 (1099-K disclosure not in income), T2 (Olympic auto line24c), T3 (user line24c overrides auto), T4 (Section 962 flag), T5 (1099-G statement auto-parse), T6 (manual + statement = sum).
- **346 total tests, 0 failures.**

Three statement-derived booleans passed as extra parameters to the method.

## 2026-04-16 — Lines 7a/7b: Phase 2 gap fixes (Gaps 3, 4, 6, 7) — 460 tests

### Gap 7 — Nonbusiness bad debt (§166)
- `nonbusinessBadDebtFaceAmount` and `nonbusinessBadDebtDescription` added to `7ab-capital-gain-loss-taxpayer.yaml` and `7ab-capital-gain-loss-spouse.yaml`.
- `addNonbusinessBadDebtTransaction()` helper creates a Box C Form 8949 entry (short-term, basis not reported to IRS) with proceeds=$0 and basis=face amount when populated.

### Gap 6 — 1099-S advisory flag
- `1099-s` entries now loaded from Firestore in `prepare()` and passed to `computeCapitalGainLoss()`.
- Non-blocking advisory flag `FORM_1099S_REAL_ESTATE_REPORTED` emitted when any 1099-S entries exist.

### Gap 3 — Form 8997 (QOF Annual Report)
- `CapitalGainLossComputation` internal record extended with `hasQofDeferral` boolean.
- `prepare()` checks `capital.hasQofDeferral()` and produces a `RequiredAttachmentForm form8997` when true.
- `form8997` added to `TaxReturnComputation` record and wired through `TaxReturnDataService` (serialize + deserialize).

### Gap 4 — Capital Loss Carryover Worksheet
- `computeCapitalLossCarryover(line7, line15, allowable)` implements the IRS carryover split:
  - Short-term losses offset by long-term gains (and vice versa) on the opposite Schedule D line.
  - Allowable $3,000 deduction applied to adjusted short-term losses first, then long-term.
- Results stored in `ScheduleD.nextYearShortTermCapitalLossCarryover` and `nextYearLongTermCapitalLossCarryover`.
- Computed inside the `if (scheduleDRequired)` block when line 16 is a loss.

- 4 new unit tests (456 → 460 total, all passing):

  - `form8997RequiredAttachmentProducedWhenQofDeferralDetected`
  - `capitalLossCarryoverWorksheetComputesNextYearShortAndLongTermCarryovers`
- `f1099SEntries` added to `TestStatementDataService`.

## 2026-04-16 — Lines 7a/7b: Phase 1 gap fixes (Gaps 1, 2, 5, 8)

### Gap 5 fix — Form 8814 child cap gain routed through Schedule D line 13
- When Schedule D is required and a child's Form 8814 line 10 is non-zero, `form8814Line10Total` is now added to `line13Amount` (capital gain distributions), which propagates through `line15Amount → line16Amount → line7a`.
- In the Exception 1 (no Schedule D) path, child amount still adds directly to line 7a as before.
- `line7a` is no longer double-counted: on the Schedule D path it equals `line7aBase` only.
- Unit test: `form8814ChildCapGainFlowsThroughScheduleDLine13WhenScheduleDRequired` — parent $100 div + child $60 Form 8814 → Schedule D line 13 = $160, line 16 = $460 = line 7a.

### Gap 8 fix — Nominee advisory flag
- Added non-blocking flag `CAPITAL_NOMINEE_DISTRIBUTIONS_REPORTED` emitted in `computeCapitalGainLoss()` whenever `nomineeAdjustments > 0`, reminding the user to issue Form 1099-DIV to the actual owner.
- Unit test: `nomineeCapitalAdvisoryFlagEmittedWhenNomineeAmountPresent`.

- `buildCapitalAttachmentForms()` added to `TaxReturnComputeService`. Produces `RequiredAttachmentForm` for each capital source form type when Firestore entries exist.
- Five new fields added to `TaxReturnComputation` record: `form2439Capital`, `form6252Capital`, `form6781Capital`, `form8824Capital`, `scheduleK1Capital`.
- `TaxReturnDataService` updated: serialize/deserialize all five new fields.
- `TaxReturnResourceTest` and `MicroserviceModelsTest` updated to match new constructor arity.

### Gap 2 fix — Schedule K-1 capital `RequiredAttachmentForm`
- `buildCapitalAttachmentForms()` also emits `scheduleK1Capital` when any K-1 (1041/1065/1120-S) entries exist, noting that capital amounts are entered via the personal capital gain form lines 4/5/11/12.
- Unit tests: `capitalAttachmentFormsProducedWhenStatementEntriesExist` and `capitalAttachmentFormsAbsentWhenNoStatementEntries`.

**Total tests: 456 (all pass)**. New tests: `nomineeCapitalAdvisoryFlagEmittedWhenNomineeAmountPresent`, `form8814ChildCapGainFlowsThroughScheduleDLine13WhenScheduleDRequired`, `capitalAttachmentFormsProducedWhenStatementEntriesExist`, `capitalAttachmentFormsAbsentWhenNoStatementEntries`.

## 2026-04-16 — Lines 6a/6b/6c/6d backend and UI fixes (Phase 1–4)

### Phase 1 — UI/E2E fixes
- **`form-social-security-benefits-spouse.component.ts`**: removed unused `SelectModule` import (all fields already used `RadioButtonModule`).
- **`form-social-security-benefits-taxpayer.component.ts`**:
  - Added `FilingStatusSnapshot` interface + filing status load in `ngOnInit`
  - Added `isMfs` and `filingStatusUnknown` boolean properties
  - Added `mfsOnly?: boolean` to `SectionDef` interface; marked "Filing separately — spousal facts" section with `mfsOnly: true`
  - Template wraps sections in `ng-container *ngFor` so the MFS-only section is hidden unless filing status is MFS or unknown
  - `isValid()`: removed redundant `hasUploadedAtLeastOneSsaOrRrbStatement` check (line 617 already gates on count ≤ 0)
- **`line6abcd-social-security-benefits.spec.ts`**: test 1 migrated from `selectOption()` (dropdown) to `selectRadioOption()` (radio buttons); removed interaction with non-rendered `hasUploadedAtLeastOneSsaOrRrbStatement` field.

### Phase 2 — Advisory flag: IRA deduction + SS benefits coordination
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java`: emits non-blocking `SOCIAL_SECURITY_IRA_COORDINATION_MANUAL_REVIEW` flag when both `iraDeduction > 0` and `taxableSocialSecurityBenefits > 0`.
- New unit test `emitsIraCoordinationAdvisoryFlagWhenIraDeductionAndTaxableSsCoexist`.

### Phase 3 — Adoption exclusion wired into worksheetLine5
- `computeSocialSecurityBenefits()`: `worksheetLine5` now includes `adoption.line1f()` alongside Form 2555 exclusions, per Pub. 915 modified AGI rules.
- Unit test for adoption-SS interaction deferred (requires full Form 8839 child data setup).

### Phase 4 — Full Pub. 915 Worksheet 3 lump-sum election
- `computeTaxableSocialSecurityLumpSum()` rewritten: correct IRS Pub. 915 Worksheet 3 method.
  - Computes taxable base on current-year regular SS (line6a minus lump-sum total).
  - For each prior-year row: calls `computeTaxableSocialSecurityNormal()` with prior-year income/adjustments/exempt-interest; additional taxable = max(0, recomputed − reported).
  - Method signature extended to receive `line6a` and all worksheet context from call site.
- Updated unit test assertion: 10850 → 11725 (MFS, $30k SS, $30k wages, $5k lump-sum with no prior income → current-year base 11725, prior-year additional 0).
- Updated E2E test assertion: 10850 → 11725.
- New unit test `lumpSumElectionUsesFullPub915Worksheet3WhenPriorYearIncomeProvided`.

### Tests
- All 325 backend unit tests pass (`./mvnw test`). Angular build passes (0 errors).

## 2026-04-15 — Lines 6a/6b/6c/6d — 5 of 6 gaps resolved (PDF CSV, worksheet line 5, worksheet line 6, negative benefits guard, E2E rewrite)

### Summary
Applied 5 gap fixes to the Social Security benefits computation and tests. The 6th gap (lump-sum full Pub. 915 recomputation) remains deferred but the method signature now accepts `normalizedFilingStatus` in preparation.

### Gap 1 — PDF CSV checkboxes mapped
Both `pdfs/f1040_field_mapping_semantic.csv` and `us-tax-ui/public/irs/f1040_field_mapping_semantic.csv` updated: `c1_41[0]` → `line6c_lump_sum_election`, `c1_42[0]` → `line6d_mfs_lived_apart_all_year`. PDF export now fills lines 6c/6d checkboxes.

### Gap 2 — Worksheet line 5 (Form 2555 exclusions)
`computeTaxableSocialSecurityNormal()` now accepts `worksheetLine5`. New helper `computeForm2555ExclusionForSsWorksheet()` instantiates a temporary `Form2555` via `computeForm2555Exclusions()` and returns line 45 + line 50. Called for both taxpayer and spouse raw form maps. `computeSocialSecurityBenefits()` signature extended with `foreignEarnedIncomeTaxpayer` and `foreignEarnedIncomeSpouse` map params; call site in `prepare()` updated.

### Gap 3 — Worksheet line 6 wired to computed Schedule 1 adjustments
`computeSocialSecurityBenefits()` now accepts `incomeAdjustments: IncomeAdjustmentsComputation`. `worksheetLine6` falls back to `incomeAdjustments.line10FromSchedule1Line26()` when no manual override is present (formerly hard-coded to zero). Call site in `prepare()` passes the already-computed `incomeAdjustments` value. New unit test: single filer SS=$20k, wages=$25k, IRA deduction=$5k → taxable SS $2,500 (vs $5,350 without adjustment).

### Gap 5 — Negative net benefits advisory flag
When `line6a < 0` (repayments exceed gross benefits), `computeSocialSecurityBenefits()` now emits non-blocking flag `SOCIAL_SECURITY_NEGATIVE_NET_BENEFITS_MANUAL_REVIEW` and returns null. New unit test verifies flag code and `isBlocking=false`.

### Gap 6 — E2E spec rewritten
`line6abcd-social-security-benefits.spec.ts` expanded to 5 tests: (1) UI save-blocking test retained; (2) MFS lump-sum election API-based; (3) zero taxable SS API-based; (4) Schedule 1 adjustments API-based (new); (5) negative benefits advisory flag API-based (new). All use `seedStatementApi` + `savePersonalFormApi` + `computeReturnApi`. `test.describe.configure({ retries: 1 })` added.

### Test counts
- Unit tests: 329 in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest` (up from 327 — 2 new SS tests). Full suite: 450 passing, 0 failures.
- E2E: 5 tests in `line6abcd-social-security-benefits.spec.ts` (up from 3).

## 2026-04-15 — Lines 5a/5b/5c — UI violations applied to both pension components (full wizard rewrite)

### Summary
Applied comprehensive UI violations fix plan (Rules 4, 6, 22, 33, 34, 36) to both pension/annuity income components. Taxpayer component was fully rewritten first; spouse component received an equivalent rewrite adapted for its differences (no StatementDataService, no early-distribution screens, MFJ gating). Build passes clean on both rewrites.

### Taxpayer component (`form-pension-annuity-income-taxpayer.component.ts`)
- **Rule 4 (one question per screen):** 11-screen wizard expanded to 17 screens. Splits: `classification` → `classification` + `routing-confirmation`; `pension-details` → `pension-rollover` + `taxable-not-determined`; uncommon screens unchanged; added `early-distribution` + `early-distribution-exception` (split from former double-barreled screen). Ephemeral `hadEarlyDistributionBeforeAge59: boolean | null` property drives navigation without persisting to backend.
- **Rule 6 (Edit buttons):** `editScreen(screen)` method added; every summary group has a `summary-group-header` with an Edit button pointing to its source screen. Return from Edit always lands back at summary via `screenHistory = ['summary']`.
- **Rule 22 (educational framing):** Plain-language intro paragraphs added to screening, upload-check, routing-confirmation, pension-rollover, taxable-not-determined, simplified-method-dates, simplified-method-basis, general-rule, and early-distribution screens.
- **Rule 33 (no IRS references):** Removed all "Form 1040 lines 5a/5b/5c", "line 1h", "box 2b", "box 5", "IRS Publication 939", "PSO", "Simplified Method", "General Rule" from all labels, titles, instructions, and summary labels.
- **Rule 34 (plain-English titles):** Renamed screen titles throughout; "Co-annuitant" → "surviving beneficiary"; "Exclusion ratio" → "Tax-free fraction of each payment".
- **Rule 36 (auto-derive from statements):** `countEntriesWithDistributionCode()` scans live 1099-R statement entries via `StatementDataService`. Code 1 count drives `earlyDistributionNoExceptionCount`; `codeGCount` and `taxNotDeterminedCount` read from backend-imported model fields. `syncAutoFieldsFromModel()` pre-populates `hadEarlyDistributionBeforeAge59` and `hasEarlyDistributionAdditionalTaxForForm5329` when counts > 0.
- **PSO auto-elect:** Uncommon-gate PSO checkbox auto-sets `electsPsoPremiumExclusion = true`; PSO screen shows only the premium amount field.
- **Dynamic step counter:** Fixed "Step N of 9" replaced with `Step ${screenHistory.length + 1} — <title>`.
- **`helpDefs` registry:** Flat `Record<string, {label, help}>` replaces `allFieldDefs` array + `sections` array. All help text plain-English.

### Spouse component (`form-pension-annuity-income-spouse.component.ts`)
- Same Rule 4/6/22/33/34/36/PSO/step-counter treatment adapted for spouse differences.
- **9 → 12 screens:** `classification` → `classification` + `routing-confirmation`; `pension-details` → `pension-rollover` + `taxable-not-determined`; `simplified-method` → `simplified-method-dates` + `simplified-method-basis`. No upload-check or early-distribution screens (kept on taxpayer per YAML).
- **Rule 36 (no StatementDataService):** `syncAutoFieldsFromModel()` reads `imported1099RNonIraCodeGCount` and `imported1099RNonIraTaxableAmountNotDeterminedCount` directly from the loaded model to auto-derive `hadPensionRollover` and `hasAnyTaxableAmountNotDeterminedPension1099R`.
- **Summary — 11 groups with Edit buttons:** Pension income, Disability payments, Confirmed as wages (separate group, Edit → `routing-confirmation`), Rollover, Pension statement taxable amount, Rollover amounts, Less common situations (Edit → `uncommon-gate`), Health insurance from pension, Pension start details, Cost basis details, Older pension calculation.
- **`ALL_SPOUSE_PENSION_FIELDS` constant:** Explicit field list used in `normalizeForSave()` replacing the former `allFieldDefs` iteration.

### Build
- Clean on both rewrites — zero TypeScript errors. Pre-existing `pako` CommonJS warning unrelated.

## 2026-04-15 — Lines 5a/5b/5c — Fix all 6 gaps identified in comprehensive review

### Plan

**Gap 1 — Line 5c PDF checkboxes unmapped (Medium)**
Rename `unmapped_c1_38/39/40` in `f1040_field_mapping_semantic.csv` to `line5c_box1_rollover`, `line5c_box2_pso`, `line5c_box3_other`. Add 4 fill lines in `form-tax-return-1040.component.ts`. Publish CSV.

**Gap 2 — PSO field label misleads (Low)**
Rename `totalPsoPremiumsPaidDirectlyToInsurer` → `totalQualifyingPsoPremiumsPaid` in both YAMLs, both Angular components, and the one backend read in `computePensionForPerson()`. Update label and help text to reflect that both direct and retiree-paid paths qualify under 2025 law.

**Gap 3 — RRB-1099-R taxable amount not read (Medium)**
In `computePensionForPerson()`, split RRB loop to read Box 4 (`contributoryAmountPaidAmount`), Box 5 (`vestedDualBenefitAmount`), Box 6 (`supplementalAnnuityAmount`), Box 3 (`employeeContributionsCostAmount`). Box 5+6 always taxable; Box 4 fully taxable only when Box 3 cost = 0. When cost > 0 Box 4 excluded from initial taxableBase (covered by Simplified/General Rule). Replace flat `grossRrb` taxable assumption.

**Gap 4 — Rollover formula starts from box 2a, not box 1 (Low)**
When `hasRollover == true`, use `gross1099R` (Box 1 sum) as the rollover reduction base instead of `taxable1099R` (Box 2a), per IRS Pub. 575. Re-add RRB taxable portion after reduction since rollovers only apply to 1099-R distributions.

**Gap 5 — Simplified Method prior-year recovery not applied (Low)**
In `computePensionTaxableViaSimplifiedMethod()`, read `priorYearTaxFreeRecoveryAmount`, compute `remainingBasis = max(0, cost - priorRecovery)`, use it for the monthly exclusion. When remainingBasis = 0, return gross (fully taxable).

**Gap 6 — Missing unit and E2E tests (Medium)**
Add 7 unit tests and 5 E2E test scenarios covering: Simplified Method, Simplified Method with prior recovery, fully-recovered basis, General Rule, RRB no-cost, MFJ two-person pension, distribution code "S" Form 5329. Plus E2E: PSO standalone, MFJ two-person, code-S Form 5329, Simplified Method compute, RRB-1099-R basic.

**Execution order:** Gap 5 → Gaps 3+4 together → Gap 2 → Gap 1 → Gap 6

## 2026-04-15 — Lines 4a/4b/4c — Form 8606 line 7 auto-computed from statements (PLAN)

### Problem
`totalTraditionalIraDistributionsFor8606Line7` was a user-entered field requiring the user to manually subtract
rollovers, QCDs, HFDs, conversions, recharacterizations, and inherited IRA distributions — arithmetic the app
already has all the inputs to perform.

### Solution
Derive Form 8606 Part I line 7 automatically from 1099-R statement data minus captured exclusion amounts.
User enters two new plain-fact fields only; the app does all arithmetic.

### Changes

**Backend (`TaxReturnComputeService.java`)**
1. Add `grossTraditionalIra` accumulator in `computeIraForPerson()` — box 1 sum for non-Roth-coded (no J/Q/T) entries only.
2. Pass `grossTraditionalIra` to `buildForm8606()` as new parameter.
3. In `buildForm8606()`, compute line 7 as:
   `line7 = grossTraditionalIra − rollover − fullQcd − hfd − conversion − returnOfContrib − inheritedTraditional`
   (all operands already in `iraForm` or the new parameter; use `subtractNonNegative` at each step)
4. Remove `getAmount(iraForm, "totalTraditionalIraDistributionsFor8606Line7")`.

**YAML (both `4abc-ira-income-taxpayer.yaml` and `4abc-ira-income-spouse.yaml`)**
- Remove `totalTraditionalIraDistributionsFor8606Line7` from `form8606PartIandPartIIInputs`.
- Add `returnOfContributionAmount` (type: amount) to `exceptionAmountsAndAttachmentFacts`.
- Add `inheritedTraditionalIraDistributionAmount` (type: amount) to `exceptionAmountsAndAttachmentFacts`.

**Angular (both `form-ira-income-taxpayer.component.ts` and `form-ira-income-spouse.component.ts`)**
- Remove `totalTraditionalIraDistributionsFor8606Line7` from template and `allFieldDefs`.
- Add `returnOfContributionAmount` field under `hadReturnOfContributionOrRecharacterization === true` gate.
- Add `inheritedTraditionalIraDistributionAmount` field (under `showUncommon`).
- Update `normalizeForSave()`: clear `returnOfContributionAmount` when boolean is false.

**Unit tests**
- Tests that set `totalTraditionalIraDistributionsFor8606Line7` → remove that key; align 1099-R gross data
  so `grossTraditionalIra − exclusions` produces the same line 7 as before (where possible), or update
  expected values where the test was using mismatched data.
- Add: `form8606Line7ComputedFromStatementsMinusExclusions` — rollover+conversion subtracted automatically.
- Add: `form8606Line7ReturnOfContributionExcluded` — `returnOfContributionAmount` reduces line 7.
- Add: `form8606Line7InheritedTraditionalExcluded` — `inheritedTraditionalIraDistributionAmount` reduces line 7.

## 2026-04-14 — Lines 3a/3b/3c — Spec Cleanup + Statement-Driven Tests

### Items addressed

**1. `lines/3abc.md` spec update**
- Sections 8.2 ("not yet implemented"), 9.2 ("no test coverage"), and 13 ("work items") rewritten to reflect implementation complete
- Section 5.3 nuance clarified: "Line 2a → parent line 3b" is technically the source, but the amount that flows is Form 8814 **line 9** (the qualified dividend proportion of income above $2,700), not the full line 2a — ordinary-only dividends without a qualified portion go to Schedule 1 line 8z via line 12, not to parent line 3b

**2. Statement-driven Form 8814 unit tests (7 new, `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`)**
1. `form8814StatementDriven_1099IntMatchingDependentSsn` — 1099-INT `recipientTIN` matches dependent → line 1a derived from `interestIncomeAmount`
2. `form8814StatementDriven_1099DivMatchingDependentSsn` — 1099-DIV → line 2a/2b/3 derived from statement fields
3. `form8814StatementDriven_multiple1099IntAggregate` — two 1099-INTs for same dependent → amounts aggregate into one Form 8814
4. `form8814StatementDriven_nonMatchingSsnProducesNoForm8814` — statement `recipientTIN` doesn't match any dependent → not attributed
5. `form8814StatementDriven_manualEntryOverridesStatementField` — dependent has both statement AND matching manual entry → manual non-null field overrides statement value
6. `form8814StatementDriven_manualEntryNoMatchingDependent_backwardCompat` — manual entry with unregistered SSN still generates Form 8814
7. `form8814StatementDriven_grossIncomeAboveLimitFromStatements` — child gross income ≥ $13,500 from statements → `FORM8814_CHILD_GROSS_INCOME_TOO_HIGH` flag emitted

**3. Statement-driven Form 8814 E2E tests (4 new, `line8814-statement-driven.spec.ts`)**
1. Upload child 1099-INT (dependent SSN as `recipientTIN`) → `form8814List[0].line1aTaxableInterest` derived from box 1
2. Upload child 1099-DIV → `line2aOrdinaryDividends`/`line2bQualifiedDividends`/`line3CapGainDistributions` match statement boxes
3. Manual `child-interest-dividends` entry for same dependent SSN → not double-counted (line 4 = statement amount only)
4. Manual entry with unregistered SSN → still flows to Form 8814 (backward compatibility)

## 2026-04-14 — Form 8814 — Statement-Driven Child Income (Implemented)

All four enhancements implemented. Backend 295/295 unit tests pass. Angular build clean.

**Files changed:** `TaxReturnComputeService.java`, `form-child-interest-dividends.component.ts`, `shell.component.ts`

---

## 2026-04-14 — Form 8814 — Detailed Plan: Statement-Driven Child Income

### Goal

The four enhancements:
1. Parent uploads the child's 1099-INT / 1099-DIV in the normal statement store (same flow as taxpayer/spouse)
2. Backend routes them to Form 8814 by matching `recipientTIN` against each dependent's SSN
3. The `child-interest-dividends` form auto-populates from those statements (same pattern as W-2 box 1 flows to line 1a)
4. Manual override fields remain for edge cases where the parent has amounts not on a statement

---

### Enhancement 1 — Dependent tab shows 1099-INT / 1099-DIV in Statements section

**Current behaviour**: The Statements sidebar section is the same for all person tabs — it shows whatever the user selected in the "Select statement" picker. 1099-INT and 1099-DIV already appear for taxpayer/spouse, but only because the user manually ticked them. The dependent tab shows the same selection, so if the user happened to pick 1099-INT for themselves, it also appears in the dependent tab.

**Target behaviour**: When the active tab is a dependent, the Statements section automatically includes 1099-INT and 1099-DIV without requiring the user to open the picker. These are the only statement types relevant to Form 8814 child income.

**Changes:**

`shell.component.ts` — `getMenuSections()` — when `selectedPerson.kind === 'dependent'`, append 1099-INT and 1099-DIV to `statementItems` if they are not already present:

```typescript
if (this.selectedPerson.kind === 'dependent') {
  const depStatementIds = new Set(statementItems.map(i => i.id));
  for (const id of ['1099-int', '1099-div']) {
    if (!depStatementIds.has(id)) {
      const form = this.statementSelection.allForms.find(f => f.id === id);
      if (form) statementItems.push({ id: form.id, label: form.label, description: form.description, icon: 'pi pi-file' });
    }
  }
}
```

No changes to `StatementFormCatalog.java` or `StatementSelectionService` — both already support 1099-INT and 1099-DIV.

---

### Enhancement 2 — Backend routes child 1099s to Form 8814

**Current behaviour**: `computeChildInterestDividends()` reads manually entered fields from `child-interest-dividends` statement entries only.

**Target behaviour**: `computeChildInterestDividends()` also receives `form1099IntEntries`, `form1099DivEntries`, and `dependents`. For each dependent, it filters entries by `recipientTIN == dependent.dependentSSN` and aggregates the statement amounts into Form 8814 lines. Manual `child-interest-dividends` entries remain as a fallback when no statements match a dependent.

**Field mappings** (field names from existing `computeInterestForPerson()` / `computeDividendForPerson()`):

| Statement | Statement field | Form 8814 line |
|---|---|---|
| 1099-INT | `interestIncomeAmount` (box 1) + `usSavingsBondsTreasuryInterestAmount` (box 3) − bond premiums (box 11/12) | Line 1a — taxable interest |
| 1099-INT | `taxExemptInterestAmount` (box 8) − `bondPremiumTaxExemptAmount` (box 13) | Line 1b — tax-exempt interest |
| 1099-DIV | `totalOrdinaryDividendsAmount` (box 1a) | Line 2a — ordinary dividends |
| 1099-DIV | `qualifiedDividendsAmount` (box 1b) | Line 2b — qualified dividends |
| 1099-DIV | `totalCapitalGainDistributionsAmount` (box 2a) | Line 3 — capital gain distributions |

**Routing logic in `computeChildInterestDividends()`:**

For each `DependentRecord dep` in `dependents`:
```java
String depSsn = normalizeSsn(dep.dependentSSN()); // note: capital N
if (depSsn == null) continue;
List<Map<String,Object>> depIntEntries = form1099IntEntries.stream()
    .filter(e -> depSsn.equals(normalizeSsn(getString(e, "recipientTIN"))))
    .toList();
List<Map<String,Object>> depDivEntries = form1099DivEntries.stream()
    .filter(e -> depSsn.equals(normalizeSsn(getString(e, "recipientTIN"))))
    .toList();
if (!depIntEntries.isEmpty() || !depDivEntries.isEmpty()) {
    // derive line1a/1b/2a/2b/3 from statements → build Form8814
}
```

Manual `child-interest-dividends` entries whose `childSsn` matches a dependent that already has statement entries are skipped to avoid double-counting. Manual entries with no matching dependent SSN (or no statement) still flow through as before.

**Call site (`prepare()`)**: pass `form1099IntEntries`, `form1099DivEntries`, and `dependents` into `computeChildInterestDividends()`.

---

### Enhancement 3 — `child-interest-dividends` form auto-populates from uploaded statements

This is the same pattern as W-2 box 1 flowing into the employment income form fields.

**Current behaviour**: `form-child-interest-dividends.component.ts` `ngOnInit` loads `child-interest-dividends` statement entries and populates fields from manually saved values.

**Target behaviour**: `ngOnInit` also calls `statementDataService.listEntries('1099-int')` and `statementDataService.listEntries('1099-div')`. For entries whose `recipientTIN` matches the child's SSN in the current `ChildEntry`, derive the Form 8814 amounts and pre-fill the corresponding fields. These pre-filled values are shown as a read-only "From statements" summary above each editable field.

**UI pattern** (per child card):
- Show a collapsible "From your statements" block listing the matched 1099-INT / 1099-DIV entries and their derived amounts
- The editable fields below show the same amounts pre-filled
- A note below the block: "These amounts were populated from uploaded statements. You can adjust them below if needed."
- When no matching statements exist, the form behaves as today (blank fields, manual entry)

**Data flow**: The component reads statement entries at load time, matches on `recipientTIN`, and merges the derived amounts into the `ChildEntry` model fields as defaults (only when the manual entry field is null, so existing saves are not overwritten).

---

### Enhancement 4 — Manual override fields remain

No structural change needed. The existing editable fields in the child card (`line1aTaxableInterest`, `line1bTaxExemptInterest`, `line2aOrdinaryDividends`, `line2bQualifiedDividends`, `line3CapGainDistributions`) remain fully editable. The auto-populated values are defaults; the user saves their own value if it differs.

The "Save" button writes to the `child-interest-dividends` Firestore entry as today. The backend `computeChildInterestDividends()` in Enhancement 2 treats a saved manual value as an override over the statement-derived value when both exist for the same field (add a manual adjustment field on the entry, or use the saved entry value directly when non-null).

---

### Tests

**Unit tests (new, `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`):**
1. Child 1099-INT `recipientTIN` matches dependent → `line1a` derived from `interestIncomeAmount`
2. Child 1099-DIV matches dependent → `line2a`/`line2b`/`line3` derived from statement fields
3. Two 1099-INTs for same dependent → amounts aggregate into one Form 8814
4. Statement `recipientTIN` does not match any dependent SSN → not attributed (no Form 8814)
5. Dependent has both matching statement AND manual `child-interest-dividends` entry → statement-derived values used; manual entry not double-counted
6. Manual `child-interest-dividends` entry with no matching dependent/statement → still generates Form 8814 (backward compatibility)
7. Child gross income ≥ $13,500 derived from statements → `FORM8814_CHILD_GROSS_INCOME_TOO_HIGH` flag still emitted

**E2E tests (new spec `line8814-statement-driven.spec.ts`):**
1. Upload child 1099-INT (with dependent's SSN as `recipientTIN`) → `form8814List[0].line1aTaxableInterest` matches statement box 1
2. Upload child 1099-DIV → `form8814List[0].line2a`/`line2b`/`line3` match statement boxes
3. Manual `child-interest-dividends` entry for same SSN → not double-counted in line 4
4. Manual entry for non-registered SSN → still flows to Form 8814

---

### Implementation Notes

- `DependentRecord.dependentSSN` — capital N (not `dependentSsn`)
- `normalizeSsn()` already exists and strips hyphens — use for all SSN comparisons
- One Form 8814 per dependent — aggregate all matching entries before building the form object
- `child-interest-dividends` in `StatementFormCatalog` and `StatementSelectionService` — keep as the manual fallback entry point
- Enhancement 3 (UI auto-populate) requires the component to call `listEntries` for both `1099-int` and `1099-div`, then iterate and match on `recipientTIN`; this is an additional two API calls on `ngOnInit`

### Status

Deferred — logged in `outstanding.md`.

---

## 2026-04-14 — Line 3c — Child Dividend Disclosure Checkboxes + UI Violation Fixes

### UI Violations Found in `form-child-interest-dividends.component.ts`

Evaluated against `ui.md` (Rules 10, 22, 25, 33, 34) and `turbotax.md` (Patterns 1, 3, 7, 8):

| # | Violation | Rule | Fix Applied |
|---|---|---|---|
| V1 | Labels used IRS line number format ("Line 1a — Taxable interest") | Rule #33: never ask user to reference form line numbers | Changed to plain-English: "Taxable interest (from 1099-INTs)" |
| V2 | No `inputId` attributes on `p-inputNumber` fields | Needed for E2E test targeting and accessibility | Added `[inputId]="'fieldName_' + i"` to all 5 amount fields |
| V3 | No `id` attributes on `pInputText` fields | Same reason | Added `[id]="'fieldName_' + i"` to name/SSN inputs |
| V4 | No help text on any field | Rule #10: every field must have both label and help | Added `<small class="help-text">` explaining each field's source |
| V5 | No cross-validation (qualified ≤ ordinary) | Rule #25: inline validation | Added `isQualifiedExceedsOrdinary()` guard — shows warning and disables Save |
| V6 | Section header "Part I — Child's income" is IRS jargon | Rule #34: frame around user's situation | Changed to "Income amounts" |
| V7 | Instructions referenced IRS line numbers ("lines 1a + 2a + 3") | Rule #33 | Rewritten to plain-English with IRS references moved to help text only |
| V8 | Card header shows "Child 1/2/..." regardless of name entry | TurboTax Pattern 4: contextualized titles | Card header now shows child's name when available |
| V9 | No `data-form-id` attribute on root element | Needed for Playwright sidebar navigation | Added `data-form-id="child-interest-dividends"` |

### Line 3c Implementation

**New for 2025.** Form 1040 line 3c — "Check if your child's dividends are included in 1 □ Line 3a  2 □ Line 3b" — requires both checkboxes to be checked when Form 8814 line 9 (child's qualified dividends) are included in the parent's return.

**Implementation plan (executed):**
1. `Income.java` — Added `line3cChildDividendsInLine3a` and `line3cChildDividendsInLine3b` Boolean fields with getters/setters
2. `TaxReturnComputeService.java`:
   - Moved `form8814List` computation before `computeInterestIncome()` to make Form 8814 results available to dividend computation
   - Added `form8814QualifiedDividendsTotal` aggregation (sum of `Form8814.getLine9QualifiedDividends()` across all children)
   - Threaded `form8814QualifiedDividendsTotal` through `computeInterestIncome()` → `computeDividendIncome()`
   - In `computeDividendIncome()`: added child qualified dividends to both line 3a and line 3b; set `line3cChildDividendsInLine3a` / `line3cChildDividendsInLine3b` booleans in `DividendComputation` record
   - Extended `DividendComputation` and `InterestComputation` records with 2 new Boolean fields
   - In `buildIncome()`: set the 2 new `Income` fields from `interest`
3. `f1040_field_mapping_semantic.csv` — Renamed `unmapped_c1_33_0` → `line3c_child_dividends_included_in_line3a` and `unmapped_c1_34_0` → `line3c_child_dividends_included_in_line3b`
4. `form-tax-return-1040.component.ts` — Added `line3cChildDividendsInLine3a` / `line3cChildDividendsInLine3b` to interface and `buildFieldValues()` mappings
5. `form-child-interest-dividends.component.ts` — Applied all 9 UI violation fixes listed above

**Tests:**
- 4 new unit tests in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java` (295 total, was 291): `form8814Line9QualifiedDividendsFlowToParentLine3aAnd3b`, `form8814NoQualifiedDividendsDoesNotSetLine3cCheckboxes`, `form8814QualifiedDividendsAggregateAcrossMultipleChildren`, `form8814ChildQualifiedDividendsAddedToParentExistingDividends`
- 3 new E2E tests in `line8814-child-interest-dividends.spec.ts` (9 total, was 6): child qualified dividends → line 3c set; no qualified dividends → line 3c null; two children aggregate

### Screening Gate + PDF Verification (same session)

**Screening gate** added to `form-child-interest-dividends.component.ts` (ui.md Rule #1):
- Three UI states: `electionMade === null` → show screening question; `false` → no-election message with "Change answer" button; `true` → full entry list
- `loadEntries()` auto-sets `electionMade = true` when existing entries are present (non-destructive for existing data)
- New CSS classes: `.screening-panel`, `.no-election-panel`, `.screening-question`, `.radio-group`, `.radio-option`

**PDF verification**: Confirmed via pdf-lib inspection of `docs/IRS-Forms/f1040.pdf` that fields `c1_33` and `c1_34` at coordinates (283.8, 187.501) and (363, 187.501) are the line 3c checkboxes. Position verified by being between line 3a/3b text (y≈198) and line 4c checkboxes (y≈163.5). CSV field name rename in `f1040_field_mapping_semantic.csv` is correct.

**New E2E tests** (4 UI tests added to `line8814-child-interest-dividends.spec.ts`, 13 total):
1. Screening gate shown when no entries exist
2. Selecting No shows no-election message + Change answer button works
3. Selecting Yes reveals entry list and Add child button
4. Screening gate bypassed when entries already exist (loads directly to entry list)

Files changed: `Income.java`, `TaxReturnComputeService.java`, `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`, `f1040_field_mapping_semantic.csv`, `form-tax-return-1040.component.ts`, `form-child-interest-dividends.component.ts`, `line8814-child-interest-dividends.spec.ts`, `lines/3abc.md` (spec renamed), `CLAUDE.md`.

## 2026-04-13 — Lines 2a/2b — Fix 7 UI/UX Gaps in Interest Income Forms

**Applied 7 UI/UX fixes to the interest income forms (`interest-income-taxpayer`, `interest-income-spouse`):**

- **Gap 1 (Statement-first):** Removed `received1099Int`, `uploaded1099Int`, `received1099Div`, `uploaded1099Div`, `received1099Oid`, `uploaded1099Oid` fields from taxpayer YAML and Angular component — these re-asked questions the system already knows from the statement store. Simplified `statementUploadCheck` to a single `confirmAllReceivedInterestStatementsUploaded` confirmation. `hasUploadedAtLeastOneInterestStatement` is now auto-derived from the statement store in `ngOnInit`/`onSubmit`.
- **Gap 2 (Derived amounts display):** Added read-only "Amounts from your statements" summary block in both taxpayer and spouse components. Shows which statement types have uploaded entries before the supplemental fields. Includes guidance: "If any amount looks incorrect, update the statement in the Statements section of the sidebar." Required adding `StatementDataService` import to spouse component.
- **Gap 3 (YAML convention fix):** Fixed `hasFbarRequirement` in taxpayer YAML: changed `id:` to `name:`, `type: radio` to `type: boolean`, added `required: false` and `help:` text.
- **Gap 4 (Expansion gate):** Added `hasInterestAdjustments` boolean gate to both taxpayer and spouse YAMLs and Angular components. Adjustment fields (`accruedInterestPaidAdjustment`, `nomineeInterestAdjustment`, all bond premium fields) are now hidden until the user answers Yes. `normalizeForSave()` clears adjustment fields when gate is false.
- **Gap 5 (Foreign conditions hierarchy):** Split `scheduleBConditions` into `scheduleBConditions` (moderate-frequency) and `scheduleBForeignConditions` (rare, behind `hasForeignFinancialSituation` gate) in taxpayer YAML and Angular component. `hasForeignFinancialSituation` and `hasForeignAccountForScheduleBPartIII` added to model and template. `normalizeForSave()` propagates gate clearing.
- **Gap 6 (Label framing):** Fixed 8 labels across both YAMLs: `hasForeignAccountForScheduleBPartIII`, `hasForeignTrustDistributionOrTransfer`, `taxExemptStatedInterestFrom1099OidBox2`, `taxablePortionFrom1099OidBox2Override`, `savingsBondExclusionAmount`, `accruedInterestPaidAdjustment`, `nomineeInterestAdjustment` — replaced IRS-mechanic framing with real-world situational language. Line references moved from labels to help text.
- **Gap 7 (Intent comment):** Added YAML comment above `hadInterestIncome` in spouse YAML explaining why it is kept as an explicit question rather than auto-derived.

**E2E test updates (`line2ab-interest-income.spec.ts`):**
- Removed all `received1099Int/Div/Oid` and `uploaded1099Int/Div/Oid` UI interactions (fields removed from form)
- Added `selectOption(page, 'hasForeignFinancialSituation', 'Yes')` before `hasForeignAccountForScheduleBPartIII` in the main flow test

## 2026-04-13 - Lines 2a/2b — Fix 6 Gaps in Interest Income Computation

**Resolved all 6 identified gaps in the Form 1040 Lines 2a/2b (interest income) implementation:**

- **Gap 1 (Bug):** Fixed box 9 double-counted into line 2a. Changed `addNonNull(box8, box9) - box13` to `box8 - box13` in `computeInterestForPerson()`. Box 9 is a subset of box 8. Updated unit test assertion (284→244) and E2E assertion (157→127).
- **Gap 2 (Bug):** Fixed FBAR required flag always equalling the foreign account flag. Added `hasFbarRequirement` boolean field to YAML, Angular component (`form-interest-income-taxpayer.component.ts`), and backend. `InterestComputation.scheduleBFbarRequired` now reads from the new independent field. Updated existing `requiresScheduleBForForeignAccountEvenBelowThreshold` unit test to pass `hasFbarRequirement: true`.
- **Gap 3 (Bug):** Nominee interest now independently triggers Schedule B. Added `boolean hasNomineeInterest` to `InterestPersonTotals` record; added `|| hasNomineeInterest` to `scheduleBRequired` chain.
- **Gap 4 (Feature):** 1099-DIV box 13 (`specifiedPrivateActivityBondDividendsAmount`) now feeds Form 6251 line 2g. Added `pabDividends` to `DividendPersonTotals`, `form6251Line2gDividends` to `DividendComputation`, and wired into `computeInterestIncome()`.
- **Gap 5 (Feature):** Schedule B Part II now populated with per-payer dividend items. Added `dividendItems` field to `ScheduleB.java`, added `List<ScheduleBInterestItem> scheduleBDividendItems` through `DividendPersonTotals` → `DividendComputation` → `InterestComputation` → `buildScheduleB()`. Frontend `ScheduleBView` and `buildFieldValues()` render up to 14 dividend rows.
- **Gap 6 (UX):** Added Form 8815 hint text in savings bond exclusion section of `form-interest-income-taxpayer.component.ts` directing users to IRS Pub 550 worksheet.

**New unit tests (5):** `box9IsNotDoubleCountedIntoLine2a`, `nomineeInterestBelowThresholdTriggersScheduleB`, `fbarFlagIsIndependentOfForeignAccountFlag`, `dividendBox13FlowsToForm6251Line2g`, `scheduleBDividendItemsArePopulated`.

**New E2E tests (3):** nominee interest below $1,500 triggers Schedule B; FBAR flag independent of foreign account flag; 1099-DIV box 13 flows to Form 6251 line 2g.

Files changed: `TaxReturnComputeService.java`, `ScheduleB.java`, `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`, `form-interest-income-taxpayer.component.ts`, `form-tax-return-scheduleb.component.ts`, `line2ab-interest-income.spec.ts`, `2ab-interest-income-taxpayer.yaml`.

## 2026-04-12 - Tax Return Form Previews — Image-Based Read-Only Preview with PDF Export

**Created `PdfReadonlyPreviewComponent`** (`us-tax-ui/src/app/components/pdf-readonly-preview.component.ts`):
- Generic standalone Angular component for displaying IRS tax return forms as read-only image overlays
- Supports 6 different CSV formats auto-detected from header: 5-col explicit, 6-col auto-naming, f1040 7-col, f1040s1/s3 6-col, 8-col separate-rect, 9-col with field_type
- Renders page images from `/irs-images/{formId}_semantic_labels/` with absolutely-positioned `<span>` overlays for data values
- "Save as PDF" button loads template PDF, parses CSV, draws text/checkmarks at rect coordinates using pdf-lib
- Inputs: `formId`, `pageCount`, `fieldValues: Record<string, string|boolean|null|undefined>`, `pdfFileName`, `hideSavePdfButton`
- Secondary lookup by `old_field_name` (for 6-col auto-naming forms) so form components can use original IRS field names as keys

**Updated all 41 Tax Return section form components** to replace HTML grid/table layout with `<app-pdf-readonly-preview>`:
- All components now show the actual IRS form image as background with data overlaid in correct field positions
- Removed redundant per-component PDF boilerplate (parseCsv, parseRect, applyPdfFieldValues, etc.) — now in generic component
- Forms with explicit semantic names: scheduleb, f6251, f8606, f4972 — full `buildFieldValues()` mapping
- Forms with opaque auto-naming CSVs: minimal fieldValues (name/SSN) — detailed mapping can be added per form
- 17 additional form components added to `all-components.spec.ts` smoke test suite

**IRS page images** (`us-tax-ui/public/irs-images/`): 44 subdirectories with 1–4 page PNGs per form at 1275×1650 px (150 DPI)

## 2026-04-12 - Line 1h PDF Export — Inline Field + Rule 44

**`form-tax-return-1040.component.ts`:**
- Extracted `resolveStatementInlineText(stmt, font, spec)` → `{ inlineText, appendPage }` private method
- Save flow now performs a fit-check at 6pt minimum font size against the `line1h_statement_text` field rect (width ≈ 128 pts after padding)
- If description fits inline: writes it directly to `line1h_statement_text`; no separate page appended
- If description overflows: writes `"Refer to attached sheet"` to `line1h_statement_text`; appends statement page via `appendLine1hStatementPage()`
- Previously the inline field was always left blank

**`ui.md`:**
- Added Rule 44: write-in description field overflow pattern (fit-check at 6pt; inline if fits, "Refer to attached sheet" + separate page if not)

**`form-tax-return-1040.component.spec.ts`** (new):
- 3 unit tests for `resolveStatementInlineText`: fits inline, overflows, spec undefined fallback
- Uses proportional font stub — no pdf-lib dependency in tests

**Manually verified:** three-category return (excess deferrals + disability pension + strike benefits) produces `"Refer to attached sheet"` in the inline field and full description on the appended statement page.

## 2026-04-11 - Line 1e: 5 Outstanding Gaps Implemented

**Item 1 — L12 box-1 deduction** (`alreadyIncludedInBox1Amount`):
- New optional field in `dependentCareBenefits` section. Backend: `line12 = max(0, totalBenefits − alreadyInBox1)`. Allows amounts already taxed in W-2 box 1 to be excluded from line 12.

**Item 2 — L21 plan maximum** (`planMaxExcludable`):
- New optional field. Backend: `line21 = min(statutoryCap, planMaxExcludable)` when field is set. Renamed local `line21` to `statutoryCap` first for clarity.

**Item 3 — Lines 18/19 non-W-2 earned income** (`additionalEarnedIncomeTaxpayer`, `additionalEarnedIncomeSpouse`):
- Two new optional fields added after W-2 SSN attribution and before deemed-income maximums. Added to `safeAmount()`-converted earned income values.

**Item 4 — Form 2441 Part II two-pass finalization**:
- New `finalizeForm2441PartII()` method called after `applyForeignTaxCreditToSchedule3()` and before `applyForm8880ToSchedule3()`. Computes lines 7 (AGI), 8 (applicable %), 9a/9c, 10 (CLW = totalTaxBeforeCredits − prior Schedule 3 nonrefundable credits), 11 (min(9c, 10)). Moved `applyChildDependentCareCredit()` call from early (line 573) to after `finalizeForm2441PartII()`.

**Item 5 — §129(d) nondiscrimination** (`employerPlanFailed129dTest`):
- New boolean field. When true: `line25 = 0`, `line26 = line15` (full amount taxable on line 1e).

**Files changed:** `TaxReturnComputeService.java` (backend logic + new method), `Form2441.java` (no changes — all existing fields), `1e-childcare-expenses.yaml` (5 new fields), `form-childcare-expenses.component.ts` (interface + model init + normalizeForSave + helpMap), `form-childcare-expenses.component.html` (new input rows), `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java` (5 new unit tests), `line1e-dependent-care.spec.ts` (4 new E2E tests, 1 existing assertion updated from `toBeNull` to `toBe(0)`, `computeReturnApi` import added, `retries: 1`).

**Total tests:** 5 new unit tests (backend); 4 new E2E tests (12 total in spec).

## 2026-04-11 - Line 1e: Gap 5 (L13 rename) + Gap 6 (DCB label)

**Gap 5 — L13 field name clarified (8 files):**
- Renamed `line13CarryoverFromPriorYearUsed` → `line13GracePeriodBenefitsUsed` everywhere. The old name implied a true fund carryover; dependent care FSAs only allow a 2½-month grace period — amounts not used by end of grace period are forfeited.
- Also renamed output model field `line13CarryoverFrom2023Used2024` → `line13GracePeriodBenefitsUsed` in `Form2441.java` (stale 2023/2024 year reference replaced).
- Files changed: `1e-childcare-expenses.yaml`, `TaxReturnComputeService.java` (input key + setter), `Form2441.java` (field + getter/setter), `form-childcare-expenses.component.ts` (interface + model + normalize + helpMap), `form-childcare-expenses.component.html` (inputId + label + ngModel), `form-tax-return-2441.component.ts` (interface + template label + PDF key), `f2441_field_map_semantic.csv` (both `C:\us-tax\pdfs\` and `us-tax-ui\public\irs\`), `line1e-dependent-care.spec.ts`, `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`.
- Help text updated to accurately describe the grace-period-only rule.

**Gap 6 — DCB label added to Form 1040 income section:**
- Added a `*ngIf` note row below line 1e in `form-tax-return-1040.component.html` showing "DCB" when `dependentCareBenefits` is nonzero. Matches the existing line 1h note row pattern.
- PDF: no separate label field exists in the f1040 PDF template — the pre-printed "Taxable dependent care benefits from Form 2441, line 26" text on the form satisfies the filing requirement. No PDF export change needed.
- E2E: `line1e-dependent-care.spec.ts` basic test now asserts `.income-row--note` with text "DCB" is visible when line 1e > 0.

**Build verification:** Backend unit tests pass (3/3); Angular build clean.

## 2026-04-11 - Line 1e: MFS Separation Exception Fix + Spec Validation

**Spec validation (`C:\us-tax\lines\1e.md`):**
- Cross-referenced 2025 IRS Form 2441 instructions and J.K. Lasser against the existing spec.
- Added §2.3 qualifying person definition (child under 13; incapable-of-self-care spouse/dependent).
- Clarified §3.2 DCFSA grace period (2½ months only; no true fund carryover).
- Added disabled/full-time student spouse deemed income rule ($250/month × 1 person, $500/month × 2+) in §5.2.
- Rewrote §7.3 MFS: ordinary MFS = $2,500 cap, no credit; separation exception (lived apart last 6 months + household tests) = treated as unmarried, $5,000 cap, credit available.
- Added §8.3 "DCB" label requirement, §8.4 nondiscrimination caveat.
- Added test scenarios 11–15 and expanded rule map (§11).

**Backend fix — `TaxReturnComputeService.java` line 8187:**
- Bug: `marriedFilingSeparatelyException` was stored on `Form2441` but never used in the `line21` cap decision; MFS separation exception always applied the $2,500 cap instead of $5,000.
- Fix: Added `mfsException` boolean check; `line21 = (isMfs && !mfsException) ? $2,500 : $5,000`.

**E2E fix — `line1e-dependent-care.spec.ts`:**
- Bug: MFS test used `employeeSSN: '333-33-3333'` (not the taxpayer SSN); W-2 wages were not attributed for earned-income limiting.
- Fix: Changed to `'111-11-1111'`.
- New test added: `'MFS with separation exception applies 5000 cap'` — seeds `marriedFilingSeparatelyException: true` via API, asserts `line21PlanLimit = 5000` and `line1e = 0`.

**Unit test — `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java`:**
- New test: `computesMfsWithSeparationExceptionUsesFiveThousandCap()` — asserts `line21 = 5000`, `line26 = 0`, `dependentCareBenefits = 0` when exception applies.
- All 3 dependent care unit tests pass.

## 2026-04-11 - UI Unit-Test Repair: TypeScript Drift vs Runtime Drift

- Resolved a UI test-bundle cleanup round where the initial failures looked like TypeScript issues, but the real problem split into two categories:
  - stale type contracts in specs and service APIs;
  - stale runtime assertions in specs that no longer matched the actual Angular components.
- `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\src\app\service\personal-data.service.ts` was updated to restore compatibility helpers and alias normalization at the service boundary instead of forcing unrelated product changes:
  - added `getTaxpayerHeader()`;
  - added spouse fallback normalization from legacy `spouse` payloads to split-form keys;
  - normalized dependent payloads so canonical YAML keys and legacy aliases coexist.
- `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\src\app\service\statement-selection.service.ts` was updated to restore the richer contract expected by the specs and sidebar:
  - added `allSections`;
  - returned richer labels from `getLabel()` in the form `Form X - Title`;
  - preserved grouped sections and added the missing `child-interest-dividends` statement option.
- Key testing lesson: fixing TypeScript errors alone is not enough. After the test bundle compiled cleanly, the remaining failures were caused by spec drift:
  - `form-dependent.component.spec.ts` still expected grouped YAML headings and masked SSN/DOB behavior that the current `form-dependent.component.ts` does not implement;
  - the create-path test reused a component instance whose internal `currentId` was still set, so the test accidentally exercised update logic;
  - direct DOM value assertions were brittle for this form and were replaced with assertions on component model state where appropriate.
- Angular HTTP testing lesson: when a service builds auth headers asynchronously, `HttpTestingController.expectOne(...)` can race the request creation. The stable pattern is to wait until the request exists before asserting on it, rather than assuming it is present immediately after calling the async service method.
- Validation result:
  - `npm run build` passed in `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui`;
  - `npx tsc -p tsconfig.spec.json --noEmit` passed;
  - the targeted Angular specs passed after updating stale assertions and request timing.
- Rule reinforced: prefer compatibility fixes at the service boundary and narrowly scoped spec corrections over changing working UI behavior just to satisfy an outdated test.

## 2026-04-11 — Line 1d Medicaid Waiver Payments: Spec Validation, Flowchart, Dependency Doc, UI Fixes

### Spec validation and enhancement (`C:\us-tax\lines\1d.md`)
- Validated spec against IRS 2025 instructions and J.K. Lasser; found missing 2024/2025 reporting change (W-2 box 12 code II as primary/required reporting vehicle for nontaxable payments)
- Added §1.1 "What's New for 2025" covering code II elevation
- Added §2.3 Common reporting sources (updated to lead with code II)
- Added §4.1 Related-party providers (exclusion applies even when provider is related to recipient)
- Added §4.2 IRC §131(c) per-individual cap (>10 under 19 / >5 age 19+)
- Added §4.3 IRA contribution eligibility from excluded payments
- Added test scenarios 10–15 and rule map items 8–10

### Outstanding.md
- Logged IRC §131(c) per-individual cap as deferred item (practically never triggered in 1915(c) HCBS waiver context; would require 2 YAML fields + backend validation + edge case logic)

### Flowchart (`C:\us-tax\flowcharts\1d.drawio`)
- Created computation decision-tree flowchart matching 1c.drawio conventions
- 18 nodes: START → 3 early-exit gates → read flags/entries → FOR EACH loop (W-2 branch right, validate+accumulate center) → END LOOP → business gate → line1d base → election gate (YES branch: election logic + flags) → return → aggregation → downstream

### Dependency document (`C:\us-tax\dependencies\1d.md`)
- Created covering: output table, Output 1 (line 1d) formula + always-required inputs + form-level inputs + per-entry inputs + W-2 cross-reference, Output 2 (line 8s) formula + downstream effect on Schedule 1 totals, earned-income election mechanics table, 7 blocking flags, absence/missing-data behavior, cross-line consistency notes, deferred items

### YAML files
- `1d-medicaid-waiver-payments.yaml` → renamed to `1d-medicaid-waiver-payments-taxpayer.yaml`; updated `name:` to `medicaid-waiver-payments-taxpayer` and `title:` to "Medicaid waiver payments (you)"
- Created `1d-medicaid-waiver-payments-spouse.yaml` with spouse-specific labels and same field structure

### UI: `form-medicaid-waiver-taxpayer.component.ts` and `form-medicaid-waiver-spouse.component.ts`
**Boolean fields converted from `p-select` to radio buttons (UI Rule 7):**
- `receivedMedicaidWaiverPayments`
- `includeQualifiedPaymentsInEarnedIncomeForEIC_ACTC`
- `hasTradeOrBusinessProvidingHomeCare`
- `livesWithCareRecipient`

**Election label reframed (UI Rule 34):** "Do you want these payments to count toward the Earned Income Credit or Child Tax Credit?" (was tax-mechanic jargon)

**Schedule C strong blocker (UI Rule 27):** Replaced passive `info-note` with amber `schedule-c-blocker` banner; payment entries section and Add payer button now hidden when `hasTradeOrBusinessProvidingHomeCare === true`; `isValid()` bypasses entry validation when business == true

**Qualification facts behind toggle (UI Rule 13):** "Qualification facts" section moved behind a "Show qualification details" expand/collapse button; hidden entirely when business == true

- Replaced `selectOptionWithRetry` with `selectRadioOption` for all boolean fields

## 2026-04-09 — UI Rules Update + 3 Employment Form Design Issues Fixed

**`C:/us-tax/ui.md`** — Added Rules 32–34:
- Rule 32: Guide users proactively to forms they may not know exist (e.g., Form 4852 when W-2 missing)
- Rule 33: Never ask the user to cross-reference another tax form by name/line number in an interview question
- Rule 34: Frame questions around the user's real-world situation, not tax form mechanics

**`C:/us-tax/issues.md`** — Created; documents all three issues with root cause and resolution.

**Issue 1 — Form 4852 discoverability** (`employment_income_taxpayer.yaml`, `employment_income_spouse.yaml`, `form-employment-taxpayer.component.ts`, `form-employment-spouse.component.ts`):
- Added `instructions` to `statementUploadCheck` YAML section explaining Form 4852
- Added blue info banner in Angular templates when `submittedW2 === false`: guides user to Form 4852 under Applications
- New E2E assertion: guidance banner appears and mentions "Applications"

**Issue 2 — `reportedOnForm8919` question removed** (both YAMLs, both Angular components, `TaxReturnComputeService.java`, unit tests, E2E):
- Removed `reportedOnForm8919` field from both employment income YAMLs and Angular `HouseholdEmployerEntry` interface
- Removed collision guard block from `computeLine1b()` in backend
- Removed 2 unit tests (`line1b_excludesEmployerFullyReportedOnForm8919`, `line1b_partialExclusionWhenOneEmployerOnForm8919`)
- Replaced E2E collision-guard test with Form 4852 guidance test
- Unit test count: 268 (was 270)

**Issue 3 — Inmate wages question reframed** (`form-w-2.component.ts`):
- Changed `includesInmateWages` label from "Does any part of Box 1 represent wages earned while incarcerated in a penal institution?" → "Was this employer a prison, jail, or correctional facility?"
- Updated help text and title to match situational framing

**Collapsible sidebar sections (`shell.component.ts`, `shell.component.scss`):**

- `toggleSection()` / `isSectionCollapsed()` methods added; search filter auto-expands all sections

- **Rule 8**: `wages` field placeholder changed from description text to `"0.00"`

- **Rule 30**: `required: true` only on `hasEmploymentIncome`; remaining fields correctly `required: false`

*Angular (`form-employment-taxpayer.component.ts`, `form-employment-spouse.component.ts`):*

- **Rule 10**: Help icons and `helpMap` entries added to all three fields inside the `householdEmployers` repeatable rows (`employerName`, `wages`, `reportedOnForm8919`)
- **Rule 25**: `formDirty` flag introduced; set on first radio click (`onFieldChange()`); validation errors shown when `employmentForm.submitted || formDirty` — live feedback as user progresses
- **Rule 27/28**: Out-of-scope warning block (`out-of-scope-warning`) replaces soft hint when `householdEmployeeUnderControlTest === false`; explicitly states Schedule C is not supported

Added 5 new E2E tests covering previously untested Form 8919 scenarios. Updated spec and diagram.

Now includes: `hasEmploymentIncome` gate, `householdWork` gate, control-test failure → blocking flag + Schedule C redirect, W-2 received → line 1a redirect, MFS → spouse skip branch, per-employer `reportedOnForm8919` collision guard, wage accumulation, and output nodes.
