## 2026-04-09 — Line 1b / Form 8919 Remaining Gap Fixes

Fixed MFS aggregation bug, PDF page duplication, and added full test coverage.

**MFS aggregation bug (backend)**
- Added `filingStatus` parameter to `buildIncome()`; derived `isMfsReturn` and passed to `sumHouseholdEmployeeWages()`.
- `sumHouseholdEmployeeWages()` now accepts `boolean isMfs`; when true, skips spouse employment data so spouse household wages don't roll into the taxpayer's line 1b.
- Also fixed the same call in the SS worksheet computation at line ~6808.

**PDF multi-page: privacy notice duplication fix**
- `buildMultiPagePdf()` in both Form 8919 display components now copies only page 0 for overflow groups (`g > 0`), avoiding the privacy/instructions page being repeated for each group of firms.

**4 new unit tests (263 total, all passing)**
- `line1b_excludesEmployerFullyReportedOnForm8919` — `reportedOnForm8919=true` → null
- `line1b_partialExclusionWhenOneEmployerOnForm8919` — mixed employers → partial sum
- `line1b_mfsSpouseWagesNotAggregatedToTaxpayerReturn` — MFS → taxpayer wages only
- `form8919_sixFirmsAllWagesSummedToLine6` — 6 firms → correct line 6 total

**1 new E2E test**
- `line1b-household.spec.ts`: collision guard scenario — employer with `reportedOnForm8919=true` excluded; only the unflagged employer's wages ($150) appear on line 1b.

## 2026-04-09 — Form 8919 Gap Fixes + Line 1b Spec Update

Fixed all four identified gaps in the Form 8919 / line 1b implementation.

**Gap 1 — Line 1b collision guard (code)**
- Added `reportedOnForm8919` per-employer boolean field to `HouseholdEmployerEntry` interface in both `form-employment-taxpayer.component.ts` and `form-employment-spouse.component.ts`.
- Added a per-employer skip in `householdEmployeeAmount()` (`TaxReturnComputeService.java`): if `reportedOnForm8919 == true`, the employer's wages are excluded from line 1b to prevent double-counting with Form 8919 line 1g.
- UI shows an inline hint ("These wages will be excluded from line 1b…") when the user sets the flag to Yes.

**Gap 2 — PDF export multi-page rule for >5 firms (code)**
- Replaced `buildPdfFieldValues()` + single `PDFDocument.load` with `buildMultiPagePdf()` in both Form 8919 display components.
- Firms are split into groups of 5. Each group generates one copy of the template. Lines 6–13 are filled only on the first copy (IRS multi-page rule). All copies are merged via `pdf-lib` `copyPages`.

**Gap 3 & 4 — `lines/1b.md` spec documentation (docs)**
- Added new subsection "Prerequisites / outer gates" documenting `hasEmploymentIncome` and `householdWork` as UI-layer gates (not IRS rules).
- Added blocking flags table: `HOUSEHOLD_WORK_SELF_EMPLOYMENT_TAXPAYER` / `_SPOUSE`.

All 259 backend unit tests pass. Angular build clean.

## 2026-04-08 — Form 8919 Updated for Tax Year 2025

Updated Form 8919 (Uncollected Social Security and Medicare Tax on Wages) across all layers for the 2025 tax year.

**Root cause:** The Social Security wage base increased from $168,600 (2024) to $176,100 (2025). `ReferenceData.SOCIAL_SECURITY_WAGE_BASE` held the stale 2024 value, causing Form 8919 line 7 and Form 4137 line 7 to compute against the wrong annual cap.

**Changes made:**

1. **`ReferenceData.java`** — `SOCIAL_SECURITY_WAGE_BASE` updated from `"168600"` to `"176100"`. Automatically fixes Form 8919 line 7, line 9 (remaining base), line 10 (capped wages), and line 11 (SS tax), as well as Form 4137 line 7 (which uses the same constant). Note: the excess SS cap in `computeExcessSocialSecurityTax()` was already hardcoded to the correct 2025 value ($10,918.20 = $176,100 × 6.2%) and required no change.

2. **`form-tax-return-8919-taxpayer.component.ts`** — Default PDF export filename updated from `Form-8919-2024.pdf` to `Form-8919-2025.pdf`.

3. **`form-tax-return-8919-spouse.component.ts`** — Same default filename update.

4. **`lines/1g.md`** — Year reference updated from `# 2024 = 168,600` to `# 2025 = 176,100`.

5. **`lines/8919.md`** — New authoritative line spec created covering all Form 8919 rules, computations, reason codes, per-spouse multiplicity, Form 8959 integration, blocking flags, compute order, and 2025 constants. Replaces the informal `1g.md` as the primary Form 8919 developer reference.

6. **Semantic PDF and CSV assets regenerated** — `f8919_semantic_labels.pdf` and `f8919_field_map_semantic.csv` regenerated from the new 2025 IRS Form 8919 PDF. Published to `C:/us-tax/pdfs/` and `C:/us-tax/us-tax-ui/public/irs/`. New generate script: `scripts/generate-semantic-f8919.js`. Images regenerated in `C:/us-tax/images/f8919_semantic_labels/` (2 pages).

**Tests:** All 380 backend unit tests pass (0 failures). The Form 8919 assertion values in existing tests (line 13 = $765, additional Medicare tax = $90, excess SS = $242) are all unaffected because the test input wages are well below the base change delta ($7,500).

---

## 2026-04-07 — Line 1a: W-2 Carve-Out Gap Fix (NQDC box 11, inmate wages, statutory employee)

Identified and fixed three correctness gaps in the line 1a computation:

1. **W-2 box 11 (nonqualifiedPlansAmount) was not subtracted from line 1a.** IRS W-2 instructions confirm box 11 amounts are already included in box 1; they must flow to Schedule 1 line 8t, not remain in line 1a.
2. **Statutory-employee W-2s were silently included in line 1a.** The `statutoryEmployee` flag stored on W-2 entries was never read during compute.
3. **W-2-sourced inmate wages were not excluded from line 1a.** The `inmateWagesAmount` field was stored but never applied.

### Backend changes (`TaxReturnComputeService.java`)
- Added `computeLine1aWages()` — carve-out-aware aggregation replacing the plain `sumW2Wages()` call in `buildIncome()`. Excludes statutory-employee W-2s (raises `STATUTORY_EMPLOYEE_W2_OUT_OF_SCOPE` flag), subtracts box 11 NQDC, subtracts `inmateWagesAmount`; uses `subtractNonNegative()` to floor at zero.
- Extended `computeOtherIncomes()` to accept `w2Entries` and merge W-2-sourced 8t/8u amounts with Other incomes form data for Schedule 1 line 8t/8u — eliminates double-counting in total income (line 9).
- Extended `OtherIncomeComputation` record with `line8tNqdc` and `line8uInmate` fields.
- Added `sumW2Box11Nqdc()` and `sumW2InmateWages()` helpers.
- Updated `hasAnySchedule1Input` guard to trigger Schedule 1 generation when W-2 carve-outs exist without Other incomes form data.

### Frontend changes (`form-w-2.component.ts`)
- Added **Uncommon W-2 situations** section (TurboTax entity-centric design): Yes/No screening gate, inmate wages sub-question with amount input, statutory-employee info note.
- New fields: `hasUncommonW2Situations`, `includesInmateWages`, `inmateWagesAmount`.

### Tests
- 5 new unit tests in `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest` (257 total): box-11 subtraction, inmate-wages subtraction, statutory-employee exclusion + flag, combined MFJ carve-outs, clamp-to-zero.
- 8 new E2E scenarios in `e2e/tests/line1a-carveouts.spec.ts`: happy path single, MFJ multi-W-2, box-11 no double-count, inmate wages no double-count, statutory-employee flag, combined carve-outs, clamp-to-zero, W-2+Other-incomes aggregation.

### Build
- Angular build clean; all 257 unit tests pass.

## 2026-04-05 — Statement Forms: Add Meaningful Placeholders to All Fields

Added `placeholder` attributes to every `<input>` and `<p-inputNumber>` field across all 35 statement form components that were missing them.

### Files changed
**34 form components updated** (form-1099-da already had complete placeholders):
`form-w-2`, `form-w-2g`, `form-1099-a`, `form-1099-b`, `form-1099-c`, `form-1099-cap`, `form-1099-div`, `form-1099-e`, `form-1099-g`, `form-1099-int`, `form-1099-k`, `form-1099-ltc`, `form-1099-misc`, `form-1099-nec`, `form-1099-oid`, `form-1099-ptr`, `form-1099-q`, `form-1099-qa`, `form-1099-r`, `form-1099-s`, `form-1099-sa`, `form-1098-t`, `form-1095-a`, `form-1095-b`, `form-1095-c`, `form-3921`, `form-5498`, `form-8606`, `form-ssa-1099`, `form-rrb-1099`, `form-rrb-1099-r`, `form-schedule-k1-1041`, `form-schedule-k1-1065`, `form-schedule-k1-1120s`

### Placeholder conventions used
- Dollar amounts: `e.g. 50000.00` (wages), `e.g. 1000.00` (generic interest/gains), contextual amounts per field
- EIN: `12-3456789` | SSN/TIN: `123-45-6789`
- Payer/company name: `e.g. Acme Corp` | Person name: `e.g. John Smith`
- Dates: `YYYY-MM-DD` | Year fields: `2025`
- Address: `e.g. 123 Main St` | State: `e.g. NY`
- Account numbers: `e.g. ACCT-123456`
- No "Box 1" / "Box 2" style placeholders used anywhere

### Also fixed (same session)
**E2E `statement-form-picker.spec.ts`** — Fixed flaky test "Select form groups statements by type and shows form numbers with titles". `setSelected()` in `statements-select.page.ts` was using `checkbox.click({ force: true })` which bypassed actionability checks without scrolling, and then polling `localStorage` for confirmation which raced against a "Saving data..." overlay blocking `networkidle`. Fix: replaced with `checkbox.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded()` + `checkbox.check()` / `checkbox.uncheck()`, and replaced the localStorage poll with a sidebar link visibility check (`expect(sidebarLink).toBeVisible()`).

**E2E `dob-required.spec.ts`** — Fixed two failures: (1) "Date of birth is required." error not visible — `click({ force: true })` on DOB field didn't reliably fire Angular's blur/touched; replaced with `focus()` + `focus()` on adjacent field. (2) Save button disabled after filling all fields — `inputTypeFor()` toggling `type` between `text`/`password` desyncs Angular NgModel during Playwright fills; replaced UI Save with `savePersonalFormApi` + `seedSpouseApi` API-first calls.

**E2E `personal-per-person-forms.spec.ts`** — Two fixes: (1) `.dependents-extra` divs now rendered per-dependent in Form 1040 preview (added `*ngFor` loop in `form-tax-return-1040.component.html`); (2) filing status option label corrected to `'Married filing jointly (nonresident spouse treated as resident)'`.

## 2026-04-05 — E2E Tests: Convert Statement Seeding to API-First Pattern

Hiding the form view in all 35 statement form components broke 15 E2E spec files that filled statement form fields via `page.fill('#fieldId', value)`. Since `pdfView=true` by default, form view inputs (`#employeeSSN`, `#payerTIN`, `#recipientTIN`, amount fields, etc.) are no longer in the DOM.

### Root cause
Tests used pattern: `openStatementForm → page.fill('#field') → clickSave`. With form view hidden, all `page.fill()` calls on statement form fields timed out (elements not found).

### Fix applied
Converted all 15 affected spec files to API-first statement seeding using existing helpers:
- `seedW2StatementApi(page, { wages, ssn, ssTips })` — W-2 entries
- New `seedStatementApi(page, formId, payload)` added to `helpers/api-flow.ts` — general-purpose create+save in one call
- `createStatementEntryApi` + `saveStatementEntryApi` — used for multi-entry or complex cases

### Files changed
**`helpers/api-flow.ts`** — added `seedStatementApi(page, formId, payload)` helper

**15 spec files updated** (removed local UI helpers, replaced with API calls):
`line1z-total-wages`, `line1e-dependent-care`, `line1f-adoption-benefits`, `line1g-uncollected-ss-medicare`, `line1h-other-earned-income`, `line1i-combat-pay`, `line11ab-adjusted-gross-income`, `line13a-qbi-deduction`, `line2ab-interest-income`, `line2ab-interest-income-low`, `line3ab-dividend-income`, `line4abc-ira-income`, `line7ab-capital-gain-loss`, `line9-total-income`, `medicaid-waiver`

### Robustness principle established
All statement data seeding now uses the API layer only. Future UI changes (form layout, field visibility) cannot break statement seeding. Tests are faster and deterministic. Full plan recorded at `e2e/E2E-FIX-PLAN.md`.

### Not affected
`line1c-tip-income.spec.ts` fills personal form fields (`#employerEIN0` in tip-income form) — not a statement form, unchanged. All personal form specs remain UI-based.

---

## 2026-04-05 — Statement Forms: Entry Load/Save/Delete Fixes

### Infrastructure changes

**`statement-form.directive.ts`**
- Added `@Output() entryLoaded = new EventEmitter<void>()` — emitted after each entry load in `loadEntry()` finally block, so components can react (e.g. refresh PDF overlay)

**`statement-entry-state.service.ts`**
- Removed auto-create-replacement logic from `deleteEntry()` — previously a new blank entry was silently created when the last entry was deleted; now `remaining` is set to empty and `activeByForm` cleared

**`statement-entry-toolbar.component.ts`**
- Added `FormNavigationService` injection
- `deleteEntry()`: when the last entry is deleted, now calls `selectionService.toggle(formId, false)` + `formNav.setCurrentForm('statements-select')` to deselect the form and return to the Select form UI
- Button label changed from "Delete entry" to "Delete"

### W-2 pilot fixes (`form-w-2.component.ts`)
- Added `(entryLoaded)="syncFormToPdf()"` to the `<form>` element — PDF overlay now refreshes automatically when switching between entries
- Added `type="button"` to the Save button — prevents double-submission via both click handler and ngSubmit

### All other statement components (31 with `pdfRaw` + 3 form-1095 variants)

**Root cause:** In all non-W-2 statement components the `<form statementForm="...">` element was nested inside `<ng-template #formContent>`. Since `pdfView` defaults to `true`, the ng-template was never instantiated, so `StatementFormDirective` never ran — entry data was never loaded into `model`, and `syncFormToPdf()` always saw a blank model.

**Fix applied to all 34 components:**
- Moved `<form>` opening tag out of `<ng-template>` to wrap both the form-view div and the pdf-view div (always in DOM)
- Added `(entryLoaded)="syncFormToPdf()"` to the form element (for 31 components that have `syncFormToPdf()`)
- Closing `</form>` moved to after the pdf-view closing `</div>`
- Save buttons confirmed `type="button"` (prevents double-submit)

**Exceptions:**
- `form-1095-a`, `form-1095-b`, `form-1095-c` — no `pdfRaw`/`syncFormToPdf()`; these bind model fields directly in the PDF view so no `(entryLoaded)` binding needed
- `form-3922`, `form-5498-sa` — component files do not exist in the codebase (skipped)

Build passes with zero errors.

---

## 2026-04-04 — Schedule K-1 Dedicated PDF Overlay Components

Replaced generic `FormCapitalStatementComponent` routing for the three Schedule K-1 forms with dedicated standalone Angular components that implement proper IRS PDF overlay views.

### New components
- `form-schedule-k1-1041.component.ts` — K-1 (Form 1041), 74 page1 fields, aspect-ratio 1275/1593
- `form-schedule-k1-1065.component.ts` — K-1 (Form 1065), 111 page1 fields, aspect-ratio 1275/1655
- `form-schedule-k1-1120s.component.ts` — K-1 (Form 1120-S), 114 page1 fields, aspect-ratio 1275/1576

### Pattern applied
- Each component has `pdfView` getter/setter, `pdfRaw: Record<string, string>`, `syncFormToPdf()` / `syncPdfToForm()`, `onVerify()` button
- All field coordinates from semantic CSV files using `left=llx/612*100%`, `top=(792-ury)/792*100%` etc.
- Loop-based sync using `allKeys<form>` array and `checkboxKeys<form>` Set
- Three form sections: Recipient identification, Section 199A QBI (custom keys), Form fields page 1

### Shell changes
- Removed `schedule-k1-1041`, `schedule-k1-1065`, `schedule-k1-1120s` from `capitalStatementFormIds`
- Added three new `*ngIf` conditionals and component imports

### Mapping files
- `C:/us-tax/mappings/schedule-k1-1041-field-mapping.md`
- `C:/us-tax/mappings/schedule-k1-1065-field-mapping.md`
- `C:/us-tax/mappings/schedule-k1-1120s-field-mapping.md`

Build passes with zero new errors.

## 2026-04-03 — All Statement PDF Overlays Completed

Implemented image-overlay PDF views for all 22 remaining statement forms (plus 1099-MISC from prior session). Every statement in the Statements sidebar section now has both a form view and a PDF view backed by the actual IRS Copy B rasterized image.

### Pattern applied to all forms
- `pdfRaw: Record<string, string>` intermediate store for raw PDF field text
- Private `_pdfView` getter/setter — sets view before sync to prevent crash if sync throws
- `syncFormToPdf()` — copies model → `pdfRaw` on view toggle; uses `formatAmountAndNotes` for monetary fields; joins name components with spaces
- `syncPdfToForm()` — called by `onSubmit()` when in PDF view; parses `pdfRaw` back via `parseAmountAndNotes`
- CSS image overlay: `pdf-clip-container` (overflow:hidden, aspect-ratio clips blank lower page), `pdf-fields-overlay` (absolute-positioned inputs over the IRS PNG)
- All field coordinates computed from the form's `*_field_map_semantic.csv` using `left=llx/612*100%`, `top=(792-ury)/792*100%`, `width=(urx-llx)/612*100%`, `height=(ury-lly)/792*100%`
- Verify button uses `pi pi-check-circle` icon; corrected checkboxes disabled in overlay
- Forms with no IRS image (rrb-1099, rrb-1099-r) use styled table-based PDF view

### Forms completed (all statements)
W-2G, 1099-NEC, 1099-K, 1099-INT, 1099-DIV, 1099-OID, 1099-B, 1099-CAP, 1099-A, 1099-C, 1099-E, 1099-Q, 1099-QA, 1099-LTC, 1099-SA, 1099-R, SSA-1099, 1099-PTR, RRB-1099, RRB-1099-R, 1099-G, 1099-S

### New mapping files (`C:/us-tax/mappings/`)
1099-a, 1099-c, 1099-div, 1099-int, 1099-k, 1099-ltc, 1099-misc, 1099-nec, 1099-oid, 1099-q, 1099-qa, 1099-r, 1099-sa, rrb-1099, rrb-1099-r, ssa-1099, w-2, w-2g, 1099-patr, 1098-e

### Build
`npm run build` passes with no errors (one pre-existing pako CommonJS warning).

## 2026-04-03 — W-2 PDF Overlay Implementation

Replaced the CSS-grid PDF view on the W-2 statement form with a true image-overlay approach.

### New files
- `us-tax-ui/public/irs/fw2_page_02.png` — rasterized W-2 Copy B (page 2) image, copied from `C:\us-tax\images\fw2_semantic_labels\fw2_semantic_labels-02.png`.
- `us-tax-ui/src/app/utils/amount-notes-parser.ts` — shared utility:
  - `parseAmountAndNotes(raw)` — extracts first decimal number (handles `$`, commas, parentheses for negatives); everything else is notes. Notes is null when absent — the common case.
  - `formatAmountAndNotes(amount, notes)` — joins amount and optional notes back into a display string.
- `C:\us-tax\mappings\w-2-field-mapping.md` — full field mapping document: Form View ↔ PDF View, with CSS coordinates table and source file references.

### Modified files
- `us-tax-ui/src/app/forms/form-w-2.component.ts`:
  - Added `pdfRaw: Record<string, string>` — stores raw text per PDF field while in PDF view.
  - Added `syncFormToPdf()` — populates `pdfRaw` from the model when switching to PDF view. Monetary fields are formatted as `amount [notes]`. Box 14 entries are joined as one line per entry (`LABEL AMOUNT [notes]`). Box 13 checkboxes bind directly to model booleans (no `pdfRaw`).
  - Added `syncPdfToForm()` — called by `onSubmit()` when in PDF view; parses each `pdfRaw` entry back into `*Amount` + `*Notes` fields using `parseAmountAndNotes`. Box 14 lines use a dedicated parser: text before the number → label, number → amount, text after → notes (fixes a bug where `parseAmountAndNotes` was incorrectly putting the label into notes).
  - Replaced CSS-grid PDF view with image overlay: `<div class="pdf-clip-container">` clips whitespace at the bottom of the US Letter page image using `aspect-ratio: 1275 / 770` + `overflow: hidden`; inner `<div class="pdf-overlay-container">` maintains the full image height so all CSS percentage coordinates remain accurate.
  - All field coordinates recomputed from `fw2_field_map_semantic.csv` using the formula `left = llx/612*100%`, `top = (792-ury)/792*100%`, `width = (urx-llx)/612*100%`, `height = (ury-lly)/792*100%`.
  - Box 9 input removed — box was retired from W-2 in 2011 (formerly "Advance EIC payment", now reserved).
  - Box 13 checkbox positions corrected from CSV (`top: 27.31%`, not 28.28% as in original plan).
  - Box 14 textarea position corrected from CSV (`top: 30.30%`, not 36.36% as in original plan).
  - State/local row positions corrected from CSV: row 1 `top: 37.88%` (was 39.39%), row 2 `top: 40.91%` (was 42.42%). Box 20 width corrected to `7.75%` (was 13.73%).
  - PDF view Verify button uses `pi pi-check-circle` icon.
  - All input fields have a subtle blue-tinted background (`rgba(220,235,255,0.15)`) so field areas are discoverable, brightening on hover and going yellow on focus.

## 2026-04-02T14:10:00-04:00
Closed the remaining statement-picker and interest-income Playwright regressions from the full-suite run.

- Root cause 1: the first sidebar fix still depended on a locator `filter({ has: ... })` chain to identify sidebar sections by `.sidebar-section-title`, and that pattern missed the live `Statements` section in authenticated runs. `e2e/tests/helpers/ui-flow.ts` now resolves sections by iterating visible `.sidebar-section` nodes and comparing the actual title text directly.
- Root cause 2: `e2e/tests/pages/statements-select.page.ts` assumed the picker checkbox would remain mounted after selecting a form, but the product intentionally navigates to the selected statement form immediately. The page object now verifies persisted `statementFormsSelection` state instead of asserting on the transient checkbox node.
- Verification: `cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\e2e; npm test -- --workers=1 tests/statement-form-picker.spec.ts tests/line2ab-interest-income.spec.ts tests/line2ab-interest-income-low.spec.ts` passed with `4 passed`.
## 2026-04-02T11:35:00-04:00
Fixed the first full-suite regressions caused by the new grouped statement labels.

- Root cause: `e2e/tests/helpers/ui-flow.ts` matched sidebar sections with `.sidebar-section { hasText: sectionLabel }`. Once statement labels included titles like `Form 1099-INT - Interest Income`, the `Statements` section also matched `Income`, so `clickSidebarItem(page, 'Income', /Interest income/i)` could click the statement sidebar item instead of the Income form.
- E2E helper fix: `clickSidebarItem(...)` now matches the actual `.sidebar-section-title` text with an anchored regex, and `openSidebarFormById(...)` now scopes direct `data-form-id` lookups to `.app-sidebar`.
- E2E page-object fix: `e2e/tests/pages/shell.page.ts` now scopes `getSidebarFormLink(...)` to `.app-sidebar`, which removes the strict-locator collision in `statement-form-picker.spec.ts` between the sidebar button and the picker card sharing the same `data-form-id`.
- Verification: `cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\e2e; npx playwright test --list tests/statement-form-picker.spec.ts tests/line2ab-interest-income.spec.ts tests/line2ab-interest-income-low.spec.ts` passed.

## 2026-04-01T15:41:01-04:00
Fixed the last filing-status radio selector bug by correcting the question-label association.

- Root cause: the long MFS/HOH question prompt was still rendered as a `<label for="...Yes">`, so the `Yes` radio inherited the whole question text as part of its accessible name. The shared Playwright `selectRadioOption(...)` helper matches radio names exactly, so it could not find `Yes`.
- Frontend: `src/app/forms/form-filing-status-taxpayer.component.ts` now renders the question prompt as a group label (`span` referenced by `aria-labelledby`) instead of a label attached to the `Yes` radio. The radio values, save payload, and backend mapping remain unchanged.
- Verification: `cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui; npm run build` passed; focused Angular spec passed (`TOTAL: 8 SUCCESS`).

**Line 32 spec written (`C:\us-tax\lines\32a.md`).**

Form 1040 Line 32 = "Add lines 27, 28, 29, 30, and 31. These are your total other payments and refundable credits." Pure arithmetic subtotal; all five input lines (27�31) are already implemented. Key documentation findings:
- The backend comment `// line32 (RRC; deferred)` is incorrect � line 32 is a subtotal, NOT a Recovery Rebate Credit; `Payments.recoveryRebateCredit` is an unused legacy field that should be removed.
- Line 32 is a new structural line on the **2025** Form 1040 (did not exist as an explicit subtotal in prior years).
- Line 30 (refundable adoption credit, Form 8839 line 13) is also new in 2025 and is one of the five inputs.
- Spec includes: formula, input mapping table, compute order, backend gap analysis (proposed `totalOtherPaymentsAndRefundableCredits` field in `Payments.java`), data model change guide, 2025-specific changes, validation rules, and PDF export field map.

## 2026-03-28T25:00:00-04:00

1. **Backend bug fix** (`TaxReturnComputeService.java` line 8197): Changed `refundableAdoptionCredit = totalPart2Line11c` to `refundableAdoptionCredit = roundMoney(totalPart2Line11c)`. Root cause: `buildAdoptionChildren()` pads to 3 child slots; empty slots produce `0.0000` (scale 4) which contaminated the accumulator via `BigDecimal.add()`, propagating scale 4 to `AdoptionComputation` and `payments.refundableAdoptionCredit`. Fix normalizes to scale 0 (whole dollars) before leaving the compute block.

**Form 8862 � Fully Implemented (backend + Angular).**

`computeForm8862()` added to `TaxReturnComputeService`. Reads `form8862` personal-form data; evaluates Part II (EIC gate), Part III (CTC/ACTC/ODC per-child gate), and Part IV (AOTC per-student gate). Three new `TaxReturnFlag` codes: `FORM_8862_EIC_REQUIRED`, `FORM_8862_CTC_REQUIRED`, `FORM_8862_AOTC_REQUIRED`. `computeForm8862()` cached once in `prepare()` and shared with EIC (`computeLine27aEIC()`), CTC (`computeSchedule8812()`), and AOTC (`computeForm8863()`) gate checks. Per-student AOTC index gating applied inside the student loop of `computeForm8863()`; LLC is never blocked. Part II line 3 shortcut (income-error-only denial) immediately sets `eicEligible=true`. Part IV empty `aotcStudents` list blocks all AOTC students while LLC continues. New output model `Form8862.java`; `TaxReturnComputation` + `TaxReturnDataService` updated; `PersonalResource` accepts `form8862` + `ctc-actc-screening-taxpayer`. YAML: `8862-information-to-claim-credits.yaml`. 9 unit tests added (315 total, all passing). E2E spec `form8862-credits-after-disallowance.spec.ts` (3 scenarios).

## 2026-03-28T14:00:00-04:00
**Form 8862 � Implementation Plan.**

Form 8862 (Rev. 12-2025) � "Information To Claim Certain Credits After Disallowance." Filed with the return when EIC, CTC/ACTC/ODC, or AOTC was previously denied. One form per return (not spouse-split).

**Scope:** All four parts. Part I (year + credit checkboxes). Part II EIC (line 3 shortcut: if only reason was income error ? unblock immediately; else Section A with-children or Section B childless gates). Part III CTC/ACTC/ODC (per-child lines 14�17). Part IV AOTC (per-student lines 19a/19b; LLC unaffected).

**Screening gates added to existing forms:**
- `eicPreviouslyDenied` � already on `earned-income-credit-taxpayer`.
- `ctcPreviouslyDenied` � new personal form `ctc-actc-screening-taxpayer`.
- `aotcPreviouslyDenied` � new field on `education-credits-taxpayer`.

**Backend:**
- `computeForm8862()` private method in `TaxReturnComputeService`; returns `Form8862` output model.
- `Form8862.java` output model: `filedWithReturn`, `taxYearOfDisallowance`, `eicEligible`, `ctcEligible`, `aotcEligible`, `aotcStudentEligible` list.
- Gate changes: `computeLine27aEIC()` checks form8862 before proceeding when `eicPreviouslyDenied`; `computeSchedule8812()` checks when `ctcPreviouslyDenied`; `computeForm8863()` checks per-student when `aotcPreviouslyDenied` (LLC unaffected).
- Three new `TaxReturnFlag` entries: `FORM_8862_EIC_REQUIRED`, `FORM_8862_CTC_REQUIRED`, `FORM_8862_AOTC_REQUIRED`.
- `PersonalResource.java`: add `form8862`, `ctc-actc-screening-taxpayer`.
- `TaxReturnComputation.java` + `TaxReturnDataService.java`: wire `form8862` field.

**YAML:** `8862-information-to-claim-credits.yaml` (no person suffix).
**Angular:** `form-form8862.component.ts`; shell entry in Applications sidebar. Both complete (build passes).
**Semantic assets:** `f8862_semantic_labels.pdf` + `f8862_field_map_semantic.csv` (script `generate-semantic-f8862.js`) � deferred.
**Tests:** 9 unit tests, 3 E2E scenarios.

**Out of scope:** Ban period verification (IRS enforces), LLC gate (LLC unaffected by AOTC denial), >3 EIC children / >4 CTC children (attach statement), Schedule EIC PDF.

## 2026-03-27T01:30:00-04:00
**Bug fix**: Lines 25b/25c withholding routing corrected in `computeLine31ThroughLine38()`.

Three issues identified via IRS 2025 Form 1040 instructions and verified against J.K. Lasser:
1. **SSA-1099 / RRB-1099 misrouted to 25c** � IRS instructions explicitly place Social Security (SSA-1099 box 6) and railroad retirement (RRB-1099 box 10) withholding on line 25b alongside other 1099s. Fixed: moved both from `withholdingOther` to `withholding1099`.
2. **1099-MISC never loaded** � backup withholding on miscellaneous income (box 4) was silently dropped. Fixed: `form1099MiscEntries` now loaded in `prepare()` and included in line 25b aggregation.
3. **W-2G never loaded** � gambling withholding (box 4, 24% flat rate on winnings > $5,000) was silently dropped. IRS instructions place W-2G on line 25c. Fixed: `formW2gEntries` now loaded in `prepare()` and included in line 25c aggregation.

`computeLine31ThroughLine38()` signature updated with two new parameters. 173/173 unit tests pass.

## 2026-03-26T19:30:00-04:00
**Bug fix**: Form 5329 early IRA/pension distribution penalty tax was computed but never wired to Schedule 2 line 8 (`additionalTaxOnIras`) or Form 1040 line 23 (`TaxAndCredits.otherTaxes`). This meant the 10% early distribution penalty silently dropped out of `line24 = line22 + line23` (total tax).

- Added `applyForm5329TaxToSchedule2(Schedule2, Form1040, Form5329)` in `TaxReturnComputeService` � sets `Schedule2OtherTaxes.additionalTaxOnIras`, updates `totalOtherTaxes`, and accumulates into `TaxAndCredits.otherTaxes`.
- Called immediately after Form 5329 header is set in the main compute flow (line ~553), before `computeLine20ThroughLine24()` reads `otherTaxes`.
- Additive: accumulates on top of any existing `otherTaxes` (e.g. tip tax from Form 4137 or uncollected SS from Form 8919), so both can coexist.
- 2 new unit tests added to `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`: `form5329EarlyDistributionTaxWiresToSchedule2Line8AndLine23` (positive case, $10k distribution ? $1k penalty wired end-to-end) and `form5329AbsentWhenNoPensionEarlyDistribution` (no spurious wiring when no Form 5329). Total: **289 unit tests**, all passing.
- E2E tests for this path deferred (requires pension/1099-R E2E flow).

- **`Schedule8812.java`** (new model): Part I fields (line 3�14: MAGI, CTC/ODC counts, tentative credit, phase-out, Credit Limit Worksheet A, line 14 nonrefundable credit) and Part II-A fields (line 16a�27: excess credit, ACTC ceiling, earned income, line 27 ACTC). Part II-B deferred (EIC/SE tax not yet computed).
- **`TaxReturnComputation`**: added `Schedule8812 schedule8812` parameter (37th in record, before `flags`).
- **`TaxReturnComputeService`**: new `computeSchedule8812()` (~180 lines). Part I: MAGI = AGI + Form2555 exclusions; phase-out uses `RoundingMode.CEILING`; Worksheet A = `max(0, line18 - Form2441 credit)`; line14 = `min(line12, worksheetA)`. Part II-A: Form2555 blocks ACTC; 3-child routing (Part II-B conservative fallback when needed: `line27 = line20`). Wires line19 into `TaxAndCredits.childTaxCredit`; initializes `Payments` if needed and sets `additionalChildTaxCredit` for line28.
- **`TaxReturnDataService`**: save/load `schedule8812` Firestore field.
- **`TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`**: 11 new Schedule 8812 test cases (basic CTC, ODC-only, mixed, phase-out, Worksheet A cap, ACTC below/above floor, Form2555 block, Part II-B fallback, no dependents). `TestPersonalDataService.dependents` changed to mutable ArrayList.
- **Frontend**: new `form-tax-return-schedule8812.component.ts` + sidebar link "Schedule 8812" in Tax Return section. `TaxReturnComputation` TS interface updated with `schedule8812` field.
- **PDF assets**: `publish-schedule8812-assets.js` script; `f1040s8_semantic_labels.pdf` + `f1040s8_field_map_semantic.csv` published to `us-tax-ui/public/irs/`.
- **E2E**: new `line19-child-tax-credit.spec.ts` (5 scenarios); new `seedDependentApi()` helper in `api-flow.ts`.
- **Spec**: `C:\us-tax\lines\s8812.md` consolidated from s8812a + s8812b; `diagrams/19.drawio` created.
- **2025 constants**: CTC $2,200/child; ODC $500; ACTC $1,700 cap; phase-out $400k MFJ/$200k; ACTC floor $2,500; rate 15%.
- All 233 unit tests pass.

## 2026-03-22T18:30:00-04:00
Implemented Form 1040 Line 18 � Total Tax Before Credits.

- **No new model fields needed**: `TaxAndCredits.totalTaxBeforeCredits` already existed but was never set.
- **`TaxReturnComputeService.java`**: added `computeLine18()` (~40 lines). Reads `taxAndCredits.getTax()` (line 16) and `taxAndCredits.getAlternativeMinimumTax()` (line 17, null treated as zero), sums them, sets `totalTaxBeforeCredits`. Called in `prepare()` immediately after `computeLine17()`. Logs at INFO: actor hash, line16, line17, line18.
- **Unit tests** (`TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`): 3 new tests � zero income (line18 = 0/null), wages only no AMT (line18 = line16), high wages + PAB interest with AMT (line18 = line16 + line17). All pass. Added `TaxAndCredits` import.
- **E2E tests** (`e2e/tests/line18-total-tax-before-credits.spec.ts`): 3 tests mirroring the unit scenarios.
- **Diagram**: `C:\us-tax\diagrams\18.drawio` created.
- **Plan**: `C:\us-tax\line18-implementation-plan.md`.

## 2026-03-21T24:00:00-04:00
Fixed remaining E2E failures: form2555/form8962 auth issue and line13a navigation race condition.

- `e2e/playwright.config.ts`: when `E2E_SHARED_AUTH_EMAIL`/`PASSWORD` are set, the `regression` project now depends on `setup-shared-auth` and starts with the shared user's `storageState`. This gives `clearUserData`-based tests an authenticated page without calling `signUpWithPassword`.
- `e2e/tests/helpers/api-flow.ts` (`clearUserData`): added `page.goto('/')` + networkidle wait when the page is not yet on an HTTP origin (`about:blank`). Needed because Playwright browser contexts start at `about:blank`; the `storageState` Firebase auth is only accessible after navigating to `http://localhost:4200`.
- `e2e/tests/line13a-qbi-deduction.spec.ts` (`openW2Form`, `open1099DivForm`): replaced brittle `page.waitForResponse` GET-listener pattern with a 5-attempt retry loop (identical to `openQbiDeductionForm`). Root cause: when `ensureStatementSelected` opens and closes the "Select form" panel, the Angular component may auto-create and load the W-2 entry before the listener is registered, so the listener never fires. The retry loop checks `#employeeSSN` / `#payerTIN` visibility directly.

## 2026-03-21T23:30:00-04:00
Implemented 4 actionable outstanding.md items (3 new features + 1 sequencing fix):

- **Form 8962 ? Schedule 3 line 9 PTC wiring** (`applyPremiumTaxCreditToSchedule3()`): when `form8962.line26NetPremiumTaxCredit > 0`, the net premium tax credit is wired into `Schedule3OtherPaymentsCredits.netPremiumTaxCredit`. Called in `prepare()` after `computeForm8962()` / `computeLine17()`.

- **Form 2555 ? Schedule 1-A MAGI auto-wire**: `computeSchedule1A()` moved to run AFTER Form 2555 computation so that computed exclusion amounts (line 45 / line 50) auto-populate the MAGI add-backs (lines 2b/2c) for phaseout computation, rather than relying solely on manual YAML entries. `form2555Taxpayer` and `form2555Spouse` added as parameters; MFJ combines both spouses' amounts.

- **Form 4972 lines 5a/5b double-count fix** (`adjustPensionLinesForForm4972()`): called after Form 4972 taxpayer/spouse computation. If Part III elected, full `box2a` removed from line 5b (and `box1Gross` from 5a if set). If only Part II elected, `box3` capital gain removed from 5b. `box1GrossDistribution` field added to `Form4972.java`. Prevents double-counting the lump-sum 1099-R on both lines 5a/5b and Form 4972 ? line 16.

- **Form 4952 lines 3�8 compute + Schedule A line 9 wiring**: new `Form4952Output.java` model. `computeForm4952()` computes lines 1�8 from `investment-interest-expense-deduction` personal form. `patchScheduleAInvestmentInterest()` updates `scheduleA.deductibleInvestmentInterest` (and recomputes total interest + total itemized) when itemizing. `Form4952Output form4952` added to `TaxReturnComputation` record and `TaxReturnDataService` deserialization.

- **Unit tests**: 3 new tests (form8962Line26PtcWiredToSchedule3Line9, form4952AllowableDeductionComputedAndLine8Set, form4972PartIiiExcludesLumpSumFromLine5b). Total: 219 tests, all passing.

## 2026-03-21T19:00:00-04:00
Implemented Form 1040 Line 17 (Alternative Minimum Tax / Form 6251) end-to-end for the 2025 tax year.

- **Form 6251 output model** (`model/output/Form6251.java`): completely rewritten from 2-field stub to full model. Part I fields: `line1aTotalDeductionsMinusSenior`, `line1bAgiMinusLine1a`, `line2aStateLocalTaxes`, `line2bStateLocalRefund`, `line2gPrivateActivityBondInterest`, `line4AlternativeMinimumTaxableIncome`, `line5Exemption`, `line6AmtBaseAfterExemption`. Part II fields: `line7TentativeMinimumTax`, `line7ComputationPath`, `line8AmtForeignTaxCredit`, `line9TentativeMinimumTaxAfterFtc`, `line10RegularTaxAdjusted`, `line11AlternativeMinimumTax`. Part III fields: lines 12�40 (AMT QDCG capital-gains worksheet).
- **TaxAndCredits.java**: added `alternativeMinimumTax` field (Line 17 value wired from Form 6251 line 11).
- **TaxReturnComputeService.java**: replaced `buildForm6251()` with `computeLine17()` (~300 lines). Three AMT compute paths mirror Line 16: DIRECT_RATE (26%/28%), AMT_PART_III (QDCG capital-gains), AMT_FEITW (Form 2555). Key logic: Part I AMTI (add-back SALT, PAB interest, state refund offset, senior deduction); AMT exemption + phaseout (2025 constants); `wireLine17ToOutputs()` wires AMT to `TaxAndCredits.alternativeMinimumTax` and `Schedule2.line3AlternativeMinimumTax`. IRS filing-requirement rule: Form 6251 is generated even when AMT = 0 if PAB interest (line 2g) > 0. Schedule 2 line 1a (PTC repayment) wired from `form8962.line29RepaymentAmount`. Schedule 3 line 9 PTC credit wiring deferred (see outstanding.md).
- **TaxReturnDataService.java**: fixed bug � `form8962` was loaded but never saved to Firestore; added `payload.put("form8962", ...)`.
- **Unit tests** (`TaxReturnComputeServiceTest`): 5 new AMT scenarios � zero for typical wage earner; nonzero for high income + PAB interest; Form 6251 generated when PAB interest present even if AMT = 0; exemption phaseout at $1.5M MFJ; mid-income with high PAB interest. 3 existing Schedule 1-A tests fixed (`wagesAmount` ? `wagesTipsOtherCompAmount`). Total: 216 tests, all passing.
- **E2E tests**: `e2e/tests/line17-amt.spec.ts` (2 tests: AMT null for typical wage earner; AMT positive + Form 6251 generated for $200k wages + $100k PAB interest).
- **Frontend Tax Return view** (`form-tax-return-6251.component.ts`): expanded from single-line stub to full Part I, Part II, and conditional Part III display with all computed fields. PDF field map updated to include all Part I/II fields.
- Deferred: Schedule 3 line 9 (PTC credit), AMT FTC (line 8), ATNOLD (line 2f), passive activity (line 2m), ISO spread (line 2i) � all documented in outstanding.md.

## 2026-03-20T19:30:00-04:00
Fixed two bugs uncovered during the first E2E regression run after the Line 16 implementation.

- **`line16-tax.spec.ts` � wrong W-2 field name**: The local `seedW2` helper was saving wages under `wagesAmount`, which is silently ignored by the backend. Changed to `wagesTipsOtherCompAmount` (W-2 box 1) + `socialSecurityWagesAmount` (W-2 box 3), matching the documented convention in `api-flow.ts`. Two tests (`Tax Table bracket` and `QDCG path`) were returning tax = 0 / computationMethod = ZERO because line 15 was always zero.
- **`TaxReturnComputeService.java` � housing exclusion set to zero instead of null**: `computeForm2555Exclusions()` was calling `form.setHousingExclusionAmount(BigDecimal.ZERO)` when `claimsHousingExclusion` is false. Changed to `null` so the field is absent in the output, matching the `?? null` pattern used by callers and the contract implied by the pre-existing `form2555-foreign-earned-income.spec.ts` test.
- **`form2555-foreign-earned-income.spec.ts` � stale "deferred" assertion**: The bona-fide-residence test had `expect(f?.foreignEarnedIncomeExclusion ?? null).toBeNull()` with comment "Exclusion amounts are deferred (line 16 not yet implemented)". Updated to `expect(f?.foreignEarnedIncomeExclusion).toBeCloseTo(95000, 0)` now that line 45 is computed (full year: 365 qualifying days, wages $95,000 = $130,000 limit).
- All 135 e2e tests pass after these fixes (9 guest + 126 regression).

## 2026-03-20T00:00:00-04:00
Implemented Form 1040 Line 16 (Tax) end-to-end for the 2025 tax year.

- Added `C:\us-tax\yamls\16-tax-taxpayer.yaml` � intake for ECR (education credit recapture) and informational notices about out-of-scope box-3 write-ins (962, 1291TAX, Form 8978, 965INC).
- Added `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\src\app\forms\form-line16-tax.component.ts` and wired "Line 16 tax items" under the Incomes sidebar for the taxpayer tab.
- Added `16-tax-taxpayer` to `PersonalResource.PERSONAL_FORMS`.
- Expanded `TaxAndCredits.java` with `regularTax`, `computationMethod`, `box1Form8814Tax`, `box2Form4972Tax`, `ecrBox3Tax`, `box1/2/3Checked`, `box3Code` fields.
- Implemented `computeForm2555Exclusions()`, `computeQualifyingDaysIn2025()` � computes Form 2555 lines 45 and 50 from intake dates/amounts.
- Implemented `computeLine16()` in `TaxReturnComputeService` with full decision tree:
  1. line15 = 0 ? ZERO
  2. Form 2555 filed ? Foreign Earned Income Tax Worksheet (FEITW)
  3. Form 8615 kiddie tax required ? FORM_8615 (uses `childFinalTaxLine18`)
  4. Schedule D Tax Worksheet required ? SCHEDULE_D_TAX_WORKSHEET
  5. QDCG Worksheet required ? QDCG
  6. line15 < $100k ? TAX_TABLE (bracket formula)
  7. line15 = $100k ? TAX_COMPUTATION_WORKSHEET (bracket formula)
- Added helper methods: `computeTaxBracket()`, `computeQDCGWorksheet()`, `computeScheduleDTaxWorksheet()`, `computeForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet()`, `isScheduleDTaxWorksheetRequired()`, `isQDCGWorksheetRequired()`.
- 26 unit tests in `TaxLine16TaxTest.java` covering all bracket formulas, QDCG worksheet, FEITW, Form 8615, Form 2555 exclusion, and add-on tax scenarios.
- E2E test `line16-tax.spec.ts` with 6 scenarios: baseline, Tax Table path, ECR add-on, no-ECR, QDCG path, and form save/load round-trip.
- Data flow diagram: `C:\us-tax\diagrams\16.drawio`.

## 2026-03-17T23:10:13.1205775-04:00
Implemented the initial Form 4952 user-input workflow without tax computation.

- Backend: accepted `investment-interest-expense-deduction` in `src/main/java/com/ustax/microservices/PersonalResource.java` and added `PersonalResourceTest` coverage.
- Frontend: added `form-interest-expense.component.ts/html`, wired `Deductions > Interest expense` for the taxpayer tab only, and updated shell/component smoke coverage.
- E2E: added `e2e/tests/line4952-interest-expense.spec.ts` to verify that the new form saves through the shared personal-form API.
- Deferred Form 4952 math, Schedule A routing, AMT artifact generation, and Tax Return output wiring in `C:\us-tax\outstanding.md`.
## 2026-03-18T00:45:00-04:00
Created the shared Form 4952 intake YAML in C:\us-tax\yamls for the return-level investment interest deduction workflow.

- Added C:\us-tax\yamls\4952-investment-interest-expense-deduction.yaml using the established user-input structure: screening, statement-upload confirmation, line-entry sections, special-routing notes, backend-only imported totals, and backend-only computed outputs.
- Kept the YAML single-instance for the return because Form 4952 is attached once per return, including MFJ, rather than as separate spouse forms.

## 2026-03-17T23:00:00-04:00
Documented the Schedule D 2025 refresh and ensured the kiddie-income e2e helper exports the ingredients the tests expect.

- `e2e/tests/helpers/api-flow.ts` now re-exports `seedRequiredDob` from `helpers/seed-dob`, so the kiddie-income spec (and any other flows that relied on the helper) can invoke it directly.
- Removed the legacy 2024 Schedule D semantic pair (`schedule_d_semantic_labels.pdf` and `schedule_d_field_map_semantic.csv`) from `C:\us-tax\pdfs` after confirming that `f1040sd_semantic_labels.pdf` / `f1040sd_field_map_semantic.csv` are the authoritative 2025 assets powering the Schedule D output.

## 2026-03-18T00:30:00-04:00
Generated the semantic-labeled copy of Form 4952 and added a reusable script to produce the PDF/CSV pair.

- `scripts/generate-semantic-f4952.js` extracts the XFA form fields from `C:\us-tax\docs\IRS-Forms\f4952.pdf`, creates semantic names, and writes the renamed PDF/CSV to `C:\us-tax\pdfs\f4952_semantic_labels.pdf` / `C:\us-tax\pdfs\f4952_field_map_semantic.csv`.
- The new `f4952` assets in `C:\us-tax\pdfs` can now be used anywhere the backend or UI needs structured metadata for the Form 4952 output.

## 2026-03-16T20:00:00-04:00
Implemented Form 4972 (Tax on Lump-Sum Distributions) end-to-end, replacing the old stub that lived inside the pension-annuity-income form.

Backend:
- `Form4972.java` � completely rewritten; 23 fields covering source 1099-R data (box 2a/3/6/8/9a), Part II (lines 6-7), Part III (lines 8-16, 25, 28, 29), and line 30 total.
- `Form4972TaxTable.java` � new helper; 1986 single-filer bracket table used for Part III lines 25/28.
- `ReferenceData.java` � added `FORM4972_BIRTH_CUTOFF_DATE`, `FORM4972_MDA_BASE`, `FORM4972_MDA_PHASEDOWN_THRESHOLD`, `FORM4972_MDA_PHASEDOWN_RATE`, `FORM4972_CAPITAL_GAIN_RATE`.
- `TaxReturnComputation.java` � `form4972` (singular) ? `form4972Taxpayer`, `form4972Spouse` (two separate).
- `TaxReturnDataService.java` � saves/loads `form4972Taxpayer`, `form4972Spouse` instead of `form4972`.
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java` � removed old Form 4972 stub from `computePensionAnnuities()`; removed `form4972` from `PensionComputation` record; added `computeForm4972Taxpayer()`, `computeForm4972Spouse()`, and shared `computeForm4972()` private methods; reads `lump-sum-distribution-taxpayer` and `lump-sum-distribution-spouse` personal forms; links to 1099-R by `entryId`; emits `FORM4972_TAXPAYER_INELIGIBLE` / `FORM4972_SPOUSE_INELIGIBLE` / `FORM4972_TAXPAYER_NO_STATEMENT` / `FORM4972_SPOUSE_NO_STATEMENT` non-blocking flags.
- `PersonalResource.java` � added `lump-sum-distribution-taxpayer` and `lump-sum-distribution-spouse` to `PERSONAL_FORMS`.

YAML intake forms:
- `C:\us-tax\yamls\4972-lump-sum-distribution-taxpayer.yaml` � sections: screening, statementSource (1099-R import), partI (5 eligibility questions), partElections (Part II / Part III), partIIIAdjustments, computedOutputs (readOnly).
- `C:\us-tax\yamls\4972-lump-sum-distribution-spouse.yaml` � mirror with `spouse` prefix on all fields.

Frontend:
- `form-lump-sum-distribution.component.ts` � new Angular standalone intake component; selector `form-lump-sum-distribution`; `@Input person: 'taxpayer' | 'spouse'`; `fp(name)` helper for spouse prefix; shows 1099-R import summary, Part I eligibility, Part II/III elections.
- `form-tax-return-4972.component.ts` � completely rewritten; `@Input owner: 'taxpayer' | 'spouse'`; reads `form4972Taxpayer` or `form4972Spouse` from `TaxReturnService`; displays all Part II and Part III lines.
- `tax-return.service.ts` � `form4972?` ? `form4972Taxpayer?`, `form4972Spouse?`.
- `shell.component.ts` � added `FormLumpSumDistributionComponent` import; added income sidebar items for taxpayer/spouse; replaced single `tax-return-4972` sidebar/route with `tax-return-4972-taxpayer` / `tax-return-4972-spouse`.

E2E tests:
- New `line4972-lump-sum-distribution.spec.ts` � 6 API-first scenarios: no election, Part I ineligible flag, Part II only (line 7 = 20%), Part III only (line 29 computed), both parts (line 30 = line7 + line29), spouse variant.
- Updated `line5abc-pension-withdrawals.spec.ts` � removed `electsForm4972SpecialTaxTreatment` / `form4972LumpSumDistributionAmount` UI interactions and `form4972` assertions (Form 4972 is now a separate form).

Unit tests:
- `MicroserviceModelsTest.java` � updated `TaxReturnComputation` constructor call for new two-field signature.
- `TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java` � updated existing pension test; added 5 new Form 4972 tests (ineligible flag, Part II only, Part III only, both parts, spouse separate).
- `TaxReturnResourceTest.java` and `Line10IncomeAdjustmentsApiTest.java` � updated `TaxReturnComputation` null constructors for new arity.

## 2026-03-16T12:00:00-04:00
Implemented Form 8814 (Child's Interest and Dividends) end-to-end. Replaced old manual-entry stub on the capital-gain-loss form with a proper per-child `child-interest-dividends` statement form.

Backend:
- `Form8814.java` � completely rewritten; 14 fields covering Part I (lines 1a/1b/2a/2b/3/4/6/9/10/12) and Part II (line15), plus header, childFirstName/LastName/Ssn.
- `ReferenceData.java` � added `FORM8814_BASE_AMOUNT=$2700`, `FORM8814_UNTAXED_AMOUNT=$1350`, `FORM8814_EXTRA_TAX_MAX=$135`, `FORM8814_GROSS_INCOME_LIMIT=$13500`.
- `TaxReturnComputation.java` � `Form8814 form8814` ? `List<Form8814> form8814List`.
- `Schedule1AdditionalIncome.java` � added `otherIncomeForm8814` field.
- `StatementFormCatalog.java` � registered `child-interest-dividends`.
- `TaxReturnDataService.java` � saves/loads `form8814List` (List); added `toList()` helper.
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java` � new `computeChildInterestDividends()` (per-child Part I/II, blocking flag for gross = $13,500); new `applyForm8814Line12ToSchedule1()` (aggregates line12 ? Schedule 1 line 8z `otherIncomeForm8814`); `computeCapitalGainLoss()` now receives computed `hasChildCapGainsFromForm8814` and `form8814Line10Total` parameters instead of reading manual fields; removed `Form8814` from `CapitalGainLossComputation` record.

YAML intake form:
- `C:\us-tax\yamls\child-interest-dividends.yaml` � sections: childIdentification, partI (lines 1a/1b/2a/2b/3), computedOutputs (readOnly).

Frontend:
- `form-child-interest-dividends.component.ts` � Angular intake component; per-child cards via `StatementDataService`; Add/Save/Remove per entry.
- `form-tax-return-8814.component.ts` � rewritten to iterate `form8814List` array with `*ngFor`.
- `TaxReturnService` interface: `form8814?` ? `form8814List?`.
- Shell wiring: "Child's int. & dividends" added under Incomes; tax return sidebar shows Form 8814 when `form8814List.length > 0`.

Docs: `outstanding.md` updated with 5 deferred Form 1040 line 16 items.

E2E tests:
- New `line8814-child-interest-dividends.spec.ts` � 6 scenarios covering empty list, line4 = $2,700, line4 > $2,700 (line 12 flow), cap gain distributions (line 10 feed), two children aggregate, blocking flag for gross = $13,500.
- Updated `line7ab-capital-gain-loss.spec.ts` � removed manual Form 8814 stub fields; now creates a `child-interest-dividends` entry via API to produce line10=30 (line3=2730); assertions updated for `form8814List` array.

## 2026-03-15T14:00:00-04:00
Fixed 5 e2e regressions introduced with the line 13b spec and documented root causes in `C:\us-tax\rules.md`.

**Failures 1�4 (line13b, 409s � `MISSING_TAXPAYER_DATE_OF_BIRTH`):**
Four scenarios called `seedRequiredDob(page)` then overwrote the `you` document with a bare `PUT /api/personal/you { ssn: '...' }`, stripping `dateOfBirth`. The backend blocking flag caused `computeReturnApi` to return 409. Fix: replaced bare PUTs with `seedRequiredDob(page, { ssn: '...' })`. For the MFJ scenario, added `dateOfBirth` to the spouse PUT.

**Failure 5 (line1i-combat-pay � conditional message missing):**
`CombatPayPage._openFormById()` exited after one `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` as soon as `#electCombatPay` was visible. The combat pay component fetches W-2 data in a secondary async call; after the shell imports array grew the timing shifted. Fix: added a second `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` in the POM after the field-visible check.

New rules added to `C:\us-tax\rules.md`. All 95 tests passed (`test-results-run-20260315-133044`).

## 2026-03-15T00:00:00-04:00
Implemented Form 1040 line 13b (Schedule 1-A additional deductions) end-to-end.

Backend:
- `Schedule1A.java` output POJO with all 5-part fields (tips, overtime, car loan, senior, totals, per-person eligibility flags).
- 15 `SCHEDULE_1A_*` constants added to `ReferenceData.java` (caps, phaseout thresholds, step amounts, senior rate).
- `computeSchedule1A()` private method in `TaxReturnComputeService`: Part I MAGI, Part II tips (W-2 box 7 + Form 4137 + manual, cap $25k, FLOOR phaseout), Part III overtime (manual W-2 box 1 + non-W-2 sources, single $12.5k / MFJ $25k cap, same FLOOR phaseout), Part IV car loan interest (per vehicle, cap $10k, CEILING phaseout), Part V enhanced senior ($6k/person, 6% phaseout). MFJ gate: Parts II/III/V require MFJ when married. SSN gate applied per person.
- `Deductions.java`: `additionalDeductions` field (line 13b).
- `TaxReturnComputation` record: `schedule1A` parameter between `form8995A` and `form4797`.
- `TaxReturnDataService`: `schedule1A` persistence/retrieval.
- `PersonalResource`: `additional-deductions-taxpayer` and `additional-deductions-spouse` form IDs.
- 10 unit tests covering below-phaseout, phased-out, overtime capped, MFJ combined, car-loan phaseout, senior single/MFJ, MFS blocked, combined parts, null-when-no-inputs.

Frontend:
- `form-additional-deductions.component.ts/html`: Angular standalone component; taxpayer shows all 5 parts + vehicle list; spouse shows Parts II/III/V only.
- `shell.component.ts`: imported, wired in template and deductionItems (label "Additional deductions") before QBI deduction.

YAMLs: `C:\us-tax\yamls\13b-additional-deductions-taxpayer.yaml`, `C:\us-tax\yamls\13b-additional-deductions-spouse.yaml`.

E2E: `e2e/tests/line13b-additional-deductions.spec.ts` � 7 scenarios: single tips below phaseout, MFJ combined tips, overtime, car loan below phaseout, senior eligible, combined parts, MFS tips blocked / car loan applies.

Diagram: `C:\us-tax\diagrams\13b.drawio`.

- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.

- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.

- Coordination and sequencing:

- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.

  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:39.6208883-05:00

## 2026-01-16T18:49:40.6208883-05:00
All the forms under statements are the documents issued by the government or financial institutes. User should be able to upload the document. Later we will use an AI service to parse the document and populate these forms. We will not upload and store the files. This will be a security issue. For now just add an upload button to all these forms under statements category. You might want to add an icon within the button as well. For now it will be just a dummy button doing nothing. User will also be able to enter the forms manually. The save and load mechanism will work the same way as in the forms in Personal category. Right under the statements node in the sidebar add a sub-node "Select form". When a user clicks on it, it will show a form with a list of forms which a user can select. Only selected forms will show in the Statements category.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:42.6208883-05:00
by default none of the forms should be selected except w-2

## 2026-01-16T18:49:43.6208883-05:00
Some people might have multiple statements for example somebody worked for multiple employers during a tax year will receive multiple w-2 forms. Based on this fact change the UI so that a user can add multiple forms of the same type e.g. multi w-2 forms. This is true for all forms under statements. First propose the change then implement it.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:44.6208883-05:00
Did you consider deletion of a form as well or not?

## 2026-01-16T18:49:45.6208883-05:00
yes please. Also if a user de-select a form from select form screen then the corresponding form should be deleted after confirmation.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:46.6208883-05:00
on the spouse form data is stored in firestore but it does not load. Maybe it is a general problem in all forms can you fix it for all of them.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:47.6208883-05:00
When I remove a dependent, it does not get deleted in the database. On refresh it appears again. As a general rule each section should have a clear button to clear out the fields in that section. Apply this rule on all forms.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:48.6208883-05:00
A user should be able to Remove a section even if there is only one section in sections of multiplicity multiple. On delete it should delete the data in the database and clear the section fields but leave the section to enter different data if user requires. For any delete or clear please get confirmation from the user with a warning that data will be lost.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:49.6208883-05:00
I want clear button per section. Apply this to all forms.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:50.6208883-05:00
When I said clear per section I meant this. In the dependents form there can be more than one dependents so add a clear button for each dependent.

## 2026-01-16T18:49:51.6208883-05:00
If there is only one dependent and if I click the clear button it says the data will be deleted but actually it does not delete the data. So how would you delete all the dependents?

## 2026-01-20T23:20:52.1761258-05:00
You should create a yaml file in the yaml folder specifying a form for the employment income. Name it employment_income.yaml. In this form You should ask this question, Did you have employment income during the tax year? If the user responds yes then ask the next question, Did you upload/submit your W-2 form(s)? If the user is submitting a joint income tax with his / her spouse then the next question should be, Did your spouse have employment income during the tax year? If the answer is yes then the next question should be, Did you upload/submit your spouse's W-2 form(s)? Then based on this yaml file create a component for a form for employment income. In the sidebar there is already a subnode for Employment. Link the component with this subnode. In the form there should be two sections one for the filer and one for the spouse depending on the situation. User responses should be saved in firestore. When the user clicks on Compute return update the line 1a in the 1040. Make sure to comment the relevant code in the compute code so that in future a programmer or AI can figure out the mapping from the frontend employment form to the 1a line in 1040. Let me know where you have added the comments so that I could verify them as well.

## 2026-01-28T22:07:40.3133898-05:00
Make sure that you create a table in the code which we will call reference data. This will contain data which changes year to year. The very first entry will be TAX_YEAR. Lets use 2025 as a tax year. Then based on the rules I shared with you you need to define a parameter HOUSE_HOLD_EMPLOYEE_THRESHOLD. I am sure you know this amount for 2025. Ignore the data structure proposed in the text I shared but consider the input form questions you should ask the tax payer. Once again these questions will be for both the tax payer and their spouse. These questions will be separated into two sections. One for the tax payer and another one for the spouse. Include these sections in the existing employment_income.yaml and then add them to the corresponding component in the frontend application. Of course you will add backend endpoints to support to save and retrieve the fields in these sections. When the user computes the return you know that you need to compute the value value to go into the line 1b and later in the corresponding pdf file for the return. Make sure that you add the comments about the rules that you have applied to compute the return. It should be detailed and clear enough for a developer / AI to work with. Do not forget to update the history and context files. There is one more thing which is a general rule which you should always apply. All amounts should be rounded to the nearest dollar value. Here is a guideline. According to a document from 23-Jan-2025, the IRS allows an optional �round to whole dollars� rule when completing Form 1040 (and its schedules).
IRS currency rounding rule (Form 1040 return)
1) Whole-dollar rounding is optional
�You may round cents to whole dollars on the return and schedules.
2) If you round, you must round everything
�If you choose whole-dollar rounding, you must round all amounts on the return and schedules (not �some lines rounded, some not�).
3) The rounding threshold is 50 cents
�Drop amounts under $0.50.
�Increase amounts $0.50 to $0.99 to the next dollar.
�Examples given by IRS:
o$1.39 ? $1
o$2.50 ? $3
4) For computed lines, round only the final total
If a line is the result of adding two or more amounts:
�Add using cents (full precision), then
�Round only the total that goes on the line.
5) If you don�t round, you can still enter cents
�If you enter an amount that includes cents, include the decimal point.
�There is no separate cents column on Form 1040 / 1040-SR (so cents are written as part of the same number, e.g., 1234.56).
________________________________________
Implementation-ready �return rule� for tax software
Here�s a developer/AI-friendly rule spec you can apply across all money fields.
Global return setting
ReturnSetting.roundingMode
�Allowed values:
oWHOLE_DOLLAR (IRS optional)
oEXACT (store/display with cents)
Rounding function (Whole dollars)
Use �nearest dollar, 0.50 rounds up�:
�If roundingMode == WHOLE_DOLLAR then apply:
orounded = round_half_up(amount)
Recommended exact definition (works for negatives too):
�round_half_up(x) = floor(x + 0.5) if x >= 0
�round_half_up(x) = ceil(x - 0.5) if x < 0
IRS explicitly defines the threshold behavior for positive examples; the above is the standard symmetric extension for negative values so your system behaves consistently when negative amounts occur.
Computation rule for lines (critical)
When a line is derived from multiple inputs (sum of W-2s, sum of forms, etc.):
�Maintain inputs in cents precision internally (e.g., integer cents or decimal with 2 dp).
�Compute: raw_line_amount = S(raw_inputs) using cents precision.
�Output formatting:
oIf WHOLE_DOLLAR: line_amount = round_half_up(raw_line_amount)
oIf EXACT: line_amount = raw_line_amount (show cents via decimal point)
Validation rule
If roundingMode == WHOLE_DOLLAR:
�Every money field emitted to the �final return values� layer should be an integer dollar amount (no decimals).
If roundingMode == EXACT:
�Allow up to 2 decimal places, and display with a decimal point (since there�s no cents column).

## 2026-01-29T22:45:00.8662395-05:00
Here is information about line 1c and Form 4137/Schedule 2 mapping (tips not in W-2 box 1, allocated tips box 8, non-cash tips, Form 4137 triggers, Schedule 2 line 5, Form 1040 line 23) plus a reviewed YAML spec for a tips input form at C:\us-tax\yaml\reviewed\Personal\incomes\1c-tip-income-taxpayer.yaml. ## 2026-01-29T23:21:02.8373054-05:00

## 2026-01-29T23:28:08.6023099-05:00

## 2026-01-31T00:00:00-05:00
Added global loading overlay in the UI via an HTTP interceptor, and created a Firestore nuke utility (node script + wrappers) to delete all collections with explicit confirmation.

## 2026-01-31T16:21:35.3480321-05:00
Reviewed line 1c implementation in the backend: tip_income_taxpayer.yaml and tip_income_spouse.yaml exist; TaxReturnComputeService computes line 1c from tip entries (with W-2 allocated tips fallback), builds Form 4137, applies Schedule 2 unreported tip tax, and emits a blocking flag when W-2 Social Security wage/tip data is missing.

## 2026-01-31T16:29:33.9997647-05:00
Reviewed line 1c UI implementation in C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui: tip income taxpayer/spouse forms with W-2 auto-fill and validation, sidebar entries, Form 4137 and Schedule 2 read-only views with semantic-label PDF exports, and 1040 PDF mapping for line 1c.

## 2026-01-31T19:57:07.5375263-05:00
Ensured Firestore statement parent docs are created/updated when saving statement entries so collections appear in the console.

## 2026-01-31T21:09:51.5091834-05:00
Ensured users/{uid} parent docs are created/updated when saving statement entries so Firestore shows statement collections under users.

## 2026-01-31T21:27:50.9393578-05:00
Restructured statement storage to use users/{uid}/statements/{entryId} with a formId field, plus legacy read fallbacks.

## 2026-01-31T21:40:28.2327487-05:00
Removed statements query orderBy to avoid Firestore composite index requirements; sorting now happens in memory by createdAt.

## 2026-01-31T21:52:29.9840506-05:00
Moved statement entries to per-form collections under users/{uid} (e.g., users/{uid}/w-2/{entryId}) with legacy read fallbacks.

## 2026-02-01T02:15:00-05:00
Added a one-time migration that copies legacy statement data into per-form user subcollections, deletes legacy statement documents, and removed legacy read/write fallbacks.

## 2026-02-01T02:22:00-05:00
Removed the statement migration logic and legacy-path cleanup code, leaving only per-form statement collections under users/{uid}.

## 2026-02-01T02:30:00-05:00
Updated the UI loading overlay to display "Saving data..." for non-GET requests and "Loading data..." for GET requests.

## 2026-02-01T21:57:48.7745523-05:00
Implemented Form 1040 line 1d Medicaid waiver payments: added taxpayer/spouse UI forms with W-2 code II auto-fill, updated Income sidebar nodes and 1040 preview/PDF mapping, and added backend unit test plus Playwright e2e coverage.

## 2026-02-06T15:07:59.3947717-05:00
Implemented Form 1040 wage lines 1a-1i across backend and UI (employment income, household wages, tips/4137, Medicaid waiver, dependent care/2441, adoption/8839, uncollected SS/Medicare/8919, other earned income, and combat pay election) with per-spouse handling where required, plus line 1z as the sum of lines 1a-1h only (explicitly excluding line 1i). Added unit/e2e coverage, logging, and 1040 preview/PDF mappings for these lines, and hardened DOB input handling with datepicker-based UI tests.

## 2026-02-07T10:55:49.1845485-05:00
Clarified that imported fields (prepopulated from statements/personal forms) remain editable because users may not upload statements; updated sidebar order to place Statements above Income.

## 2026-02-08T02:55:17.3854835-05:00
Split shared Personal forms into per-person instances for Digital Assets, Standard Deduction, Age/Blindness, and Presidential Election Campaign. Updated UI routing and form inputs, added backend per-person mapping logic (with legacy fallbacks), and added unit/e2e coverage for per-person indicator mapping.

## 2026-02-10T00:00:00-05:00
Filtered Income sidebar items by person tab so Family Head hides �(Spouse)� labels and Spouse shows only �(Spouse)� labels. Added unit coverage for income filtering in ShellComponent and expanded e2e sidebar assertions.

## 2026-02-09T22:14:29.2577547-05:00
Removed (You)/(Spouse) suffixes from Income sidebar labels, added data-form-id attributes to sidebar links for stable selection, updated income filtering tests to use id suffixes, and adjusted Playwright navigation to click income forms by form id (stabilized line 1b, 1d, 1g, and 1i e2e flows).

## 2026-02-09T22:38:31.1852571-05:00
Stabilized Income navigation tests by relying on data-form-id selectors and selectOption for combat pay dropdowns; updated e2e specs for line 1b/1c/1d/1g/1i/1z and income filtering assertions.

## 2026-02-10T00:36:12.0000000-05:00
Added compute-time validation requiring a W-2 when employment income is reported without household work; added blocking flags plus unit and e2e coverage.

## 2026-02-10T00:41:30.0000000-05:00
Added Employment income UI warnings when wages are reported without household work and no W-2 is present, aligning with compute-time blocking.

## 2026-02-10T00:55:00.0000000-05:00
Added a backend /api/reference-data endpoint and a UI ReferenceDataService so tax-year constants (tax year, household employee W-2 threshold, deferral limits, adoption phaseouts) are loaded instead of hardcoded in forms.

## 2026-02-10T01:12:00-05:00
Removed the Employment Income Form 8919 household-wage question and always map household employee wages without a W-2 to Form 1040 line 1b. Updated UI validation/help text, backend household wage computation, and line 1b e2e coverage.

## 2026-02-13T23:26:38.6484734-05:00
Gaps noted for Form 1040 line 1c implementation: 
1. Additional Medicare Tax (0.9%) on wage/tip amounts over filing-status thresholds is not computed or mapped to Schedule 2.
2. Form 4137 trigger does not enforce the -per-month rule; forms are created whenever line 4 > 0.
3. Allocated tips override logic does not allow actual unreported tips to exceed allocated tips unless substantiated lower.
4. Tip computations use unfiltered W-2 entries for SS wage base and allocated-tip fallback; per-spouse filtering is missing.
5. Tip-form SS wages/tips inputs are ignored when W-2 SS data is missing (missing-SS flag fires even if user supplied values).

## 2026-02-13T23:26:38.6795956-05:00
Implementation plan to address line 1c gaps:
1. Add Additional Medicare Tax thresholds + rate to ReferenceData for 2025 (single/HOH/QSS 200k, MFJ 250k, MFS 125k).
2. Update tip computation to filter W-2 data per person (SSN), use tip-form SS box 3/7 values as fallback for SS wage base, and adjust allocated-tip override to use actual unreported tips when higher unless substantiated lower.
3. Enforce the -per-month Form 4137 trigger: only generate when unreported tips subject to Medicare are > 0 or allocated tips exist.
4. Compute Additional Medicare Tax from W-2 Box 5 + unreported tips subject to Medicare using filing status thresholds; map to Schedule 2 (additionalMedicareTax) and include in totals.
5. Add a backend unit test covering Additional Medicare Tax calculation.

## 2026-02-14T06:40:26.0044251-05:00
Completed line 1c gap fixes: added Additional Medicare thresholds/rate, updated tip computation for per-person W-2 filtering and SS wage/tip fallback, corrected allocated-tip override logic, enforced the Form 4137  trigger, computed Additional Medicare Tax into Schedule 2 totals, and added a unit test for Additional Medicare Tax.

## 2026-02-14T06:46:24.3522110-05:00
Implementation plan: expand test coverage for line 1c updates.
1. Add backend unit tests for allocated-tip override (both directions), -per-month 4137 trigger, tip-form SS wage/tip fallback, and MFJ Additional Medicare threshold behavior.
2. Add Playwright e2e tests for allocated tip override, actual > allocated, and -per-month 4137 suppression.
3. Run no tests (unless requested) and record completion in history.

## 2026-02-28T21:27:00.0652156-05:00
Completed: deep-pass architecture and risk analysis across C:\us-tax\us-tax-be and C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui.

Scope covered:
- Backend architecture, resources/services, Firestore data model, compute engine flow, config/security filters, and tests.
- Frontend routing/guards, service/API integration, proxy/runtime config, shell/form orchestration, and tests.

Key findings:
- AuthService.disableUser / enableUser build update requests but do not persist via firebaseAuth.updateUser(...).
- /auth/users/disable and /auth/users/enable are not protected by @Authenticated/roles.
- Compute UX currently does loadFlags() then computeReturn(), causing duplicated prepare(uid) work.
- Inactivity timeout mismatch: code uses 1 hour while context history notes a 5-minute policy.
- Frontend tests are mostly smoke-level; form-heavy business logic has limited targeted unit tests.
- FE/BE statement-form catalogs are duplicated, increasing drift risk.

References captured in analysis:
- Backend hotspots: AuthService.java, AuthResource.java, TaxReturnResource.java, TaxReturnComputeService.java, StatementDataService.java, PersonalDataService.java.
- Frontend hotspots: app.topbar.ts, tax-return.service.ts, statement-selection.service.ts, app.settings.ts, shell.component.ts, form components.


## 2026-03-01T18:45:53.7879220-05:00
Note: frontend statement YAML files are located in C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\YAML.


## 2026-03-01T18:54:58.9498535-05:00
Note: YAML/PDF alignment decisions for 1099-INT, 1099-DIV, 1099-OID.
- Item 3 accepted: extra YAML *Notes fields are intentional; some printed amounts are followed by explanatory text and we capture that text separately.
- Item 2 accepted: splitting payer combined fields (including phone) in YAML is allowed.
- Item 4 accepted: YAML state rows may be modeled as multiple; row limits will be enforced in frontend component code.


## 2026-03-01T22:48:16.4073288-05:00
Rule: self-employment income is out of scope; exclude any self-employment-related inputs/logic from implementation.



## 2026-03-01T23:20:36.3300606-05:00
Approach update for line-level YAML/user-input forms:
- Keep workflow gating first (eligibility, required statement-upload checks, and block-continue conditions).
- Keep supplemental user-entered fields needed for the line computation.
- It is acceptable to keep imported statement fields and computed fields in the same YAML for mapping/compute contracts, but mark these sections clearly as backend-only and not for actual form-component rendering.
- Keep structure consistent with prior YAML patterns (sections/fields with readOnly/computed flags and explanatory comments).
- Self-employment scope remains excluded.

## 2026-03-02T05:02:01.1148322-05:00
Approach update for implementing a tax-return line (spouse-aware):
- Create separate taxpayer and spouse YAML/input forms when both spouses can contribute amounts.
- Keep any return-level or conflict-prone questions (upload gating, schedule-level gating, override switches that affect whole-return calculations) only on the taxpayer/family-head form.
- Allow spouse form to collect only spouse-specific supplemental/adjustment amounts used by computation.
- Keep imported statement fields and computed fields in YAML as backend-only sections with explicit comments; do not render those sections in UI components.
- Backend compute aggregates taxpayer + spouse contributions into Form 1040 lines, and conditionally generates supplemental forms (for example Schedule B and Form 6251) that the frontend shows in Tax Return sidebar when present.

## 2026-03-02T05:06:59.2906407-05:00
Cleanup: removed legacy single-file 2ab YAML (C:\us-tax\yamls\2ab-interest-income.yaml) and retained spouse-split specs (2ab-interest-income-taxpayer.yaml, 2ab-interest-income-spouse.yaml).
