## 2026-03-15T00:00:00-04:00 � E2E Problems Encountered and Fixes Applied

This section documents every Playwright e2e problem encountered across the full 88-test suite build-out, the root causes, and how each was resolved. All 88 tests pass cleanly after these fixes.

---

### Problem 1 � Playwright worker `spawn EPERM` in Claude Code sandbox

**Symptom:** Running `npm test` inside the Claude Code sandbox threw `spawn EPERM` immediately; no tests could start.

**Root cause:** The sandbox restricts `child_process.spawn`, which Playwright uses internally to launch browser workers.

**Fix:** Run all Playwright commands outside the sandbox (directly in PowerShell/bash terminal). The sandbox restriction does not affect test execution in a real terminal. No code changes required.

---

### Problem 2 � Stale `--output` directory causes `EPERM` on re-run

**Symptom:** Re-running a test with the same `--output=<dir>` path threw an `EPERM` access error before tests could start.

**Root cause:** Playwright tries to clean the output directory on startup and Windows holds locks on subdirectories from the previous run.

**Fix:** Always pass a fresh `--output=<dir>` value (e.g. timestamp-suffixed) on each run, or omit `--output` and let Playwright use the default `test-results/` folder which it reuses safely.

---

### Problem 3 � `injectFirebaseAuth: E2E_SHARED_AUTH_EMAIL and E2E_SHARED_AUTH_PASSWORD must be set`

**Symptom:** Tests failed immediately with the above error when run from bash.

**Root cause:** The env vars were set in the PowerShell session but bash does not inherit them.

**Fix:** Set env vars explicitly in the shell that invokes Playwright:
```powershell
$env:E2E_SHARED_AUTH_EMAIL = "user@example.com"
$env:E2E_SHARED_AUTH_PASSWORD = "yourpassword"
npx playwright test --project=regression --workers=1 <spec>
```

---

### Problem 4 � `run-all-tests.ps1 -PlaywrightArgs` runs all tests instead of the specified files

**Symptom:** Passing `-PlaywrightArgs "tests/line1i-combat-pay.spec.ts tests/line7ab-capital-gain-loss.spec.ts"` ran the full 88-test suite.

**Root cause:** The PS1 script splatted `$args` to the inner `run-e2e.ps1` wrapper; the file path strings were not forwarded correctly to `npx playwright test` when passed as a single argument string.

**Fix:** Bypass the wrapper scripts entirely when running a subset of tests. Set env vars directly and invoke `npx playwright test` with explicit file paths:
```powershell
$env:E2E_SHARED_AUTH_EMAIL = "ali.muhammad.ca@gmail.com"
$env:E2E_SHARED_AUTH_PASSWORD = "..."
cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\e2e
npx playwright test --project=regression --workers=1 tests/line1i-combat-pay.spec.ts tests/line7ab-capital-gain-loss.spec.ts
```

---

### Problem 5 � Filing-status dropdown flake (`Unable to set filing status to MFJ`)

**Symptom:** Several specs failed intermittently when trying to navigate to and select `Married filing jointly` from the PrimeNG dropdown during `beforeEach` setup. The failure was timing-sensitive and only appeared in long multi-spec batches.

**Root cause:** The PrimeNG p-select dropdown requires Angular to have rendered and the filing-status personal form to have loaded. In back-to-back test runs the API response from the previous test's save was still in-flight, causing the component to be in a loading state when the test tried to interact with it.

**Fix:** Replaced the UI-only approach with an API-first save path. `api-flow.ts` helper `setupFilingStatus()` now:
1. First tries `page.request` with the real Firebase bearer token to POST/PUT `filing-status` directly to the API.
2. Falls back to an authenticated browser `fetch` with the same token.
3. Only if both API paths fail, falls back to the PrimeNG UI dropdown.

This eliminated the filing-status flake from all affected specs (`line2ab`, `line3ab`, `line4abc`, `line6abcd`, `line13a`).

---

### Problem 6 � Statement selection auto-created blank entries, satisfying upload gates incorrectly

**Symptom:** After the `form-statements-select.component.ts` change that auto-opened a statement form when selected, the interest/dividend/IRA "block save when no statements uploaded" e2e tests started passing when they should have blocked.

**Root cause:** Selecting a statement now auto-created a blank first entry. The taxpayer interview forms counted raw statement entry counts (=1 = uploaded), so blank placeholder entries incorrectly satisfied the upload gate.

**Fix:**
- `form-statements-select.component.ts`: stopped creating placeholder entries during selection.
- `form-interest-income-taxpayer.component.ts`, `form-dividend-income-taxpayer.component.ts`, `form-ira-income-taxpayer.component.ts`: changed upload gate to count only entries with meaningful saved data (at least one non-empty field), not raw counts.
- Same fix applied to `form-pension-annuity-income-taxpayer.component.ts` for the 1099-R / RRB-1099-R entries.

---

### Problem 7 � Sidebar `hasText` assertions caused strict-mode collisions with new sidebar items

**Symptom:** `person-tabs-labels.spec.ts` failed with a strict-mode "multiple elements matched" error after new sidebar items (e.g. `1099-NEC`) were added.

**Root cause:** The spec was locating sidebar sections with broad `hasText` matching, which started matching across multiple sections when new labels with overlapping text were added.

**Fix:** Changed sidebar section locators to use `.sidebar-section-title` CSS class instead of broad `hasText`, making matches exact and immune to new items being added to other sections.

---

### Problem 8 � `Form1099DivPage.navigate()` timeout (old `waitForResponse` + race pattern)

**Symptom:** `line3ab-dividend-income.spec.ts` failed with a timeout when navigating from a different statement context; `waitForResponse` on the `GET /entries/{id}` request never fired within 10 seconds.

**Root cause:** `navigate()` set up a `waitForResponse` listener for the statement entry GET before clicking the sidebar item. When navigating from another statement form, the first sidebar click sometimes triggered a response for the *other* form's entry GET, not 1099-DIV. The race resolved on a mis-matched response and the 1099-DIV form was never confirmed.

**Fix:** Replaced the `waitForResponse` + race with a retry-click loop:
- Up to 4 attempts: click sidebar ? wait for `h3[hasText='Form 1099-DIV']` to appear (8 s timeout per attempt).
- After heading confirmed: wait for the first amount input field (`#totalOrdinaryDividendsAmount`) to become visible (15 s).
- This pattern is robust to navigation from any starting state.

---

### Problem 9 � `Form1099BPage.navigate()` timeout (same `waitForResponse` + race pattern)

**Symptom:** `line7ab-capital-gain-loss.spec.ts` failed with a timeout in the 1099-B form navigation step.

**Root cause:** Identical to Problem 8 � `Form1099BPage.navigate()` had the same `waitForResponse` + field-race pattern with a short 10 s timeout.

**Fix:** Applied the identical retry-click + h3-heading confirmation pattern:
- Up to 4 attempts: click sidebar ? wait for `h3[hasText='Form 1099-B']` (8 s per attempt).
- After heading confirmed: wait for `#proceedsAmount` visible (15 s).

File changed: `e2e/tests/pages/statement-forms/form-1099-b.page.ts`.

---

### Problem 10 � `line1i-combat-pay.spec.ts` spouse test: `nontaxableCombatPayElection = 0` instead of 1200

**Symptom:** The spouse combat-pay test filled a W-2 with `employeeSSN: '222-22-2222'` and Box 12 code Q = 1200, elected spouse combat pay, then triggered compute. The result came back with `nontaxableCombatPayElection = 0` instead of 1200.

**Root cause (multi-step async race):**

1. `FormW2Page.navigate()` used a `Promise.race` between `waitForResponse(GET /entries/{id})` and `field.waitFor({ state: 'visible' })`.
2. `#employeeSSN` became **visible** as soon as Angular rendered the template � this is a synchronous DOM operation.
3. `loadEntry()` (inside `StatementFormDirective`) is **async**: it resets the Angular model to `initialModel` (all empty strings), then fetches the entry from the API, then applies the API data. This happens milliseconds *after* the DOM renders.
4. The race resolved on `field visible` (step 2), so `fill()` ran immediately, writing `employeeSSN = '222-22-2222'` and `addBox12Entry(code Q = 1200)`.
5. The `loadEntry()` async callback then completed (step 3), resetting `employeeSSN` back to `''` (the initial model value). The box12 entry survived because it was filled after the reset by a re-entry click, but `employeeSSN` was permanently empty.
6. Backend `computeCombatPay` called `sumW2Box12ByCodes` which filtered W-2 entries by `normalize(employeeSSN) == spouseSSN`. With `employeeSSN = ''`, no W-2 matched. `spouseTotal = null` ? `spouseElected = BigDecimal.ZERO` ? `line1i = 0`.

Note: The taxpayer test coincidentally passed because the timing was slightly different and box12 filled after the loadEntry reset, but the root cause was always present.

**Fix:** Removed the `Promise.race` from `FormW2Page.navigate()`. Replaced with:
```typescript
await field.waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 10000 });
await this.page.waitForLoadState('networkidle', { timeout: 15000 }).catch(() => undefined);
```
`waitForLoadState('networkidle')` waits for all pending HTTP requests to complete (the `ensureEntries` list GET and `loadEntry` single-entry GET) AND for Angular to process their responses (microtask queue drains). This guarantees `loadEntry()` has fully reset and applied the model before `fill()` runs.

File changed: `e2e/tests/pages/statement-forms/form-w2.page.ts`.

---

### Problem 11 � `hasCombatPay()` returns false until W-2 async load completes, disabling `#electCombatPay`

**Symptom (related to Problem 10):** Even after Problem 10 was fixed, there was concern that `selectOption()` on `#electCombatPay` might be silently rejected because `[disabled]="!hasCombatPay()"` returns `true` while the combat-pay-spouse component is still loading W-2 totals.

**Root cause:** `FormCombatPaySpouseComponent.ngOnInit()` is async: loads the form data, loads the spouse SSN, then calls `refreshW2Totals()` which GETs all W-2 entries and sums code Q. Until that chain completes, `combatPayTotal` is `null` and `hasCombatPay()` is `false`, disabling the p-select.

**Fix:** `CombatPayPage._openFormById()` already calls `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` and waits for the loading overlay to disappear and `#electCombatPay` to be visible. The networkidle wait covers the `refreshW2Totals()` async chain as well, so `hasCombatPay()` is `true` by the time `selectOption()` runs. No additional fix needed � the existing open pattern is sufficient.

---

### Final verification

After all fixes above, the full regression suite was run:
```powershell
$env:E2E_SHARED_AUTH_EMAIL = "ali.muhammad.ca@gmail.com"
$env:E2E_SHARED_AUTH_PASSWORD = "..."
cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\e2e
npx playwright test --project=regression --workers=1
```
Result: **88/88 tests passed**.

---

## 2026-03-15T12:00:00-04:00 � Line 13b Implementation Plan and Context Update

### Context corrections from user review
- **1099-MISC and 1099-K are already in the statement catalog** (`StatementFormCatalog.java` includes both `"1099-misc"` and `"1099-k"`). Their PDFs live in `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\PDF\` (as `1099msc.pdf` and `1099k.pdf`), and their intake YAMLs are in `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\YAML\` (`1099-MISC.yaml`, `1099-K.yaml`). They appear in the Statements section of the UI. Data from these statements can be auto-imported; manual entry fallback must also be available.
- **Form 4563** (`f4563.pdf`) was added to `C:\us-tax\docs\IRS-Forms` by the user. The existing script `generate-semantic-line13b-input-forms.js` already includes it in FORM_CONFIGS � it was previously skipped because the file was missing.
- **Form 2555** semantic assets already exist in `C:\us-tax\pdfs\` (`f2555_semantic_labels.pdf`, `f2555_field_map_semantic.csv`).
- **Remaining true gaps**: W-2 box 1 overtime is not separately identified on the W-2 (must be user-entered); all Part IV car loan interest inputs are manual (no IRS statement form); Form 4563 was missing but is now available.

### Schedule 1-A input sources (confirmed)
| Source | Part | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Form 1040 line 11b | Part I MAGI base | Computed internally |
| Puerto Rico excluded income | Part I line 2a | Manual entry |
| Form 2555 lines 45/50 | Part I lines 2b/2c | Semantic assets exist; statement not in catalog (out of scope for most filers) |
| Form 4563 line 15 | Part I line 2d | PDF now added; semantic assets to be generated |
| W-2 box 7 | Part II tips ? line 4a | Auto-imported from W-2 |
| Form 4137 | Part II unreported tips ? line 4b | Auto-imported from 4137 |
| 1099-NEC box 1 | Parts II/III | In catalog; semantic assets exist |
| 1099-MISC box 3 | Parts II/III | In catalog; semantic assets to be generated from UI PDF |
| 1099-K box 1a | Part II | In catalog; semantic assets to be generated from UI PDF |
| W-2 box 1 (overtime portion) | Part III ? line 14a | User-entered (not separately on W-2) |
| Vehicle loan interest + VIN | Part IV | Fully manual entry |
| DOB (born < Jan 2 1961) | Part V senior check | Already in personal data |
| Filing status / SSN | Parts II/III/V gates | Already in personal data |

### Output form
- **Schedule 1-A** ? `Form1040.line13b = Schedule1A.line38`
- Semantic assets: `f1040s1a_semantic_labels.pdf` + `f1040s1a_field_map_semantic.csv` already in `C:\us-tax\pdfs\`

## 2026-03-15T13:00:00-04:00 � Line 13b Step 1 and Step 2 Completed

- `f1099msc_semantic_labels.pdf` + `f1099msc_field_map_semantic.csv` (119 fields) � Form 1099-MISC. Source: `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\PDF\1099msc.pdf`. Generated via new `generate-semantic-13b-statement-forms.js`.
- `f1099k_semantic_labels.pdf` + `f1099k_field_map_semantic.csv` (151 fields) � Form 1099-K. Source: `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\PDF\1099k.pdf`. Generated via same new script.
- All outputs in `C:\us-tax\pdfs\`.

**YAML specs created:**
- `C:\us-tax\yamls\13b-additional-deductions-taxpayer.yaml` � taxpayer/family-head form covering: screening, statement upload confirmation (W-2/4137/1099-NEC/MISC/K), MAGI exclusions (Puerto Rico, Form 2555, Form 4563), qualified tips (Part II taxpayer), qualified overtime (Part III taxpayer), car loan interest entries (Part IV, return-level, multiple vehicles with VIN), enhanced senior deduction (Part V taxpayer), and backend-only imported/computed sections.
- `C:\us-tax\yamls\13b-additional-deductions-spouse.yaml` � spouse form covering: spouse-specific qualified tips (Part II), qualified overtime (Part III), and enhanced senior deduction (Part V), plus backend-only imported/computed sections.

**Key design decisions recorded:**
- Car loan interest (Part IV) is return-level � on taxpayer form only; no spouse equivalent.
- MAGI exclusions (Part I) are return-level � on taxpayer form only.
- MFJ requirement for Parts II, III, V is enforced as an instruction/gate; the backend must validate filing status before computing those parts.
- W-2 box 1 overtime is not separately broken out on the W-2 � must be user-entered manually; help text directs user to pay records.
- Tips from 1099-MISC box 3 and 1099-K box 1a can be auto-imported from uploaded statements OR manually entered as fallback.
- 1099-MISC and 1099-K are confirmed already in the statement catalog; their semantic assets were missing and have now been generated.

**Pending (Steps 3-5):**
- Backend compute: `computeSchedule1A()` in `TaxReturnComputeService.java`, `PersonalResource.java` acceptance, `Schedule1A` output persistence.
- Frontend: Angular form components, `Deductions` sidebar wiring, Tax Return Schedule 1-A view.
- E2E: `line13b-additional-deductions.spec.ts`.

## 2026-03-15 � Lines 14 and 15 Implemented

### Line 14 � Total Deductions

**Formula:** `line14 = line12e + line13a + line13b` (no floor; floor is applied on line 15).

**What changed:**
- `Deductions.java`: `totalDeductions` field already existed; added Javadoc noting it is Form 1040 line 14.
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java`: `totalDeductions` already computed as `line12e + line13a + line13b` in `computeLine12()` and finalized in `prepare()` after Schedule 1-A.
- Unit tests: `TaxLine15TaxableIncomeTest.java` covers `totalDeductions` indirectly via the line 15 formula.
- E2E: `e2e/tests/line14-total-deductions.spec.ts` � 5 scenarios asserting `deductions.totalDeductions` directly:
  1. Standard deduction only (13a = 0, 13b = 0)
  2. Standard deduction + tips deduction (line 13b)
  3. Standard deduction + overtime deduction (line 13b)
  4. Standard deduction + QBI deduction (line 13a, K-1 1065 seed)
  5. MFJ standard deduction
- Diagram: `C:\us-tax\diagrams\line14.drawio` (updated title; shows 12e+13a+13b ? totalDeductions ? line 15).

### Line 15 � Taxable Income

**Formula:** `line15 = max(0, line11b - line14)` � floor at zero; taxable income is never negative.

**What changed:**
- `Deductions.java`: `line15TaxableIncome` field added.
- `TaxReturnComputeService.java`:
  - `computeLine12()`: sets interim `line15TaxableIncome` (before line 13b is available).
  - `prepare()`: sets final `line15TaxableIncome = max(0, AGI - totalDeductions)` after Schedule 1-A is wired.
  - Log: `LOG.infof("Form 1040 line15 computed ...")` in `prepare()`.
- Unit tests: `TaxLine15TaxableIncomeTest.java` (renamed from `TaxLine14TaxableIncomeTest.java`) � 7 scenarios: standard only, QBI, additional deductions, all three, floor at zero, null AGI, MFJ high-income.
- E2E: `e2e/tests/line15-taxable-income.spec.ts` (renamed from `line14-taxable-income.spec.ts`) � 5 scenarios asserting `deductions.line15TaxableIncome`:
  1. Single, standard deduction only
  2. Single with tips deduction (line 13b)
  3. MFJ both spouses
  4. Floor at zero (wages < standard deduction)
  5. Overtime deduction (line 13b)
- Diagram: `C:\us-tax\diagrams\line15.drawio` � shows line11b + line14 ? `max(0, 11b-14)` ? line15 ? line16 (downstream).
- Spec `C:\us-tax\lines\14.md` and `C:\us-tax\lines\15.md` created with 2025-implementation-ready rule maps.

**Naming clarification (recorded for future reference):**
- On 2025 Form 1040: line 14 = total deductions (12e+13a+13b); line 15 = taxable income = max(0, 11b-14).
- Internal backend field `totalDeductions` = line 14; `line15TaxableIncome` = line 15.

**All tests passing (10 e2e + 7 unit).**

## 2026-03-14 � Dev Commands Reference

**Start backend (Quarkus dev mode):**
```powershell
cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-be
.\run-dev.ps1
```

**Start UI (Angular dev proxy):**
```powershell
cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui
npx ng serve --proxy-config proxy.conf.json
```

**Run all e2e tests (prompts for email + password):**
```powershell
cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\e2e
.\run-all-tests.ps1
```
Or pass credentials directly:
```powershell
.\run-all-tests.ps1 -Email "user@example.com" -Password "secret"
```
Or via env vars:
```powershell
$env:E2E_SHARED_AUTH_EMAIL = "user@example.com"
$env:E2E_SHARED_AUTH_PASSWORD = "secret"
.\run-all-tests.ps1
```

## 2026-04-01T01:11:06.9939429-04:00
Patched the last observed Playwright regression in `C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\e2e\tests\line1e-dependent-care.spec.ts`.

- Root cause: the final Form 2441 scenario was still using a local bare `saveChildcareForm()` and `computeReturn()` implementation. The error snapshot showed the app in `Saving data...` with the header `Compute return` button disabled, so the direct `page.waitForResponse('/api/tax-return/compute')` helper timed out without a request ever being sent.
- Fix: the spec now uses the shared guarded helpers from `C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\e2e\tests\helpers\api-flow.ts` for the childcare save and compute path: `savePersonalFormViaUi(page, 'childcare-expenses')` and `computeReturnViaUi(page, { timeoutMs: 45000 })`.
- This aligns the spec with the shared E2E stability rule already used by the earlier API-first Playwright refactors and avoids racing compute against an in-flight save.
- Live rerun of the Playwright spec is still pending here because this shell does not have the shared-auth env vars required by `clearUserData()`.

## 2026-04-01T09:44:33.7803692-04:00
Patched the last observed spouse standard-deductions Playwright regression in the shared sidebar helper.

- Root cause: the failing `personal-per-person-forms.spec.ts` scenario was not missing `someoneCanClaimSpouse` from the UI. The Playwright error snapshot showed the app still on spouse `Identification` after clicking `Deductions > Standard deductions`.
- Fix: `C:\us-tax\us-tax-be\e2e\tests\helpers\ui-flow.ts` now reads the clicked sidebar link's `data-form-id`, waits for that form to become `.active`, and retries when the click does not actually switch the active form.
- This keeps the existing Page Object Model approach intact and hardens all sidebar-driven E2E flows against silent no-op clicks.

## 2026-04-01T12:37:40.3541956-04:00
Patched the final full-suite spouse combat-pay regression at the UI attribution layer.

- Root cause: `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\src\app\forms\form-combat-pay-spouse.component.ts` still read the raw legacy `spouse` form to obtain the spouse SSN. After the spouse identification split, flows that saved only `identification-spouse` could leave that legacy read empty, and the spouse combat-pay component would then count any W-2 code Q row instead of filtering to the spouse.
- Fix: the spouse combat-pay component now uses `PersonalDataService.getSpouseIdentificationForm()`, normalizes SSNs to digits only, requires an exact spouse SSN match for W-2 combat-pay attribution, and treats missing spouse SSN as "no spouse combat pay" rather than counting unmatched W-2 rows.
- Coverage: added `C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui\src\app\forms\form-combat-pay-spouse.component.spec.ts` with focused tests proving taxpayer code Q W-2 rows do not appear on the spouse combat-pay flow and that exact spouse SSN matches still work.
- Verification: `cd C:\us-tax\us-tax-ui; npm run build` passed, and focused Angular test `npm test -- --watch=false --include="src/app/forms/form-combat-pay-spouse.component.spec.ts"` passed (`TOTAL: 2 SUCCESS`). The authenticated Playwright rerun still needs to be executed from the user shell.

## 2026-04-04T00:00:00-04:00
Implemented proper IRS PDF overlay views for statement forms (1099-LTC, 1099-SA, 1099-DA, 3921, 5498) and cleaned up the Statements sidebar.

### PDF Overlay Pattern (established across all statement forms)
- Background image extracted from IRS source PDF using `pdftoppm -r 150` (150dpi → 1275×1650px)
- Coordinate formula from `f{form}_field_map_semantic.csv`: `left=llx/612*100%`, `top=(792−ury)/792*100%`, `width=(urx−llx)/612*100%`, `height=(ury−lly)/792*100%`
- Clip container: `overflow:hidden; aspect-ratio:1275/N` (N chosen so all fields are visible)
- `pdfRaw: Record<string,string>` holds overlay field values ('X' for checkboxes, string amounts for text)
- `pdfView` getter/setter calls `syncFormToPdf()` on switch-to-PDF; Verify button calls `syncPdfToForm()` to write back to model
- Copy B (for recipient) page used as the overlay background in all cases
- Mapping files created in `C:\us-tax\mappings\` documenting rect coords, CSS positions, and model field bindings

### 1099-LTC (`form-1099-ltc.component.ts`)
- Removed corrected checkbox from PDF overlay (user request)
- Fixed Box 4 "Qualified contract" checkbox position using CSV coordinates
- Fixed INSURED's TIN field visibility (added border-bottom, placeholder)

### 1099-SA (`form-1099-sa.component.ts`)
- Removed corrected checkbox from PDF overlay (1099-SA has no corrected checkbox on recipient copy)

### 1099-DA (`form-1099-da.component.ts`) — full new implementation
- Created new dedicated standalone Angular component replacing the previous generic `FormCapitalStatementComponent` routing
- Extracted `f1099da_page_04.png` (Copy B, page 4) from IRS PDF via pdftoppm; published to `public/irs/`
- PDF overlay: 16 checkboxes (void, corrected, box2 basis, box3a gross/net, box3b reserved/QOF, box5 loss, box6 short/long/ordinary, box7 cash, box8 broker, box9 noncovered, box11a stablecoins/NFTs) + 42 text/textarea fields; clip `1275/1120`
- Form view: 9 sections covering all 122 YAML fields (copyA_ and copyOther_ prefixes); form binds to copyOther_ fields; `syncPdfToForm()` mirrors copyOther_ → copyA_
- Registered in `shell.component.ts` imports + template; removed from `capitalStatementFormIds`

### 3921 (`form-3921.component.ts`) — PDF overlay added
- Replaced fake CSS grid layout with proper IRS PDF overlay
- Extracted `f3921_page_03.png` (Copy B, page 3) via pdftoppm; published to `public/irs/`
- 13 text/textarea overlay fields (no void/corrected checkboxes); clip `1275/560`
- Employee name (firstName+suffix+lastName) and underlyingCorp (name+street+city) combined into single PDF fields; `syncPdfToForm()` splits them back
- Created `C:\us-tax\mappings\3921-field-mapping.md`

### 5498 (`form-5498.component.ts`) — PDF overlay added
- Replaced fake CSS grid layout with proper IRS PDF overlay
- Extracted `f5498_page_03.png` (Copy B, page 3) via pdftoppm; published to `public/irs/`
- 25 text/textarea fields + 5 checkboxes (Box 7 IRA/SEP/SIMPLE/Roth IRA, Box 11 RMD); clip `1275/830`
- No void/corrected checkboxes; all box placeholders use descriptive IRS label text
- Created `C:\us-tax\mappings\5498-field-mapping.md`

### Statements sidebar cleanup
- Removed Form 6781 from the Statements sidebar (`statement-selection.service.ts`) — not a statement; also removed from `capitalStatementFormIds`
- Removed Form 8814 (`child-interest-dividends`) from the Statements sidebar — not a statement

### Verification
- `npm run build` passed after each change (Angular 21, no errors)

## 2026-04-18T00:00:00-04:00
Line 20 / Schedule 3 audit — backend gaps fixed, UI violations fixed, tests added.

### Backend fixes (G1, G3, G5, G6)

- **G6 — Line 7 PDF bug fixed**: `finalizeSchedule3Totals()` now computes `totalOtherNonrefundableCredits` = sum(6a..6z) instead of 6z only. `totalNonrefundableCredits` formula changed to IRS structure (1+2+3+4+5a+5b+7) to eliminate double-count. File: `TaxReturnComputeService.java`.
- **G1 — Adoption credit CLW-B implemented**: `applyAdoptionCredit()` call site moved to after `applyForm8801ToSchedule3()` so lines 1–6b are set before CLW-B runs. CLW-B computes `creditLimit = totalTaxBeforeCredits − priorCredits`; line 18 = min(line16, creditLimit). `form8839.part2Line17CreditLimit` and `part2Line18NonrefundableAdoptionCredit` set; `schedule3.nc.adoptionCredit` (line 6c) wired. File: `TaxReturnComputeService.java`.
- **G3 — Fuel tax credit (Form 4136) → Schedule 3 line 12**: Manual-entry fields `hasFuelTaxCredit` + `creditForFederalTaxOnFuels` added to `31-other-payments-taxpayer.yaml`, `FormOtherPaymentsComponent`, and `applyOtherPaymentsFormToSchedule3()`.
- **G5 — §965(i) deferred tax → Schedule 3 line 13d**: Manual-entry fields `hasDeferred965Tax` + `deferredNet965TaxLiability` added to `31-other-payments-taxpayer.yaml`, `FormOtherPaymentsComponent`, and `applyOtherPaymentsFormToSchedule3()`.

### Unit tests added (TaxReturnComputeServiceTest.java — 5 new)
- `schedule3Line7TotalOtherNonrefundableCreditsIsSum6aThroughZ` (G6)
- `adoptionCreditNonrefundableWiredToSchedule3` (G1)
- `adoptionCreditZeroWhenPriorCreditsExhaustTax` (G1)
- `fuelTaxCreditFlowsToSchedule3Line12` (G3)
- `deferred965TaxFlowsToSchedule3Line13d` (G5)

### E2E tests added (line20-nonrefundable-credits.spec.ts — 2 new, total 5)
- Scenario 4: fuel tax credit manual entry → Schedule 3 line 12 → line 31
- Scenario 5: §965(i) deferred tax manual entry → Schedule 3 line 13d

### UI violations fixed — all 14 Schedule 3 input forms (Rules 7, 10, 33, 34)

## 2026-04-22 to 2026-04-25 — Azure Document Intelligence Integration

AI-powered document upload for all 42 statement types.

**Backend**: `DocumentExtractionResource.java` (multipart upload), `GenericFieldMapper.java` (config-driven, reads `field-mappings.json`), PDFBox fallback for fillable PDFs. Two Azure API paths: prebuilt tax models via `/documentintelligence/` (v2024-11-30), generic via `/formrecognizer/` (v2023-07-31).

**Frontend**: `StatementFormDirective` extended with extraction signal + `extractionApplied` event. All 36 form components updated with banner, Verify & Save, placeholder hiding.

**PDF-overlay components rebuilt**: Form 6252, Form 8606 (2-page), Form 1098-T (Copy B+A mirror), Form 1099-DA, Schedule K-1 (1041/1065/1120-S) — all using IRS background images with positioned overlay fields.

**Key fixes**: Azure SDK version mismatch (use REST), two API paths, PDFBox fallback, Angular signal infinite recursion, fieldRows getter caching, component reuse with individual *ngIf entries. Default form changed to identification-taxpayer.

## 2026-04-27 — Application Shell Redesign

Visual redesign of the application shell — topbar, person tabs, sidebar, and footer. No logic or routing changes. Purely presentation.

### Changes

**Topbar** (`app.topbar.ts` + `styles.scss`):
- Removed the 100-line PrimeNG shield SVG logo → replaced with `<i class="pi pi-calculator">` icon
- Reduced padding to `0.5rem 1.5rem`, removed shadow, clean bottom border only
- Kept: brand text, Compute button, user label, theme toggle, sign-out

**Person tabs** (`shell.component.scss`):
- Changed from filled pill style (teal background on active) to underline tab style
- Active: teal bottom border + teal text on white background
- Hover: subtle gray underline
- Bar background changed to white with bottom border

**Sidebar** (`shell.component.ts` + `shell.component.scss`):
- Removed sidebar brand block (brand-mark "T", "Personal Tax Assistant", "Filing Workspace") — redundant with topbar
- Removed sidebar footer/user block (avatar, name, role, settings) — user info is in topbar
- Freed ~120px vertical space for navigation
- Background softened: `#0f172a` → `#1e293b`, border `#1f2937` → `#334155`
- Width narrowed: 288px → 260px
- Border-radius reduced: `1.25rem` → `0.75rem`
- Shadow reduced: `0 18px 40px` → `0 8px 24px`
- Section title font-size: `0.65rem` → `0.7rem`

## 2026-05-25 — Phase 2b Steps 1–5c: Azure SQL schema design + deployment

Designed and deployed the complete normalized SQL schema for the Cloud
Firestore → Azure SQL Database migration. The schema replaces all
Firestore document collections with typed relational tables. Application
code (services, compute pipeline) still reads/writes Firestore;
service-layer rewiring is Step 5d, scheduled for the next session.

### Step 1 — Persistence discovery

Audited every read/write site in the backend. Findings written to
`C:\us-tax\data_model.md` (277 lines):
- 7 domains: Profile, Personal Forms (~50), Dependents, Statement Entries
  (~30), Tax Return Computation, User Messages, Support Requests
- 7 persistence services × ~14 REST mutation endpoints
- Typed Java models (Profile, DependentRecord, UserMessage, SupportRequest)
  versus loosely-typed `Map<String,Object>` for personal forms and
  statement entries
- ~130 computed-output classes that today persist as a single
  `users/{uid}/tax-return/latest` Firestore document

### Step 2 — Field-level inventories

Six parallel agents produced detailed field inventories (4,424 lines
total) across `C:\us-tax\`:
- `data_model_personal_identity.md` (8 forms, 207 lines)
- `data_model_personal_credits.md` (28 forms, 675 lines)
- `data_model_personal_income.md` (27 forms, 684 lines)
- `data_model_statements.md` (41 forms, 916 lines)
- `data_model_output_1040.md` (Form 1040 + schedules + container, 810 lines)
- `data_model_output_specialforms.md` (38 special forms, 1,132 lines)

### Step 3 — Schema design + ER diagrams

User-directed design choices captured up-front:
- Full normalization (no JSON-blob "tax_return" payload)
- Per-archetype personal-form tables with `owner_role` discriminator (~25
  tables for 50 legacy form IDs)
- Semantic translation of opaque PDF-slot fields (not JSONB)
- 1:1 sub-objects inlined into parent tables; only `List<T>` becomes
  child tables

Deliverables:
- `data_model_schema.md` (2,482 lines) — entity catalog: 5 core + 5
  identity + 19 credits + 21 income + ~50 statement entries + 6 tax_return
  anchor + 16 schedules + ~53 special forms = ~175 tables + 6 reference
- `data_model_er.md` (720 lines) — 11 Mermaid ER diagrams (overview + 10
  domain detail)

### Step 4 — Validation + semantic-slot generation

Surfaced a blocker: 8 statement forms (4684, 4797, 6252, 6781, 8824, K-1
1041/1065/1120s) had no semantic field names — their PDF labels are
unmapped structural identifiers like `page1_0_f1_12_0`. Used parallel
agents to manually label all 826 fields via direct visual reading of the
IRS PDFs. Outputs:
- 8 mapping JSON files at
  `us-tax-be/scripts/capital-mappings/<form>.json`
- New generator `generate-semantic-capital-forms-mapped.js` that emits
  `<form>_semantic_labels.pdf` and `<form>_field_map_semantic.csv` to
  `C:\us-tax\pdfs\` (replaces the structural-only files previously there)
- Validation doc `data_model_validation.md` documenting the resolution +
  spot-checks of `Profile.java`, W-2 Box 12, and pf_identification
  artifacts + conditional-required matrix (32 rules)

### Step 5 — Schema deployment to Azure SQL

User-selected tooling: Liquibase (over Flyway) + Hibernate ORM Panache.
User-selected migration strategy: SQL-only from 5b (no dual-write).

Three deployment-time issues hit and resolved:

1. mssql-jdbc + MSAL4J classloader visibility under Quarkus dev mode.
   `quarkus.datasource.jdbc.url` with `authentication=ActiveDirectoryDefault`
   failed at startup with "Failed to load MSAL4J Java library" even with
   msal4j 1.17.2 explicitly on the classpath and indexed via
   `quarkus.index-dependency`. Root cause: mssql-jdbc 13.2.1 rejects
   `accessToken=...` in the JDBC URL (security; tokens leak into logs).
   Resolution: use
   `quarkus.datasource.jdbc.additional-jdbc-properties.accessToken=${SQL_ACCESS_TOKEN}`
   and have `run-dev.ps1` grab a token via `az account get-access-token`
   into the env var. Production unchanged (still uses MSI on Container
   Apps).

2. Personal AAD account had no SQL user in `ustaxdb`. Phase 2a only
   granted the Container App's MSI. Wrote one-shot `grant-aad-db-user.ps1`
   that uses `SqlConnection.AccessToken` to connect as AAD-admin and run
   `CREATE USER [ali.muhammad.ca@outlook.com] FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER` +
   `ALTER ROLE db_owner ADD MEMBER`.

3. Hibernate validate mode rejected NCHAR(2) when expecting VARCHAR(2).
   `quarkus.hibernate-orm.database.generation=validate` failed on
   `pf_address.state_code` because the SQLServerDialect maps `String`
   columns to VARCHAR (not NVARCHAR) by default. Worked around for now by
   setting generation to `none`; type alignment is queued for Step 5d as
   either `@Nationalized` on entities or
   `hibernate.dialect.sqlserver.use_national_character_data=true`.

V1–V9 changesets (200 total, 193 tables):

| File | Changesets | Tables | Purpose |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| V1__core_reference_identity.sql | 21 | 15 | 6 reference + 5 core + 4 identity-domain |
| V2__personal_credits.sql | 22 | 19 | 13 archetypes + 6 children (credits/deductions) |
| V3__personal_income.sql | 21 | 21 | 13 archetypes + 8 children (income) |
| V4__statement_w2_basic_1099.sql | 23 | 21 | W-2/W-2G + 1099-NEC/MISC/K/INT/DIV/OID/B/R + state_info children |
| V5__statement_1095_1098_retirement.sql | 30 | 30 | 1095-A/B/C + covered_individual + coverage_monthly + part2/part3; 1098-T/E; 1099-Q/QA/LTC/SA/A/C/G/S/CAP/PTR; 5498; RRB-1099/-R; SSA-1099; 8606; 2439; 3921; child-interest-dividends |
| V6__statement_capital_k1.sql | 9 | 9 | 4684/4797/6252/6781/8824 + K-1 1041/1065/1120s + 1099-DA. 971 cols. Auto-generated by `scripts/generate-v6-statement-ddl.js` from `pdfs/*_field_map_semantic.csv`. |
| V7__tax_return_form_1040.sql | 7 | 6 | tax_return anchor + 154-column out_form_1040 + dependent/other-earned-income/required-attachment children + tax_return_flag |
| V8__schedules.sql | 17 | 16 | out_schedule_1/1a/2/3/a/b/d/8812 + 8 child line-item tables |
| V9__special_forms.sql | 35 | 53 | 38 special forms + List<T> children (6251, 2441/+, 5329, 4952, 8995/8995A, 8962/+, 8863/+, 8839/+, 8880, 8862, 8859, 8396, 8834, 8911/+, 8912/+, 8801 with 55 lines, 4868, 8959, 2210/+, 8888/+, ScheduleR, per-person 8606/2555/4972/4137/+/8919/+, list 4852/8814/1116/+/8911sa/8936sa/5695/+, 8949 3-level) |

Final verification (via `verify-schema.ps1`):

    DATABASECHANGELOG: 200 rows
    Total base tables: 193
      core: 7   reference: 6   pf_*: 47   se_*: 60   out_*: 71   liquibase: 2
    ref_filing_status: 5 rows (S/MFJ/MFS/HOH/QSS seeded)
    ref_relationship: 16 rows seeded

### Remaining work (Step 5d/5e)

- Re-enable Hibernate validate mode (national-character data alignment).
- Write ~183 more `@Entity` classes for V2–V9 tables.
- Repository interfaces.
- Rewire `ProfileService` → `PersonalDataService` → `StatementDataService`
  → `TaxReturnDataService` + `TaxReturnComputeService.compute()` to use
  SQL instead of Firestore.
- Re-run 765 unit tests + e2e + smoke.
- Decommission Firestore paths from migrated services (5e).
